gmw_00004555.name: NGC 3344
gmw_00004555.overview: NGC 3344 is an isolated, mildly inclined barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:2206.10051, arXiv:2108.04387] featuring a flocculent spiral structure [arXiv:2206.10051], a small bar [arXiv:1704.06321, arXiv:2108.04387], and ring-like features [arXiv:1704.06321]. It hosts an extended ultraviolet disk with active outer-disk star formation [arXiv:2110.07590, arXiv:2105.13394] supported by a large atomic-gas reservoir [arXiv:2110.07590].

sources.summary: The paper investigates NGC 3344's XUV disk, finding active outer-disk star formation, a large atomic-gas reservoir, inside-out growth evidence, and CGM absorption consistent with gradual gas accretion.

quotes.quote: We present our investigation of the Extended Ultraviolet (XUV) disk galaxy, NGC 3344, conducted as part of Deciphering the Interplay between the Interstellar medium, Stars, and the Circumgalactic medium (DIISC) survey. We use surface and aperture photometry of individual young stellar complexes to study star formation and its effect on the physical properties of the interstellar medium. We measure the specific star-formation rate (sSFR) and find it to increase from 10^ 10 yr^ 1 in the inner disk to >10^ 8 yr^ 1 in the extended disk. This provides evidence for inside-out disk growth.

This galaxy is known to exhibit a Type-I extended UV disk showing UV-bright structures at large galactocentric distances ( > R_ 25 ), indicating recent star formation in the outer disk thilk07 . NGC 3344 is ideal to probe the connections between gas flows and young stars, as -- (1) it is undergoing active star formation in the outer disk, where stellar densities are low, thus making it easy to identify individual star-forming regions, (2) it possesses a huge gas reservoir in the form of an extended \ disk of M( )= 2.2 10^9 M_ that is sustaining star formation, and (3) there is a UV-bright Quasi-stellar object (QSO) at an impact parameter of 29.5 kpc that allows us to probe the CGM close to the disk (Figure fig:ngc3344 ).

Interestingly, the cold gas mass of this galaxy is dominated by atomic gas (87 of total gas mass), and the depletion time for \ is almost an order-of-magnitude that of molecular gas. This may suggest that perhaps this is an accretion event that is smooth and gradual, not to disrupt the disk but to build the immense gas reservoir.

sources.summary: WiNDS finds NGC 3344 is an isolated, mildly inclined spiral with a coherent residual-velocity oscillation, previous HI warping, and strong evidence for vertical disk perturbations.

quotes.quote: In Figure  fig:candidate_ngc3344 , we show the results obtained for the galaxy NGC 3344. This galaxy has a very low morphologycal inclination angle i 18 , 1997A&AS..124..109P and, thus, represents an ideal candidate to search for vertical perturbations on its galactic disk. In panel a) we show a B band image of the galaxy, obtained from the XDSS. The galaxy shows a well-defined flocculent spiral structure and a weak bar inside the inner ring 2000A&A...356..827V .

NGC 3344 is a galaxy that is cataloged as isolated, with no nearby galaxies within a projected radial distance of 150 kpc and radial velocity difference 500 \ km \ s^ 1 . We recall that NGC 3344 has a very mildly inclined disk, i 18^ . Thus, the contribution from in-plane flows to the resulting V _ res , associated with e.g. bars and/or spiral structure, is expected to be small. Our analysis of NGC 3344, with a spectral resolution 10 km s ^ 1 , reveals a very complex structure of NGC 3344.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3344 has a strong color gradient and XUV disk, with red central light, young blue outskirts, radial SFH variation, and extended H I.

quotes.quote: NGC 3344 NGC 3344 has a well-established strong B-R color gradient prugniel1998 . NGC 3344 has also been identified as having an XUV disc that fades more slowly than the optical disc thilker2007 . The analysis of the observations done here show both of these attributes of NGC 3344.

As is seen in the colour image of Figure fig:ngc3344_4panel , as well as the profiles of Figure fig:ngc3344_fulldata , NGC 3344 has a very red (bright in the NIR) centre, but a strong young, blue (particularly in the B+NUV) stellar component in the outskirts of the galaxy. The SED-fitted, cumulative SFH results show a variation in time of the onset of recent star formation across the disc.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC3344 among the six nearby spirals with apparent pattern coupling, noting its bar and arguing prior CR interpretations missed the coupling.

quotes.quote: All galaxies demonstrate a multi-arm morphology, often with two main arms of greater contrast, as seen in images from the DESI Legacy Imaging Surveys 2019AJ....157..168D in Figure fig:thumbnail . According to the literature, only NGC3344 and NGC5371 contain a noticeable bar. It is easy to see that all 6 galaxies demonstrate the apparent pattern coupling, visible in this Figure. Finally, if we also mark the bar size, which is 25.5 arcsec for NGC3344 and 19.3 arcsec for NGC5371 2015A&A...582A..86H , and taking into account that the bar ends near its CR, we find that the bar is coupled with ILR of spiral pattern for both galaxies.

As already mentioned, 2011ApJ...741L..14F,2014ApJS..210....2F studied changes of radial velocity sign and found them in many cases, including the presented NGC3344, NGC3893, and NGC5676 (UGC5840, UGC6778 and UGC9366 in their works). For example, 2014ApJS..210....2F for NGC3344 interpret last CR as OLR of the second pattern, without mentioning the coupling.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3344 as an isolated barred spiral with inner coverage, ring-like structure, double-power-law clustering, and slight age evolution.

quotes.quote: NGC 3344 is an isolated barred spiral galaxy with a morphological classification of SABbc, located at a distance of 7 Mpc. With apparent dimensions of 7 1 x 6 5, we have a single pointing of NGC 3344 that covers most of the inner region of the galaxy. There is a presence of ring-like morphological features at 1 kpc and 7 kpc, with a small bar present within the inner ring verdes-montenegro00 .
All galaxies are best-fit with a single power law, excluding NGC 628 and NGC 3344, which are best-represented by a double power law.

NGC 7793, NGC 6503, and NGC 3344 only exhibit a slight change in the clustering with age, and within each of the cases, there are a lack of any older ( 50 Myr) clusters at the smallest spatial scales.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3344 as an isolated barred SABbc spiral with ring-like structures and a small inner-ring bar, previously studied for spiral dynamics and included in LEGUS.

quotes.quote: NGC 3344 NGC 3344 is an isolated barred spiral galaxy (morphology SABbc) with two ring-like morphological features at 1 and 7 kpc, and a small bar within the inner ring see, for e.g. Montenegro_2000 . This galaxy was included in the sample studied in Grasha_2017_Spatial . Meidt_2009 analysed the spiral structure and dynamics in this galaxy using H i and CO gas. The LEGUS survey observed NGC 3344 with a single-pointing.

sources.summary: NGC 3344 is described as an isolated spiral with an updated distance and no gas-containing satellite candidates in ALFALFA, a result confirmed by ELVES coverage.

quotes.quote: NGC 3344 NGC 3344 is a relatively isolated SAB()bc galaxy. Similar to NGC 3274, we adopt the updated TRGB distance D=8.3 Mpc sabbi_resolved_2018 in replacement of D_ = 9.8 Mpc. All of our satellite search methods consistently found zero candidates for NGC 3344, indicating that its vicinity is lacking gas-containing satellites at ALFALFA detectability, which at a higher than average rms beam noise, _ S21 = 3.71 mJy, decreases from approximately 10^ 6.7 M_ to 10^ 6.9 M_ locally (similar to subsec:ngc925 ). This result is confirmed by ELVES, which also finds zero gaseous satellites within a 300 kpc coverage (comparable to 2R_ 200 ; see sec:elves_saga_discussion ).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3344 as a low-inclination sample galaxy, one of the most discrepant M91/PT05 gradient cases, with integrated abundance matching gas-phase abundance near 0.8 R25.

quotes.quote: The distribution of inclination angles in our sample is fairly broad, ranging from 11^ in NGC 3344, to 72^ in NGC 3198; the mean inclination angle is 41^ 19^ .

The two slopes generally correlate, although the PT05 gradients are systematically shallower than the corresponding gradients derived using the M91 oxygen abundances. The four most discrepant examples are NGC 3521, NGC 3344, NGC 1058, and NGC 4303. In all cases, the PT05 calibration predicts a much flatter relationship between galactocentric position and oxygen abundance, a result which has profound implications for our understanding of the chemical evolution properties of spiral galaxies e.g., molla96, pilyugin06 .
We find that _ int / _ 25 ranges from 0.2 in NGC 2903, to 0.8 in NGC 3344, with a mean value of 0.47 0.15 .

gmw_00004556.name: NGC 5668
gmw_00004556.overview: NGC 5668 is a nearby, nearly face-on late-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:2206.10051, arXiv:1205.1936] that features a weak central bar or oval structure [arXiv:2206.10051]. The galaxy exhibits a high rate of star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0004295] and contains an aligned neutral hydrogen and ionized gas warp [arXiv:2206.10051].

sources.summary: WiNDS presents NGC 5668 as a nearly face-on late-type spiral with global H-alpha residual perturbations, an aligned HI/H-alpha warp, high-velocity gas, and possible past interaction.

quotes.quote: In Figure  fig:candidate_ngc5668 we present NGC 5668, a nearly face-on late-type spiral galaxy with an estimated inclination angle 18^ 1996AJ....112..960S and R _ opt of 14.3 kpc, located at 27.6 \ Mpc . The galaxy is classified as a SA(s)d by RC3 and, on its optical image (panel a), it shows a weak bar or oval structure on its inner 12'' region. Observations of NGC 5668 were extracted from the GHASP sample 2008MNRAS.390..466E .

The V _ res map, shown on panel d), reveals global perturbations with amplitudes > 20 \ km \ s^ 1 km/s which are also consistent with a warped H_ disk. High-resolution observations in the 21-cm line of the neutral hydrogen velocity field, from the Very Large Array (VLA), have detected a kinematic warp that begins, at least, at the end of the optical radius 1996AJ....112..960S . Note that the H_ warp is very well aligned with the previously reported HI warp, indicating that the perturbation extends even within the optical radius of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 5668 as a nearly face-on spiral with strong star formation, HVCs, turbulent ionized gas, and likely shell or chimney structures in its disk.

quotes.quote: We have observed the nearly face-on spiral galaxy NGC 5668 with the TAURUS II Fabry-Perot interferometer at the William Herschel Telescope using the H line to study the kinematics of the ionized gas. From the extracted data cube we construct intensity, velocity and velocity dispersion maps. We calculate the rotation curve in the innermost 2 arcmin and we use the residual velocity field to look for regions with important vertical motions. By comparing the geometry of these regions in the residual velocity field with the geometry in the intensity and velocity dispersion maps we are able to select some regions which are very likely to be shells or chimneys in the disk.

Therefore we have chosen the spiral galaxy NGC 5668 which is already known to have HVC's and an important rate of star formation. These facts make it a perfect candidate for us to detect important vertical motions in its disk.

sources.summary: NGC 5668 is described as a nearby, nearly face-on bulgeless galaxy with less prominent spiral structure and diffuse extended radio emission over its inner disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 3346 and NGC 5668 : Both galaxies are relatively nearby and nearly face-on in orientation. NGC 3346 has a small bar in the center that is associated with a fairly strong spiral structure. NGC 5668 has less prominent spiral structure. The NVSS maps shows diffuse and extended radio emission distributed over the inner disks of both galaxies.

gmw_00004557.name: NGC 2763
gmw_00004557.overview: NGC 2763 is an isolated, low-inclination spiral disk galaxy [arXiv:2206.10051]. Its structure features a small bar, two main spiral arms with multiple sub-arms, and a significantly lopsided morphology [arXiv:2206.10051].

sources.summary: WiNDS describes NGC 2763 as an isolated low-inclination spiral with lopsided morphology, extended H-alpha emission, complex residual velocity flows, and uncertain perturbation origin.

quotes.quote: In Figure fig:candidate_ngc2763 we show NGC 2763. This low inclination disk galaxy ( i 29.5^ ) is the only object in WiNDS observed in the Southern Hemisphere, and one of the first galaxies observed with the SAM-FP instrument 2017MNRAS.469.3424M . The B band image on panel a) reveals two main spiral arms, each with multiple sub-arms, and a small bar. In addition, it shows a significant lopsided structure.

Interestingly, no detailed kinematical studies of NGC 2763 velocity field, nor companion galaxies within 150 kpc, have been reported. We have confirmed the lack of massive nearby companions by performing a systematic search within a radial projected distance of 250 kpc and radial velocity difference 1000 \ km \ s^ 1 using NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Data base. Thus, NGC 2763 is considered as an isolated galaxy. As such, it is not clear whether its significantly perturbed morphology and kinematics are the results of a previous minor merger event or due to significant misaligned smooth gas accretion.

gmw_00004558.name: The Centaurus A core
gmw_00004558.overview: The Centaurus A core is the closest active galactic nucleus, located at a distance of 3.8 Mpc [arXiv:1907.03666]. Classified as a Fanaroff-Riley type-I radio galaxy, it features a parsec-scale jet and counter-jet system alongside giant radio-emitting lobes [arXiv:1907.03666].

sources.summary: The paper describes the Centaurus A core as the closest AGN, a weak VHE-emitting Fanaroff-Riley radio galaxy with jets, inner lobes, and giant radio lobes.

quotes.quote: The Centaurus A core (R.A.: 13 25 26.4; Dec. -43 00 42; 3.8 Mpc). This is by far the closest (3.8 Mpc) AGN, which was found to be a weak VHE emitter by the HESS observatory among others Aharonian2009 . The source is also classified as a type-I Fanaroff-Riley radio galaxy, and includes a parsec-scale jet and counter-jet system, a kiloparsec-scale jet and inner lobes, and giant radio-emitting lobes on a scale of hundreds of kiloparsecs. The region containing the radio core and the jets was observed with HESS from 0.3 to 10 TeV during a first long campaign from 2004 to 2008 and a second one from 2008 to 2010. Remarkably, over such a long sampling period of 6 years, no significant spectral or intensity variations were found, in particular none between the two cumulated datasets.

gmw_00004559.name: NGC 4396
gmw_00004559.overview: NGC 4396 is an edge-on spiral galaxy in the Virgo cluster that is undergoing ram-pressure stripping [arXiv:0911.2476, arXiv:2306.04513]. It features a neutral hydrogen and radio continuum tail extending to the northwest [arXiv:1001.3597, arXiv:2306.04513], along with enhanced star formation on its leading southeastern edge [arXiv:2306.04513].

sources.summary: NGC 4396 is interpreted as an edge-on Virgo galaxy whose HI and polarized-intensity hot spots coincide, with disk-aligned and extraplanar polarized emission tracing magnetic draping.

quotes.quote: NGC 4396 Small, blue galaxy at 1 o'clock, edge-on orientation ( = -133 km/s ). The location of the HI hot spot coincides with that of the PI and is centred on the galaxy in projection ( =12:25:58 , =15:40:27 ). There is a polarised radio continuum ridge in the northwestern part of the outer disk where the B-field mostly aligned with the disk. In the southeastern part we observe weak extraplanar polarised emission with field vectors pointing perpendicular to the disk. In our interpretation, the galaxy is moving out of the plane towards southeast and draping the radial cluster field which has a non-negligible LOS component around it while the planar and extraplanar emission directly traces the magnetic drape.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4396 has truncated total-power emission, a northwest radio continuum tail, faint extraplanar polarization, and a polarization degree increasing toward the tail.

quotes.quote: The 20 cm and 6 cm total power emission of NGC 4396 is truncated within the optical disk and shows a tail structure to the northwest (Fig.  fig:n4396 ). As the H i tail, the radio continuum tail is bend to the north, but it is less extended than the H i tail. We do not detect a disk-wide radio halo. The 6 cm polarized emission is mainly found in the southeast of the galactic disk extending into the tail.

sources.summary: VCC 865 shows LOFAR gradients, a tail-like extension, and enhanced leading-side emission consistent with ram-pressure stripping and induced star formation.

quotes.quote: VCC 865 (NGC 4396) The presence of a tail in neutral hydrogen Chung2007Tails is a sign of ram-pressure stripping also acting on this galaxy. However, a lack of compression in the contours Chung2009 and a radio-to-infrared deficit Murphy2009 on the leading edge make the interpretation somewhat unclear. In the low-resolution LOFAR map, the radio emission shows a strong gradient towards southeast with a tail-like extension in the opposite direction, thus, favoring the scenario that the galaxy suffering from ram-pressure stripping while falling into the cluster center. Within the optical disk, the LOFAR images reveal enhanced radio emission in the leading-half of the galaxy, coincident with a blue region of current or recent star-formation in the optical.

gmw_00004560.name: NGC 4654
gmw_00004560.overview: NGC 4654 is a Milky Way-like spiral galaxy in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:2404.08910] that features an off-center molecular bar [arXiv:astro-ph/0401104] and a complex nuclear star-cluster system [arXiv:2404.08910]. It exhibits an asymmetric gas distribution with a southeastern atomic tail [arXiv:1204.0430, arXiv:2305.14823] and compressed gas along its northwestern edge [arXiv:2210.05381, arXiv:2305.14823], driven by a past tidal interaction and ongoing ram pressure [arXiv:astro-ph/0211321, arXiv:astro-ph/0505021].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4654 as an extended-H I Virgo spiral with tidal and low ram-pressure effects, asymmetric molecular gas, central high efficiency, and low southeastern star formation efficiency.

quotes.quote: NGC 4654 (Fig. fig:plot_n4654 ): has a very extended H i distribution with a southeastern low surface density tail (Phookun Mundy 1995). The star formation distribution also shows two small tails in this direction. Dynamical modelling suggests that this galaxy had a rapid tidal interaction in the past and is now undergoing low ram pressure stripping (Vollmer 2003). The distribution of the molecular gas is asymmetric with more CO to the northwestern sharp edge of the H i distribution. SFE_ tot is high in the galaxy center and the northwestern gas surface density maximum.

It is particularly low in the southeast. The star formation is approximately proportional to the molecular gas surface density with a large scatter ( 0.7 dex) due to the poor signal-to-noise ratio of the CO data.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4654 as a Virgo spiral with asymmetric H I and stellar structure, concluding that a past tidal encounter plus continuing ram pressure best explains its gas tail and kinematics.

quotes.quote: The Virgo cluster spiral galaxy NGC 4654 is supposed to be a good candidate for ongoing ram pressure stripping based on its very asymmetric H i distribution. However, this galaxy also shows an asymmetric stellar distribution. Numerical simulations using ram pressure as the only perturbation can produce a tail structure of the gas content, but cannot account for its kinematical structure.

Simulations using a gravitational interaction with the companion galaxy NGC 4639 can account for the asymmetric stellar distribution of NGC 4654, but cannot reproduce the observed extended gas tail. Only a mixed interaction, gravitational and ram pressure, can reproduce all observed properties of NGC 4654. It is concluded that NGC 4654 had a tidal interaction 500 Myr ago and is continuing to experience ram pressure.

sources.summary: NGC 4654 is interpreted as a Virgo spiral whose cometary H I tail and polarized structure are best explained by combined tidal interaction and ram-pressure effects.

quotes.quote: Another galaxy in which tidal forces may at least play some role is NGC 4654, which is probably interacting with the neighbouring NGC 4639. However, the cometary shape of its H i distribution and the gaseous tail extending towards the SW may be caused by ram pressure effects as the galaxy moves through the ICM (Phookun Mundy ). The galaxy shows no strong central source, probably because of less pronounced central mass concentration as expected for its much later type (Sc) than NGC 4438. We note that the B-vectors clearly follow the tail (as expected for its outflowing motion), rather than the spiral pattern in this part of the disk. Recent simulations of the magnetic field of this galaxy (Vollmer et al. , confirmed by Soida et al. ) suggest that a mixture of tidal interaction with some ram pressure effects is the best explanation of the radio polarization structure of NGC 4654.

sources.summary: NGC 4654 is highlighted as an inclined Virgo galaxy with a ram-pressure-stripped tail, northwest HI hot spot, and polarized ridge used as a clear magnetic-draping case.

quotes.quote: NGC 4654 Medium size, green galaxy at 9 o'clock, inclined orientation ( = 1035 km/s ). This galaxy has a large velocity component in the plane of the sky as its redshift corresponds to the cluster mean. The location of the `HI hot spot' in the northwest and the ram pressure stripped tail pointing in the opposite direction makes this galaxy a beautiful case for studying magnetic draping. The fact that the HI hot spot is not exactly at the rim but in projection slightly inwards located suggests an additional small LOS velocity component. Almost coincident with the HI hot spot ( =12:43:52 , =13:08:18 ), we observe a ridge of polarised emission at the northwestern outer rim of the galactic disk with a maximum that is slightly shifted north due to the `direction-of-motion asymmetry'.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 4654 as a Virgo ram-pressure case where compressed atomic gas affects molecular ratios, raises molecular star-formation efficiency, and produces an elevated CO-to-H2 conversion ratio.

quotes.quote: Moreover, detailed studies of Virgo's NGC 4654 galaxy by show that, even though R_ mol values in the north-west appear to be lower when compared to other regions with similar total gas surface density, both the atomic gas surface density, _ atom , and the SFE _ mol seem to be higher. They associate this effect with atomic gas being compressed, which consequently increases the molecular gas surface density, _ mol .

For instance, note that NGC 4654 shows some strongly compressed atomic gas that exceeds _ crit . They also found a CO-to-H _2 conversion ratio a factor 2 higher than the Galactic value and SFE _ mol values around 1.5-2 higher than the rest of the disk.

Out of the 38 VERTICO galaxies selected for this work, at least five of them are well studied cases of RPS: NGC 4501, NGC 4548, NGC 4569, NGC 4579, and NGC 4654 e.g., see also Boselli2022 and references therein Cayatte1994,Boselli2006,Vollmer2012a,Boselli2016,Lizee2021 .

sources.summary: NGC 4654 is described as a Milky Way-like Virgo spiral whose centre hosts a complex nuclear star-cluster system; JWST/HST data reveal three clusters, distinct gas emission, and stellar kinematics.

quotes.quote: We present a detailed study of the centre of NGC 4654, a Milky Way-like spiral galaxy in the Virgo cluster that has been reported to host a double stellar nucleus, thus promising a rare view of ongoing star cluster infall into a galaxy nucleus. Analysing JWST NIRSpec integral-field spectroscopic data in combination with Hubble Space Telescope Wide Field Camera 3 imaging of the inner 330 330 pc, we find that the NGC 4645 nucleus is in fact more complex than previously thought, harbouring three massive star clusters within 25 pc of the centre. Maps of infrared emission lines in the NIRSpec spectra show different morphologies for the ionised and molecular gas components. The emission from molecular hydrogen gas is concentrated at the NSC location, while emission from hydrogen recombination lines is more extended beyond the central cluster. The velocity fields of both gas and stars indicate that the three clusters are part of a complicated dynamical system, with the NSC having an elevated velocity dispersion in line with its high stellar mass.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4654 with asymmetric radio emission, a northwest sharp edge, faint emission toward its southeastern H I tail, and peak polarization near the south-eastern tip.

quotes.quote: The 20 cm and 6 cm total power emission roughly follows the stellar distribution of the galactic disk (Fig.  fig:n4654 ). Along the major axis, the emission is stronger to the northwest than to the southeast. We observe a sharp edge of the emission distribution to the northwest and some faint extended emission in the direction of the prominent southeastern H i tail. The 6 cm polarized emission distribution is asymmetric with a maximum south of the galaxy center well within the H i distribution.

sources.summary: The VERTICO paper uses NGC4654 as an example galaxy, showing its one-sided atomic-gas tail, a gas feature visible in both atomic and molecular maps, and reported molecular-gas compression.

quotes.quote: In Fig. fig:example_img we show optical, , and images for an example galaxy from our sample, NGC4654. As a visual reference for A_ mod , we also show maps of _ 180 as applied to the CO and gas surface density maps. For , the one-sided tail to the southeast stands out in the _ 180 map as a large group of pixels with _ 180 = +1.0 . There is also a relatively bright gas feature on the northwest edge of NGC4654 that is visible in the _ 180 map for both and CO.
NGC4522, NGC4654, evidence for molecular gas compression has already been reported in literature chung2014,nehlig2016,lee2017,cramer2020,lizee2021 .

sources.summary: The paper notes that VCC1987 lacks obvious central activity but probably underwent tidal interaction, is now experiencing ram pressure, has an off-center molecular bar, and may be developing central activity.

quotes.quote: VCC1450, VCC1535 and VCC1987 do not show any particular central activity. It might be worth noting that VCC1987 (NGC 4654) has most probably undergone a tidal interaction and is experiencing ram pressure now (Vollmer 2003). Moreover, it shows an off-center molecular bar (Sofue et al. 2003). So one could speculate that it is just developing a central activity.

sources.summary: NGC 4654 is used as the close analogue to NGC 4254, with a one-armed structure and gas tail explained by tidal interaction plus ram pressure.

quotes.quote: NGC 4254 resembles in many respects NGC 4654 (Phookun Mundy 1995), another bright Virgo cluster spiral galaxy that is located at about the same projected distance from the cluster center, but at the opposite side of the cluster, since both galaxies (i) show a prominent m=1 spiral structure, (ii) are not H i deficient, and (iii) have an extended, very low surface density gas tail. Vollmer (2003) showed that the m=1 spiral structure of NGC 4654 can be explained by a tidal interaction of NGC 4654 with its companion NGC 4639 that took place 500 Myr ago, while its extended gas tail can be explained by ongoing ram pressure stripping (Gunn Gott 1972). Since the outer part of the gas disk was perturbed by the gravitational interaction, only a small amount of ram pressure is needed to create the gas tail. Thus, only the combination of a close encounter with NGC 4639 followed by continuing moderate ram pressure can reproduce all observed properties of NGC 4654.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 4654 resembles NGC 4501 in disk behavior, prefers an enhanced source term at 20 cm, and belongs to the streaming-dominated perturbed Virgo spirals.

quotes.quote: The galactic disk of NGC 4654 behaves in a similar way as that of NGC 4501.
For NGC 4654, only for the 20 cm model an enhanced source term is preferred.
Streaming dominates in the disks of the perturbed Virgo spiral galaxies NGC 4321, NGC 4303, NGC 4254, NGC 4501, and NGC 4654.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4654 simulations of a rapid flyby and finds strong radio/SFR anticorrelation plus shear-driven high fractional polarization in low-SFR regions.

quotes.quote: NGC 4654 is perturbed by a rapid flyby of a spherical galaxy with a mass of 9.2 10^ 10 M _ .

The two perturbed galaxies NGC 4254 and NGC 4654, which show a weak or absent correlation between the surface brightness of polarized radio continuum emission and the radio/SFR ratio, present a moderate or strong ( > 0.5) correlation between the fractional polarization (FP) and the radio/SFR ratio (second column of Table tab:coeffs2 ).
The steep SFR--FP correlations of NGC 4535 and NGC 4654 (fourth column of Table tab:coeffs2 ) are due to a high FP in regions of low SFR caused by interaction-induced shear motions.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4654 as having an extended southern outer-disk ridge of polarized emission, additional western and northern rim emission, and no polarized detection in its southeastern H i tail.

quotes.quote: NGC 4654 shows an extended ridge of polarized emission in the southern part of the outer disk. We also find emission at the western and northern outer rim of the galactic disk. We do not detect any polarized emission in the south eastern extended H i tail (Phookun Mundy 1995).

gmw_00004561.name: PJ105353.0
gmw_00004561.overview: PJ105353.0 is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of z = 3.0053 [arXiv:1603.05622]. It is one of the brightest SPIRE/AzTEC sources, exhibiting strong dusty emission that is interpreted as being gravitationally lensed [arXiv:1603.05622].

sources.summary: PJ105353.0 is one of the brightest SPIRE/AzTEC sources, placed at z=3.0053 by CO, interpreted as high-redshift lensed dusty emission with possible radio AGN excess.

quotes.quote:
One of 
egal sources observed by the "Must-Do" 	extit{Herschel} SPIRE program, PJ105353.0, also known as the radio source NVSS J105352+055623, is one of our brightest sources. It has a 350  flux density of 996 100 mJy, and it peaks in the 250  band at 1028 103 mJy. This is the second brightest AzTEC source we detected with S_ 1100 =98 10 mJy, well centered on the 	extit{Herschel} SPIRE 350  peak position (see Fig.  fig:aztec ). The bright line detected by the RSR at 86.334 GHz (Fig.  fig:RSR ) is robustly identified as CO (3--2) line by the photometric redshift support (see Fig.  fig:photoz ), placing this object at z = 3.0053 0.0003 .

gmw_00004562.name: PJ112714.5
gmw_00004562.overview: PJ112714.5 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy at a redshift of approximately 2.236 [arXiv:1811.11215, arXiv:1603.05622]. It forms a giant submillimeter arc that is strongly gravitationally lensed [arXiv:1811.11215], and it contains multiple gas phases with comparable component masses [arXiv:1811.11215].

sources.summary: The Emerald is a z=2.236 giant submillimeter arc; its CO modeling robustly requires multiple gas phases, with excited gas dominating the SLED and comparable component masses.

quotes.quote: Secondly, in Ca nameras et al. (2018a, A A accepted), we have extensively studied the foreground mass distribution of PLCK G165.7+67.0 (the ``Emerald'') which forms a giant submm arc at z=2.236 , behind two small groups of galaxies embedded within a massive cluster at z=0.351 .

The two components are also apparent in PLCK G113.7+61.0, PLCK G138.6+62.0, and PLCK G165.7+67.0, where the single-component model significantly underestimates the CO(1--0) and CO(3--2) fluxes (see Fig. fig:sled2 ). The overall SLEDs of these sources are nevertheless nearly constant up to J_ up =6 and dominated by the excited gas phase. For this reason we checked that the second lower excitation phase is also detected after removing the CO(1--0) lines from the analysis, and found that the CO(3--2) fluxes remain underestimated by the new models. We therefore conclude that the detection of multiple gas phases in PLCK G113.7+61.0, PLCK G138.6+62.0, and PLCK G165.7+67.0 is robust and that the properties of the low excitation component inferred with and without the CO(1--0) fluxes are consistent within 1 .

The best-fitting models in Figs. fig:g244 and fig:sled2 indicate that the low-excitation component dominates the overall mass budget in PLCK G092.5+42.9, PLCK G113.7+61.0, PLCK G138.6+ 62.0, and PLCK G244.8+54.9, while the mass of both components are comparable in PLCK G165.7+67.0.

sources.summary: PJ112714.5 is a bright Must-Do source with AzTEC and mid-infrared counterparts; its RSR line gives z=2.2357, and foreground structure suggests possible group lensing.

quotes.quote:
PJ112714.5 is a 
egal ``Must-Do" survey source and is one of our brightest sources with a flat SED across the 	extit{Herschel} SPIRE channels, peaking in the 250  band. An AzTEC source with S_ 1100 =24 2 mJy is detected coincident with the 	extit{Herschel} source as seen in Fig.  fig:aztec , and it has a red 	extit{WISE} mid-IR counterpart with detections in all four bands. The observed SED supports the RSR detected line at 106.877 GHz to be CO (3-2) at a z = 2.2357 0.0002 (see Fig.  fig:photoz ). canameras15 identify the single line they detected using the IRAM 30m telescope as CO (3--2) line at `` z=2.2 ".

gmw_00004563.name: PJ120207.6
gmw_00004563.overview: PJ120207.6 is a gravitationally lensed, high-redshift dusty starburst galaxy [arXiv:2207.06967] with a CO redshift of z=2.4417 [arXiv:1603.05622]. It is characterized by high apparent far-infrared luminosity [arXiv:2207.06967] and exhibits detected HCN(1-0) emission [arXiv:2207.06967].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1202+5354 as a lensed Planck Dusty GEM with high apparent FIR luminosity, uncertain Tully-Fisher magnification, and a detected HCN(1-0) line.

quotes.quote: We detect HCN(1--0) emission in one source (J1202+5354, 4.4 ), with a tentative HCO ^+ (1--0) detection in another (J1609+6045, 3.3 ).

The remaining two sources are drawn from the Planck 's Dusty GEMs - Gravitationally Enhanced sub-Millimetre Sources ( Canameras2015 , where J1202 is listed as PLCK G138.6+62.0 and J1609 as PLCK G092.5+42.9). All Planck DSFGs have very high apparent (lensed) FIR luminosity ( L_ FIR 10^ 14 L_ ). Although the observations towards these sources are relatively shallow (1-2 hours per source), they still achieve high sensitivity in HCN/FIR and HCN/CO ratios.

All sources except J1202 have robust lens models derived from high-resolution mm-wave continuum imaging; for J1202, the magnification is derived from a Tully-Fisher relation and can carry significant uncertainty Harrington2021 .

sources.summary: PJ120207 is described as a very bright 350-micron peaker with AzTEC and red mid-infrared counterparts, CO redshift z=2.4417, and likely foreground-galaxy lensing.

quotes.quote:
PJ120207 is another 
egal ``Must-Do" survey source and is one of our brightest 350  peakers ( S_ 350 = 646 mJy) with L_ IR 10^ 14 L_ . The observed SED is flat across the 250 and 350  bands and drops slightly at 500 . An AzTEC source with S_ 1100 =69 7 mJy is detected coincident with the 	extit{Herschel} source as seen in Fig.  fig:aztec , and it has a red 	extit{WISE} mid-IR counterpart with detections in all four bands. No optical galaxy is found at the AzTEC position, but there is an SDSS source (SDSS J120207.68+533432.5) located 6.8  away from the AzTEC position, and this z_ ph 0.43 galaxy is a possible lensing source canameras15 .

gmw_00004564.name: PJ132302.9
gmw_00004564.overview: PJ132302.9 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy [arXiv:1811.11215, arXiv:1603.05622] that is strongly gravitationally lensed [arXiv:1811.11215]. It features multiple robust gas phases [arXiv:1811.11215], including a low-excitation molecular gas component [arXiv:1811.11215] and a flat CO excitation ladder [arXiv:1811.11215].

sources.summary: PLCK G113.7+61.0 has a flat CO ladder and robust multiple gas phases; its low-excitation component peaks at J=2-3 and may contain most molecular gas.

quotes.quote: For this reason, the only two CO transitions detected in PLCK G145.2+50.9 and the flat ladder of PLCK G113.7+61.0 in the range J_ up =4 -8 will, to a certain extent, prevent us from deriving detailed gas properties in these sources.

The two components are also apparent in PLCK G113.7+61.0, PLCK G138.6+62.0, and PLCK G165.7+67.0, where the single-component model significantly underestimates the CO(1--0) and CO(3--2) fluxes (see Fig. fig:sled2 ). The overall SLEDs of these sources are nevertheless nearly constant up to J_ up =6 and dominated by the excited gas phase. For this reason we checked that the second lower excitation phase is also detected after removing the CO(1--0) lines from the analysis, and found that the CO(3--2) fluxes remain underestimated by the new models. We therefore conclude that the detection of multiple gas phases in PLCK G113.7+61.0, PLCK G138.6+62.0, and PLCK G165.7+67.0 is robust and that the properties of the low excitation component inferred with and without the CO(1--0) fluxes are consistent within 1 .

For PLCK G113.7+61.0 and PLCK G138.6+62.0, the two other GEMS with CO(1--0) measurements, the lower excitation component included in the LVG analysis peaks at J_ up =2 -3. This is characteristic of diffuse gas, as already discussed in Sect. ssec:lvg , and includes between 50 and 80 of the total molecular gas mass in the systems, depending on whether CO(1--0) is included in the fit or not.

sources.summary: PJ132302 is a bright 350-micron source with L_IR near 10^14 Lsun; AzTEC, mid-infrared, CO, and radio data support a lensed high-redshift SMG interpretation.

quotes.quote:
PJ132302 is a bright 350  source ( S_ 35mu = 590 mJy) with L_ IR 10^ 14 L_ , and the dust peak of this 
egal ``Must-Do" survey source occurs in the 250  band with S_ 250 = 652 mJy. An AzTEC source with S_ 1100 =33 3 mJy is detected coincident with the 	extit{Herschel} source as seen in Fig.  fig:aztec , and it also has a red 	extit{WISE} mid-IR counterpart with detections in all four bands (see Fig.  fig:postage2 ). The SDSS image shows a crowded field with a cluster of sources that are identified as ``stars" by the SDSS database. The nearest match to the 	extit{Herschel} source is SDSS J132302.83+5535555.1, which is consistent with a z=0.48 galaxy when analysed together with the 	extit{WISE} and 2MASS photometry (see Fig.  fig:SEDs ), possibly representing the foreground lensing galaxy.

gmw_00004565.name: PJ142823.9
gmw_00004565.overview: PJ142823.9 is a hyperluminous infrared galaxy located at a redshift of z = 1.325 [arXiv:1603.05622]. It is also identified as a bright extragalactic source with detected CO (2-1) line emission [arXiv:1603.05622].

sources.summary: PJ142823.9 is identified with HBootes03 and MIPS J142824.0+352619, a z=1.325 HyLIRG whose CO, luminosity, and [C II]/FIR ratio are discussed in a lensing context.

quotes.quote:
PJ142823.9 is a bright 
egal source previously identified as `HBootes03' by wardlow13 and is also a well-known z=1.325 identified hyperluminous infrared galaxy (HyLIRG) MIPS J142824.0+352619 borys06,swinbank06 . An extensive discussion of this object is found in these papers, and here we highlight only the details relevant to our discussions. We identify the line detected at 99.131 GHz as that of CO (2--1) line at z = 1.3257 0.0003 , and our measured line integral of S V=4.5 0.7 Jy  and the line width of V = 422 21  agree very well with the previously reported Nobeyama Millimeter Array measurements by S V=5.3 1.2 Jy , V = 386 64 iono06 .

gmw_00004566.name: PJ160722.6
gmw_00004566.overview: PJ160722.6 is a high-redshift submillimeter galaxy with a flat spectral energy distribution across the Herschel SPIRE bands [arXiv:1603.05622]. The source is likely gravitationally lensed by a foreground galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.65 [arXiv:1603.05622].

sources.summary: PJ160722.6 is the faintest Planck-matched source but has a flat SPIRE SED, AzTEC and mid-infrared detections, a foreground lens candidate, and CO redshift z=1.4820.

quotes.quote: PJ160722.6 is the faintest 	extit{Planck} source matched with the 	extit{Herschel} catalog, with S_ 250 = 167 mJy and S_ 350 = 167 mJy, and its flat SED across the 	extit{Herschel} SPIRE bands is suggestive of a high redshift source. Our AzTEC imaging revealed a relatively faint source coincident with the 	extit{Herschel} source (see Fig.  fig:aztec ), with S_ 1100 =7.6 1.0 mJy. This source is detected in all four 	extit{WISE} bands (see Fig.  fig:postage2 ), but its mid-IR SED is flatter than those of the others. When combined with the photometry from the United States Naval Observatory B1.0 Catalog monet03 , the optical to mid-IR SED indicates the presence of a foreground galaxy at z 0.65 (see Fig.  fig:SEDs ), likely lensing the background SMG.

gmw_00004567.name: PJ160917.8
gmw_00004567.overview: PJ160917.8 is a gravitationally lensed, high-redshift submillimeter galaxy located at a redshift of z=3.2557 [arXiv:1603.05622, arXiv:2207.06967]. It features a resolved Einstein arc with a fainter counterimage [arXiv:2207.06967], and it exhibits a very high apparent far-infrared luminosity [arXiv:2207.06967].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1609+6045 as a lensed Planck Dusty GEM with high apparent FIR luminosity, a tentative HCO+(1-0) detection, and a resolved Einstein arc.

quotes.quote: We detect HCN(1--0) emission in one source (J1202+5354, 4.4 ), with a tentative HCO ^+ (1--0) detection in another (J1609+6045, 3.3 ).

The remaining two sources are drawn from the Planck 's Dusty GEMs - Gravitationally Enhanced sub-Millimetre Sources ( Canameras2015 , where J1202 is listed as PLCK G138.6+62.0 and J1609 as PLCK G092.5+42.9). All Planck DSFGs have very high apparent (lensed) FIR luminosity ( L_ FIR 10^ 14 L_ ). Although the observations towards these sources are relatively shallow (1-2 hours per source), they still achieve high sensitivity in HCN/FIR and HCN/CO ratios.

J0209 and J1609 are well resolved by the 3 arcsec beam; their K-band morphology resembles the FIR continuum (see Canameras2015 ). J1609 in particular shows an extended Einstein arc with a fainter counterimage.

sources.summary: PJ160917 is a bright 350-micron peaker with AzTEC, optical, radio, and two-line RSR detections, unambiguously placing it at z=3.2557 with strong-lensing morphology.

quotes.quote: PJ160917 is another bright 	extit{Herschel} SPIRE source in the ``Must-Do" survey and a ``350  peaker" with S_ 350 = 693 mJy. It has a bright 	extit{WISE} counterpart with a relatively flat SED across its four bands, and it also has a relatively bright optical counterpart in SDSS J160918.25+604522.2 with z_ ph =0.43 (see Fig.  fig:postage2 ). A bright AzTEC source with S_ 1100 =73.8 7.0 mJy, unresolved by the 8.5 beam, is detected with a high S/N , coincident with the 	extit{Herschel} source position (see Fig.  fig:aztec ). Our RSR spectrum shows 2 bright lines (see Fig.  fig:RSR ), unambiguously identifying it as the highest redshift object in our sample at z=3.2557 0.0003 .

gmw_00004568.name: IRAS 00521--7054
gmw_00004568.overview: IRAS 00521--7054 is a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1202.0400, arXiv:1901.05016] that exhibits a strong, broad iron K line in its X-ray spectrum [arXiv:1202.0400, arXiv:1408.6095]. The system hosts a rapidly rotating supermassive black hole [arXiv:1202.0400, arXiv:1901.05016] and shows evidence of an ionized, ultrafast outflow [arXiv:1901.05016].

sources.summary: The paper presents XMM-Newton spectra of IRAS 00521-7054, fitting a broad Fe K line and absorbed continuum; a reflection interpretation implies a fast-spinning SMBH and possible light bending.

quotes.quote: We present XMM-Newton spectra of the Seyfert 2 Galaxy IRAS 00521-7054. A strong feature at 6 keV (observer's frame) can be formally fitted with a strong (EW= 1.3 0.3 keV in the rest frame) and broad Fe K _ line, extending down to 3 keV. The underlying X-ray continuum could be fitted with an absorbed powerlaw (with = 1.8 0.2 and N _H = 5.9 ^ +0.6 _ 0.7 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 ) plus a soft component. If due to relativistically smeared reflection by an X-ray illuminated accretion disk, the spin of the supermassive black hole is constrained to be 0.97^ +0.03 _ 0.13 (errors at 90 confidence level for one interesting parameter), and the accretion system is viewed at an inclination angle of 37 4 ^ .

This would be the first type 2 AGN reported with strong red \ wing detected which demands a fast rotating SMBH. The unusually large EW would suggest that the light bending effect is strong in this source.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Suzaku and XMM-Newton spectra of the Seyfert 2 IRAS 00521 7054, interpreting its broad 6 keV feature through ionized absorption or blurred reflection scenarios.

quotes.quote: IRAS 00521 7054 is the first Seyfert 2 in which the presence of an extremely large Fe K line has been claimed. We report here on the analysis of a 100 ks Suzaku observation of the source. We confirm the existence of a very strong excess over the power-law X-ray continuum at E 6 keV ( EW 800 eV), extending down to 4.5 keV, and found that the X-ray spectrum of the source can be explained by two different models.

This seems to exclude that the source was in a low-flux state at the time of the Suzaku observation, which could account for the very large EW observed. Therefore, IRAS 00521 7054 might be one of the very few obscured AGN to show evidence of strong light-bending. In both scenarios we found that the energy centroid of the Fe K line, and the ratio between the Fe K and K intensities, are consistent with the narrow iron lines being produced by ionized iron.

sources.summary: The paper presents broadband X-ray observations of the Seyfert 2 galaxy IRAS 00521--7054, finding complex relativistic reflection, absorption, high black-hole spin, and an ultrafast outflow capable of feedback.

quotes.quote:
We present results from a deep, coordinated + 
u observation of the Seyfert 2 galaxy 	arget. The 
ustar data provide the first detection of this source in high-energy X-rays ( E > 10 keV), and the broadband data show this to be a highly complex source which exhibits relativistic reflection from the inner accretion disc, further reprocessing by more distant material, neutral absorption, and evidence for ionised absorption in an extreme, ultrafast outflow ( v_ out 0.4c ). Based on lamppost disc reflection models, we find evidence that the central supermassive black hole is rapidly rotating ( a > 0.77 ), consistent with previous estimates from the profile of the relativistic iron line, and that the accretion disc is viewed at a fairly high inclination ( i 59 ).

Black hole physics -- Galaxies: active -- X-rays: individual (IRAS 00521--7054) Relativistic reflection from the accretion disc is one of the primary tools at our disposal for placing constraints on the innermost accretion geometry around black holes.

gmw_00004569.name: SBS 1425+606
gmw_00004569.overview: SBS 1425+606 is a very bright high-redshift quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0603835, arXiv:1106.3875] that features an asymmetric extended emission-line region [arXiv:astro-ph/0603835]. The object exhibits continuum variations over multi-year timescales that lack a corresponding response in its C IV line flux [arXiv:1106.3875].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Q1425+606 as a very bright quasar with one of the sample’s brightest EELRs, asymmetric emission, and measurable line flux but no detected extended C IV.

quotes.quote: The narrow-band image for Q1425+606 corresponds to 5099--5122 . To estimate the QSO redshift a Gaussian function is fit to the O i 1302 emission line with z_ em =3.2030 0.0008 . This is somewhat higher than z=3.17 reported in the literature, which we also derive from the C ii 1335 and C iv 1549 lines in the data cube. As a reference we use z_ em =3.203 because the O i line is a more reliable redshift indicator tytler92 . Q1425+606 has one of the brightest EELR in the sample, as shown in Fig. fig:qlya_qso_lya , and it is also the brightest QSO in the sample.

It has large QSO subtraction residuals, but these are at a level consistent with Poissonian statistics. Any narrow \ emission that may be present within a radius of 0 5 from the QSO center is heavily affected by these subtraction residuals, hence we do not recover the structure of the nebula here. The \ emission is clearly asymmetric with most of the emission coming from a region to the south-east of the QSO.

sources.summary: The paper uses SBS 1425+606 as a quasar monitoring example where continuum variations lack a matching C IV line-flux response, supporting a microlensing interpretation.

quotes.quote: This shows continuum and C IV 1550 light curves for the quasar SBS 1425+606, adapted from Kaspi et al. (2007). We first note that within each year, on a timescale of a few weeks, there is some evidence of a weak correlation between continuum and line flux changes. This presumably corresponds to a response of the BLR to changes in continuum strength, as seen in Seyfert galaxies such as NGC 5548 . However, the continuum light curve is clearly dominated by a rise of 0.6 magnitudes over a period of about 2 years, followed by a gentle decline. This has no counterpart in the C IV flux, which remains constant to within 0.1 magnitudes.

gmw_00004570.name: J0943+3326
gmw_00004570.overview: J0943+3326 is a compact, extremely metal-poor dwarf galaxy located in an under-dense void environment [arXiv:2002.11723, arXiv:2105.05100]. This star-forming, gas-rich galaxy hosts a population of young, massive stars and shows signs of interaction with its nearby companion, UGC 5186 [arXiv:2002.11723, arXiv:2212.03478].

sources.summary: The paper measures Leoncino at 12.1 Mpc and describes it as a compact, gas-rich, extremely metal-poor dwarf whose LZ offset likely reflects recent star formation from interaction.

quotes.quote: The recently discovered XMP galaxy Leoncino, with an oxygen abundance below 3 , provides an opportunity to explore these different scenarios. Using Hubble Space Telescope imaging of the resolved stellar populations of Leoncino, we measure the distance to the galaxy to be D =12.1^ +1.7 _ 3.4 Mpc and find that Leoncino is located in an under-dense environment. Leoncino has a compact morphology, hosts a population of young, massive stars, has a high gas-to-star mass ratio, and shows signs of interaction with a galaxy nearby on the sky, UGC 5186. Similar to nearly all XMP galaxies known in the nearby universe, Leoncino is offset from the Luminosity-Metallicity (LZ) relation. Yet, Leoncino is consistent with the stellar Mass-Metallicity (MZ) relation defined by Local Volume galaxies.

Leoncino was discovered via its \ line emission in ALFALFA. Initial gas-mass estimates using a velocity-based distance from flow models suggested that it was very low mass ( M_ HI 10^7 ) and, thus, was included in the Survey of \ in Extremely Low-mass Dwarfs SHIELD; Cannon2011c , a follow-up study of Local Volume ALFALFA galaxies with \ masses below 10 ^ 7.2 . As part of the SHIELD program, we carried out a coordinated effort to characterize the chemical, stellar, and gas content of Leoncino; a summary of these properties is presented in Table tab:properties .

sources.summary: The paper re-examines AGC 198691 as an extremely metal-poor dwarf, confirming very low oxygen abundance and deriving helium abundance from new LBT/MODS spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: We re-examine the extremely metal-poor (XMP) dwarf galaxy AGC 198691 using a high quality spectrum obtained by the LBT's MODS instrument. Previous spectral observations obtained from KOSMOS on the Mayall 4-m and the Blue Channel spectrograph on the MMT 6.5-m telescope did not allow for the determination of sulfur, argon, or helium abundances. We report an updated and full chemical abundance analysis for AGC 198691, including confirmation of the extremely low ``direct" oxygen abundance with a value of 12 + log(O/H) = 7.06 0.03. AGC 198691's low metallicity potentially makes it a high value target for helping determine the primordial helium abundance ( Y_p ). Though complicated by a Na I night sky line partially overlaying the He I 5876 emission line, the LBT/MODS spectrum proved adequate for determining AGC 198691's helium abundance.

The oxygen abundance in AGC 198691 was measured to be 12 + log(O/H) = 7.02 0.03 hirschauer2016 , based on composite spectrum from two night's observations using the MMT. At the time of publication, this was the lowest metallicity XMP known. hsyu2018 subsequently obtained a spectrum of AGC 198691 (referred to as J0943+3326 in that work) with the Keck observatory LRIS instrument and derived an oxygen abundance of 12 + log(O/H) = 7.16 0.07.

sources.summary: AGC 198691 is treated as an exceptional SHIELD galaxy with deeper HST-based properties, no recent CMD-derived SFR, void location, and a possible nearby interacting companion, UGC 5186.

quotes.quote: The exception is AGC 198691 a.k.a. the Leoncino dwarf; Hirschauer2016 ; for this galaxy, deeper HST observations were obtained separately in HST-GO-15423. We adopt the distance and galaxy properties reported from this program in McQuinn2020 and discuss these results in more detail below.
The distance to AGC 198691 is from McQuinn2020 ; due to the spareness of the CMD, no recent SFR for this galaxy was measured from the CMD-fitting technique.

No galaxies were identified within 1 Mpc of 5 SHIELD galaxies (AGC 102728, AGC 123352, AGC 174605, AGC 198507, and AGC 198691). Of these, AGC 102728, AGC 176405, and AGC 198691 are located in Void Numbers 25, 8, and 12 respectively, identified by Pustilnik2019 . Note, however, the in-depth study of AGC 198691 suggests that a galaxy nearby-on-the-sky and also located in Void Number 12, UGC 5186, may be much closer than 1 Mpc and interacting McQuinn2020 .

sources.summary: The paper compares Peekaboo with AGC 198691/Leoncino, describing both as compact, star-forming, extremely metal-poor dwarf galaxies with similar appearance, stellar populations, and scant red giant branches.

quotes.quote: Hirschauer2016 , McQuinn2020 , and Aver2022 investigated the properties of another compact dwarf galaxy AGC 198691 (= Leoncino), discovered in the ALFALFA blind \ survey Haynes2011 and taken to lie at D=12.0 Mpc. In general appearance and stellar population, both galaxies look like twins.

In its general shape, stellar population, and star formation rate, the compact Peekaboo dwarf galaxy looks like a twin of another star-forming dwarf galaxy AGC 198691 = Leoncino, investigated by Hirschauer2016 and McQuinn2020 . Both the dwarfs are extremely metal-poor objects with similarities to the better known I Zw 18. In all these cases, the RGB are scantily populated compared with the numbers of bluer young stars at similar magnitudes.

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0943+3326 as a Lynx-Cancer void XMP dwarf, also AGC198691/Leoncino, with disturbed HI gas and likely companionship to UGC5186.

quotes.quote: One more XMP dwarf in the Lynx-Cancer void, J0943+3326 (AGC198691 = Leoncino) was found independently among the ALFALFA blind HI survey ALFALFA newly identified gas-rich objects Hirschauer16 .

This comes for J0015+0104 from Pustilnik13 , for J0706+3020 from U3672 , for J0926+3343, J0929+2502, J0723+3622 and J0723+3624 from PaperIV , for J0943+3326 we adopt (g-) presented in the SDSS DR14 database DR14 .

J0943+3326 (AGC198691), with a radial velocity difference of only 37 \ and a projected distance of 40 kpc at the adopted D=10.8 Mpc Hirschauer16,PaperVII , has clearly disturbed HI gas and is very likely a fainter companion of a nearby more massive dwarf, UGC5186.

gmw_00004571.name: J0956+2850
gmw_00004571.overview: J0956+2850, also known as DDO68B, is an extremely metal-poor, gas-rich dwarf galaxy located in the Lynx-Cancer void [arXiv:1912.06740]. It is a detectable remnant of a recent minor merger associated with the larger galaxy DDO68 [arXiv:1912.06740].

sources.summary: The paper treats J0956+2850/DDO68B as an extremely metal-poor, gas-rich Lynx-Cancer void dwarf and a recent minor-merger remnant associated with DDO68.

quotes.quote: Four of eight XMP gas-rich objects in the upper part of Table tab:prop_summary (J0706+3020, J0926+3343, J0929+2502 and J0956+2850) were found as a result of an unbiased study of a sample of about a hundred galaxies in the nearby Lynx-Cancer void DDO68,J0926,PaperIII,Triplet,PaperVI,PaperVII,U3672 . The entry J0956+2850 corresponds to the smaller component of the well known dwarf merging system DDO68 DDO68,Ekta2008,DDO68_HST for which 12+ (O/H) is shown to be below 7.15 dex DDO68,IT07,DDO68_OH .

For J0956+2850 (DDO68B), the range of colours for various parts of the assumed fainter component of the merger comes from DDO68sdss , for J2104--0049 we adopt our unpublished measurement from the SDSS images; Col.
J0956+2850 (DDO68B), an outer part of the well-known XMP galaxy, DDO68 DDO68,Ekta2008,Annibali2016,DDO68_HST , is treated as a detectable remnant of a recent minor merger.

gmw_00004572.name: the arc at = 08 ^ h 48 ^ m 48 76 = 44 \ 55 \ 48 61 (J2000)
gmw_00004572.overview: The arc at 08h 48m 48.76s +44d 55m 48.61s (J2000) is a gravitationally lensed object at a redshift of z=3.356 [arXiv:astro-ph/0105011]. It consists of two components that exhibit a weak continuum and several strong emission lines with unique relative ratios [arXiv:astro-ph/0105011].

sources.summary: The paper identifies a z=3.356 lensed arc with two components, narrow emission lines, unusual line ratios, and a likely hot photoionizing source.

quotes.quote: We find the arc at = 08 ^ h 48 ^ m 48 76 = 44 \ 55 \ 48 61 (J2000) with z=3.356 . In Figure cl2_xray_cont , we mark the confirmed arc with a box. The gravitational arc lies 6 1 from the z=0.543 elliptical galaxy in the south, near the center of the southern X-ray peak. We show the arc and the elliptical galaxy in Figure bcg2 . Based on the northeast-southwest orientation of this arc, it is probably not being lensed by the X-ray cluster at all, but rather by the elliptical galaxy or a group of galaxies centered on the elliptical (see Figure bcg2 ).

Also apparent in Figure bcg2 is that the arc has two components. The LRIS spectra of the arc components extend from 5340 to 7895 \ in the observed frame, and are plotted in Figure arc . Both components show a weak continuum and several strong emission lines: a doublet around 6470 , a triplet near 6750 , a weak singlet at 7150 \ and another doublet around 7250 . There are no other convincingly detected lines in the observed wavelength range.

While these emission lines are seen in objects ranging from quasars and radio galaxies to planetary nebulae and symbiotic stars, their relative ratios are, as far as we can discover from searching the relevant literature and data archives, unique.

gmw_00004573.name: EX 0302--223
gmw_00004573.overview: EX 0302--223 is a quasar with a corrected catalog redshift of 1.409 [arXiv:astro-ph/0406072]. This corrected value resolves a typographical error in previous catalogs that had incorrectly listed its redshift as 1.400 [arXiv:astro-ph/0406072].

sources.summary: The paper corrects the catalog redshift for EX 0302--223 and explains that the former value would have substantially changed the inferred absorption-system velocity.

quotes.quote: Discussion of Individual Objects EX 0302--223. -- The redshift quoted for this object in quasar catalogs is 1.400. This value was taken from Cha81 and is incorrect. The correct redshift is 1.409 and is reported by Marg85 , who also note that the former value had a typographical error. The difference in the two redshifts translates into a velocity difference of about 1100 , which would have led to a significant error in the velocities of Table tab_var . In fact, the absorption system at v 450 \ would have appeared at a positive velocity if the former value of the redshift were used.

gmw_00004574.name: QSO J0909--095
gmw_00004574.overview: QSO J0909--095 is a quasar with an unusually uncertain redshift determined from a low-resolution spectrum [arXiv:astro-ph/0406072]. Observations of the object have successfully detected both the C 4 absorption doublet and the Ly line [arXiv:astro-ph/0406072].

sources.summary: The paper reports C 4 and Ly absorption detections for QSO J0909--095, while emphasizing that its low-resolution redshift is unusually uncertain.

quotes.quote: QSO J0909--095. -- Although the C 4 doublet is detected in the FOS spectrum, the poor S/N and lower sampling of the STIS spectrum lead to a large and uninteresting upper limit to the EW at the second epoch. We do, however, detect the Ly line in the STIS spectrum. The redshift of this object comes from a low-resolution spectrum by Kne98 in which no narrow, forbidden lines were detected. Therefore, it is unusually uncertain and is quoted only to two decimal places.

gmw_00004575.name: PKS 2135--14
gmw_00004575.overview: PKS 2135--14 is a radio-loud quasar hosted by an elliptical galaxy with two close companions [arXiv:0808.0918]. The system exhibits a highly disturbed morphology featuring a western shell, an amorphous halo, and a faint tidal tail [arXiv:1008.2683], alongside a C IV absorption line that varies over time [arXiv:astro-ph/0406072].

sources.summary: The paper finds significant C 4 absorption variability in PKS 2135--14 and argues that the redshifted absorber is intrinsic to the quasar central engine.

quotes.quote: PKS 2135--14. -- The C 4 absorption line of this object has been the subject of detailed study by several authors. It is an interesting case because the absorption lines are redshifted relative to the peaks of the broad emission lines and relative to the systemic redshift, as defined by the narrow, forbidden lines. In particular, BK83 compared IUE spectra taken approximately 2.6 years apart and found a 25 change in EW (from 2.0 \ in 1979 to 2.5 \ in 1981) as well as a shift of the absorption-line centroid by 300 \ between the two epochs. We find a significant change in EW between the STIS and FOS observations (from 4.7 \ in 1992 to 3.2 \ in 2000; see illustration in Fig. fig_overp ), as well as a significant change between the HST (1992 and 2000) and IUE (1979 and 1981) observations. However, the EW we measure from the FOS spectrum is higher than the values measured by Be02 and Ha97c (4.7 \ by us vs 3.1 \ and 3.6 \ by them).

BB86 and Ha97c have studied the ionization conditions in this particular absorption system and concluded that the ionization parameter is likely to be high ( U 0.25 ), although lower values (as low as U 3 10^ 3 ) could not be ruled out. They discussed a number of possible scenarios for the absorber, including galaxies within the cluster harboring the quasar, the quasar host galaxy itself, and gas intrinsic to the quasar central engine, but they were not able to select one of these scenarios as an obvious favorite. The variability results we present here, especially when combined with the earlier results of BK83 , make a strong case that this absorber is intrinsic to the quasar central engine.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 2135-147 as a radio-loud quasar in an elliptical host with close companions, average radio-loud host size, low surface brightness, emission lines, and likely star formation.

quotes.quote: PKS 2135-147 PKS 2135-147 (labeled 8 in our plots) is a radio-loud quasar with M _ V = 24.7 and L _ 5 GHz =7.7x10 ^ 42 erg s ^ 1 . The host galaxy (M _ V = 22.4) is classified as an elliptical with two companions within 5.5 bahcall97,hamilton02 . The size of this host (R _ e =8.6 kpc) is average for our sample, but slightly above the average of the high mass early-type galaxies. Its surface brightness (20.83 mag arcsec ^ 1 ) is average for our radio-loud hosts, but lower than all other groups of objects. The velocity dispersion (307 km s ^ 1 ) is slightly below average for the radio-loud hosts.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 2135-14 as a powerful radio source whose GMOS-S morphology shows disturbed structure, companions, shells, fans, dust, tails, bridges, or multiple nuclei indicating interaction or merger activity.

quotes.quote: The optical image of this QSO/FRII (Figure pks2135_14_online ) shows a disk galaxy companion at 18 kpc SE and a close-in companion, or secondary nucleus, at a distance of 6 kpc. However, analysis of its spectrum showed that the latter object is actually a foreground star Canalizo97 . Our GMOS-S image reveals a very disturbed morphology, including a shell in the West side of the quasar nucleus, which is embedded in an amorphous halo, and a faint tidal tail pointing to the SE. This tidal tail, which in the original image extends up to 36 kpc and has a surface brightness of _V =24.2 mag arcsec ^ 2 , is very likely the brightest part of a bridge linking the radio galaxy and the disturbed galaxy at 134 kpc to the SE.

gmw_00004576.name: NGC 5000
gmw_00004576.overview: NGC 5000 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1810.08624] that features a vertically thick inner bar component with an X-shaped barlens [arXiv:1807.01092]. The galaxy's bar and barlens regions are dominated by metal-rich, intermediate-age stars, and it hosts solar-neighborhood analog regions with an apparent azimuthal symmetry [arXiv:2204.13725, arXiv:1807.01092].

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 5000 with barred-galaxy simulations, noting imperfect morphology matches but qualitatively successful age-profile agreement at selected snapshots.

quotes.quote: Fig. fig:simprofile shows that the maximum value of the radial age profile for NGC 5000 is around 12.5 Gyr. To reach such ages, we thus need to constrain our comparisons to times as near the final time as possible and certainly above 12.5 Gyr. This sets very strong restrictions to our choice. We chose a snapshot in the same way as we had chosen NGC 5000, i.e. a snapshot which has a bar and an inner structure such as a lens or inner ring (Fig. fig:simmap ). It has a clear bar; however, compared to the observations, the simulated bar is less thin, and thus is presumably not as strong.

We calculated the azimuthally-averaged age radial profile from the simulation both at 9 and 13 Gyr, and we compare them in Fig. fig:simprofile with two age radial profiles of NGC 5000, calculated with maximum SSP ages of 9 and 13 Gyr. Given that the simulation we use was not specifically run to match NGC 5000, that there are considerable morphological differences between the simulations and observations, and that there are no free parameters to improve the match, we could have expected a very poor fit. Our results, however, are good qualitatively and even acceptable quantitatively.

sources.summary: NGC 5000 is presented as a CALIFA example with solar-neighborhood analog regions; it has the most concentrated SNA-spaxel likelihood, apparent azimuthal symmetry, and spectra matching the reference.

quotes.quote: Fig. fig:sna-showcase summarizes our main results for three galaxies hosting SNA regions, as an illustrative example: NGC 5947 , NGC 5000 and NGC 309 . For each galaxy we show the contour of the likelihood map ( L _ ij ^2 _ ij /2 ) on top of the RGB composite image, highlighting the location of the most likely SNA. In addition we show the corresponding spectra of those regions together with the reference SNA spectrum. We observe that the SNA spectrum of the three example galaxies is well within the uncertainty band, _ (shaded region), for most of the wavelength range. A higher L _ SNA indicates that the likelihood of finding a SNA within the target galaxy is more concentrated around a specific region.

In top panels of Fig. fig:sna-showcase , NGC 5000 shows the most concentrated likelihood of hosting SNA spaxels, with an apparent azimuthal symmetry. At first sight, this pattern is also followed by NGC 5947 and NGC 309 , whereby likely SNA spaxels group in a ring-like structure.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 5000 has an X-shaped barlens confirming a vertically thick inner bar, with metal-rich intermediate-age bar/barlens regions and similar old-star fractions.

quotes.quote: Galaxies with X-shapes NGC 6941 (T = 3), UGC 8781 (T = 3) , and NGC 5000 (T = 4): In these galaxies the barlenses show also X-shape features in the unsharp-mask images, which confirms that they are vertically thick inner bar components. Therefore, it is interesting that also in these galaxies the bar/barlens regions have similar age and metallicity distributions as the prototypical barlens galaxies discussed above, i.e. they are dominated by metal-rich (log _ 10 Z/Z _ = 0.20) intermediate age stars, with a significant contribution of the oldest stars (age > 10 Gyrs). In UGC 08781 and NGC 5000 the fraction of the oldest stars is similar in the galaxy center and in the bar/barlens region, whereas in NGC 6941 their fraction drops in the galaxy center. In these galaxies the metallicity starts to drop toward the galaxy center already at the edge of the bar.

gmw_00004577.name: NGC3381
gmw_00004577.overview: NGC3381 is a blue, low-mass barred galaxy with a stellar mass of 10^9.6 solar masses [arXiv:1810.08624]. It exhibits strong hydrogen absorption consistent with a post-starburst classification [arXiv:1810.08624], and contains young star-forming clusters [arXiv:1605.03991].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3381 R1 to illustrate blue-bump fitting, warns WR-ratio comparisons may mislead, and reports a nonphysical O-star estimate.

quotes.quote: Fig. fig:example_bb_fits shows an example of our procedure using the fit to R1 in NGC 3381.
Thus, in a few cases ( NGC 3353 , NGC 3381 , NGC 5954 ) our results may still be somehow misleading provided that there is a cluster young enough so that WRs are still not present.
In two cases (R1 in NGC 3353 and NGC 3381 ), the computed number of O stars is negative, a non-physical result.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC3381 as a blue, low-mass barred galaxy with the sample’s strongest H absorption, consistent with a post-starburst classification.

quotes.quote: NGC3381: the barred galaxy with a very high \ but with only intermediate c_ mol . This galaxy is blue in colour ( \ =2.0 , the bluest of all our barred galaxies), and has a low stellar mass ( 10^ 9.6 M_ ; the lowest of all our barred galaxies). This galaxy has an observed value of =5.05 , the highest in our sample (middle panel of Fig. fig:obs_extrap_scatter ), and is consistent with the definition of a post-starburst galaxy.

gmw_00004578.name: NGC7819
gmw_00004578.overview: NGC7819 is an unbarred, blue star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1810.08624] that exhibits a highly centrally concentrated star formation rate distribution [arXiv:2208.14950]. It possesses a high molecular gas concentration [arXiv:1810.08624, arXiv:2208.14950], yet it lacks enhanced central star formation [arXiv:1810.08624].

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC7819 as an unbarred, blue star-forming galaxy with high molecular gas concentration but no enhanced central star formation indicators.

quotes.quote: NGC7819: the unbarred galaxy with the highest c_ mol . With an \ of 2.1 mag (the bluest of the unbarred galaxies), this galaxy is clearly blue and star-forming. This galaxy has quite a large optical radius (23.8 ), and a relatively small r_ 50,mol . Given this high concentration, why does it not show enhanced central star formation in any of our SFH indicators? With a central _ H_2 of 2.27 M_ pc ^ 2 and central _ SFR of 0.58 M_ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 , which are not dissimilar from the central values of these quantities in barred upturn galaxies (Fig. fig:ks ), it appears that central SFR and H _2 surface densities being similar to barred galaxies, and having a high molecular gas concentration are not sufficient conditions for enhanced central star formation.

A bar or galaxy interaction appears to be needed too, as discussed above. It would be interesting to study the dense gas in this system -- it could be that the gas is not sufficiently dense to form stars without a bar.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7819 as the unbarred galaxy with the highest CO and molecular-gas concentration, and links its strong H-alpha equivalent-width upturn to a centrally concentrated SFR distribution.

quotes.quote: The unbarred galaxies with the highest concentration is NGC 7819, which has C_ CO =0.85 and C_ mol =0.69 , meaning that the average central CO intensity and molecular mass surface density are, respectively, a factor of 7 and 5 higher than the disk-averaged values. The concentration in NGC 7819 is comparable to the highest gas concentration of barred galaxies.

For instance, the EW _ H profile of NGC 7819 has at least two turn-up and one turnover, and the turn-up at R 7 7 is chosen by . However, this chosen turning is the weakest one among others. We use the change point analysis described in to estimate the amplitude of the turning. Instead, if the strongest turning at R 16 is used, NGC 7819 has a strong EW _ H central upturn 1 rather than 0.3 reported by Chown2019 . The EW _ H central upturn of 1 is consistent with our result that NGC 7819 has a highly centrally concentrated SFR distribution.

gmw_00004579.name: NGC4211NED02
gmw_00004579.overview: NGC4211NED02 is a merger galaxy in a close pair with NGC4211A, separated by a projected distance of approximately 16 kpc [arXiv:1810.08624]. The galaxy exhibits an irregular morphology, a high concentration of molecular gas, and very compact CO emission [arXiv:1810.08624].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC4211NED02 as a close-pair merger galaxy with compact CO emission, high molecular gas concentration, and an unreliable central turnover measurement.

quotes.quote: NGC4211NED02: the merger galaxy with the highest c_ mol but no significant central upturn. This galaxy has \ of 3.7, and stellar mass of 10^ 10.0 M_ . It is in a close pair with NGC4211A (not in our sample), with a projected separation of approximately 16 kpc. The CO emission for this galaxy is very compact, and since r_ 50,mol is less than twice the CARMA beam scale, c_ mol should be considered a lower limit (as indicated in Table tab:tableA2 ). This galaxy does not show an upturn, however it shows a significant \ turnover (see the bottom-right panel of Fig. fig:strengthvsco ).

The turnover strength for this particular galaxy should be interpreted with caution, because the size of the central region of this galaxy is quite close to the resolution of CALIFA. Although the optical radius of this galaxy is 19 , this is likely due to the irregular morphology.

gmw_00004580.name: NGC 4647
gmw_00004580.overview: NGC 4647 is an Sc-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:0801.4382, arXiv:astro-ph/0509156] located in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0606410, arXiv:astro-ph/0609786]. It forms the pair Arp 116 with the elliptical galaxy NGC 4649 [arXiv:astro-ph/0509156, arXiv:astro-ph/0609786], showing asymmetric gas distributions [arXiv:astro-ph/0606410, arXiv:astro-ph/0609786] and signs of tidal interaction [arXiv:1401.2994, arXiv:astro-ph/0609786].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC4647 as a Virgo spiral companion of NGC4649 with concentrated X-ray sources, enhanced southern star formation, and signs of tidal interaction.

quotes.quote: NGC4647 is a spiral galaxy in Virgo that can be seen to the N-W of NGC4649. Based on its aspect and photometric properties, conclude that it is tidally interacting with NGC4649. There is a concentration of X-ray sources (positive residuals) associated with this spiral galaxy. The high-luminosity X-ray sources concentrate in the southern half of NGC4647. This is the part of the galaxy showing a prominent asymmetric arm suggesting interaction with NGC4649, and exhibiting more intense star formation lanz2013,mineo2013 .

These luminous sources are likely younger HMXBs, associated with the ongoing intense star formation e.g. mineo2012 . Our results agree with the overall picture of tidal interaction between NGC4647 and NGC4649. NGC4647 may well be the next accreted satellite.

sources.summary: The paper maps H I and CO in NGC 4647, linking its asymmetric rotation curve and molecular gas distribution to pressure, interactions, or an m=1 gravitational perturbation.

quotes.quote: We present matched-resolution maps of HI and CO emission in the Virgo Cluster spiral NGC 4647. The galaxy shows a mild kinematic disturbance in which one side of the rotation curve flattens but the other side continues to rise. This kinematic asymmetry is coupled with a dramatic asymmetry in the molecular gas distribution but not in the atomic gas. An analysis of the gas column densities and the interstellar pressure suggests that the /HI surface density ratio on the east side of the galaxy is three times higher than expected from the hydrostatic pressure contributed by the mass of the stellar disk. We discuss the probable effects of ram pressure, gravitational interactions, and asymmetric potentials on the interstellar medium and suggest it is likely that a m=1 perturbation in the gravitational potential could be responsible for all of the galaxy's features.

NGC 4647 is a gas-rich Virgo Cluster spiral whose interstellar medium has been gently disturbed and whose surroundings suggest some possible causes of the disturbance. It is 3.2 \ = 0.95 Mpc in projection from M87. The galaxy's distance has been estimated via the Tully-Fisher method to be 17.3^ +3.0 _ 2.6 Mpc gavazzi99 , so that it is close to the midplane of the Virgo Cluster.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4647 as an Sc galaxy with a difficult nuclear X-ray detection, soft residual emission, possible infrared counterpart, and inconclusive AGN evidence.

quotes.quote: NGC 4647 NGC 4647 is a galaxy of type Sc, its nucleus classified as H 2 in hfs97-3 , at a distance of 16.8 Mpc t88 . The \ observation is of the elliptical galaxy NGC 4649, and NGC 4647 is on the chip, at an off-axis angle of 2.5 . Two factors complicate the detection of the nucleus of NGC 4647. First, the nucleus lies within the extended emission from NGC 4649.

However, once the elliptical galaxy is modeled and subtracted out of the image, there is a positive residual at the location of the nucleus of NGC 4647 (see Fig. fig:n4647xo ). To extract source counts we used a circular region centered on the centroid of the residual and with radius 2.3 , which is approximately the 95 encircled-energy radius at 1.5 keV at that position. To extract background counts we located another region on the same node boundary that was at the same distance from the center of NGC 4649 as was the source circle.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4647 as NGC 4649's Sc companion, part of the Arp 116/VV 206 system, with projected X-ray sources including a soft source near its optical center.
