quotes.quote: NGC 4649 has a close companion galaxy NGC 4647, which is an Sc galaxy. This pair of galaxies is also referred to as Arp 116 or VV 206. With a third more distant galaxy, this pair forms a group of galaxies WBL 421 (White et al. \ 1999). NGC 4647 was the host of the Type-I supernova SN1979a (Barbon et al. \ 1984).

As shown in Figure fig:dss_field , NGC 4649 has a nearby companion galaxy, the Sc galaxy NGC 4647. Some of the X-ray sources are projected on the optical image of this galaxy and might therefore be associated with it. In particular, the position of Src. 21 is within 3 \ (0.24 kpc) of the center of NGC 4647 given by optical observations.

sources.summary: The appendix treats NGC 4647 as a nearby spiral whose UV/FIR emission and star formation imply an HMXB population distinct from NGC 4649's LMXBs.

quotes.quote: The spiral galaxy NGC 4647 appears to be approximately 2 .5 away from the elliptical NGC 4649. Due to their proximity, it is difficult to disentangle their X-ray populations. NGC 4647 should be dominated by short-lived ( <10-50 Myr, M_ opt >5 M_ ) high-mass X-ray binaries (HMXBs), in contrast to the long-lived ( > 1 Gyr, M_ opt <1 M_ ) low-mass X-ray binary (LMXB) population hosted by the elliptical galaxy NGC 4649. The number of HMXBs in late-type galaxies is proportional to the star formation rate (SFR) of the host galaxy 2003MNRAS.339..793G,2012MNRAS.419.2095M . In order to estimate the predicted number of HMXBs in NGC 4647, we used the calibration from 2012MNRAS.419.2095M .

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 4647 as a nearby disturbed spiral that may be interacting with NGC 4649, while finding no strong interaction evidence and excluding contaminating GCs.

quotes.quote: We find no strong evidence to support an interaction between NGC 4649 and the neighbouring spiral galaxy NGC 4647.

The proximity to the disturbed spiral galaxy NGC 4647 (13 kpc in projection) potentially makes NGC 4649 an example of a pre major-merger between two massive galaxies in the local Universe, although this connection is still debated Young06,deGrijs .

GCs belonging to the spiral galaxy NGC 4647 (2.6 arcmin from NGC 4649) may also contaminate our GC sample. However, Strader12 estimated that only 10 objects in their GC catalogue are actually associated with NGC 4647. From our spectroscopic GC catalogue, we exclude one GC because of its proximity to NGC 4647 and because the spiral arms compromise the photometry of this object.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 4647 as M60's nearby spiral companion in Arp 116, comparing velocities, projected separation, uncertain interaction evidence, and distance estimates needed to assess tidal interaction.

quotes.quote: NGC 4647, a spiral galaxy, is located only 2 6 (corresponding to a projected distance of 12 kpc) in the north-west from the center of M60 in the sky. This pair of galaxies is called, Arp 116, and is a rare example of a combination of an elliptical galaxy and a spiral galaxy. The heliocentric radial velocity of NGC 4647 ( 1409 1 , NED) is only about 300 larger than that of M60 ( 1110 5 , NED). Because of the projected proximity and the small radial velocity difference of M60 and NGC 4647, several studies investigated any possibility of tidal interaction between these two galaxies deg06,lan13,dab14,min14,pot15 . However whether NGC 4647 is interacting with M60 or not is still controversial ( deg06 , pot15 and references therein).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4647 as the Sc spiral companion to NGC 4649, with asymmetric gas disks and signs consistent with early tidal interaction in Arp 116.

quotes.quote: We selected Arp 116 , a combination of the bright ( M_B = -21.48 mag) early-type (E2) galaxy NGC 4649 ( M60 ) and its Sc-type companion NGC 4647 ( M_B = -19.81 mag), as our prime target. This is one of the closest systems of mixed-type interacting galaxies, at a distance of 16.8 Mpc in the Virgo cluster [ Tonry et al. 2001; based on surface brightness fluctuations (SBF), see Sect. distances.sec ] , and with a projected separation of 2 5 12 kpc.

Rubin et al. (1999) point out that, although their long-slit optical spectra show that the kinematics of the spiral component, NGC 4647 , appear nearly normal (but see Young et al. 2006), the H and molecular (CO and H _2 ) gas disks are clearly asymmetric (Koopmann et al. 2001; Young et al. 2006), while the more extended H i disk is also slightly extended toward NGC 4649 (Cayatte et al. 1990), but not nearly by as much as the molecular gas (Young et al. 2006). This might reflect an H i infall scenario, as Cayatte et al. (1990) suggest, or possibly the onset of a tidal interaction between the two galaxies (Rubin et al. 1999; Cullen et al. 2006).

gmw_00004581.name: ID 891
gmw_00004581.overview: ID 891 is an emission line galaxy with a continuum structure characterized by several high-surface brightness knots connected by a diffuse, low-surface brightness component [arXiv:2404.17415]. At lower spatial resolution, the galaxy displays a head-tail shape, while its ionized gas velocity field reveals a clear rotating disk structure [arXiv:2404.17415].

sources.summary: The paper uses ID 891 as a spatially resolved EELG example, noting its HST knots, MUSE head-tail shape, line maps, and rotating ionized-gas disk.

quotes.quote: Spectroscopic and spatially resolved properties Figure fig:HST_white-light_spectrum_maps_891 presents an example of photometric and spectroscopic properties derived for the galaxy ID 891. The upper row shows the HST 775W and MUSE white-light images, as well as the MUSE spectrum with an inset zooming around the H and [ OIII ] 4363 \ emission lines. In the bottom row we present the spatially resolved spectroscopic and kinematic information of the galaxy which will be discussed in Sec:velocitymaps .

About 43 of EELGs in the primary sample are in this class: ID 891: The HST F775W continuum image reflects a non-nuclear shape, with several high-surface brightness knots connected by a diffuse low-surface brightness component. This structure is not shown in the MUSE white light image (given the lower spatial resolution), which present a head-tail shape (i.e. a main bright star-forming clump located at the centre of the image and a low-surface brightness tail). The SNR map shows its highest values in the inner galaxy, though peaking slightly south of the flux maximum likely a consequence of the different line profile shapes and the higher velocity dispersion measured. The ionised gas velocity field presents a clear rotating disk structure, showing higher values in the region corresponding to the knots identified in the HST image.

gmw_00004582.name: PKS B1017-325
gmw_00004582.overview: PKS B1017-325 is a radio galaxy with a projected linear diameter of 44 kpc [arXiv:1711.03157]. Because of this large size, it is excluded from the formal classification of a compact steep-spectrum source [arXiv:1711.03157].

sources.summary: The paper reports XMM, VLA, and optical observations of PKS B1017-325, deriving a new redshift and finding its large projected size excludes it from formal CSS classification.

quotes.quote: While from our new spectroscopic redshift, PKS B1017-325 falls outside the formal definition of a CSS, the \ observations are consistent with ISM emission with either a contribution from hot shocked gas or non-thermal jet emission.

Based on our new spectroscopic redshift, the candidate CSS source \ is now known to have a projected linear diameter of 44 kpc which is significantly above the cutoff for CSS sources. Thus, \ is not considered a CSS for the analysis in sec:compare .
Using our new spectroscopic redshift, the projected linear size for PKS B1017-325 falls outside the formal definition of a CSS, and thus, we drop \ from consideration as a CSS source.

gmw_00004583.name: 3C 268.3
gmw_00004583.overview: 3C 268.3 is a compact steep-spectrum broad-line radio galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0510563, arXiv:1711.03157] located at a redshift of approximately 0.37 [arXiv:astro-ph/0510563, arXiv:1711.03157]. It features an extended emission-line region with high-excitation neon emission [arXiv:1510.06979] and a northern radio lobe associated with redshifted neutral hydrogen absorption [arXiv:astro-ph/0510563].

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 268.3 as a compact steep spectrum galaxy whose northern radio lobe is bright, complex, depolarized, and associated with redshifted H I absorption.

quotes.quote: 3C 268.3 is a CSS galaxy Spinrad85, Gelderman94 at a redshift z=0.37116, with two main radio components oriented roughly north-south with a total angular size of 1.5 arcsec or 7.6 kpc i.e., Nan91, Breugel92, Neff95, Sanghera95, Ludke98 . Our observations are consistent with the previous results. Ludke98 have found a faint, possible core at 5 GHz, about 1.5 kpc south of the northern lobe. The northern component has an integrated flux density of 2.25 0.01 Jy, which is roughly 22 times more than the southern component.

3C 268.3 shows an absorption profile in the center of the northern lobe. ^2 fitting shows that an adequate representation for the detection is just one Gaussian line redshifted in the host galaxy rest frame; the column depth is 3.2x10 ^ 20 ( T_ S /100K) cm ^ 2 . Inspection of Table measurements2 shows that the limits on the other components are too high to rule out the presence of H i at similar levels in the southern component, which is much fainter than the northern component.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 268.3 as a compact steep-spectrum broad-line radio galaxy whose Chandra data show a nuclear point source fit by a double-power-law spectrum.

quotes.quote: 3C 268.3 is a CSS broad line radio galaxy whose \ data are consistent spatially with a point source centered on the nucleus and spectrally with a double power-law model.
3C 268.3, z=0.3717, is classified optically as a BLRG. It is a member of the original fanti1990 sample of CSS sources. The projected total size of the radio source is 6.9 kpc.
The \ observations of 3C 268.3 are consistent with a point source centered on the nucleus whose spectrum can be fit by two power-law components.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C268.3 as an aligned CSS source where emission extends beyond the radio source, registration was difficult, and broad lines suggest it may not lie in the sky plane.

quotes.quote: 3C268.3 (Figs. 3C268.3-montage 3C268.3-overlay ) is an aligned CSS source deVries99 . The emission extends beyond the radio source. Registration of the radio core and the emission line image was problematic due to the lower resolution of the optical image. 3C268.3 shows broad lines, indicating this source may not be in the plane of the sky.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes 3C 268.3 with Gemini long-slit spectra, finding an extended stable rotation component plus surprisingly extended high-excitation Ne V emission in its EELR.

quotes.quote: 3C 268.3 In 3C 268.3, a clear and extended rotation curve is identified by the (blue) asterisks (Fig. VF , left-hand panels), which stabilizes at around 300-400 km s ^ 1 . This is the narrowest component of the fitting, its FWHM is very stable ( v 150 km s ^ 1 ) along the entire region. In the innermost, circumnuclear regions ( ||<1 '') we found the already known disordered motions reported by holt08 , but there is no sign of high-speed motions even in this circumnuclear region, plotted as (green) triangles.

It is straightforward to note that the [ NeV ] 3426 emission is very extended: in 3C 268.3 it is observed up to 2.5'' ( 18.5 kpc) to the SE, a distance that represents 75 per cent of the EELR's extension (given by [ OIII ] 5007 ).

gmw_00004584.name: A1795
gmw_00004584.overview: The central cD galaxy of the Abell 1795 cluster [arXiv:1708.08935, arXiv:astro-ph/9606146] is a brightest cluster galaxy [arXiv:2212.11313] hosting radio jets that have inflated relativistic plasma bubbles [arXiv:1708.08935]. Molecular gas filaments are entrained around these radio bubbles [arXiv:1708.08935, arXiv:1902.09227], feeding an ongoing star formation rate of 9.3 solar masses per year [arXiv:2212.11313].

sources.summary: The paper maps molecular gas in the central galaxy of Abell 1795, finding filaments around radio bubbles with ordered velocities and arguing direct lifting by radio bubbles requires infeasible coupling.

quotes.quote: We present new ALMA observations tracing the morphology and velocity structure of the molecular gas in the central galaxy of the cluster Abell 1795. The molecular gas lies in two filaments that extend 5-7 to the N and S from the nucleus and project exclusively around the outer edges of two inner radio bubbles. Radio jets launched by the central AGN have inflated bubbles filled with relativistic plasma into the hot atmosphere surrounding the central galaxy. The N filament has a smoothly increasing velocity gradient along its length from the central galaxy's systemic velocity at the nucleus to 370 , the average velocity of the surrounding galaxies, at the furthest extent.

The close spatial association with the radio lobes, together with the ordered velocity gradients and narrow velocity dispersions, show that the molecular filaments are gas flows entrained by the expanding radio bubbles. Assuming a Galactic X_ CO factor, the total molecular gas mass is 3.2 0.2 10^ 9 . More than half lies above the N radio bubble. Lifting the molecular clouds appears to require an infeasibly efficient coupling between the molecular gas and the radio bubble.

sources.summary: The paper studies the Abell 1795 BCG, linking its radio jets to molecular gas flows, offset star formation, shocks, turbulence, and short-timescale positive feedback.

quotes.quote: We present XSHOOTER observations with previous ALMA, MUSE and HST observations to study the nature of radio-jet triggered star formation and the interaction of radio jets with the interstellar medium in the brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) in the Abell 1795 cluster. Using HST UV data we determined an ongoing star formation rate of 9.3 M _ yr ^ 1 . The star formation follows the global Kennicutt-Schmidt law, however, it has a low efficiency compared to circumnuclear starbursts in nearby galaxies with an average depletion time of 1 Gyr. The star formation and molecular gas are offset by 1 kpc indicating that stars have decoupled from the gas.

We detected an arc of high linewidth in ionized gas where electron densities are elevated by a factor of 4 suggesting a shock front driven by radio jets or peculiar motion of the BCG. An analysis of nebular emission line flux ratios suggests that the gas is predominantly ionized by star formation with a small contribution from shocks. We also calculated the velocity structure function (VSF) of the ionized and molecular gases using velocity maps to characterize turbulent motion in the gas. The ionized gas VSF suggests that the radio jets are driving supersonic turbulence in the gas.

aliases_in_paper: cooling flow cD galaxy in Abell 1795
sources.summary: The paper presents WFPC2 imaging of the A1795 cD galaxy, emphasizing its asymmetric dust lane, blue knotty emission, radio-lobe alignment, possible induced star formation, and dust extinction measurements.

quotes.quote: We present WFPC2 images of the core of the cooling flow cD galaxy in Abell 1795. An irregular, asymmetric dust lane extends 7 75 kpc in projection to the north-northwest. The dust shares the morphology observed in the H and excess UV emission. We see both diffuse and knotty blue emission around the dust lane, especially at the ends. The dust and emission features lie on the edge of the radio lobes, suggesting star formation induced by the radio source or the deflection of the radio jets off of pre-existing dust and gas.

The images clearly reveal a dust lane along with bluish filamentary and knotty emission features. There is a close correspondence between the radio source and the dust lane and emission features, and we discuss the nature of the radio/dust interaction. We also estimate the mass of the dust lane and compare the extinction law with the Galactic law.

sources.summary: The paper describes A1795 as filament-dominated, with most molecular gas in extended filaments that align with cavities or radio bubbles and show ordered velocity gradients.

quotes.quote: In A1795 and PKS 0745, >70 of the molecular gas reservoir lies in extended filaments.
Filaments are entrained around or clearly extend toward X-ray cavities or radio bubbles in at least six of the twelve targets (A1795, Phoenix, PKS 0745, A1835, A2597, A262).

A1795 and Phoenix represent the most spectacular examples where filaments are exclusively drawn up around radio bubbles. The gas velocities are remarkably ordered over 5-15 in length and several hundred .

sources.summary: The paper discusses A 1795 observations and finds it among BCGs with burst histories extending to 6 Gyr and small-double radio morphology.

quotes.quote: The SBC/F140LP observations of A 1795 had two pointings (north and south) separated by about 30 .

While RXC J1504 is best fitted by a model with a single burst at 10 Myr, the other three seem to prefer multiple bursts with the shortest possible spacing (10 Myr), with ZwCl 3146 and A 1795 going up to the oldest age of 6 Gyr and A 1835 going up to the oldest age of about 3.5 Gyr.

However, given the BCGs in our sample have very different radio morphologies varying from core dominated (RXC J1504), to compact and diffuse (PKS 0745-191 and A 1835), to small doubles (A 2597 and A 1795), to classic FR-Is (Hydra-A and A 2199), it is difficult to imagine a scenario where the power input in the large lobes is matched to the ongoing star formation.

sources.summary: The paper reports that the BCG in Abell 1795 has a very young inner stellar population, a fairly young outer age, and emission lines.

quotes.quote: Two of these BCGs, Abell 780 and Abell 2055, have intermediate/old SSP-equivalent ages in their outer bins (and thus relatively large SSP-equivalent age gradients), whereas Abell 1795 has a fairly young ( 2 Gyr) SSP-equivalent age in its outer aperture.

Unlike the BCGs in Abell 780, 1795 and 2055, which show young components as well as emission lines in their spectra, the BCG in Abell 646 shows some emission lines in its spectrum, but a stellar component in the inner aperture of age 3.7 Gyr.

We summarise our main conclusions as follows: Of the fourteen MENeaCS BCGs, we find three BCGs (Abell 780, Abell 1795 and Abell 2055) with very young stellar populations ( < 200 Myr) in their inner (0 -- 5 kpc) apertures.

gmw_00004585.name: PKS 0745
gmw_00004585.overview: PKS 0745 is a central brightest cluster galaxy [arXiv:1407.8008, arXiv:1602.05962] hosting an active galactic nucleus with disrupted radio jets [arXiv:1407.8008] and a compact nuclear dust lane [arXiv:1311.2831]. The galaxy undergoes periodic starbursts [arXiv:1504.00921] and features massive molecular gas filaments [arXiv:1602.05962, arXiv:1902.09227] that trail behind X-ray cavities [arXiv:1602.05962, arXiv:2207.14326].

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS0745 as a central radio source/BCG with star formation, molecular gas, disrupted jets, and line-emitting gas tied to the cluster core.

quotes.quote: PKS 0745--191 is the radio source located at the centre of a rich galaxy cluster at a redshift of 0.1028. The cluster has been well studied by X-ray observatories since the launch of Einstein Fabian85 . The galaxy cluster is relaxed and has a steeply-peaked surface brightness profile. It is the X-ray brightest at z > 0.1 Edge90 , with a 2 to 10 keV X-ray luminosity of 1.6 10^ 45 and is the nearest cluster with a mass deposition rate inferred from the X-ray surface brightness profile of greater than 1000 Allen96 .

The central galaxy is undergoing considerable star formation. Optical spectra suggest 50 of star formation Johnstone87 and infrared observations using Spitzer give its total IR luminosity as 3.8 10^ 44 , which corresponds to a star formation rate of 17 ODea08 .
The very unusual and interesting radio source in PKS0745 was studied in detail by . The radio jets appear to have been disrupted on small scales so that the large scale structure is amorphous.

sources.summary: The paper studies molecular gas in the PKS 0745-191 brightest cluster galaxy, finding massive low-velocity filaments trailing radio bubbles and star formation in the BCG.

quotes.quote: Here we present ALMA Cycle 1 observations of the molecular gas in the PKS 0745-191 BCG, which with P_ cav 5 10^ 45 has undergone an even more powerful radio jet outburst than Abell 1835 ( Rafferty06 ; Sanders14 ). Single dish observations of PKS 0745-191 detected the BCG at CO(1-0) and CO(2-1) and found a total molecular gas mass of 3.8 0.9 10^ 9 ( Salome03 ). Optical, UV and IR observations show significant star formation at a rate of 20 and a luminous emission line nebula with clumpy filaments extending >10 in the BCG ( Fabian85 ; Johnstone87 ; Donahue00 ; HicksMushotzky05 ; Tremblay15 ).

The velocities of the molecular clouds in the filaments are low, lying within 100 of the galaxy's systemic velocity. Their FWHMs are less than 150 , which is significantly below the stellar velocity dispersion. Although the molecular mass of each filament is comparable to a rich spiral galaxy, such low velocities show that the filaments are transient and the clouds would disperse on <10^7 timescales unless supported, likely by the indirect effect of magnetic fields. The velocity structure is inconsistent with a merger origin or gravitational free-fall of cooling gas in this massive central galaxy. If the molecular clouds originated in gas cooling even a few kpc from their current locations their velocities would exceed those observed.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0745-19 as a central cluster galaxy with triangular optical line emission, irregular radio emission, compact nuclear dust, and no significant derived line polarization.

quotes.quote: PKS 0745-19: Heckman et al. (1989), Donahue et al. (2000), Wilman et al. (2009) show that the optical line emission forms a roughly triangular or conical shape to the West of the nucleus, with two dominant arms of emission. The radio source is irregular and coincident in scale with the optical filaments, located primarily to the South. Slits centered on the nucleus with position angles 90 ^ and 45 ^ were used, Fig. 3.

PKS0745-19 The strong emission lines of PKS0745-19 are readily apparent in the spectra displayed as Fig. 10. The overall appearance of the spectra is very similar to that of Hydra A, even to the presence of a compact nuclear absorbing dust lane. As in the case of Hydra A, the apparent presence of slightly polarized emission is attributable to the random errors, and no significant line polarization was derived for this object, in either of the two slit positions, Table 3.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0745-191 as a central active galaxy with extended nebular emission tied to asymmetric X-ray cavities and redshifted [OII] gas.

quotes.quote: Above a stellar flux per spaxel of 2.5 10^ 16 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 , the stellar continuum is detected in a roughly circular region with a radius of 8.3 kpc around the brightest stellar spaxel. This target has a total stellar luminosity of 1.78 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 , half of which is emitted within an inner region of 4.1 kpc in radius. This target has one projected galaxy within the FOV shown in Fig. fig:flux:PKS0745 , which is located 8.5 kpc SW of the nucleus of the central galaxy.

The [ OII ] emission spans over 33 kpc in RA and 30 kpc in Dec, covering a total area of 542 kpc ^2 (see Table tab:OII_lum ). The [ OII ] morphology is related to the position of the X-ray cavities. As previously observed in many galaxies Russell2016,Olivares2019 , the [ OII ] emission extends from the nucleus towards and around the edges of the two X-ray cavities.

sources.summary: The paper confirms twin-arm emission in the central galaxy of PKS 0745-191 and interprets its kinematics as rotation around an axis aligned with the radio jet.

quotes.quote: For PKS 0745-191, we confirm the twin-arm morphology in the narrow-band images of Donahue et al.; the kinematics suggest rotational motion about an axis aligned with the kiloparsec-scale radio jet; on nucleus, we find an underlying broad component (FWHM 1700 ) and a secondary 2 velocity system redshifted by +500 . Our findings suggest that the three clusters may be captured in different stages of the `cold feedback' cycle of Pizzolato Soker, with A1664 in a short-lived phase of extreme cooling and star formation prior to an AGN heating event; PKS 0745-191 in an outburst state with the AGN accreting from a cool gas disk, and A2204 in a later phase in which cool gas is dragged out of the galaxy by the buoyant rise of old radio bubbles. Donahue et al. (2000) presented Hubble Space Telescope imaging of the central cluster galaxy of PKS 0745-191 in emission lines of 2 v=1-0 S(3) and + [ NII ] .

Our and 2 v=1-0 S(3) emission line maps shown in Fig. fig:PKS0745totals are in excellent correspondence with their observations. The extended line emission is characterised by two roughly-perpendicular, 5 long, arm-like features which protrude on the western side of the nucleus, and by a short spur which extends 1.5 from the nucleus on the eastern side.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS0745+091 as a BCG whose molecular gas is arranged in three filaments, partly trailing an X-ray cavity, with velocity gradients interpreted as gas flows.

quotes.quote: PKS0745+091 The molecular gas in PKS0745+091 is distributed in three filaments extending north, southwest (SW), and southeast (SE) directions, respectively. The northern filament is most extended at 5.73 kpc followed by SW and SE filaments at projected lengths of 3.6 and 2.7 kpc, respectively. The north and SW filaments appear to be trailing behind an X-ray cavity. The PV diagrams reveal a a velocity gradient from 150 to 50 km s ^ 1 see Fig. 8, russell16 . We consider these filaments as flows in this BCG and are shown in Fig. maps .

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0745 as a filament-dominated central galaxy, with over 70 percent of molecular gas in filaments linked to cavities and a possible new AGN outburst.

quotes.quote: In A1795 and PKS 0745, >70 of the molecular gas reservoir lies in extended filaments.

Filaments are entrained around or clearly extend toward X-ray cavities or radio bubbles in at least six of the twelve targets (A1795, Phoenix, PKS 0745, A1835, A2597, A262). In PKS 0745, for example, the SE filament extends toward the SE cavity, the N filament extends towards the more distant NW cavity. The SW filament is aligned with a lobe of radio emission that may originate in a new AGN outburst ( Russell16 ).

sources.summary: The paper includes PKS 0745-191 as a BCG with limited photometric coverage, compact diffuse radio morphology, and periodic starbursts on 20-200 Myr intervals.

quotes.quote: We have in total 8 data points for all BCGs except one (PKS 0745-191), which is lacking GALEX and SDSS data altogether, and for which there are 5 data points available.

However, given the BCGs in our sample have very different radio morphologies varying from core dominated (RXC J1504), to compact and diffuse (PKS 0745-191 and A 1835), to small doubles (A 2597 and A 1795), to classic FR-Is (Hydra-A and A 2199), it is difficult to imagine a scenario where the power input in the large lobes is matched to the ongoing star formation.

While ZwCl 3146, A 1795, A 1835 and RXC J1504 seem to require continuous star formation, NGC 1275, PKS 0745, Hydra-A, A 1068 and A 2597 seem to require periodic starbursts with the interval ranging between 20 Myr and 200 Myr.

gmw_00004586.name: RXJ 0821
gmw_00004586.overview: RXJ 0821 is a central cluster galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0208254] that is likely interacting with a secondary galaxy projected 7.8 kiloparsecs to its southeast [arXiv:2404.02212]. The system features offset molecular gas clumps [arXiv:1709.09679, arXiv:1902.09227] and substantially redshifted nebular gas [arXiv:2404.02212].

sources.summary: The paper maps RXJ0820.9+0752 central-galaxy emission, finding a nearby secondary galaxy and offset, redshifted nebular gas shaped by interactions.

quotes.quote: This system has a secondary galaxy projected 7.8 kpc to the south-east of the central galaxy. It is seen in Fig. fig:flux:RXJ0820 as a secondary peak in the stellar continuum map. The stellar flux of the central galaxy is 3 times greater than for the secondary galaxy, with stellar luminosities within 5 kpc of their respective peaks of 1.7 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 and 6.0 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 . The two galaxies have a relative velocity of almost 100 km s ^ 1 , with the secondary galaxy being more redshifted. The proximity of the galaxies and their relative velocity indicate that they are likely interacting.

Fig. fig:kinematics:RXJ0820 displays the LOS kinematic maps of the nebular gas in the central galaxy of RXJ0820.9+0752, which has a redshift of z= 0.1103 (see Table tab:target_properties ). As shown in the top left panel of Fig. fig:kinematics:RXJ0820 , the nebular gas is substantially redshifted with respect to the systemic velocity. The [ OII ] median velocities range between 12 km s ^ 1 and +286 km s ^ 1 , with a flux weighted mean v _ 50 of 150 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper describes RX J0821 as a central cluster galaxy with a luminous line-emitting nebula, weak radio core, molecular gas, dust, and star-forming blobs shaped by cooling-flow and tidal effects.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: the coordinates of the nucleus of the CCG being =08 :21:02.28, =+07 :51:46.8 (J2000)

quotes.quote: The galaxy is at the centre of the cluster associated with the ROSAT X-ray source RX J0820.9+0752 For convenience, we shall use `RX J0821' throughout the rest of this paper to refer to the central galaxy and its associated line-emitting nebula. , the coordinates of the nucleus of the CCG being =08 :21:02.28, =+07 :51:46.8 (J2000). The cluster was discovered as part of the extended Brightest Cluster Sample (Ebeling 2000), and optical spectroscopy on the central galaxy yielded a redshift of 0.110 (Crawford 1995). It has a ROSAT 0.1-2.4 \ (rest frame) luminosity of L _X=2.09 10^ 44 We assume a cosmology with a Hubble constant of H_0=50 \ and a cosmological deceleration parameter of q_0=0.5 throughout this paper. , and an estimated temperature of 4.4 . The spectrum of the central cluster galaxy showed it to have strong + [ NII ] emission lines (a slit \ luminosity of around 3.3 0.2 10^ 41 ) but with a lack of \ that implied large amounts of intrinsic obscuration to be present in this system (Crawford 1995).

The radio power of RX J0821 is the third lowest of all line-emitting CCGs in the BCS (only A262 and RX J1733.0+4345/IC1262 have lower powers) and may not be associated with the core of the CCG (as is found in the vast majority of BCS line-emitting CCGs) but instead be coincident with the main star-forming regions. The discovery of radio emission from star formation rather than nuclear activity is intriguing and needs more detailed radio imaging to verify.

aliases_in_paper: BCG of the RXJ0821+0752 galaxy cluster
sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA CO observations of the RXJ0821+0752 brightest cluster galaxy, finding offset molecular clumps, extended diffuse emission, and ^13CO confined to the bright clumps.

quotes.quote: We present ALMA Cycle 4 observations of CO(1-0), CO(3-2), and ^ 13 CO(3-2) line emission in the brightest cluster galaxy of RXJ0821+0752. This is one of the first detections of ^ 13 CO line emission in a galaxy cluster. Half of the CO(3-2) line emission originates from two clumps of molecular gas that are spatially offset from the galactic center. These clumps are surrounded by diffuse emission that extends 8 kpc in length. The detected ^ 13 CO emission is confined entirely to the two bright clumps, with any emission outside of this region lying below our detection threshold.

sources.summary: The paper describes RXJ 0821 as having an offset molecular peak, complex velocity structure, CO line ratios, and a conversion-factor test from detected carbon monoxide isotopologue emission.

quotes.quote: RXJ0821 features the largest projected offset of 3 between the central galaxy's nucleus and the molecular gas peak. Vantyghem18 suggest the wholesale displacement of the 10^ 10 molecular reservoir in this system is due to sloshing motions in the intracluster medium triggered by the close passage of a nearby galaxy.

Similarly, ALMA observations of the molecular filaments around or beneath radio bubbles in A1795, Phoenix, PKS 0745, 2A 0335, RXCJ 1504 and RXJ 0821 have smooth velocity gradients along their lengths or over few kpc-long sections.

Vantyghem17 showed that adopting a Galactic conversion factor could overestimate the molecular gas mass by a factor of two in RXJ 0821. This is easily within the object-to-object scatter from extragalactic sources.

gmw_00004587.name: VCC 740
gmw_00004587.overview: VCC 740 is a highly inclined dwarf spiral galaxy with a normal neutral hydrogen gas content [arXiv:0909.3615]. Its gas velocity field exhibits warping, but the galaxy shows no signs of interaction with NGC 4356 [arXiv:0909.3615].

sources.summary: The paper describes VCC 740 as a high-S/N, highly inclined dwarf spiral with normal H I content, warped velocity field, and no interaction signs with NGC 4356.

quotes.quote: VCC 740, another highly inclined galaxy detected in this same pointing, has, in contrast, quite a strong S/N and a total flux that is about the same that in NGC 4307. Its map suggests that the neutral gas has a sharp cutoff at the optical radius of the disk on the approaching side, while the other side has a more gradual falloff and is somewhat more extended. In spite of giving the impression that part of the gas might be missing, both ours and the ALFALFA flux measurements actually result in a negative deficiency parameter of 0.2 , indicative of a perfectly normal content.

The gas velocity field of VCC 740, on the other hand, exhibits some warping, as well as inner contours parallel to the minor axis, indicating that V_ rot (R) R , as the PV diagram shows. Both Brandt and tilted-ring models yield an estimated inclination of 70 for the internal disk region that drops to 45 on the outside. The fact that the velocity field of this galaxy shows a clear rotation pattern reinforces its morphological classification as a dwarf barred disk instead of the IB type assigned in the LEDA. No signs of interaction are found between VCC 740 and NGC 4356.

gmw_00004588.name: NGC 4411A
gmw_00004588.overview: NGC 4411A is a low surface brightness spiral galaxy with a normal abundance of gas [arXiv:0909.3615]. Its neutral hydrogen distribution extends up to nearly twice its optical radius, except on the northeast side where the gas contours appear compressed [arXiv:0909.3615].

sources.summary: The paper reclassifies NGC 4411A as normally gas rich, maps extended ring-like H I features, and finds uncertain low-inclination rotation-curve fits.

quotes.quote: NGC 4411A/B, VCC 933 and VCC 976 Contrarily to what has happened with NGC 4307 and NGC 4356, the two low surface brightness spirals NGC 4411A and B have produced integrated fluxes that are inconsistent with the values quoted in Sol02 , which were estimated in earliest mapping attempts done with the Arecibo antenna Hay81. Our new data, reinforced by the newest single-dish measurements done at Arecibo by the ALFALFA team Ken08 , imply that NGC 4411A and NGC 4411B loose their initial status of objects with moderate and strong deficiency, respectively (see Table toribio_tab1 ), to be both reclassified as galaxies with quite a normal gas abundance, in accordance with the visual impression obtained from the contours overlaid on the Digitized Sky Survey (DSS) image. These maps show that these two galaxies have distributions extending beyond their optical disks, which for NGC 4411A reach up to nearly twice the optical radius, except on the NE side where the contours appear compressed.

In this latter galaxy the is concentrated in a ring with two important regions of emission that emanate perpendicularly from the ends of the bar. In NGC 4411A, the turnover in the rotation velocity is not complete and the 21 cm line profile is affected by a larger asymmetry. The apparent inclination inferred from the tilted-ring technique for NGC 4411A is 29^ +5.2 _ 3.7 degrees, while for NGC 4411B we get i=26^ +4.4 _ 4.7 degrees.

gmw_00004589.name: NGC 4411B
gmw_00004589.overview: NGC 4411B is a low surface brightness spiral galaxy with a near face-on orientation [arXiv:0909.3615]. It exhibits a normal abundance of neutral hydrogen gas arranged in an extended, ring-like structure with a central depression [arXiv:0909.3615].

sources.summary: The paper reclassifies NGC 4411B as normally gas abundant, with an extended ring-like H I structure, no bridge to NGC 4411A, and face-on kinematic uncertainties.

quotes.quote: Our new data, reinforced by the newest single-dish measurements done at Arecibo by the ALFALFA team Ken08 , imply that NGC 4411A and NGC 4411B loose their initial status of objects with moderate and strong deficiency, respectively (see Table toribio_tab1 ), to be both reclassified as galaxies with quite a normal gas abundance, in accordance with the visual impression obtained from the contours overlaid on the Digitized Sky Survey (DSS) image. The 0 th-order map of NGC 4411B shows an even wider major ring-like structure with a noticeable excess of emission at its northern half near the outer edge. We do not find important displacements from the optical disks, the most remarkable feature being the presence of a depression in the center of both galaxies, as found in other LSB galaxies dBMvH96.

We fail in detecting neither gas bridges nor significant intergalactic signals between these two targets. Regarding the kinematics of the neutral gas, we note that the PV diagram of NGC 4411B shows a steep rise well within the stellar disk (of small amplitude given its near face-on orientation) followed by a sharp bend towards a flat part, ---with indications of a modest decline on the receding side at the largest radii where the is detected---, consistent with the shape of the radial velocity contours and with the well behaved double-horned global profile. The apparent inclination inferred from the tilted-ring technique for NGC 4411A is 29^ +5.2 _ 3.7 degrees, while for NGC 4411B we get i=26^ +4.4 _ 4.7 degrees.

gmw_00004590.name: AzTEC/C61
gmw_00004590.overview: AzTEC/C61 is a high-redshift submillimeter galaxy that harbors an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1702.07527]. This active nucleus is indicated by a high rest-frame X-ray luminosity, a very high brightness temperature, and a mildly inverted radio spectrum [arXiv:1702.07527].

sources.summary: The paper identifies AzTEC/C61 as a VLBA- and X-ray-detected SMG whose high brightness temperature, X-ray luminosity, and inverted radio spectrum indicate an AGN.

quotes.quote: One of these three SMGs, AzTEC/C61, was also detected in the X-rays with Chandra (the 1.8 Ms Chandra COSMOS Survey (C-COSMOS; ; ) and the Chandra COSMOS Legacy Survey ( )). The source is identified as CID-1787 in the Chandra COSMOS Legacy Survey ( 0 25 south-west from the ALMA position), and its flux density in the 0.5--2 keV (soft), 2--10 keV (hard), and 0.5--10 keV (full) bands is S_ X =1.60 10^ 15 , 4.24 10^ 15 , and 6.54 10^ 15 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , respectively ( ). The absorption-corrected, rest-frame 2--10 keV luminosity of AzTEC/C61 is L_ 2-10 keV =1.7 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 (scaled from the photometric redshift of z_ phot =1.56 reported in the catalogue to z_ spec =3.2671 ; ), which is too high to arise only from stellar processes, and hence AzTEC/C61 very likely harbours an AGN.

On the other hand, AzTEC/C61 exhibits a mildly inverted radio spectrum, which is yet another indication of the presence of an AGN; a rising radio spectrum is a characteristic of self-absorbed, black hole accretion-driven synchrotron jet component (e.g. ; ; ). However, it is likely that the observed 3 GHz radio emission from our SMGs is predominantly powered by SN activity (hence linked to star formation) with the exception of the VLBA and X-ray detected SMG AzTEC/C61, where the very high observed (rest-frame) brightness temperature of >10^ 4.03 K ( >10^ 4.66 K) together with a positive spectral index between the rest-frame frequencies of 6.0 GHz and 12.8 GHz suggest an AGN-powered radio emission (e.g. ; ; ).

gmw_00004591.name: AzTEC/C22
gmw_00004591.overview: AzTEC/C22 is a candidate high-redshift galaxy merger system at a spectroscopic redshift of z = 1.60 [arXiv:1702.07527, arXiv:1312.1173]. It consists of two dust-emitting bodies separated by 13.8 kpc in projection, which are connected by a radio-emitting bridge [arXiv:1702.07527].

sources.summary: The paper treats AzTEC/C22 as a candidate high-redshift Taffy-like merger, with two dust bodies, associated radio emission, and a radio-emitting bridge.

quotes.quote: Among our target SMGs, AzTEC/C22 and AzTEC/C42 are good candidates for Taffy-like systems, as illustrated in Fig. figure:taffy. The ALMA 1.3 mm image of AzTEC/C22 shows the presence of two dust-emitting bodies separated by 13.8 kpc in projection, both associated with 3 GHz radio emission with a radio-emitting bridge connecting them. A well-separated binary nature of AzTEC/C22 and C42 suggests an incomplete, or an early-stage SMG-SMG merger, and part of the radio emission from these systems is likely to arise from a magnetised medium between them.

Among the aforementioned systems, we derived a flat rest-frame spectral index of _ 3.6 GHz ^ 7.8 GHz =-0.37 0.27 for AzTEC/C22a (the northern component), and a fairly flat value of _ 6.5 GHz ^ 13.9 GHz =-0.50 0.24 for AzTEC/C42. Hence, our candidate Taffy systems, AzTEC/C22 and C42, could potentially be high-redshift manifestations of the trend found by Murphy (2013).

sources.summary: The paper treats AzTEC11 as one extended source split by SMA imaging, with z phot matching z = 1.60 and secure recovery by all four association methods.

quotes.quote: AzTEC11. This source is split into two components by the SMA imaging, but it may be an extended object and therefore we continue to treat it as a single source. z_ phot is in excellent agreement with the spectroscopic redshift z = 1.60 , while this time z_ LW is somewhat over-estimated. This source would have been securely identified using all four types of statistical association on the basis of the original AzTEC position.

sources.summary: The paper says source 2 had an incorrect catalogue redshift; the VLA setup missed its CO(1-0) line, so it was excluded from further analysis.

quotes.quote: Because we relied upon the catalogued redshifts when taking our VLA data, the observed frequency intervals for sources 2 and 9 are not optimised for the correct redshift (see note below Table tab:source_info ). Thus, the frequency range of the observations for source 2 does not encompass the CO(1-0) line, whereas for source 9, the expected position of CO(1-0) falls at the edge of the observed frequencies, where the spectral noise is greatest.
Thus, we exclude sources 2, 6, 9 and 14 (starred in Table tab:source_info ) from further analysis.

gmw_00004592.name: SGP 8884
gmw_00004592.overview: SGP 8884 is an unresolved Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a redshift of 5.652 [arXiv:astro-ph/0605019]. It exhibits a star formation rate of 32 solar masses per year and shows evidence of a second spectral line component on its red side [arXiv:astro-ph/0605019].

sources.summary: The paper spectroscopically confirms SGP 8884 as an unresolved z=5.652 Lyman-alpha emitter with high line flux, inferred star formation, and a possible red-side second line component.

quotes.quote: Here we present the spectral confirmation of a new candidate, J004525.38 292402.8 (hereafter SGP 8884), at z=5.652 . We also show its pre-imaging and compare its \ profile to S11 5236. SGP 8884 and S11 5236 are the only two out of the seven candidates presented in this paper for which we have obtained spectra.

SGP 8884 is unresolved, implying that the FWHM of the emitting region is 2.2 kpc. With a total spectral line flux of (1.0 0.1) 10 ^ 16 \ (slit-loss corrected), SGP 8884 is the brightest LAE at redshift 5.7 to date. The spectral line flux corresponds to a line luminosity of L_ line = 3.5 43 \ and a star formation rate of 32 M_ yr ^ 1 , using the star formation conversion rate of Ajiki03 .
We find an excess of flux in the observed data compared to the model around 8110 . This suggests the presence of a second line component redward of the main peak.

gmw_00004593.name: S11 5236
gmw_00004593.overview: S11 5236 is a compact, high-redshift Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a redshift of z=5.721 [arXiv:astro-ph/0411782, arXiv:astro-ph/0605019]. It is a starburst galaxy with an asymmetric emission line profile and an apparent star-formation rate of 16 solar masses per year [arXiv:astro-ph/0411782].

sources.summary: The paper confirms S11 13368 as a compact high-redshift Lyman-alpha emitter at z=5.721, with an asymmetric line, high line flux, and inferred star formation.

quotes.quote: In a pilot study to test the effectiveness of the selection strategy one of the brighter candidates ( J114334.98-014433.9 , hereafter S11 13368 ) was observed with FORS2 on Yepun (UT4) at the Cerro Paranal Observatory (Figure fig:thumbs ). A pre-image with an intermediate-band filter (13 nm) centered at 815 nm was taken with FORS2 on 2004 February 16th in which S11 13368 clearly was detected. Figure fig:pre shows a 30 \ region around S11 13368 . The FWHM of stars in this field are 0 5 and S11 13368 is unresolved.

Given the absence of all of these potential neighbouring features, and also the clear asymmetry of the line, we identify it as \ emission at z=5.721 . The integrated line flux of the line derived from the spectrum is 5 10 ^ 17 , making it one of the brightest \ emitting sources at this redshift cf. Ajiki03,Rhoads03,Maier03 . The 2 lower limit on the rest frame equivalent width is 100 . At z=5.721 , this translates to a \ luminosity of 1.8 10 ^ 43 \ suggesting an apparent star-formation rate of 16 , using the conversion rate from Ajiki03 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies S11 5236 as a previously confirmed z=5.721 Lyman-alpha emitter and compares its stronger, narrower red line component with SGP 8884.

quotes.quote: In Westra05 we reported the spectroscopic follow-up of one of the candidates, J114334.98 014433.7 (S11 13368 in that paper, hereafter S11 5236 The object names are derived from \ IDs. Refinements to our detection procedures since Westra05 caused a change in the ID and, therefore, in the object name ). It was confirmed to be a LAE at z=5.721 . We also show its pre-imaging and compare its \ profile to S11 5236.

Figure  fig:fitSGPS11 shows a comparison between the line profiles of the two LAEs discovered with WFILAS. S11 5236 differs from SGP 8884 in that a clear peak, 20 90 \ wide, is seen 400 \ redward of \ Westra05 . The red component is narrower ( 15 ) and relatively stronger than SGP 8884.

gmw_00004594.name: NGC 4344
gmw_00004594.overview: NGC 4344 is a face-on S0 galaxy [arXiv:1507.05094] that is also classified as a blue compact dwarf [arXiv:astro-ph/0111178]. Its disk features a 1.7 kpc broken ring of blue starburst knots where young stars are forming, alongside visible patches of cold dust [arXiv:1507.05094, arXiv:astro-ph/0111178].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4344 as a Virgo mid-infrared enhanced face-on S0 with a broken ring of blue starburst knots, dust patches, and young-star formation in its disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 4344 is a face-on S0 whose optical appearance is dominated by a prominent, broken ring of blue starburst knots. Patches of dust are visible also, possibly causing the gaps in the ring. At the adopted distance, this ring has a diameter of 1.7 kpc. No HST images are available for this system.

Blue knots are interspersed within each structure indicating that young stars are forming within these disks. We can conclude that the MIEG phenomenon in the Virgo systems arises from an excess amount of dust, which appears to be related to the stellar disk most but not all systems.

sources.summary: The paper identifies BCD VCC 655 as the coldest three-wavelength cold-dust object, with the largest cold-dust surface density and dust-to-HI mass ratio.

quotes.quote: The galaxy with the largest content of cold dust with respect to its optical size is VCC 655.
The galaxy with the largest dust-to-HI mass ratio is VCC 655.
The BCD VCC 655 has the coldest dust temperature (12.6 K) among the galaxies with 3 wavelengths detection.

gmw_00004595.name: DES J025216.64--050331.8
gmw_00004595.overview: DES J025216.64--050331.8 is a luminous quasar at a redshift of z=7.00 [arXiv:2004.10877]. It hosts a supermassive black hole of approximately 1.39 billion solar masses and exhibits a strong Lyman-alpha damping wing absorption feature from a highly neutral intergalactic medium [arXiv:2004.10877].

sources.summary: The paper presents DES J025216.64--050331.8 as a luminous z=7 quasar hosting a billion-solar-mass SMBH and showing strong IGM damping-wing absorption implying a high neutral fraction.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present new high quality optical and near-infrared spectroscopy of the z=7.00 quasar DES J025216.64--050331.8 obtained with Keck/NIRES and Gemini/GMOS. 1 By using the Mg ii single-epoch virial method , we find that it hosts a (1.39 0.16) 10^ 9 M_ SMBH accreting at an Eddington ratio of _ Edd =0.7 0.1 , consistent with the values seen in other luminous z 7 quasars. Furthermore, the Ly region of the spectrum exhibits a strong damping wing absorption feature. The lack of associated metal absorption in the quasar spectrum indicates that this absorption is imprinted by a neutral IGM.

Using a state-of-the-art model developed by Davies et al., we measure a volume-averaged neutral hydrogen fraction at z=7 of x_ HI = 0.70^ +0.20 _ 0.23 (^ +0.28 _ 0.48 ) within 68 (95 ) confidence intervals when marginalizing over quasar lifetimes of 10^3 t_ Q 10^8 yr. This is the highest IGM neutral fraction yet measured using reionization-era quasar spectra.

gmw_00004596.name: J0326 0623
gmw_00004596.overview: J0326 0623 is a face-on spiral galaxy with two major spiral arms located at a redshift of z = 0.18 [arXiv:2301.01548]. It serves as the brightest cluster galaxy of its host cluster and hosts slightly bent radio lobes spanning 430 kpc [arXiv:2301.01548].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0326 0623 as a new face-on spiral galaxy with double radio lobes, BCG environment, a foreground-source-contaminated upper lobe, and 430 kpc radio size.

quotes.quote: J0326 0623 is a face-on galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.18 with two major spiral arms, clearly shown in the zoomed DESI image and the model-subtracted residual image in Fig. fig1 . This galaxy is the brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) of a galaxy cluster in the catalog of Yang07 , which contains 13 bright member galaxies of M^ e _ 20.5 mag. The total flux density detected by NVSS at 1.4 GHz is about 6 mJy.

However, the upper lobe is superimposed by a point-like radio source as detected by FIRST, which is associated with J032624 062212, a foreground galaxy at z 0.16 . The morphology of the radio lobes is slightly bent. It has a size of 430 kpc as inferred by the NVSS 5 contour.

gmw_00004597.name: J1110+0321
gmw_00004597.overview: J1110+0321 is a blue spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.03 [arXiv:2301.01548]. It belongs to the Tempel12 galaxy group and hosts radio lobes with an overall scale of approximately 100 kpc [arXiv:2301.01548].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1110+0321 as a new blue spiral with radio lobes, group membership, nearby NVSS lobes, FIRST peak sources, and an overall radio-lobe scale near 100 kpc.

quotes.quote: J1110+0321 is a blue galaxy at z = 0.03 . The optical observational and residual images displayed in Fig. fig1 indicate spiral-arm structures. This galaxy belongs to a galaxy group Tempel12 , which contains seven members brighter than 20.5 mag.

The NVSS image features an elongated morphology with two lobes close to each other, and FIRST detects bright sources at the peak in each lobe. The inner-west component detected by FIRST is probably partially associated with a background quasar QSO B1107+0337 at z=0.965 . The overall scale of the radio lobes measured based on the NVSS 5 contour is about 100 kpc.

gmw_00004598.name: J1128+2417
gmw_00004598.overview: J1128+2417 is a blue spiral satellite galaxy located within a four-member galaxy group [arXiv:2301.01548]. High-quality Hubble Space Telescope imaging confirms its spiral structure, while radio observations reveal unresolved lobes and two bright jets spanning approximately 380 kpc [arXiv:2301.01548].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1128+2417 as an independently found blue spiral satellite with HST-confirmed spiral structure, unresolved NVSS lobes, FIRST jets, and radio emission around 380 kpc.

quotes.quote: J1128+2417 is a blue galaxy at z = 0.169 . The optical residual image for this galaxy presents a faint imprint of spiral patterns, while the high-quality deeper image from the Hubble Space Telescope Wu22 clearly shows the existence of the spiral structures. With the method introduced in Section envir , we find this galaxy is a satellite galaxy in a galaxy group, which contains four members with M^ e _ 20.5 mag. The NVSS map shows unresolved radio lobes with an elongated morphology, but FIRST detects two bright jets with some bridge emission. The scale for the radio emission indicated by the NVSS is around 380 kpc.

gmw_00004599.name: J1134+3046
gmw_00004599.overview: J1134+3046 is a blue spiral galaxy located within a small galaxy group [arXiv:2301.01548]. The galaxy features clear spiral arms and exhibits radio emission spanning approximately 190 kpc, which includes elongated radio lobes and clear jets [arXiv:2301.01548].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1134+3046 as a new blue spiral in a small group, with clear spiral arms, elongated NVSS radio lobes, FIRST jets, and radio emission about 190 kpc across.

quotes.quote: J1134+3046 is also a blue galaxy at z = 0.046 . The optical residual map of this galaxy presents clear structures of spiral arms. This galaxy is a member in a galaxy group Tempel12 , which contains four bright member galaxies with M^ e _ 20.5 mag. Similar to J1110+0321 and J1128+2417, the NVSS map of J1134+3046 shows unresolved radio lobes with an elongated morphology, while the FIRST image presents clear jets. The overall scale of radio emission presented by the NVSS map is approximately 190 kpc.

gmw_00004600.name: J0855+5751
gmw_00004600.overview: J0855+5751 is a compact Fanaroff-Riley class II radio galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.54186 [arXiv:1607.05995]. The galaxy is classified as a Compact Symmetric Object and exhibits a distorted radio structure indicative of a strong jet-cloud interaction [arXiv:1607.05995].

sources.summary: The paper measures J0855+5751 as a compact FRII radio source at z=0.54186, notes evidence for jet-cloud interaction, and suggests H I and OH absorption searches.

quotes.quote: In addition, the distorted appearance of the background radio source is indicative of a strong jet-cloud interaction in its host galaxy. We have measured its redshift ( z = 0.54186 ) using optical spectroscopy on the William Herschel Telescope and this confirms that J0855+5751 is a FRII radio source with a physical extent of < 1 kpc and supports the previous identification of this source as a Compact Symmetric Object. These sources often show absorption associated with the host galaxy and we suggest that both H i and OH should be searched for in J0855+5751.

The background source, J0855+5751, has a 1.4-GHz flux density of 636 mJy white97 and a projected distance from the foreground galaxy of 6.8 kpc, a typical impact parameter for H i absorption detected in the gas disk of a galaxy with a luminosity of 0.05 L^* zwaan05a . VLBA observations at 2.3 beasley02 and 5 GHz helmboldt07 have demonstrated that this source consists of two resolved components separated by about 60 mas (Fig. fig:vlba ). On the basis of the observed VLBI structure, taylor05 have classified this source as a Compact Symmetric Object (CSO).

gmw_00004601.name: 4C +55.17
gmw_00004601.overview: 4C +55.17 is a high-redshift flat-spectrum radio quasar located at a redshift of approximately 0.901 [arXiv:1705.11185]. The active galactic nucleus exhibits very high energy emission above 100 GeV [arXiv:1705.11185].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 4C +55.17 as a high-redshift FSRQ VHE source, adopts an SDSS redshift near 0.901, and predicts CTA observability consistent with EBL models.

quotes.quote: A second approach uses 5 sources (PKS 0426-380, 4C +55.17, Ton 116, PG 1246+586 and RBS 1432) which were found to exhibit very high energy emission ( E_ >100 GeV). Through Monte Carlo simulations it is shown that the observation of VHE photons, despite the large distances of these objects, is consistent with current EBL models. Many of these sources would be observable with the upcoming ground based observatory the Cherenkov Telescope Array (CTA), leading to a better understanding of the EBL.

4C +55.17: Defined as a FSRQ, and located in the northern hemisphere at RA=149.41 ^ , Dec=55.38 ^ , in the 2FHL the redshift is quoted as 0.899, most likely obtained from . However, more recent measurements from the 13th SDSS data release sdssdr13 finds a redshift of 0.901 0.00019 which will be adopted in this work. This source was considered in where a redshift of 0.8955 was adopted.

gmw_00004602.name: Ton 116
gmw_00004602.overview: Ton 116 is a high-redshift BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1705.11185]. It exhibits very high energy emission above 100 GeV, though its exact redshift remains uncertain due to conflicting Sloan Digital Sky Survey measurements [arXiv:1705.11185].

sources.summary: The paper uses Ton 116 as a high-redshift BL Lac example in EBL scaling analysis, discusses uncertain SDSS redshifts, and expects its VHE emission to be CTA-observable.

quotes.quote: A second approach uses 5 sources (PKS 0426-380, 4C +55.17, Ton 116, PG 1246+586 and RBS 1432) which were found to exhibit very high energy emission ( E_ >100 GeV). Through Monte Carlo simulations it is shown that the observation of VHE photons, despite the large distances of these objects, is consistent with current EBL models. Many of these sources would be observable with the upcoming ground based observatory the Cherenkov Telescope Array (CTA), leading to a better understanding of the EBL.

Ton 116: A BL Lac located in the northern hemisphere at RA=190.80 ^ , Dec=36.46 ^ with a redshift of 0.0 in the 2FHL indicating that no redshift was available at the time or that it was determined to be unreliable. However, measurements from SDSS indicate a redshift of 1.066 0.00150 (data release 7, sdssdr7 ) or 1.182 0.00132 (data release 13 sdssdr13 ); both have data flags associated with the spectrum. This source was also considered in in which a redshift of 1.065 was used.

gmw_00004603.name: PG 1246+586
gmw_00004603.overview: PG 1246+586 is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1705.11185] located at a redshift of 0.847 [arXiv:1705.11185]. The active galaxy has exhibited very high energy emission, including the detection of a 251 GeV photon [arXiv:1705.11185].

sources.summary: The paper describes PG 1246+586 as a BL Lac with SDSS redshift 0.847, detected high-energy photons consistent with simulations, and expected CTA observability.

quotes.quote: A second approach uses 5 sources (PKS 0426-380, 4C +55.17, Ton 116, PG 1246+586 and RBS 1432) which were found to exhibit very high energy emission ( E_ >100 GeV). Through Monte Carlo simulations it is shown that the observation of VHE photons, despite the large distances of these objects, is consistent with current EBL models. Many of these sources would be observable with the upcoming ground based observatory the Cherenkov Telescope Array (CTA), leading to a better understanding of the EBL.

PG 1246+586 A BL Lac located in the northern hemisphere at RA=192.08 ^ , Dec=58.34 ^ and with an unknown or uncertain redshift in the 2FHL. Measurements from SDSS (data release 7 sdssdr7 ) show a redshift of 0.847 0.00169. This source was considered in who applied the same redshift as used here.

From these simulations, it is evident that, in the 8 years of Fermi LAT observations, the highest energy photons detected are within expectation, with all but the 251 GeV photon from PG 1246+586 at 2.2 found at an energy less than one standard deviation from the simulated mean value.

gmw_00004604.name: RBS 1432
gmw_00004604.overview: RBS 1432 is a high-redshift BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1705.11185] with an uncertain redshift generally accepted to be 1.565 [arXiv:1705.11185]. The active galaxy exhibits very high energy emission above 100 GeV [arXiv:1705.11185].

sources.summary: The paper treats RBS 1432 as a high-redshift BL Lac VHE source with uncertain redshift, large inferred optical depth, variability concerns, and expected CTA observability.

quotes.quote: A second approach uses 5 sources (PKS 0426-380, 4C +55.17, Ton 116, PG 1246+586 and RBS 1432) which were found to exhibit very high energy emission ( E_ >100 GeV). Through Monte Carlo simulations it is shown that the observation of VHE photons, despite the large distances of these objects, is consistent with current EBL models. Many of these sources would be observable with the upcoming ground based observatory the Cherenkov Telescope Array (CTA), leading to a better understanding of the EBL.

RBS 1432: A northern hemisphere BL Lac at RA=221.50 ^ , Dec=36.35 ^ for which the 2FHL again defines the redshift as unknown or uncertain. The redshift in the literature is generally accepted as 1.565 which originates from , although the redshift is quoted as being between 1.440 and 1.840.
Of the VHE sources, the largest derived optical depth corresponds to _ GIL12 =4.39 for RBS 1432, indicating that a photon was detected despite 98.8 of the flux being attenuated.

gmw_00004605.name: NGC 5077
gmw_00004605.overview: NGC 5077 is an active S0 [arXiv:2103.03277] or intermediate-size elliptical galaxy [arXiv:0808.3573] classified as a LINER [arXiv:0808.3573, arXiv:2103.03277] and a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2305.06529]. It features a counter-rotating stellar core [arXiv:2103.03277, arXiv:2201.05080], warped ionized gas [arXiv:2103.03277], and a nuclear outflow [arXiv:2201.05080] shaped by past external interactions [arXiv:2103.03277].

sources.summary: The paper monitors NGC 5077 as a nearby LLAGN, finds variability and increasing flux, and measures a damped-random-walk timescale while noting poor long-term stability constraints.

quotes.quote: Six LLAGNs, Centaurus A (hereafter Cen A), NGC 4374, NGC 4552, NGC 4579, NGC 4278, and NGC 5077, were selected. These sources are predicted to have variability timescales ranging from a few days to ten days, due to their black hole mass being situated between Sgr A* and M87. This is expected to provide clarity on the sub-millimeter mass scaling relationship.

We accumulate four years of observations using Submillimeter Array for Centaurus A, NGC 4374, NGC 4278, and NGC 5077 and one year of observations for NGC 4552 and NGC 4579. All sources are variable. We measure the characteristic timescale at which the variability is saturated by modeling these sources' light curve as a damped random walk. We detect a timescale for all the sources except NGC 4552. The detected timescales are comparable to the orbital timescale at the event horizon scale for most sources.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5077 as an active S0 galaxy shaped by external interactions, with a counter-rotating stellar core, warped ionised gas, LINER-like AGN excitation, and fresh gas for black hole fuelling.

quotes.quote: We use MUSE integral field spectroscopy data to study the active S0 galaxy NGC 5077, known to have disturbed dynamics, indicative of a past external interaction. We confirm the presence of a stellar kinematically distinct core with a diameter of 2.8 kpc, counter-rotating with respect to the main stellar body of the galaxy. We find that the counter-rotating core consists of an old stellar population, not significantly different from the rest of the galaxy. The ionised gas is strongly warped and extends out to 6.5 kpc in the polar direction and in a filamentary structure. The gas dynamics is complex, with significant changes in the position angle as a function of radius.

The stellar and gas dynamics indicate that NGC 5077 has had at least two external interactions, one that resulted in the formation of the counter-rotating core followed by late time external gas accretion. NGC 5077 illustrates the importance of external interactions in the replenishment of the galaxy gas reservoir and the nuclear gas content available for black hole fuelling.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5077 as an intermediate-size elliptical/LINER whose weak NIR metal lines and outlying index behavior suggest an unusual enrichment history.

quotes.quote: Six of our galaxies are giant ellipticals (NGC 4472, NGC 4621, NGC 4649, NGC 4697, NGC 6909, IC 4296), five are intermediate-size ellipticals (NGC 4478, NGC 4564, NGC 4742, NGC 5077, NGC 5576), one is a bright dwarf elliptical (NGC 3641), and two are bulges of disk galaxies (NGC 4281, NGC 4594; our spectra are dominated by their spheroids). Four galaxies in the sample exhibit evidence for weak nuclear activity: NGC 4594 and NGC 5077 were identified as LINERs by hec80 ; NGC 4472 is a weak Seyfert 2 ho97 and IC 4296 shows radio and X-ray emission rin05 .

Among the galaxies, NGC 5077 ( 12) and NGC 6909 ( 14) possess very weak Na I , Ca I lines, and their relative errors are significant. Three galaxies -- NGC 4281 ( 4), NGC 5077 ( 12) and NGC 6909 ( 14) -- are above the relation of S08 for the cluster stars, which hints at different chemical enrichment history with respect to the rest of the sample. Figure fig:IR_FeH shows the EW of the NIR spectral indices versus the Mg _2 measurements from ben93 . Na I , and to a lesser extent Ca I and CO, do show trends with respect to Mg _2 , with NGC 4281 ( 4), NGC 5077 ( 12) and NGC 6909 ( 14) standing out.

sources.summary: The atlas classifies NGC 5077 as Disky but cites MUSE data showing a counter-rotating stellar core, complex gas dynamics, and a nuclear outflow shaped like a hollow cone.

quotes.quote: NGC 5077 (see Fig. Figure_AB8 ): In our image the H gas seems to follow the galactic spiral arms or disc; thus we classified it as a Disky morphology. The analysis of MUSE IFS data by confirmed the presence of a stellar distinct, counter-rotating core with complex gas dynamics. In fact, our H distributions are very similar. She reports the discovery of a nuclear outflow, consistent with a hollow cone intersecting the plane of the sky.

gmw_00004606.name: 1HerMES S250 J142825.7+345547
gmw_00004606.overview: 1HerMES S250 J142825.7+345547 is a high-redshift, dusty starburst galaxy [arXiv:1504.05254]. High-resolution imaging reveals that the system is gravitationally lensed into an Einstein ring by an edge-on foreground galaxy [arXiv:1504.05254].

sources.summary: The paper describes this lensed dusty star-forming galaxy as an Einstein-ring system with robust lens light-profile fits, though the 4.5 micron fitting was poorer than 3.6 micron.

quotes.quote: 1HerMES S250 J142825.7+345547 ( 02): The lensing morphology in high-resolution imaging shows an Einstein ring lensed by an edge-on foreground galaxy. A well-isolated source in IRAC imaging, de-blending the foreground and background lenses was straightforward. Light profile fits to the lensing galaxy are the most robust and reliable of the sample in this study, with ^2_ values on the order of unity. The fitting at 4.5 m was significantly worse than in 3.6 m, most likely due to poor imaging and consequentially a suboptimal convolution with the PSF.

gmw_00004607.name: 1HerMES S250 J171544.9+601239
gmw_00004607.overview: 1HerMES S250 J171544.9+601239 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy [arXiv:1504.05254]. High-resolution near-infrared imaging reveals that the galaxy is gravitationally lensed, displaying an arc located approximately 3 arcseconds east of a foreground galaxy [arXiv:1504.05254].

sources.summary: The paper describes HFLS08 as a lensed DSFG with a near-infrared arc in a crowded field, making the convolved models degenerate and its photometry cautious.

quotes.quote: 1HerMES S250 J171544.9+601239 (HFLS08): High-resolution near-IR imaging shows an arc about 3'' east of the foreground galaxy. Moreover, this target is located in a crowded region, with two other sources a few arcseconds away to the east, whose light could potentially pollute the contributions from the foreground and background galaxies. Attempts to fit for these secondary sources only introduced additional uncertainties. Final convolved models are highly degenerate and the photometry and subsequent analyses of this source should be treated with caution.

gmw_00004608.name: HATLASJ085358.9+015537
gmw_00004608.overview: HATLASJ085358.9+015537 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy that exhibits a strong gravitational lensing feature [arXiv:1801.01831, arXiv:1504.05254]. This background galaxy is detected in the near-infrared as an Einstein ring centered almost exactly on an nearly spherical foreground lens [arXiv:1801.01831, arXiv:1504.05254].

sources.summary: Keck near-infrared imaging detects the background galaxy as a ring-like structure; the SMA lens model agrees with prior work on an almost spherical lens despite rotation-angle differences.

quotes.quote: HATLASJ085358.9+015537 : This system was observed with Keck /NIRC2 in the K_ s band 2014ApJ...797..138C . The background galaxy is detected in the near-IR in the form of a ring-like structure that was modelled by Calanog et al. assuming a SIE model for the lens and a S e rsic profile for the background source surface brightness. Our modelling of the SMA data gives results for the lens mass model consistent with those of Calanog et al., both indicating an almost spherical lens. B13 also find a nearly spherical lens ( q_ L 0.94 ) but with a different rotation angle ( _ L 160^ versus _ L 62^ ), even though the discrepance is less than 3 once considered the higher confidence interval consequence of a spherical lens.

sources.summary: The paper describes G09v1.40 as an Einstein-ring lens system whose de-blending required fitting an additional southwestern source because PSF imperfections increased modeling uncertainty.

quotes.quote: HATLASJ085358.9+015537 (G09v1.40): The lensing feature is an Einstein ring centered almost exactly on the foreground lens. The de-blending process for this source is unique, because a separate fit was made for a source located about 2 '' to the southwest. Fitting for this object was deemed necessary because imperfections in the PSF introduced degeneracies and an increased systematic uncertainty to the light profile modeling.

gmw_00004609.name: HATLASJ142935.3-002836
gmw_00004609.overview: HATLASJ142935.3-002836 is a strongly lensed system where a foreground edge-on spiral galaxy at redshift 0.218 forms an Einstein ring around a background source [arXiv:1406.4859, arXiv:1705.05413]. This background source, located at redshift 1.027, is a massive, gas-rich merger composed of two distinct components and a long tidal tail [arXiv:1904.00307, arXiv:1406.4859].

sources.summary: The paper revisits HATLAS J142935.3-002836 as a lensed merger with a foreground lens and two background galaxies, emphasizing molecular gas conditions in the dusty North-South component.

quotes.quote: This manuscript reports the latest advances in comprehending the gravitationally-lensed system in HATLAS J142935.3-002836 also known as H 1429-0028 ; M14 henceforth messias14 . M14 reported the initial findings on H 1429-0028 which appeared as the brightest of an early set of candidates for gravitationally-lensed galaxies in HATLAS (an observed FIR flux at 160 m of S_ 160 m 1.2 Jy). It was found that H 1429-0028 comprises an edge-on disc galaxy at z=0.218 acting as lens, surrounded by an almost complete Einstein ring with a 3 to 4 knot morphology. Sub-arcsec imaging at near-IR to radio wavelengths enabled reconstruction of the background source to reveal a merging system magnified by an overall factor of 10 and comprising two distinct galaxies at z=1.027 (M14).

One galaxy has an East-West orientation dominating the rest-frame optical spectral range (henceforth known as the EW galaxy/component). The other, appearing North-South oriented and with a compact intrinsic half-light radius r_ 1/2 =0.9 0.3 kpc, dominates the gaseous and dusty content of the system, being the main contributor to the long-wavelength spectral regime flux (henceforth known as the NS galaxy/component).

The molecular gas in the NS component in H 1429-0028 is found to have a temperature of 70 K, a volume density of ( n [ cm^ 3 ] ) 3.7 , to be expanding at 10 km/s/pc, and amounts to M_ H_2 =4_ 2 ^ +3 10^9 M_ . The CO to H _2 conversion factor is estimated to be _ CO =0.4_ 0.2 ^ +0.3 M _ /(K km/s pc ^2) .

sources.summary: The paper confirms H1429 0028 as a strongly lensed high-redshift system: a foreground disk lenses a massive, gas-rich, star-forming background galaxy with two components and a long tidal tail.

quotes.quote: This work focuses on one of these lensed systems, HATLAS J142935.3 002836 (H1429 0028), selected in the Herschel ATLAS field. Gathering a rich, multi-wavelength dataset, we aim to confirm the lensing hypothesis and model the background source's morphology and dynamics, as well as to provide a full physical characterisation. Multi-wavelength high-resolution data is utilised to assess the nature of the system.

The system comprises a foreground edge-on disk galaxy (at z_ sp =0.218 ) with an almost complete Einstein ring around it. The background source (at z_ sp =1.027 ) is magnified by a factor of 8-10 depending on wavelength. It is comprised of two components and a tens of kpc long tidal tail resembling the Antenn \ merger. As a whole, the system is a massive stellar system (1.32 _ 0.41 ^ +0.63 10^ 11 M _ ) forming stars at a rate of 394 90 M _ yr ^ 1 , and has a significant gas reservoir M_ ISM = 4.6 1.7 10^ 10 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper revisits H-ATLAS J142935.3-002836 with higher-resolution ALMA data, finding an edge-on spiral lens, comparable lens models, disturbed source morphology, and the sample’s highest SFR-to-dust-mass ratio.

quotes.quote: H-ATLAS J142935.3-002836 This lens system has been previously investigated in detail by M14 hereafter messias14 who analysed a broad range of multi-wavelength imaging, including ALMA Band 3 and Band 6 data (with central wavelengths of 3.1 mm and 1.3 mm respectively and maximum resolutions of 1.4 arcsec and 0.6 arcsec respectively). Optical imaging acquired with the Keck telescope see calanog14 indicates that the lens is an edge-on spiral and optical spectroscopy by M14 from the Gemini-South telescope gives a lens redshift of 0.218.

In our reconstruction, there is a hint of morphological disturbance at the southern end of the source. This is exactly where M14 find that a second optically detected source intersects in what they interpret as a possible merger. This source has an extremely high SFR to dust mass ratio, the highest in our sample. The source lies >3 away from the mean in the distribution of SFR to dust mass ratios of high redshift submm galaxies (SMGs) and lower redshift ULIRGs determined by rowlands14 as Figure SFR_Md shows.

sources.summary: The paper describes G15v2.19 as an incomplete Einstein ring around a star-forming edge-on spiral, isolated enough for fitting but with non-robust IRAC morphology results.

quotes.quote: HATLASJ142935.3-002836 (G15v2.19): The lensing morphology is an incomplete Einstein ring around a star-forming, edge-on spiral galaxy and has been studied extensively in Messias2014 and Timmons2015 . The source is sufficiently isolated so that no light pollution from nearby sources can affect the fitting. Despite this, the final results were not very robust. This raises the possibility that the lensing morphologies observed at 3.6 m and 4.5 m vary significantly from those observed in the 1--2 m range.

gmw_00004610.name: 1HerMES S250 J110016.3+571736
gmw_00004610.overview: 1HerMES S250 J110016.3+571736 is a gravitationally lensed galaxy situated in a moderately crowded field [arXiv:1504.05254]. High-resolution near-infrared imaging reveals its lensing morphology, which consists of an arc to the northwest of the foreground lens and a counter-image to the southeast [arXiv:1504.05254].

sources.summary: The paper describes HLock12 as a lensed DSFG with a northwest arc, a southeast counter-image, and robust de-blending despite a moderately crowded field.

quotes.quote: 1HerMES S250 J110016.3+571736 (HLock12): High-resolution near-IR imaging shows that the lensing morphology is an arc approximately 1'' northwest of the foreground source. Subtraction of the foreground galaxy in high-resolution imaging has also revealed a counter-image less than 1'' to the southeast of the foreground lens Calanog2014 . The system lies in a moderately crowded field, so separate fits were made for the sources 4 '' to the southwest, 4 '' to the northwest, and 1 '' to the northeast. Despite the number of sources to fit for, the final model fit and de-blending was found to be robust.

gmw_00004611.name: UGC 2936
gmw_00004611.overview: UGC 2936 is a highly inclined galaxy featuring a short, oval bar and distinct spiral arms [arXiv:1607.01889]. The galaxy exhibits H II regions scattered throughout its disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0612741], and it may contain dust and ongoing star formation [arXiv:1607.01889].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 2936 as highly inclined, with a short oval bar, distinct spiral arms, greater deprojection uncertainty, and visible old stellar arms that may indicate dust and ongoing star formation.

quotes.quote: UGC 2936- Highly inclined galaxy that has a short, oval bar and distinct spiral arms. The disk spiral arms are surprisingly clear.
Unfortunately some of our galaxies 0223-0033, UGC 2936, UGC 9634, UGC 8794 and 1300+0144 have high inclination angles; hence, the results for these galaxies will have greater error.

(iv) In case of the galaxy UGC 2936, even the spiral arms are visible in the disk representing the arm of older stars. this galaxy may have significant dust and ongoing star formation pickering.etal.1999 .

sources.summary: The paper finds UGC 2936 has only modest H I asymmetry, an integrated line flux broadly consistent with some measurements, lower than others, and rare CO detections for an LSB Giant.

quotes.quote: UGC 2936 : The Arecibo spectrum of UGC 2936 published by Sprayberry (1995b) is highly asymmetric; however only a modest asymmetry is seen in our new data and in the VLA spectrum of Pickering et al. (1999). Our new integrated line flux of 11.23 \ agrees to within errors with that of Tifft Cocke (1988) ( 11.7 ). In comparison, the Arecibo spectra by Hewitt et al. (1983), Haynes Giovanelli (1984), and Sprayberry et al.
