The spectrum shows a relatively large number of line ratios including [ OII ] / and [ OIII ] / in the optical range, reproduced by Fosbury et al by a pure photoionization model with =8 10 ^4 K log U =-1 and Z=0.05Z _ , which was suggested by comparison with synthetic SEDs calculated by STARBURST99 (Leitherer et al 1999). The FWHM of the profiles are 100 for lines in the optical and higher for those in the UV.

We searched by modelling the spectrum for a (hidden) AGN, but AGN dominated models lead to HeII/ 1, higher by a factor 10 than observed. The shock dominated model MF0 (Table 6, col. 12) calculated by a relatively high shock velocity (320 ) and high preshock density ( 0=2000 3) fits most of the UV line ratios but underpredicts [ OIII ] 5007+/ by a factor 10. A SB radiation dominated model MFSB, calculated by =1.1 10 ^ 5 K, U =0.3, D =4.9 10 ^ 20 cm, =100 , 0=300 3 and O/H 0.5 solar, reproduces satisfactorily most of the line ratios from relatively low ionization levels.

sources.summary: The paper summarizes the Lynx arc as a strongly magnified dwarf HII galaxy at z=3.357 with unusual emission lines and very narrow intercombination lines implying a dwarf-galaxy mass.

quotes.quote: The Lynx arc is a dwarf HII galaxy at z=3.357, lensed by the z=0.570 cluster RX J0848+4456, and it has been discovered serendipitously by during spectroscopic follow--up of the cluster. The HII galaxy is magnified by a factor of about 10, as evaluated by a detailed analysis of the complex cluster environment from HST WFPC2 images. The unusual emission line spectrum has been thoroughly studied with the LRIS, ESI and NIRSPEC at the Keck telescopes, covering the rest--frame ranges 900--2500 and 3300--5700 . It shows strong and self--absorbed Lyman line and CIV 1548,1551 \ doublet, strong intercombination lines of NIV ] 1483,1487 , CIII ] 1907,1909 , and OIII ] 1661,1666 , moderate HeII 1640 , absent [ OII ] 3727,3729 .

The intercombination lines have a very narrow width ( 30-35 km s^ 1 ), indicating a small gravitating mass of about 10^9 M_ , typical of a dwarf galaxy. The absence of NV 1240 \ and the weakness of NIII ] 1750 \ indicate that the ionizing source is a blackbody, rather than a power law, as it would be expected in case of ionization by an AGN. The intensity of the continuum observed longward of Lyman is completely explained by the nebular continuum, as accurately predicted from the observed strength of H .

sources.summary: The Lynx arc is treated as a lensed z=3.36 N-emitting galaxy with elevated N/O and C/O ratios, possible WR-star enrichment, and an alternative obscured-QSO interpretation.

quotes.quote: Apart from objects clearly identified as AGN, the Lynx arc, a lensed z=3.36 galaxy identified for \ and \ emission is probably the first N-emitter studied in detail Fosb03,Villar-Martin2004Nebular-and-ste . From photoionization modeling derive a high N/O ratio and sub-solar metallicity.

Among the non-AGN in their list one finds the peculiar galaxy named the Lynx arc (at z=3.36 ), which has been studied by and , although have argued that this object may be an obscured QSO. According to the photoionization models of , both the N/O and C/O abundance ratios of this object are elevated, as seen in Fig. \ fig_abund . Although suspected, no direct signs of WR stars have been found in this object Villar-Martin2004Nebular-and-ste and the inferred abundances are not explained.
Enrichment in the Lynx arc and Mrk 996 is likely due to normal massive stars (WR), which implies that the star-forming regions in these objects cannot become GCs.

gmw_00004654.name: 3C46
gmw_00004654.overview: 3C46 is a large Fanaroff-Riley II radio galaxy [arXiv:0710.3105]. Its optical continuum image displays a double structure, while its emission-line nebula is concentrated in the southern portion and misaligned with the radio source [arXiv:0710.3105].

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C46 as a large FR II radio source whose emission-line nebula is misaligned with the radio source and concentrated in the southern part of a double-looking continuum structure.

quotes.quote: 3C46 (Figs. 3C46-montage 3C46-overlay ) is a large FR II radio source. The emission line nebula and the radio source are not well aligned. The optical continuum image seems to show a double structure. However, the line emission is predominantly in the southern portion.

gmw_00004655.name: 3C171
gmw_00004655.overview: 3C171 is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.2384 [arXiv:astro-ph/0209612]. It features an extended emission-line region that traces its knotty jets from the core to the hotspots, with additional emission-line gas surrounding the eastern hotspot [arXiv:astro-ph/0209612, arXiv:0710.3105].

sources.summary: The paper presents 3C 171 as an unusual z=0.2384 radio galaxy whose jets, hotspots, and extended emission-line region are linked to shock ionization, depolarization, and a dense warm environment.

quotes.quote: One good candidate low-redshift shock-ionization source is 3C 171, an unusual z=0.2384 radio galaxy. Its large-scale radio structure has been well studied (Heckman, van Breugel Miley 1984; Blundell 1996). In its inner regions it is similar to a normal FRII, but low-surface-brightness plumes instead of normal lobes extend north and south from the hotspots. High-resolution images show knotty jets connecting the radio core with the hotspots (Hardcastle \ 1997).

3C 171 is associated with a prominent EELR, elongated along the jet axis. Heckman \ found that the emission-line gas was brightest near the hotspots, suggesting that the radio-emitting plasma is responsible for powering the emission-line regions. More recently Clark \ (1998) have made WHT observations of the emission-line regions and shown that their ionization states are most consistent with a shock-ionization model. The unusually strong EELR in this source may be indicative of an environment unusually rich in dense, warm gas; the interactions between the jets and this gas might then be responsible for the peculiar large-scale radio structure.

sources.summary: The paper says 3C171 has an emission-line region tracing its jets from core to hotspots, with additional emission around the east hotspot and prior detailed discussion of the ELR structure.

quotes.quote: 3C171 (Figs 3C171-montage 3C171-overlay ) The emission line region traces the jets, linking the core to the hotspots. There is emission around the east hotspot. The ELR structure has been discussed in detail in Tilak05 .

gmw_00004656.name: 3C268.2
gmw_00004656.overview: 3C268.2 is a radio galaxy [arXiv:0710.3105] that may be surrounded by a galaxy group [arXiv:2011.04668]. The galaxy features an asymmetric emission-line nebula closely aligned with its radio source [arXiv:0710.3105], as well as extended X-ray emission to the northeast [arXiv:2011.04668].

sources.summary: The paper finds 3C268.2 has a radio-aligned but asymmetric emission-line nebula, little nuclear line emission, and an undetected radio core that limits spatial-coincidence analysis.

quotes.quote: 3C268.2 (Figs. 3C268.2-montage 3C268.2-overlay ) shows an emission line nebula which is closely aligned with the radio source. The ELR is asymmetrical around the center of the host galaxy (measured from the broadband continuum image). There is little to no line emission detected from the nucleus in our images. The core was not detected in the radio maps, however the location was inferred using the WCS coordinates given on NED. As the core was not detected in the radio map, we are unable to examine spatial coincidence of the ELR and the radio core, but are still able to measure the alignment of the ELR and radio source.

sources.summary: The paper reports no significant perpendicular emission for 3CR 268.2, but finds northeast extended X-ray emission and optical evidence for nearby groups or clusters.

quotes.quote: 3CR 268.2 ( z=0.362 ). We found no significant emission perpendicular to the radio axis. Nevertheless, we detected extended X-ray emission 302 arcsec (1.5 Mpc at the source redshift) above 5 confidence level to the north-east of this source that could be due to the presence of an unrelated galaxy cluster (see Fig. fig:3c268p2 ). We explored the optical data available in the SDSS archive (see e.g., ahn12 ) and in Figure fig:3c268p2 we show the R band image of the field around 3CR 268.2. There are 8 sources with spectroscopic redshift estimates, two of them, marked in blue in Figure fig:3c268p2 , have a velocity dispersion of 725 km s ^ 1 , thus corresponding to the typical velocity dispersion in groups and clusters of galaxies (see e.g., moore93 , eke04 , berlind06 and massaro19 ). This suggests the possible presence of a galaxy group surrounding 3CR 268.2.

gmw_00004657.name: 3C303.1
gmw_00004657.overview: The compact steep spectrum radio galaxy 3C303.1 features a complex optical center that may indicate an ongoing merger [arXiv:astro-ph/0701619]. Its aligned emission-line nebula is elongated along the radio axis [arXiv:0710.3105], displaying a non-rotating velocity field [arXiv:1510.06979] and ionization from shocked gas [arXiv:1510.06979, arXiv:astro-ph/0504330].

sources.summary: The paper finds 3C 303.1 has a complex non-rotating velocity field, disturbed EELR emission, compact high-excitation lines, and better shock-model fits for some line ratios.

quotes.quote: 3C 303.1 The velocity field of 3C 303.1 is intriguing (Fig. VF , right-hand panels). Although we observe stable motions within each region, it is very difficult to identify a rotation curve. A similar result was obtained by odea02 by using STIS/HST long-slit spectrum with a slightly different PA (PA=151 ). We identify three kinds of kinematical components, namely: the most extended, the internal, and the highest-velocity components.

The EELR in 3C 303.1 is more extended toward the NW, but the emission-lines are more intense to the SE, which also coincides with the most perturbed region. The highest excitation lines are [ NeV ] 3426 and HeII, as it was the case of 3C 268.3. The former is only detected in the innermost SE region, up to 1.2'' (7.1 kpc); it is also present in the central region, but absent from 0.3'' (1.6 kpc) toward the NW.

But when the diagrams are formed with medium- and low-excitation lines ( [ NeIII ] 3869 , [ OIII ] 5007 in the former group, and [ OII ] 3727 , H in the latter) self-ionizing, recombination-region-dominated, shock models reproduce the observations of 3C 303.1 in a better way.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1443+77 as a CSS radio galaxy with aligned radio, emission-line, and near-UV structures, making it the sample's strongest jet-induced star-formation candidate.

quotes.quote: 1443+77 : 3C 303.1 . The optical counterpart of this CSS corresponds to a galaxy of m _v 20 e.g., Sanghera95 , at z = 0.267 Kristian78 . The MERLIN map Sanghera95 shows a 1.8 long ( 7 kpc) double radio source aligned NW-SE (PA 47 ) aligned with the inner emission line gas e.g. Vries99 . XMM-Newton observations detect the ISM of the host galaxy as well as a second component which could be either Synchrotron Self Compton from the Southern radio lobe or hot gas shocked by the expansion of the radio source O'Dea06 .

The center of 1443+77 shows a complex structure in the optical, which could be due to an ongoing merger e.g., Vries97, Axon00 and it also shows up in our UV image. Axon00 and Vries99 find an arc of emission south of the nucleus of the source and McCarthy95 detects circumnuclear [ O iii ] as far as 3 from the center. Our image suggests the presence of an arc of emission 0.9 ( 4 kpc) from the nucleus. The integrated magnitude of this arc is 23.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C303.1 as having an emission-line nebula comparable in size to the radio source, closely aligned with it, symmetric around the nucleus, and elongated along the radio axis.

quotes.quote: 3C303.1 (Figs. 3C303.1-montage 3C303.1-overlay ) has an emission line nebula which is of similar size in our WFPC images as the radio source. They are closely aligned. The emission line region is symmetrical about the nucleus and is elongated along the radio axis.

sources.summary: The paper studies 3C 303.1 as a compact steep spectrum galaxy with misaligned emission-line gas and a high modeled shocked-gas luminosity contribution.

quotes.quote: We are carrying out a study of the kinematics and ionization of the aligned emission line nebulae in three CSS radio sources: 3C 67 (galaxy, z=0.310 , linear size D=10.1 Kpc), 3C 277.1 (quasar, z=0.321 , D=6.9 Kpc), 3C 303.1 (galaxy, z=0.267 , D=6.2 Kpc) We adopt a Hubble constant of H_o = 75 km s \ Mpc \ and a deceleration parameter of q_o = 0.0 .

In 3C 67 and 3C 303.1 , where the emission line gas is slightly misaligned with respect to the radio axis, we rotated the slit to place it along the emission-line nebula axis, rather than the radio source axis.
In 3C 303.1 , the contribution to the luminosity from shocked gas is between 30 and 70 .

gmw_00004658.name: WISEA J083238.73--402705.3
gmw_00004658.overview: WISEA J083238.73--402705.3 is a galaxy with an H I redshift of 0.0357 that hosts the fast radio burst FRB 20230718A [arXiv:2311.16808]. It is interacting with a nearby companion galaxy, as evidenced by an H I bridge connecting the two systems [arXiv:2311.16808].

sources.summary: The paper identifies WISEA J083238.73--402705.3 as the FRB 20230718A host, measures an H I redshift of 0.0357, and finds evidence for interaction via an H I bridge.

quotes.quote: This FRB, henceforth FRB 20230718A, was found to be coincident with the galaxy WISEA J083238.73--402705.3, with no available redshift in the literature. The host galaxy is also seen in imaging for the DECam Plane Survey as an apparent faint red galaxy due to dust extinction (see Fig.  fig:momentmaps ).

We obtain an based redshift of z = 0.0357 for the host galaxy of FRB 20230718A, an apparently non-repeating FRB detected in the CRAFT survey and localized at a Galactic latitude of --0.367 ^ . Our observations also reveal that the FRB host galaxy is interacting with a nearby companion, which is evident from the detection of an \ bridge connecting the two galaxies.

The FRB host galaxy is not isolated, given the clear detection of a \ bridge between it and the neighbour galaxy. Therefore, this would be the fifth FRB host galaxy with a sign of galaxy interaction within the \ observations to date out of six published so far, akin to . Owing to the higher dispersion seen in the neighbour galaxy towards the south (FRB host galaxy) side rather than the centre (Fig.  fig:momentmaps , bottom-right panel), it is possible that the FRB host galaxy is accumulating gas from the neighbour.

gmw_00004659.name: SDSS J014733.58+000323.2
gmw_00004659.overview: SDSS J014733.58+000323.2 is a weak-line quasar located at a redshift of z = 1.98 [arXiv:1203.0215]. The object is slightly reddened by dust and exhibits C IV absorption at z = 1.8815 [arXiv:1203.0215].

sources.summary: The paper reclassifies SDSS J014733.58+000323.2 as a slightly reddened weak-line quasar at z = 1.98 after C IV absorption showed it was not a stellar-mass black-hole candidate.

quotes.quote: Finally, SDSS J014733.58+000323.2 (Fig. fig:stellar-cont h ) is an example of a spectrum that was erroneously rejected in our first analysis attempt. The object was classified as a stellar mass black-hole candidate by Chisholm et al. ( ). Though there is a blue continuum with apparent broad emission-line components, these lines do not fit the positions of the typical broad lines in quasar spectra. Our referee, Dr. Patrick B. Hall, pointed out that there is C iv 1548.20,1550.77 absorption at z=1.8815. This high redshift clearly proves that SDSS J014733.58+000323.2 is a quasar. The closer inspection of the spectrum leads to its classification as a slightly reddened ( E(B-V) = 0.07 for SMC-like dust) weak-line quasar at z = 1.98 without C iii ] emission and with a shift between the C iv and Mg ii emission lines.

gmw_00004660.name: SDSS J100237.22+270056.5
gmw_00004660.overview: SDSS J100237.22+270056.5 is a highly luminous quasar [arXiv:1203.0215]. The object exhibits strong iron emission lines and hosts a high-velocity outflow detected in several ultraviolet absorption lines [arXiv:1203.0215].

sources.summary: The paper highlights SDSS J100237.22+270056.5 as an unusual, very luminous quasar with strong iron emission and a high-velocity outflow seen in several ultraviolet lines.

quotes.quote: In the first panel, we present SDSS J100237.22+270056.5 A similar object (not shown here) is SDSS J124244.36+624659.1 . which exhibits emission from the multiplets Fe ii 1785 (UV67,UV191), Fe iii 1926 (UV34), Fe iii 2070 (UV48), Fe ii 2400 (UV2), Fe ii 2600 (UV1), and Fe ii 2750 (UV62,UV63). The positions of these lines, as well as the positions of the typical quasar emission lines ( Si iv 1400, C iv 1542.2,1550.8, Al iii 1854.7,1862.8, C iii ] 1908.7, Mg ii 2796.3,2803.5), are marked by the vertical solid lines. There is a high-velocity outflow at z_ abs = 1.724 seen in C iv , Al ii , Al iii , and Mg ii . The absorption line redward of C iv , close to He ii 1640, is probably a narrow intervening system. SDSS J100237.2+270057 is one of the most luminous quasars in our sample with M_ i = -28.94 .

gmw_00004661.name: SDSS J094225.42+565613.0
gmw_00004661.overview: SDSS J094225.42+565613.0 is a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:1203.0215]. The object exhibits absorption troughs at a redshift of 0.735, with overlapping iron absorption troughs that depress its continuum [arXiv:1203.0215].

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSS J094225.42+565613.0 as an example with absorption troughs, possible red-side Mg II absorption, and overlapping Fe II troughs depressing the continuum.

quotes.quote: A nice example is SDSS J094225.42+565613.0 in panel () . The positions of absorption troughs at z_ abs = 0.735 are marked by the dashed lines. If we adopt the systemic redshift of SDSS J094225.42+565613.0 from the (weak) [ O ii ] 3728 emission line, the Mg ii emission line is either blueshifted or absorbed at the red side.

The Mg ii emission of SDSS J094225.42+565613.0 may instead be affected in this case by broad Fe ii absorption on its red side. As a consequence of the overlapping Fe ii troughs, the continuum of SDSS J094225.42+565613.0 is depressed shortwards of Mg ii down to the lowest observed wavelengths.

gmw_00004662.name: SDSS J090334.94+502819.3
gmw_00004662.overview: SDSS J090334.94+502819.3 is a gravitationally lensed background quasar [arXiv:1203.0215]. It is viewed through the inner region of a foreground luminous red galaxy [arXiv:1203.0215].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J090334.94+502819.3 as a gravitationally lensed unusual quasar whose red continuum and absorbed C IV wing are explained by lens-galaxy decomposition.

quotes.quote: Hence, we cannot exclude that a fraction of the red quasars (types 3 and 4) were selected as unusual owing to properties related to the foreground rather than the quasar itself (as is indeed the case for the two gravitationally lensed quasars SDSS J081959.80+535624.2 and SDSS J090334.94+502819.3 ).

SDSS J090334.94+502819.3 was classified as an unusual quasar of type C because of its red continuum between Ly and C iv . In addition, the C iv emission line is absorbed at the red wing. However, as demonstrated by Johnston et al. ( ), decomposing the spectrum into a luminous red galaxy component at z=0.388 and the background quasar component yields a normal quasar spectrum.
The gravitationally lensed quasar SDSS J090334.9+502819 mentioned above is seen through the inner region of a luminous featureless galaxy.

gmw_00004663.name: GDN-8231
gmw_00004663.overview: GDN-8231 is a massive, compact star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 1.7 [arXiv:1503.07164]. It exhibits a low star formation rate and a bluer center, indicating that it is undergoing rapid inside-out quenching [arXiv:1503.07164].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GDN-8231 as a compact z~1.7 star-forming galaxy rapidly quenching, with weak star formation indicators, Balmer absorption, colder gas than stars, and a blue center.

quotes.quote: We present Keck-I MOSFIRE spectroscopy in the Y and H bands of GDN-8231, a massive, compact, star-forming galaxy (SFG) at a redshift z 1.7 . Its spectrum reveals both and emission lines and strong Balmer absorption lines. The and Spitzer MIPS 24 m fluxes are both weak, thus indicating a low star formation rate of SFR 5-10 .

This, added to a relatively young age of 700 Myr measured from the absorption lines, provides the first direct evidence for a distant galaxy being caught in the act of rapidly shutting down its star formation. Such quenching allows GDN-8231 to become a compact, quiescent galaxy, similar to 3 other galaxies in our sample, by z 1.5 . Moreover, the color profile of GDN-8231 shows a bluer center, consistent with the predictions of recent simulations for an early phase of inside-out quenching.

gmw_00004664.name: 01a
gmw_00004664.overview: The galaxy 01a is a massive, highly star-forming starburst galaxy at z~2.5 that is heavily obscured by dust [arXiv:2405.07986]. It exhibits a disturbed spiral morphology featuring multiple clumps, extended tails to the north and south, and two substructures resembling spiral arms [arXiv:2405.07986].

sources.summary: The paper describes 01a as a massive dusty starburst at z~2.5, with high star formation, heavy asymmetric extinction, multiple clumps, extended tails, and disturbed spiral morphology.

quotes.quote: Based on the SED modeling, the three galaxies are massive, highly star-forming, and heavily obscured.
Extinction values 7 mag are measured in the central and western regions of 01a, 4 -5 mag to the southeast of the center in 01b, and \ gradually increases to 5 mag from the south to the north in 01c.

Source 01a shows multiple clumps, extended tails to the south and to the north (the latter is more visible in Fig. fig:targets ), and two sub-structures that mimic spiral arms appear at 2.7 m ( ^ rest 0.78 m). Overall, 01a looks like a disturbed dusty spiral.

gmw_00004665.name: 01b
gmw_00004665.overview: The galaxy 01b is a massive, highly star-forming starburst galaxy that is heavily obscured by dust [arXiv:2405.07986]. Its morphology is consistent with a face-on spiral galaxy, though asymmetric extinction makes only its center and one spiral arm visible at short wavelengths [arXiv:2405.07986].

sources.summary: The paper describes 01b as a massive dusty starburst with radio excess, CO emission, asymmetric extinction, faint rest-UV emission, and face-on spiral morphology.

quotes.quote: Based on the SED modeling, the three galaxies are massive, highly star-forming, and heavily obscured. Interestingly, the SED of source 01b exhibits a small excess of radio emission with respect to that predicted by the best fit model and based on the estimated SFR .

The galaxy 01b is visible but much fainter than at longer wavelengths, and 01c is invisible in F090W and barely hinted at in F115W . In the rest-frame visible (F150W--F277W), all galaxies are much brighter and are clumpy. Source 01a shows multiple clumps, extended tails to the south and to the north (the latter is more visible in Fig. fig:targets ), and two sub-structures that mimic spiral arms appear at 2.7 m ( ^ rest 0.78 m). Overall, 01a looks like a disturbed dusty spiral. The appearance of 01b is consistent with a face-on spiral galaxy, but at short wavelengths only the center and one spiral arm are visible.

gmw_00004666.name: 01c
gmw_00004666.overview: The galaxy 01c is classified as an edge-on disk galaxy that contains several clumps along its major axis [arXiv:2405.07986]. It is a massive, obscured starburst that remains undetected in both sub-millimeter continuum and radio bands [arXiv:2405.07986].

sources.summary: The paper describes 01c as a massive obscured starburst, undetected in continuum/radio bands but modeled as an edge-on disk with clumps and strong dust extinction.

quotes.quote: Because 01c was not detected in the continuum by NOEMA, we assigned an upper limit to its sub-mm flux. The measured and scaled \ flux densities are listed in Table tab:herschel_fluxes . LOFAR observations The G191 field was observed with LOFAR at 144 MHz as part of the mosaic P160 + 35. The LOFAR data from the LOFAR LoTSS second data release DR2; shimwell17 include images at an angular resolution of 6 arcsec \ and rms noise 0.074 mJy beam ^ 1 and a radio source list. Two LOFAR sources fall within the H01 beam and match the two CO emitters 01a and 01b (Fig. fig:g191_01_image ), but source 01c was not detected.

The galaxy 01b is visible but much fainter than at longer wavelengths, and 01c is invisible in F090W and barely hinted at in F115W . In the rest-frame visible (F150W--F277W), all galaxies are much brighter and are clumpy. Source 01a shows multiple clumps, extended tails to the south and to the north (the latter is more visible in Fig. fig:targets ), and two sub-structures that mimic spiral arms appear at 2.7 m ( ^ rest 0.78 m). Overall, 01a looks like a disturbed dusty spiral. The appearance of 01b is consistent with a face-on spiral galaxy, but at short wavelengths only the center and one spiral arm are visible. Source 01c is an edge-on disk with several clumps along the disk major axis.

gmw_00004667.name: 4C53.37
gmw_00004667.overview: 4C53.37 is a radio galaxy located within the Abell 2220 galaxy cluster [arXiv:1412.2076]. It hosts kiloparsec-scale jets that are bent by the galaxy's movement through the surrounding intercluster medium [arXiv:1412.2076].

sources.summary: The paper models the bent kiloparsec-scale jet of 4C53.37 in Abell 2220, finding that its jet morphology changes strongly with intercluster-medium density and broadly matches observations.

quotes.quote: Kilo-parsec scale jets We consider the well-known jets of the structures 4C53.37 and 1159+583 in the galaxy clusters Abell 2220 and Abell 1446, respectively. The bending of the jets is now due to the relative movement of the galaxy through the ``intercluster medium'' (ICM). Since, in this case, we have a much larger scale (hundreds of kilo-parsecs), we adopt a nonrelativistic hydrodynamic expression for the velocity given by the Euler equation u t + (u )u = P + g.

Results for 4C53.37 and 1159+583 are shown in Table  tbl-3 and in Figs.  f6 and f7 , respectively. The observational values for n in BB are n = 0.5 for 4C53.37, and n = 12 for 1159+583. Figs.  f6 and f7 show how the shape of the jet does change with different intercluster densities. For the case of 4C53.37 there is a formidable variation in the shape of the curves between density ratios, whereas for the case of 1159+583 the differences are small.

gmw_00004668.overview: The radio galaxy 1159+583 is located within the Abell 1446 galaxy cluster [arXiv:1412.2076]. It features kiloparsec-scale bent jets that are shaped by the galaxy's relative motion through the intercluster medium [arXiv:1412.2076].

sources.summary: The paper uses 1159+583 in Abell 1446 as a kiloparsec-scale bent-jet test case, comparing simulated jet shapes with observations and noting modest density-dependent shape changes.

quotes.quote: Kilo-parsec scale jets We consider the well-known jets of the structures 4C53.37 and 1159+583 in the galaxy clusters Abell 2220 and Abell 1446, respectively. The bending of the jets is now due to the relative movement of the galaxy through the ``intercluster medium'' (ICM). Since, in this case, we have a much larger scale (hundreds of kilo-parsecs), we adopt a nonrelativistic hydrodynamic expression for the velocity given by the Euler equation u t + (u )u = P + g.

Results for 4C53.37 and 1159+583 are shown in Table  tbl-3 and in Figs.  f6 and f7 , respectively. The observational values for n in BB are n = 0.5 for 4C53.37, and n = 12 for 1159+583. Figs.  f6 and f7 show how the shape of the jet does change with different intercluster densities. For the case of 4C53.37 there is a formidable variation in the shape of the curves between density ratios, whereas for the case of 1159+583 the differences are small.

gmw_00004669.name: R06-D23
gmw_00004669.overview: R06-D23 is a star-forming galaxy at a redshift of z=3.123 [arXiv:1907.11113]. It is unresolved in Hubble Space Telescope images [arXiv:1907.11113], shows Lyman-alpha emission [arXiv:1907.11113], and displays no signs of a broad-line active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1907.11113].

sources.summary: The paper treats R06-D23 as a real z=3.123 LyC-emitting star-forming galaxy, with spectroscopy confirming its redshift, no broad-line AGN signature, and no foreground contamination evidence.

quotes.quote: Object 021: R06-D23 Object 021 (R06-D23) is unresolved in the HST /ACS images, which means that the FWHM is less than 0 1 0.8 kpc at z=3.12 . We confirmed its redshift with near-infrared spectroscopy (Section  subsec:nirspec ). The optical spectrum of the object taken with Keck/LRIS (C. Steidel, private communication) shows Ly emission with line width of 700 km s ^ 1 and rest-frame UV absorption lines such as O i , C ii which give its redshift z=3.123 , and there is no sign of broad-line AGN. No counterpart is found in the Chandra 2Ms X-ray catalogs Alexander2003, Xue2016 . There is no indication of a foreground source in the HST images and spectra.

Although weak AGN may contribute to its LyC emission, based on these observations we consider that this object is a real LyC emitting SFG at z=3.123 . This object is also identified as a star-forming LAE GN-NB5-5878 with Ly redshift of 3.129 in Sobral2018 .

gmw_00004670.name: MOSDEF 06336
gmw_00004670.overview: MOSDEF 06336 is a candidate Lyman-continuum emitting galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of z=3.412 [arXiv:1907.11113]. High-resolution imaging reveals faint nearby sources, which may be physically associated with the galaxy or represent foreground or background objects [arXiv:1907.11113].

sources.summary: The paper retains MOSDEF 06336 as a candidate LyC-emitting galaxy at z=3.412, despite a puzzling SED and possible neighboring source, because spectroscopy supports the high-redshift origin.

quotes.quote: Object 284: MOSDEF 06336 The situation for object 284 (MOSDEF 06336) is more complicated. Its HST /ACS images show faint nearby sources (see Fig. fig:montage_SFGAGN ), which may be physically associated with the object or sources in the background / foreground. The position of and F336W images align with the brightest component. As discussed in Section  subsec:lyc_SED , the SED of the brightest component is difficult to be explained with a source redshift of z=3.4 . A quick photometric redshift estimate using EAZY Brammer2008 with default templates returns z_ phot =2.02 0.07 as the maximum probability photometric redshift.

On the other hand, MOSDEF K band spectrum of the object (N. Reddy, private communication) shows [ O iii ] 4959, 5007 and H which give its systemic redshift at z=3.412 . The slit configuration of the MOSDEF spectra is such that a fainter nearby source to the south east may partially be falling in the slit, merging with the spectrum of object 284. The spectroscopic redshift of this nearby source is unknown but it has ID 6381 with a photometric redshift of z 0.5 in the 3D-HST v4.1 photometric catalog Skelton2014 .

gmw_00004671.name: SDSSJ1117+4623
gmw_00004671.overview: SDSSJ1117+4623 is an extremely red quasar that represents the brightest radio source in its VLA sample [arXiv:1803.02821]. It exhibits a flat spectral index and is hypothesized to host a jet core-lobe system with a hotspot located at a projected distance of 80 kpc [arXiv:1803.02821].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSSJ1117+4623 as the brightest ERQ radio source in the VLA sample, with a flat spectrum and possible 80 kpc jet core-lobe structure.

quotes.quote: ERQ SDSSJ1117+4623 is the brightest object (1.25 mJy at 6.2 GHz, or L_ [ 6GHz ] =1.9 10^ 42 ) in our VLA observations. It is also marginally detected in FIRST with the brightest pixel having an intensity of 0.73 mJy , though it is not bright enough to be listed in the FIRST catalog. Its spectral index between rest-frame 12 and 25 GHz is +0.29 0.15 (MFS mode) or alternatively +0.37 0.09 (two-band imaging), while the spectral index between rest-frame 4 and 19 GHz is +0.17 0.10 , corrected for the CLEAN bias of FIRST. These measurements are consistent with one another within the uncertainties. Among the sources with measurable spectral indices, SDSSJ1117+4623 is the only one with a flat spectral index of >-0.5 .

Interestingly, while the main source itself is not spatially resolved, there is a marginally resolved faint radio source with a peak intensity of 55 \ and an integrated flux density of 90 \ to the south-east of SDSSJ1117+4623 at a projected distance of 80 kpc (Figure fig:image-example ). This object lacks an SDSS counterpart, and we hypothesize that SDSSJ1117+4623 consists of a flat-spectrum core and a lobe with a hotspot. We find no sources extended on 10 kpc scales except for one powerful source SDSSJ1117+4623 on the radio-loud end of the distribution, which can be explained as a jet core+lobe system.

gmw_00004672.name: UM 461
gmw_00004672.overview: UM 461 is a star-forming dwarf H II galaxy [arXiv:1109.2081, arXiv:1803.00537] featuring a distorted external envelope [arXiv:1109.2081] and two weakly connected star-forming knots [arXiv:1808.04508]. Its brightest region is an off-center eastern knot [arXiv:1109.2081], while a low-metallicity southwestern region suggests the recent infall of metal-poor gas [arXiv:1803.00537].

sources.summary: UM 461 is described as a well-studied H II galaxy with a compact off-center nucleus, distorted envelope, disputed tidal link to UM 462, and bright eastern star-forming region.

quotes.quote: UM 461 (PGC 037102) is a well studied H ii galaxy. This object has a very compact and bright off-center nucleus, some small regions spread along the galaxy N03 , and an external envelope that is strongly distorted towards the south-west. D01 and N03 have obtained integrated near-IR colors that differ significantly from each other, with J-H=0.99 and H-K=-0.68 and J-H=0.47 mag and H-K=0.2 mag, respectively. TE95 proposed that UM 461 was formed together with UM 462 by tidal interaction. But the HI maps of UM 461 and UM 462 do not show that these galaxies are tidally interacting, therefore it is unlikely that these objects induced the star formation on each other V98 .

Finally, the K _ p band morphology of UM 461 is similar to that reported in previous works e.g., N03 . The brightest region is off-center and located in the eastern part of the galaxy. This region is extremely compact and bright in Br .

sources.summary: The paper finds UM 461's two star-forming knots are weakly connected kinematically, with feedback-affected gas and an eastern-knot ring-like velocity-dispersion structure consistent with collect-and-collapse star formation.

quotes.quote: We have used integral field spectroscopy to study the internal kinematics of the H ii galaxies CTS 1020 and UM 461. We based our analysis on the velocity and velocity dispersion maps, and intensity-velocity dispersion ( I- ) and velocity-velocity dispersion ( V_r- ) diagrams. We found that the motion in both star-forming knots of UM 461 has different patterns, suggesting a weak kinematical connection between the knots. The overall kinematics of the galaxy is probably affected by stellar feedback.

The velocity dispersion in the eastern knot of UM 461 form a ring-like structure that resembles what we could expect in a collect and collapse scenario ElmegreenLada1997 in which the first generation of stars produces an H ii region that expands and sweep up the interstellar medium creating a shell of material that will collapse and start to form stars within it. In that shell, the newly formed stars also create H ii regions that expands into the shell ionizing it. In that way, the velocity dispersion induced to the gas in the most central region increases as the gas encounter an inhomogeneous medium and increases further as the stars within the shell drive H ii regions.

sources.summary: The paper studies UM 461 as a star-forming dwarf galaxy whose large-scale ISM is mostly mixed, but whose off-center low-metallicity region suggests recent metal-poor gas infall.

quotes.quote: Using VIMOS-IFU observations, we study the interstellar medium (ISM) of two star-forming dwarf galaxies, UM 461 and Mrk 600. Our aim was to search for the existence of metallicity inhomogeneities that might arise from infall of nearly pristine gas feeding ongoing localized star-formation. The IFU data allowed us to study the impact of external gas accretion on the chemical evolution as well as the ionised gas kinematics and morphologies of these galaxies.

For UM 461 our results show that the ISM is fairly well mixed, at large scales, however we find an off-centre and low-metallicity region with 12 + log(O/H) < 7.6 in the SW part of the brightest H ii region, using the direct method. This result is consistent with the recent infall of a low mass metal-poor dwarf or H i cloud into the region now exhibiting the lowest metallicity, which also displays localized perturbed neutral and ionized gas kinematics.

sources.summary: The paper treats UM 461 as a DGS metallicity outlier; its discrepancy is resolved by adopting a higher SFR from H-alpha and a low ionization correction factor.

quotes.quote: UM 461 : The difference ( O/H )=0.56 cannot be explained by \ and , which are both similar to the fiducial values Lagos2018 . However, report an based SFR =0.077 (indicating higher \ luminosity .f. 0.01 from Madden2013 ) and a relatively low ICF of 0.03 dex. Adopting these measurements, the difference is ( O/H )=-0.14 .

gmw_00004673.name: 2MASS J09343014-1721215
gmw_00004673.overview: 2MASS J09343014-1721215 is a quiescent BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2301.09828] that is also classified as a high-synchrotron-peaked blazar [arXiv:1609.08501]. Its broadband X-ray spectrum is well-described by a simple power law [arXiv:2301.09828], and the source exhibits a lack of long-term variability [arXiv:2301.09828].

sources.summary: The paper finds that 2MASS J09343014-1721215 is a quiescent BL Lac whose broadband X-ray spectrum is well fit by a simple power law with little long-term variability.

quotes.quote: Our analysis also shows that the broadband X-ray spectra (0.3-195 keV) of our sources can be described with different power law models. As is the case with most blazars, we find that 2 sources (2MASS J09343014-1721215 and PKS 0312-770) are well-modeled with a simple power law, while the remaining two (1RXS J225146.9-320614 and PKS 2126-15) exhibit curvature in the form of a log-parabolic power law. We also find that, in addition to the continuum, PKS 2126-15 requires significant absorption at the soft X-rays ( 1 keV) to fully describe the observed curvature, possibly due to absorption from the intergalactic medium.

For PKS 0312-770 and 2MASS J09343014-1721215, the signal of the co-added spectra dominated the background out to 3 keV and 5 keV, respectively, so we use the spectra up to those energies. We also exclude the last 3 channels of the BAT data for 2MASS J09343014-1721215 due to low signal-to-noise. The data for both of these sources are well-described by a simple power law with Galactic absorption (i.e. tbabs*po , see Figure fig:bestfits , top panel), with = 2.15 0.03 for PKS 0312-770 ( ^ 2 /d.o.f. = 241/236) and = 1.81 0.02 for 2MASS J09343014-1721215 ( ^ 2 /d.o.f. = 349/364). For PKS 0312-770, the normalization of the BAT data is 4 times larger than that for the NICER data, implying some variability on very long timescales. For 2MASS J09343014-1721215, the normalization across the two instruments is practically identical (see Table tab:bestfit ), confirming a lack of variability on long timescales.

sources.summary: The paper treats 2WHSP J093430.1-172120 as the fainter HSP blazar in a confused gamma-ray field, with residual TS maps matching it and a lower-significance detection added to the 2WHSP-FGL list.

quotes.quote: Here we provide strong evidence for source confusion in rays involving two objects: a blazar candidate (brighter ray source in the field) and 2WHSP J093430.1-172120 (fainter ray source in the field).

Clearly, the residual map shows our 2WHSP blazar matching the TS peak, well within the 68 containment region for the ray signature. ht This is clearly a case of source confusion where the 2WHSP is a counterpart for the residual ray signature, and the brighter source is a counterpart of an, as yet, unidentified object.

Although the blazar candidate could be of HSP type, it is not part of the 2WHSP catalog (because the X-ray data was not available at the time of the sample selection) and therefore we do not count it within the 2WHSP-FGL ray subsample; the source 2WHSP J093430.1-172120 is considered a lower-significance ray detection, so we count it within the 150 sources listed in Table tableFermi1 .

gmw_00004674.name: PKS 0312-770
gmw_00004674.overview: PKS 0312-770 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:2301.09828] that hosts a wide-ionization absorption system, which may trace an outflow [arXiv:1307.6799]. Its broadband X-ray spectrum is well-described by a simple power law [arXiv:2301.09828], and it exhibits variability on very long timescales [arXiv:2301.09828].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0312-770 as a faint FSRQ with low-amplitude X-ray variability, a simple power-law broadband spectrum, and long-timescale normalization changes.

quotes.quote: Our analysis also shows that the broadband X-ray spectra (0.3-195 keV) of our sources can be described with different power law models. As is the case with most blazars, we find that 2 sources (2MASS J09343014-1721215 and PKS 0312-770) are well-modeled with a simple power law, while the remaining two (1RXS J225146.9-320614 and PKS 2126-15) exhibit curvature in the form of a log-parabolic power law. We also find that, in addition to the continuum, PKS 2126-15 requires significant absorption at the soft X-rays ( 1 keV) to fully describe the observed curvature, possibly due to absorption from the intergalactic medium.

For PKS 0312-770 and 2MASS J09343014-1721215, the signal of the co-added spectra dominated the background out to 3 keV and 5 keV, respectively, so we use the spectra up to those energies. We also exclude the last 3 channels of the BAT data for 2MASS J09343014-1721215 due to low signal-to-noise. The data for both of these sources are well-described by a simple power law with Galactic absorption (i.e. tbabs*po , see Figure fig:bestfits , top panel), with = 2.15 0.03 for PKS 0312-770 ( ^ 2 /d.o.f. = 241/236) and = 1.81 0.02 for 2MASS J09343014-1721215 ( ^ 2 /d.o.f. = 349/364). For PKS 0312-770, the normalization of the BAT data is 4 times larger than that for the NICER data, implying some variability on very long timescales.

sources.summary: PKS 0312-77 shows a wide-ionization associated absorption system whose smooth profiles may trace an outflow, while coverage-fraction tests mostly support full coverage.

quotes.quote: PKS 0312-77 ( =0.2029) -- As shown in Figure fig:pks0312sys , an absorption-line system is detected in the E140M spectrum of this quasar over a very wide range of ionization species, from \ to . Both \ and \ show smooth profiles possibly indicative of an outflow origin. The results of the coverage fraction test on the , , and doublets is presented in Figure fig:pks0312pc . Neither , nor doublet shows signs of trough-dilution to a high confidence. That is, the derived coverage fractions are consistent with unity at the 3 \ level.

gmw_00004675.name: 1RXS J225146.9-320614
gmw_00004675.overview: 1RXS J225146.9-320614 is a blazar and BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2301.09828]. Its broadband X-ray spectrum exhibits curvature that is best fit by a log-parabolic power law, and the source shows flux variability between BAT and NICER observations [arXiv:2301.09828].

sources.summary: The paper finds that 1RXS J225146.9-320614 has a curved broadband X-ray spectrum best fit by a log-parabolic model and shows BAT-to-NICER variability.

quotes.quote: Our analysis also shows that the broadband X-ray spectra (0.3-195 keV) of our sources can be described with different power law models. As is the case with most blazars, we find that 2 sources (2MASS J09343014-1721215 and PKS 0312-770) are well-modeled with a simple power law, while the remaining two (1RXS J225146.9-320614 and PKS 2126-15) exhibit curvature in the form of a log-parabolic power law. We also find that, in addition to the continuum, PKS 2126-15 requires significant absorption at the soft X-rays ( 1 keV) to fully describe the observed curvature, possibly due to absorption from the intergalactic medium.

1RXS J225146.9-320614 For this source, the signal of the co-added NICER spectrum dominates the background up to 7 keV, so we use the 0.3-7 keV spectrum. When fitting with a simple power law, we find that the residuals show significant curvature. We therefore proceed to fitting with a log-parabolic power law ( tbabs*zlogpar ). This model provides the best fit to the data, improving from the simple power law fit by ^ 2 = 233 for one additional free parameter, with a significance of >99.99 evaluated using the F test (see Figure fig:bestfits , bottom left and Table for fit details), with ^ 2 /d.o.f. = 407/422. We also find variability between the BAT and NICER spectra by a factor of 3 (see normalizations in Table ).

gmw_00004676.name: PKS 2126-15
gmw_00004676.overview: PKS 2126-15 is a high-redshift quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0306530] featuring a compact, core-dominated active galactic nucleus jet [arXiv:1307.5121]. Its curved X-ray spectrum exhibits low-energy absorption [arXiv:2301.09828, arXiv:astro-ph/0306530], and the source displays short-timescale X-ray variability [arXiv:astro-ph/0306530].

sources.summary: The paper finds that PKS 2126-15 has a curved X-ray spectrum requiring a log-parabolic model plus excess soft-X-ray absorption, possibly from the intergalactic medium.

quotes.quote: Our analysis also shows that the broadband X-ray spectra (0.3-195 keV) of our sources can be described with different power law models. As is the case with most blazars, we find that 2 sources (2MASS J09343014-1721215 and PKS 0312-770) are well-modeled with a simple power law, while the remaining two (1RXS J225146.9-320614 and PKS 2126-15) exhibit curvature in the form of a log-parabolic power law. We also find that, in addition to the continuum, PKS 2126-15 requires significant absorption at the soft X-rays ( 1 keV) to fully describe the observed curvature, possibly due to absorption from the intergalactic medium.

PKS 2126-15 As with 1RXS J225146.9-320614, for PKS 2126-15 the signal dominates the background up to 7 keV. Following our usual procedure, we initially fit the co-added spectrum of PKS 2126-15 with a simple power law and once again observe curvature in the residuals. We thus proceed to fit with a log-parabolic power law. This improves the fit by ^ 2 = 642 for 1 additional free parameter, at a significance of > 99.99 . However, this is not enough to completely describe the observed curvature, and significant negative residuals remain at the soft X-rays (see Fig. fig:2126res ). We find that an additional absorption component at the redshift of the source ( ztbabs , assuming solar abundance), in excess of the Galactic absorption, improves the fit by ^ 2 = 96 for 1 additional free parameter, at a significance of > 99.99 .

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS2126-158 as a z=3.27 X-ray quasar with elevated BeppoSAX flux, short-timescale variability, hard X-ray detection, and strong low-energy absorption possibly linked to metal-line systems.

quotes.quote: BeppoSAX observed the z=3.27 quasar PKS2126-158 on 1999 May 24-28 when its 2-10keV and 0.1-2.5keV fluxes were 1.1 10^ 11 and 4.4 10^ 12 respectively, a factor of 2 higher than in all previous ROSAT and ASCA observations and 40 higher than in two more recent Chandra and XMM-Newton observations. The shortest detected rest frame variability timescale is of a few months, comparable to the causal timescale associated to an emission region of 10 Schwarzschild radii around a few 10^ 10 M _ black hole. The source is detected with a signal to noise ratio S/N 3 up to 50 keV, 215 keV rest frame.

The BeppoSAX observations confirm the presence of low energy absorption along the line of sight, independent on the continuum model adopted, at high confidence level. Despite the limited spectral resolution of the BeppoSAX LECS and MECS it is possible to put constraints on different absorption and continuum models, but not to unambiguously determine the redshift of the absorber. If the absorber is not significantly ionized the BeppoSAX data do prefer an absorber at z 2.7 . Strong and complex metal line systems along the line of sight to PKS2126-158 have been found at z=0.6631 and at 2.64 < z < 2.82.

sources.summary: The paper presents broadband radio polarization of quasar PKS B2126--158, finding strong circular polarization from a compact core-dominated AGN jet and evidence for Faraday conversion.

quotes.quote: We present full-Stokes radio polarization observations of the quasar PKS B2126--158 ( z=3.268 ) from 1 to 10 GHz using the Australia Telescope Compact Array. The source has large fractional circular polarization, m_c |V|/I , detected at high significance across the entire band (from 15 to 90 per 128 MHz sub-band). This allows us to construct the most robust circular polarization (CP) spectrum of an AGN jet to date.

Spatial location of the emission PKS B2126--158 has been observed at high angular resolution ( 5 mas) with VLBI fomalont, scott, charlot , showing that the total flux is almost completely dominated by the core with only a small extension of emission to the south. This indicates a potentially very small angle to the line of sight for this source and hence, strong Doppler boosting of the approaching jet emission.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2126-158 as an elongated core-jet source with a flat-spectrum peak, a curved southern jet, possible counter-jet emission, and weak core polarization.

quotes.quote: 2126-158 : our VLBI image shows an elongated NS core-jet structure with a total extension of about 3 mas. The comparison with the 5 GHz image reveals that the peak of the emission has a flat spectrum, and we identify it as the core. The MEM image shows a weak jet emerging from the core and leading with a curved trajectory to the South. The spot on the opposite side of the core may be an artifact but the 5 GHz image also shows a hint of emission in that direction, so it is also possible in this source we detect traces of a counter-jet. The core region is 1 polarized at p.a. +52^ , while the total fractional polarization at 8.4 GHz is below 0.2 .

gmw_00004677.name: GRB 021211 host
gmw_00004677.overview: The GRB 021211 host galaxy is located at a redshift of z = 1.006 [arXiv:astro-ph/0503574]. It is classified as an ultraluminous infrared galaxy due to its exceptionally high star formation rate [arXiv:1205.4239], despite showing low dust extinction [arXiv:astro-ph/0503574].

sources.summary: The paper reports a strong radio detection for the GRB 021211 host, deriving an unusually high star formation rate consistent with an ultraluminous infrared galaxy.

quotes.quote: The star formation rate (SFR) for the GRB 021211 host of 825 M_ yr ^ 1 , the highest ever reported for a GRB host, places it in the category of ultraluminous infrared galaxies.

Our 1.43 GHz detection of the host of GRB 021211 ( 10 ) corresponds to SFR of 825 (Table tab:flux ), which places it in the category of ultraluminous infrared galaxies (ULIRGs; L_ IR >10^ 12 L_ , or SFR 172 using the conversion of kennicutt ). This is the highest SFR ever reported for a GRB host compare with berger, bergerkulkarni,michalowski08,stanway10,watson11 . Because of this unusually high SFR we present the investigation of the data quality for this object.
Further observations at various radio frequencies are needed to resolve this issue. Our 1.43 GHz detection is consistent with the (sub)millimeter limits of smith05 and priddey06 .

sources.summary: The paper reports an R 25.2 host at z=1.006 for GRB 021211, with low extinction and no SCUBA submillimeter detection, implying it is not a bright submillimeter source.

quotes.quote: An underlying R 25.2 host galaxy was found (Della Valle et al. ). The redshift of the host was z = 1.006 (Vreeswijk et al. ).

As shown in Table table:host , the combination of our ToO observations limits the quiescent sub-millimeter flux of the host galaxy. Given the relatively low redshift, and the lack of extinction in the host galaxy (Holland et al. ), it is not surprising that it is not a bright sub-millimeter source. The fact that no SCUBA source was detected confirms that this burst had an intrinsically faint afterglow, rather than one that was heavily absorbed by dust in the host galaxy.

gmw_00004678.name: NGC 2993
gmw_00004678.overview: NGC 2993 is a star-forming spiral galaxy undergoing an interaction with its northern companion, NGC 2992 [arXiv:1502.04501, arXiv:1005.4791]. The two galaxies are connected by a dusty bridge and a tidal tail [arXiv:1502.04501, arXiv:1005.4791].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 2993 as the spiral star-forming member of Arp 245, linked to NGC 2992 by a dusty bridge and having similar infrared SED properties.

quotes.quote: This system is formed by NGC 2992, the spiral star-forming galaxy NGC 2993 Usui98 and the tidal dwarf galaxy Arp 245 North (hereafter Arp 245N; duc00 ). Two bright tidal features connect these three galaxies, suggesting that the system is in an early stage of the interation duc00 .

The bridge (Arp 245 Bridge) between NGC 2992 and NGC 2993 is more conspicuous in the Herschel/SPIRE maps, indicating the presence of cooler dust. We measured total galaxy fluxes for the three galaxies to construct their IR SEDs, which are shown in Fig. SED and Table flux_results .

As can be seen from Fig. SED , the total SEDs of NGC 2992 and NGC 2993 are very similar at wavelengths longer than 8 . This similarity is likely related to the presence of intense SF in both galaxies, which heats the dust at similar temperatures. This is corroborated by the SED fitting presented in Section dust .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2993 as NGC 2992's southern interacting companion, connected by a tidal tail, with perigalacticon about 100 Myr ago possibly triggering NGC 2992's starburst.

quotes.quote: Later, Glass ( ) suggested that it is a hybrid between an intermediate Seyfert and a starburst galaxy, induced by the interaction with NGC 2993 to the south. Both galaxies are connected by a tidal tail with a projected length of 2 9.

We also briefly consider whether the interaction between NGC 2992 and NGC 2993 might be responsible for the starburst. The gravitational (tidal) force during an interaction can be approximated as a delta-function that occurs at perigalacticon. According to Duc et al. ( ) perigalacticon of NGC 2992 and NGC 2993 occurred about 100 Myr ago.
Given that it takes time for the gas to respond and, for example, be driven to the centre, it is perhaps plausible for the interaction to have triggered the starburst.

gmw_00004679.name: Arp 245N
gmw_00004679.overview: Arp 245N is a tidal dwarf galaxy within the early-stage interacting system Arp 245 [arXiv:1502.04501, arXiv:2101.10875]. It exhibits a distorted morphology, is connected to its companion galaxies by a bright tidal tail, and displays weak star formation activity [arXiv:1502.04501, arXiv:2101.10875].

sources.summary: The paper describes Arp 245N as the tidal dwarf member of Arp 245, morphologically distorted, connected by a tidal tail, and showing weak mid-infrared star formation.

quotes.quote: This system is formed by NGC 2992, the spiral star-forming galaxy NGC 2993 Usui98 and the tidal dwarf galaxy Arp 245 North (hereafter Arp 245N; duc00 ). Two bright tidal features connect these three galaxies, suggesting that the system is in an early stage of the interation duc00 .

NGC 2992 and Arp 245N show clearly distorted morphologies, and they are linked by a tidal tail which appears very bright in the Spitzer/IRAC images. The bridge (Arp 245 Bridge) between NGC 2992 and NGC 2993 is more conspicuous in the Herschel/SPIRE maps, indicating the presence of cooler dust.

The fainter SED of Arp 245N has a similar shape to those of NGC 2992 and NGC 2993 SEDs, except in the 24-100 m range, where the fluxes are lower compared to other wavelengths. This can be indicative of a relatively weak illuminating radiation field that may possibly be consistent with weak SF activity (see Section dust ).

sources.summary: The paper treats Arp 245 as an early-stage merger system containing NGC 2992, NGC 2993, and A245N, about 100 Myr after pericentre, with developed tidal tails.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present a case study of the Seyfert galaxy NGC 2992, which together with NGC 2993 forms the early-stage merger system Arp 245.

Alongside with the starburst galaxy NGC 2993 and the tidal dwarf galaxy A245N it forms the system Arp 245. Using hydrodynamic simulations, reported the system is currently at an early stage of the interaction, about 100 Myr after pericentre passage. Nevertheless, tidal tails have already developed, which can be seen by optical images ( fig:Data ), in CO and HI lines Duc_2000 and infrared (IR) images 2015MNRAS.449.1309G .

gmw_00004680.name: UGC 04780
gmw_00004680.overview: UGC 04780 is a blue barred spiral galaxy classified as an Sdm star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1807.11526, arXiv:1811.10061]. It features blue clumps in its spiral arms that indicate ongoing star formation, and its light is dominated by a young stellar population [arXiv:1807.11526].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 04780 as the blue barred spiral host of ASASSN-18bt, estimates its distance, fits its SED, and derives stellar mass and star formation rate.

quotes.quote: Using Fit 6 in Table 9 of folatelli10 and the properties derived from the supernova light curve we estimate the distance to UGC 04780 to be d= 49 3 Mpc. This distance is consistent with the redshift (47.7 Mpc for z=0.01098 and H_0=69.6 , _M=0.286 , _ = 0.714 ; schneider90 ) and is used throughout this work.

The host galaxy of ASASSN-18bt is UGC 04780 ( z=0.01098 , schneider90 ), a blue barred spiral galaxy with blue clumps in its arms, indicating the likely presence of ongoing star formation. Using archival photometry from Pan-STARRS (optical), the Galaxy Evolution Explorer (GALEX; ultraviolet), and the Wide-field Infrared Explorer (WISE; near-infrared), we fit the spectral energy distribution of UGC 04780 with the publicly available Fitting and Assessment of Synthetic Templates fast ; kriek09 . Given the clumpy nature of the light distribution, we measure the optical magnitudes from the PS1 images by hand and find g 14.9 mag, 14.5 mag, i 14.5 mag, z 14.4 mag, and y 14.3 mag.

Based on the FAST fit, the host galaxy has a stellar mass of (4.68^ +0.33 _ 0.61 ) 10^8 and a star formation rate of 0.05 yr ^ 1 , which is largely consistent with the results from the MPA-JHU Galspec pipeline. However, the galaxy light is dominated by a young stellar population, and the modeling has difficulty fitting both the optical and infrared data, suggesting that our mass estimate should be regarded as an upper limit.

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 4780 as SN 2018oh's Sdm star-forming host, gives its redshift and distance, derives a low host-galaxy mass, and adopts the supernova-based distance.

quotes.quote: The supernova is located at =09^ h 06^ m 39^ s .592 , =+19^ o 20 17 .47 (J2000.0) Cornect18ATel , 7.8 North and 2.0 East of the center of UGC 4780, a Sdm starforming galaxy, with a redshift of z = 0.010981 and a distance of 49.4 Mpc.

UGC 4780 was included as a Campaign 16 target through `The K2 ExtraGalactic Survey (KEGS) for Transients' (PI Rest) and the `Multi-Observatory Monitoring of K2 Supernovae' (PI Foley) programs as part of the K2 SCE (internal Kepler ID 228682548). After the end of Campaign 16, the data were transferred to MAST, from which we retrieved the UGC 4780 data.

Regarding the host galaxy mass step _M Kelly10,Lampeitl10,Sullivan10 , we use SDSS g and i magnitudes with the relation of their Equation 8 Taylor11 to derive the host galaxy mass of UGC 4780. We find the mass to be 8.81 dex, comfortably on the low-mass side of the step function, and we correct with _M=-0.06 mag. The final distance modulus, assuming H_0 = 73 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , is estimated to be = 33.61 0.05 mag, corresponding to a distance of 52.7 1.2 Mpc. As UGC 4780 is not in the Hubble flow and has no independent distance measurement, the distance using the SN itself is the most accurate and precise distance, and we use this distance for the remainder of the analysis.

gmw_00004681.name: NGC 3726
gmw_00004681.overview: NGC 3726 is a late-type barred spiral galaxy located in the Ursa Major cluster [arXiv:1907.09573, arXiv:1605.01638] that features a weak bar [arXiv:0806.0471], flocculent spiral arms [arXiv:1509.02356], and a distorted, asymmetric disk [arXiv:1907.02934]. Its neutral hydrogen gas is distributed in a ring-like structure [arXiv:1605.01638], while its blue nucleus shows evidence of a relatively recent, moderate starburst [arXiv:1509.02356].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3726 as one of three Ursa Major spiral galaxies, fitting its observed rotation curve with a generalized Miyamoto-Nagai potential and analyzing possible regular or chaotic stellar orbits.

quotes.quote: In the present paper, using a generalization of the Miyamoto and Nagai potential we adjusted the observed rotation curves of three specific spiral galaxies to the analytical circular velocities. The observational data have been taken from a 21 cm-line synthesis imaging survey using the Westerbork Synthesis Radio Telescope, for three particular galaxies in the Ursa Major cluster: NGC 3726, NGC 3877 and NGC 4010. Accordingly, the dynamics of the system is analyzed in terms of the Poincar e sections method, finding that for larger values of the angular momentum of the test particle or lower values its total energy the dynamics is mainly regular, while on the opposite cases, the dynamics is mainly chaotic. Our toy model opens the possibility to find chaotic bounded orbits for stars in those particular galaxies.

In panels (a) of Figures 1, 2, and 3, we show the observational data (points) of the rotation curve with the corresponding velocity dispersions (error bars) as reported by for NGC 3726, NGC 3877 and NGC 4010. The solid lines correspond to the analytical expressions eq6 fitted to the rotation curves. As can be seen, in each case the model fits the observed data with good accuracy. Additionally, in panels (b) of Figures 1, 2, and 3, we plot the normalized mass-density distribution eq5 at z=0 for the three galaxies, as a function of the dimensionless radial coordinate R .

sources.summary: The paper characterizes NGC 3726 as a late-type barred ring galaxy with flocculent spiral arms, a blue nucleus, and a likely recent moderate nuclear starburst.

quotes.quote: NGC 3726. This is a late-type galaxy with a bar, ring, and flocculent spiral arms. The outer regions of NGC 3726 have a well defined asymmetric shape which was not studied by us in gus02a . The positions of the components on the two-color diagrams are typical for Sc galaxies. An exception is the nucleus, which is the bluest component of the galaxy and deviates to the right of the NCS on the (B-V) (V-I) diagram.

The position of the nucleus of NGC 3726 on the two-color diagrams corresponds best to models with a moderate ( M_2/M_1 = 0.1 ), relatively recent secondary burst of star formation ( T_2 300 Myr and _2 100 Myr). Unfortunately, we did not observe this galaxy in the U band, hindering unambiguous determination of the parameters of the nuclear burst of star formation.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 3726 as a barred spiral, finding three corotation-radius sign changes and linking its outer resonances and first density-wave mode to bar-driven structure.

quotes.quote: Fig.  figure:offset_NGC3726 shows the radial change in azimuthally averaged offset in the annulus calculated for 254 SC--H ii R pairs in the barred galaxy NGC 3726. There are three cases of a sign change in the azimuthal offset in NGC 3726 at galactocentric distances of about R_ C1 2 kpc, R_ C2 4.4 kpc and R_ C3 9.8 kpc. The innermost resonance appears to be associated with an inner mass concentration of the bar and coincides with the first resonance detected by Font2011,Font2014 using the Font-Beckman (FB) method from residual velocity field analysis and by Buta2009 using the potential-density phase-shift method. While the two outer resonances are features of the disc.

The radial variation of criterion Eq.  equation:criterion_2 (case m=2 ), calculated using Fourier coefficients derived from analysis of the velocity field of the NGC 3726, shows that at distance R_ C, m=2 = 9.3 1.0 kpc the spiral pattern speed is equal to that of the disc (upper panel in Fig.  figure:crit_2_NGC3726 ). The radial variation of criterion Eq.  equation:criterion_1 (case m=1 ), shows that at distance R_ C, m=1 = 4.5 1.0 kpc the spiral pattern speed is equal to that of the disc (bottom panel in Fig.  figure:crit_2_NGC3726 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3726 as an Ursa Major SABc galaxy with ring-like HI, possible diffuse gas extensions, a warped outer disc, and bar-influenced ionized-gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: NGC 3726 NGC 3726 is an SABc galaxy in the Ursa Major cluster. HI synthesis data from the WHISP survey displayed in Fig.  fig:n3726sum shows that the neutral gas is distributed in a ring-like structure with decreasing column density towards the centre. There are hints of extended diffuse gas to the north (approaching side) and south (receding side) along the major axis. The HI velocity field and isovelocity contours hint that the outer disc may be warped. This warp manifests itself as a dip in the rotation curve around 180 arcsec, which doesn't seem to be well-modeled by the baryonic contributions (Fig.  fig:rcdecomp ).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3726 as a distant, asymmetric-disc galaxy with 254 selected pairs, chaotic age-separation behavior, and steeper star-formation propagation trends.

quotes.quote: The final sample includes 503 SC-H ii R pairs in NGC 628, 70 pairs in NGC 3184, 254 pairs in NGC 3726, 40 pairs in NGC 5585, and 577 pairs in NGC 6946.

NGC 5585 has rather the Large Magellanic Cloud type. NGC 3726 has a distorted asymmetric disc. In addition, it is the most distant galaxy of the sample and, therefore, we were unable to identify a sufficient number of close SC-H ii R pairs with S<80 pc in it (Fig. figure:age_sep ).

SC-H ii R pairs in the asymmetric galaxy NGC 3726 show a rather chaotic distribution on the diagram t - S . This scatter can be caused by various parameters of the medium in the asymmetric disc of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 3726 has a weak small bar, polygonal inner spiral structure, and a best-fitting model that reproduces this appearance with R 2.

quotes.quote: NGC 3726 : this SBc ( T=5 ) galaxy has a weak and small bar. The inner part of the spiral structure is somewhat polygonal. The best-fitting model reproduces this appearance and gives R 2 (see Fig. modsel1 ).

gmw_00004682.name: RXJ13334+0001
gmw_00004682.overview: RXJ13334+0001 is an obscured quasar located at a redshift of z=2.35 [arXiv:astro-ph/9810413]. It is classified as a quasar equivalent to a Type 1.9 Seyfert galaxy due to the detection of broad H-alpha emission and the non-detection of H-beta [arXiv:astro-ph/9810413].

sources.summary: The paper identifies RXJ13334+0001 as a z=2.35 obscured QSO, using UKIRT and X-ray data to infer reddening, hard or absorbed spectra, and type-II-QSO-like behavior.

quotes.quote: During March and May 1995, observations of RXJ13334+0001 were made at the UK Infrared Telescope in Hawaii. Photometry with IRCAM3 revealed a point-like object in 0.6 arcsec seeing with a magnitude K'=16.6 . This gives B-K=5.4 compared to B-K 2.5 for normal QSOs at this redshift (Webster et al. 1995; Barvainis 1993), suggesting 3 magnitudes of UV extinction (although, as discussed in Section 5, the internal extinction could be significantly larger if the UV flux is dominated by surrounding star-forming activity rather than the AGN).
Hence the UKIRT observations confirm that RXJ13334+0001 is a QSO obscured by a moderate amount of dust, sufficient to obliterate the UV broad emission lines but not the broad H_ .

The detection of broad H_ but non-detection of H_ formally classifies this object as a QSO equivalent to `Type 1.9' Seyferts (Osterbrock 1981). The service observation also revealed [ OIII ] 5007 at 1.673 microns (consistent with z=2.341 0.005 ) with an equivalent width of 32 and a FWHM of 540 km s ^ 1 . Assuming a similar width for narrow H_ one can obtain a limit on the line ratio [ OIII ] /H_ >5.6 (using the same technique described above).

gmw_00004683.name: NGC 4527
gmw_00004683.overview: NGC 4527 is a spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0011440] that hosted the Type Ia supernova SN 1991T [arXiv:astro-ph/0011440, arXiv:astro-ph/9909117]. Measurements of its distance modulus using surface brightness fluctuations help resolve the apparent overluminosity of this supernova [arXiv:astro-ph/0011440].

sources.summary: The paper measures a surface-brightness-fluctuation distance to NGC 4527, the host of SN 1991T, finding a shorter distance modulus that reduces the supernova's apparent overluminosity.

quotes.quote: We present a distance to NGC 4527 , the host galaxy of the type Ia SN 1991T , measured by surface brightness fluctuations. This supernova has been labelled ``peculiar'' both on the grounds of its spectroscopic behaviour and its apparent overluminosity with respect to other supernovae. The distance modulus to NGC 4527 and thus to SN 1991T is 30.26 0.09 . This relatively short distance largely removes the discrepancy with other Ia supernovae having similar light-curve characteristics and also removes the motivation for interpreting SN 1991T as a super-Chandrasekhar explosion.

sources.summary: NGC 4527 is discussed as the probable host-distance problem for SN 1991T; a shorter distance modulus would align the supernova with other events.

quotes.quote: Another ``peculiar'' Ia SN, which, according to its probable distance of the host galaxy, turns out to be too bright, is SN 1991T in NGC 4527 (e.g. ). Although its photometric behaviour is normal, NGC 4527 should have a distance modulus of about 29.90 mag to bring SN 1991T in agreement with other SNe. A Cepheid distance to this galaxy would be very desirable.

gmw_00004684.name: PKS 1017+109
gmw_00004684.overview: PKS 1017+109 is a quasar at z=3.164 that hosts a compact continuum-emitting source with a high star formation rate [arXiv:2111.15392, arXiv:1709.08228]. It is surrounded by a Lyman-alpha nebula spanning 297 projected kiloparsecs, which features a large-scale asymmetry and contains four active companion sources [arXiv:1808.10857, arXiv:1709.08228].

sources.summary: PKS 1017+109 is identified as the survey outlier hosting a rare ELAN, with extreme Ly-alpha extent, large-scale asymmetry, large covering factor, and active companions.

quotes.quote: On the contrary, there are a handful of cases for which the centroid of the nebulosity is clearly separated from the quasar ( >20 kpc), with ID 13 (or PKS 1017+109) being the extreme case ( d_ QSO-Neb =64.4 kpc). Also, 7 nebulosities show an asymmetry <0.5 . However, while most of these small asymmetries are driven by features on small scales, ID 13 (or PKS 1017+109) appear to be a clear outlier with the small asymmetry driven by structures on large scales, i.e. encompassing an area 3.4 that of the other 6 nebulosities with <0.5 . These characteristics are probably evidences of the powering mechanism for the \ emission (see Section sec:powering ), of the local environment of the system studied (e.g., hennawi+15,fab+2018 ), and possibly encode information on the different orientations of the systems with respect to our observational vantage point.

For comparison, we show in red the profile of the covering factor for the target with ID 13 (or PKS 1017+109), which is associated with an ELAN. This object is clearly characterized by a larger covering factor than the average population, with f_ C >0.8 for R<50 kpc, and f_ C >0.1 at R=140 kpc (our last bin).

Of all these quasars, only one object shows the extreme observed surface brightnesses and extents found in the ELAN at z 2 , i.e. the ID 13 in this work or PKS 1017+109. All the other nebulosities show lower levels of \ emission or extends only to much smaller distances from the central quasar down to the same SB limits (see Figure fig:asymmetry ). The ELAN around the quasar PKS 1017+109 has been studied in detail in fab+2018 , where we have indicated the common peculiarities with the other ELAN discovered at z 2 , with particular emphasis on the identification of active companions in their surroundings.

sources.summary: The paper treats QSO as the central quasar host in the Fabulous ELAN, detecting compact continuum emission and estimating a high SFR consistent with z 2-3 quasars.

quotes.quote: The detected sources are QSO, QSO2, AGN1, LAE1, and a newly discovered source SMG1. The positions, S/N and fluxes for the five detections are listed in Table tab:Interf , together with the deboosted fluxes estimated as explained in Appendix app:SMADeboosting . Summing up the deboosted fluxes of all detected sources, we find agreement within uncertainties with the detections in the single-dish datasets, 14.7 2.8 mJy. Therefore, all the continuum emission detected by LABOCA and SCUBA-2 is ascribed to compact sources.

Further, CIGALE determined the instantaneous SFR for each source, indicating SFR =521 74,183 49,104 21,50 11 M _ yr ^ 1 for QSO, QSO2, AGN1, LAE1, respectively. These SFRs are in line with values from the literature. In particular, the SFR in the QSO host agrees well with values usually found in z 2-3 quasars (e.g., Harris2016 ).

sources.summary: The paper reports a z=3.164 quasar surrounded by a 297 kpc ELAN, four embedded sources, and a coherent velocity pattern interpreted as accreting substructure in its host halo.

quotes.quote: In this work, we report the discovery of an enormous \ nebula (ELAN) around the quasar SDSS J102009.99+104002.7 at z=3.164 , which we followed-up with deeper MUSE observations. This ELAN spans 297 projected kpc, has an average \ surface brightness SB _ Ly 6.04 10^ 18 (within the 2 isophote), and is associated with an additional four, previously unknown embedded sources: two \ emitters and two faint active galactic nuclei (one Type-1 and one Type-2 quasar). By mapping at high significance the line-of-sight velocity in the entirety of the observed structure, we unveiled a large-scale coherent rotation-like pattern spanning 300 km s ^ 1 with a velocity dispersion of <270 km s ^ 1 , which we interpret as a signature of the inspiraling accretion of substructures within the quasar's host halo.

gmw_00004685.name: QSO B0624+6907
gmw_00004685.overview: QSO B0624+6907 is a quasar that exhibits spectropolarimetric signatures of equatorial scattering [arXiv:2309.12944]. This scattering indicates that radiation from its accretion disk and broad-line region interacts with a surrounding dust torus [arXiv:2309.12944].

sources.summary: The paper reports ISM-polarization checks, unavailable H coverage, equatorial scattering signatures, and high inferred inclination for QSO B0624+6907.

quotes.quote: For PG 0052+251 the maximum value of P_ ISM = 0.2 , and for Mrk 957, QSO B0624+6907 and LEDA 2196699 P_ ISM = 0.5 .

For each object, the spectral range of observations was selected so that a broad H line was present in the resulting spectrum (in cases of PG 0137+06 and QSO B0624+6907 H turned out to be out of range, then we used the broad H line).

In addition, we note the large value of the angle i 70^ 85^ for QSO B0624+6907, which also exceeds the value obtained by other methods 45^ [ note that in our work piotrovich17a for this object, the value of the angle i 16^ was obtained by another method ] . Such a large value of the angle may already indicate that the method used to determine the spin is not quite suitable for this object. In addition, the observed spectropolarimetric characteristics of this object show signs of equatorial scattering, indicating that we see effects from the interaction of radiation from the accretion disk and BLR with the dust torus.

gmw_00004686.name: LEDA 3095636
gmw_00004686.overview: LEDA 3095636 is an active galactic nucleus that exhibits signs of equatorial scattering [arXiv:2309.12944]. Polarimetric observations of the galaxy show V-band and R-band polarization without any change in the polarization angle along the wavelength [arXiv:2309.12944].

sources.summary: The paper gives LEDA 3095636 an unbiased polarization estimate, identifies equatorial scattering, and measures its V- and R-band polarization without a wavelength-dependent angle change.

quotes.quote: In the observed sample, the unbiased estimate was calculated for three objects -- PG 0052+251, LEDA 3095636 and LEDA 2196699.
We found signs of equatorial scattering in the objects LEDA 3095636 and QSO B0624+6907 (marked "ES" in Table sum ).
In the case of LEDA 3095636 P(V)=(2.0 0.6) , and P(R)=(0.8 0.3) , the angle does not change along the wavelength.

gmw_00004687.name: SDSS J100145.30+283330.3
gmw_00004687.overview: SDSS J100145.30+283330.3 is an active galactic nucleus that has an interacting companion galaxy to its northwest [arXiv:1111.2862]. Long-slit spectra reveal emission features physically linking the two galaxies, which may indicate an inflow or outflow of gas between them [arXiv:1111.2862].

sources.summary: The paper notes an interacting companion to SDSS J100145.30+283330.3 and possible gas linkage, suggesting merger-driven fueling of its double-peaked AGN.

quotes.quote: SDSS J100145.30+283330.3 This galaxy has an interacting companion to the northwest (Figure fig:unusual_images , bottom), and we aligned one of the slit position angles ( =142^ .3 ) to include this companion. The long-slit spectra show emission features physically linking the two galaxies (Figure fig:unusual_spectra , bottom), possibly indicating an inflow or outflow of gas between the two galaxies. This could be an indication of the triggering mechanism that funnels gas onto SMBHs during galaxy mergers.

gmw_00004688.name: SDSS J095207.62+255257.2
gmw_00004688.overview: SDSS J095207.62+255257.2 is a dual active galactic nucleus candidate undergoing a major merger [arXiv:1111.2862, arXiv:1504.01391]. The system features two stellar bulges [arXiv:1504.01391, arXiv:1810.11543], extended low-surface-brightness emission [arXiv:1501.00325], and central oxygen emission wings indicative of an outflow [arXiv:1501.00325, arXiv:1810.11543].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J095207.62+255257.2 as one of its strongest dual-AGN candidates, with double stellar and AGN components consistent with a late-stage merger.
