The procedure for determining the velocity dispersion across the galaxy disks as a function of radius is described in ianjamasimanana12 and ianjamasimanana.etal.2017 as well as in das.etal.2020 . We basically co-added the individual velocity profiles over radial bins to obtain azimuthally averaged, high signal-to-noise ratio (S/N) velocity dispersion values for each radial bin. The stacked profiles were fitted with single Gaussian functions, where the half width of the fitted Gaussian represents the velocity dispersion of the gas. This method gives a better estimate of the velocity dispersion compared to the moment2 maps. Figure 4 in the appendix shows the radial variation of the velocity dispersion across the galaxy disks. Its value is less than 10 kms ^ 1 at the R _ 25 radius, except for a few cases (NGC 5194, NGC 4190, DDO 187 and IC 10), where _ H i lies between 11 and 14 kms ^ 1 .

gmw_00004714.name: GRB 140629A host galaxy
gmw_00004714.overview: The GRB 140629A host galaxy is a low-mass, star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1910.10747] with negligible optical dust extinction [arXiv:1804.06046]. It is classified as a damped Lyman-alpha system [arXiv:1910.10747] and exhibits negligible neutral hydrogen absorption in its afterglow spectrum [arXiv:1804.06046].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the GRB 140629A host as a low-mass, star-forming galaxy at z=2.276, fitting late-time photometry to derive its age, stellar mass, luminosity, and SFR.

quotes.quote: Fitting the host galaxy photometry, we find the host to be a low mass, star-forming galaxy with a star formation rate of log(SFR)= 1.1_ 0.4 ^ +0.9 M _ yr ^ 1 . We obtain a value of the neutral hydrogen density by fitting the optical spectrum, log N_ H I =21.0 0.3 , classifying this host as a damped Lyman-alpha.

The four photometric magnitudes for the host galaxy were fit with a set of galaxy templates based on the models from 2003MNRAS.344.1000B at a fixed redshift 2007A&A...475..101C,2011A&A...534A.108K using the LePhare package (v.2.2; 1999MNRAS.310..540A,2006A&A...457..841I ). As shown in Figure Host galaxy SED , the optical SED is reproduced best (reduced ^ 2 /dof=0.1/3) by a template of a galaxy with a starburst age of 1.14_ 0.35 ^ +1.03 Gyr and a mass of log( M_ * /M_ )= 8.3_ 0.4 ^ +0.9 , which is lower but consistent within errors with 10^ 9.3 M_ , i.e. the average value of GRB hosts 2009ApJ...691..182S . The absolute bolometric magnitude of the host galaxy is -22.49 mag and the star formation rate (SFR) is log(SFR)= 1.1_ 0.4 ^ +0.9 M _ yr ^ 1 , which is determined from the UV luminosity of the rest-frame SED 1998ApJ...498..541K .

sources.summary: The paper models the GRB 140629A host galaxy in the afterglow spectrum, finding negligible optical extinction and neutral hydrogen absorption while Galactic foreground absorption explains the soft X-ray absorption.

quotes.quote: We fit the spectrum with a model zdust*zpha*pha*powerlaw by using the Xspec package, where ``zdust" is for the dust extinction of the GRB 140629A host galaxy, ``zpha" and ``pha" are for the neutral hydrogen absorption of the GRB host galaxy and our Galaxy, respectively, and ``powerlaw" is a single power law function. We find that the optical extinction is negligible even when the extinction laws of the Galaxy, SMC and LMC were used in our fit. In addition, the neutral hydrogen absorption of the GRB host galaxy is also negligible.

gmw_00004715.name: J1332--3308
gmw_00004715.overview: J1332--3308 is an extended central radio galaxy located approximately at the center of the galaxy cluster A3560 [arXiv:astro-ph/0209399]. Associated with a Dumb-bell galaxy, it exhibits a complex radio morphology with a total power that sits at the transition between Fanaroff-Riley class I and II [arXiv:astro-ph/0209399].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1332--3308 as an extended central radio galaxy in A3560, associated with the Dumb-bell galaxy and showing a complex high-power morphology with lobes, a filament, and diffuse emission.

quotes.quote: At radio wavelengths A3560 is dominated by the extended radio galaxy J1332--3308, associated with the Dumb-bell galaxy cited in Sect.2. This source is located approximately at the center of the peaked component of the hot gas distribution (see Sect.3). No other radio sources in the A3560 field are associated with cluster galaxies. This radio galaxy was observed over a wide range of radio frequencies with the Very Large Array (VLA, New Mexico, USA) and with the Australia Telescope Compact Array (ATCA, Narrabri, Australia) as part of a larger project addressing extended radio galaxies in the central region of the Shapley Concentration (Venturi et al. in preparation). In this section we will present only those radio properties of this source relevant to the astrophysical problems dealt with in the present paper.

In Figure fig:radiogal we show the 1.4 GHz VLA image of the radio galaxy overlaid on the DSS--2 optical image. As can be seen, J1332--3308 is a complex radio source, as is often found at the centre of galaxy clusters, with a total power of log P (W Hz ^ 1 ) = 24.41 at 1.4 GHz. This value can be considered the transition power between FRI and FRII radio galaxies (Fanaroff Riley ), and among the largest values found for radio galaxies located at the centre of rich clusters (Burns ; Ball et al. ; Gregorini et al. ).

gmw_00004716.name: And XXV
gmw_00004716.overview: Andromeda XXV is a dwarf spheroidal satellite galaxy of M31 [arXiv:2209.15022]. It has a systemic velocity of -107.7 km/s, a metallicity of -1.9 dex, and an unambiguous dark matter component [arXiv:2209.15022].

sources.summary: The paper remeasures And XXV as an M31 dwarf spheroidal, finding a clear dark matter component, low central dark matter density, and metallicity consistent with earlier work.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we investigate another potential outlier, Andromeda XXV (And XXV), a dSph satellite galaxy of M31 first discovered as part of the Pan-Andromeda Archaeological Survey Richardson2011 . And XXV was previously identified as a potential outlier within the LG in a 2013 study ( Collins2013 , hereafter known as Collins2013 ) that performed a kinematic analysis of numerous M31 dSph satellites. And XXV was found to have a low velocity dispersion, _ v =3.0 ^ +1.2 _ 1.0  kms ^ 1 , which results in a mass-to-light ratio of [ M/L ] _ half =10.3 ^ +7.0 _ 6.7  M _ /L _ consistent with a simple stellar system with no appreciable dark matter component, albeit with large uncertainties due to the small number of member stars (26) with stellar kinematics in their sample.

In this study, we double the number of member stars and re-derive the kinematics and mass of And XXV. We find that And XXV has a systemic velocity of _ =-107.7 1.0  kms ^ 1 and a velocity dispersion of _ =4.5 1.0  kms ^ 1 . With this better constrained velocity dispersion, we derive a mass contained within the half-light radius of M( < h )=6.9^ +3.2 _ 2.8 10^6  M . This mass corresponds to a mass-to-light ratio of [ M/L ] _ r_ h =37^ +17 _ 15  M _ /L _ , demonstrating, for the first time, that And XXV has an unambiguous dark matter component. We also measure the metallicity of And XXV to be Fe/H =-1.9 0.1  dex, which is in agreement with previous results.

gmw_00004717.name: PSO J215.1512-16.0417
gmw_00004717.overview: PSO J215.1512-16.0417 is a high-redshift broad-absorption-line quasar located at a redshift of approximately 5.73 [arXiv:1109.6241, arXiv:1611.02870]. It features a very blue continuum spectrum [arXiv:1109.6241], and its surrounding field shows no overdensity of Lyman Alpha Emitting galaxies [arXiv:1611.02870].

sources.summary: The paper reports PSO J215.1512-16.0417 as a PS1-discovered high-redshift BAL quasar with z=5.73, high luminosity, a massive black hole, and a blue continuum.

quotes.quote: We present the discovery of the first high redshift ( z > 5.7 ) quasar from the Panoramic Survey Telescope and Rapid Response System 1 ( \ or PS1). This quasar was initially detected as an dropoutout in PS1, confirmed photometrically with the SAO Widefield InfraRed Camera (SWIRC) at Arizona's Multiple Mirror Telescope (MMT) and the Gamma-Ray Burst Optical/Near-Infrared Detector (GROND) at the MPG 2.2 m telescope in La Silla. The quasar was verified spectroscopically with the the MMT Spectrograph, Red Channel and the Cassegrain Twin Spectrograph (TWIN) at the Calar Alto 3.5 m telescope. It has a redshift of 5.73, an AB magnitude of 19.4, a luminosity of 3.8 10 ^ 47 erg s ^ 1 and a black hole mass of 6.9 10 ^9 M _ . It is a Broad Absorption Line quasar with a prominent Ly- peak and a very blue continuum spectrum.

We did find one very promising candidate (finding chart in Fig. fig:chart and multi-filter imaging in Fig. fig:GROND ), PSO J215.1512-16.0417 (at J2000 215.1512, -16.0417 or 14 ^h 20 ^m 36.3 ^s , -16 ^ 02 ^m 30.2 ^s ). It had i_ GROND z_ GROND = 2.06 and z_ GROND J_ GROND = 0.60 (complete photometry in Table tab:GROND ).

sources.summary: The paper presents PSO J215.1512 16.0417 as a luminous z=5.73 broad-absorption-line quasar whose surrounding LAE field shows no overdensity relative to blank-field studies.

quotes.quote: To circumvent such uncertainties, we here perform a search for Lyman Alpha Emitting galaxies (LAEs) in the field of the quasar PSO J215.1512 16.0417 at z 5.73, through narrow band, deep imaging with FORS2 at the VLT. We study an area of 37 arcmin ^ 2 , i.e. 206 comoving Mpc ^ 2 at the redshift of the quasar. We find no evidence for an overdensity of LAEs in the quasar field with respect to blank field studies.

Here we present a search for LAEs in the field around the Broad Absorption Line quasar PSO J215.1512 16.0417 (hereafter PSO J215 16). It has a bolometric luminosity of 3.8 10 ^ 47 erg s ^ 1 , with 0.2 dex of uncertainty, and a black hole mass of 6.7 10 ^ 9 M _ , with an uncertainty of 0.3 dex. The redshift is z =5.732 0.007, measured from the OI emission line ( _ rest =1307 ).

gmw_00004718.name: 3C 138
gmw_00004718.overview: 3C 138 is a compact steep-spectrum quasar located at a redshift of z = 0.759 [arXiv:astro-ph/0102083, arXiv:1211.1300]. Its radio structure features a central core, an approaching polarized jet, and a weaker receding jet that exhibits superluminal motion [arXiv:astro-ph/0102083, arXiv:astro-ph/0505080].

sources.summary: The paper presents 5 GHz VLBI observations of 3C 138, identifying it as a compact steep-spectrum quasar with jet/lobe structure, a shifted self-absorbed core, and slower confirmed superluminal motion.

quotes.quote: We present the results of 5 GHz VLBI observations of a compact steep spectrum source 3C 138. The data are consistent with the western end being the location of the central activity. The observed offset between different frequencies in the central region of 3C 138 can be accounted for by a frequency dependent shift of the synchrotron self-absorbed core. Our new measurements confirm the existence of a superluminal motion, but its apparent velocity of 3.3c is three times slower than the reported one. This value is consistent with the absence of parsec-scale counter-jet emission in the inner region, but seems still too high to allow the overall counter-jet to be seen in terms of Doppler boosting of an intrinsically identical jet.

The radio source 3C 138 (= 4C 16.12 = J0521+1638) is identified with a quasar with m _v =18.84 and z =0.759 (Hewitt Burbidge ). It is a compact, powerful quasar with a well-defined turn-over in the spectrum at about 100 MHz and a steep high frequency spectrum of 0.65 (S ^ ), making it a prototype of the compact steep spectrum (CSS) radio sources (Fanti et al. ). Its spectrum remains straight up to 22 GHz, supporting that no dominant flat spectrum core exists (Kameno et al. ).

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 138 as a bright quasar target for VLBA H I absorption, emphasizing its strong optical-depth variations and continuum morphology with core, approaching-jet, and receding-jet components.

quotes.quote: We present three epochs of VLBA observations of Galactic absorption toward the quasar 3C 138 with resolutions of 20 mas ( 10 AU). This analysis includes VLBA data from observations in 1999 and 2002 along with a reexamination of the 1995 VLBA data, reported by fgdt98 .

The extragalactic source 3C 138 was selected to continue investigation into small scale structure in neutral opacities for a variety of reasons. The size and brightness of 3C 138 makes it a favorable candidate for an absorption study. In addition, among the seven sources examined by fgdt98 and fg01 , 3C 138 shows the most significant spatial variations in optical depth.

Based on Cotton97 the component just to the SW of image center is the core, while the linear structure to the northeast is the approaching part of the jet. The low surface brightness component in the SW corner of the image is the receding jet. These authors also review previous observations of 3C 138 and find that the source as a whole is not significantly variable.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C138 as a compact steep-spectrum quasar with polarized jet structure, useful for VLA gain calibration but strongly variable after a flare beginning in 2003.

quotes.quote: 3C138 is a compact steep-spectrum quasar at redshift z=0.759. VLBI images show it has structure on a maximum scale of 0.62 arcseconds, with a highly polarized, one-sided jet leading from the nucleus, and some weak structure on the other side ( Cot97a ). These structures are shown in a 60 milliarcsecond resolution VLA image at 43 GHz shown in Fig. fig:3C138Maps .

Due to its high flux density and small angular size, this source is commonly utilized by the VLA for amplitude gain calibration. 3C138 is not one of the Baa77 sources, probably because it was known to be significantly variable. Our data show that this is indeed the case, with the slow decline noted after 1983 abruptly terminated with a large increase beginning in 2003, as shown in the right-hand side of Fig. fig:3C138Maps .

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 138 as a gamma-ray CSS with a steep spectrum, compact jet/lobe radio structure, a likely nucleus component, and reported superluminal motion.

quotes.quote: 3C 138 located at z=0.759 (PKS 0518+16, Spinrad et al. 1985). It has a steep spectrum of =0.65 straight up to 22 GHz with a turn-over at about 100 MHz (Fanti et al. 1990; Shen et al. 2001). The VLBI observations at 15 and 22 GHz show that its main jet consists of several knots extending about 400 mas in a position angle of 65 ^ and a weak counter-jet about 250 mas is presented in the opposite direction (Cotton et al. 1997 and references therein). The VLBI image at 5 GHz also exhibits two notable emission regions, i.e., the core and the eastern lobe, which are separated by 400 mas at a position angle of 70 ^ , and there are several discretely lower surface brightness regions between them, but no counter-jet was significantly detected at 5 GHz (Shen et al. 2001). Due to the flattest spectral index and the highest brightness temperature, component A is suggested as a nucleus component (Shen et al. 2005).

gmw_00004719.name: 3C 216
gmw_00004719.overview: The active galactic nucleus 3C 216 is a radio-loud quasar [arXiv:2005.11535, arXiv:astro-ph/0610042] that exhibits blazar-like optical behavior [arXiv:2005.11535] and point-like gamma-ray emission [arXiv:1804.03703]. Its compact radio jet displays superluminal motion [arXiv:2005.11535] and a reliable Faraday rotation measure gradient [arXiv:1212.3423].

sources.summary: The VLBA data show 0906+430 has the clearest robust jet RM gradient, extending about 2 mas, dominated by external Faraday rotation and consistent with a helical magnetic field.

quotes.quote: The quasar 0906+430 displays a constant RM gradient that spans along the jet up to a distance of 2 mas. At a redshift of z=0.67 and its cosmology corrected scale 6.831 pc/mas, this implies a rotation measure gradient extending at least 14pc. The amount of rotation of the polarization angles that we find is greater than 90^ Algaba12 and the fractional polarization, although relatively weak ( <1 at 12 GHz), is smoothly increasing with frequency. Hence, we can estimate that the contribution of internal Faraday rotation, if any, is negligible. In turn, this indicates that the screen producing the Faraday rotation gradient must be external.

We find that the RM gradient in the jet of 0906+430 (and possibly 1633+382) is reliable. The combination of the results from the RM , the spine polarization structure and the concave shape of the degree of polarization across the jet seem to indicate the presence of a helical magnetic field in 0906+430 beyond 1 mas away from the core.

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 216 as a controversial CSS/quasar, noting superluminal motion, a small viewing angle, compact jet structure, projection effects, and blazar-like optical behavior.

quotes.quote: 3C 216 also identified with the quasar 0906+430 at z=0.67 (Smith Spinrad 1980). The VLBI observations indicate the superluminal motion of 4c and a small viewing angle of <20^ (Taylor et al. 1995 and references therein). A steep spectrum of =0.79 at low frequencies is observed, but it flattens above 5 GHz with =0.29 (Taylor et al. 1995). The VLBI Space Observatory Programme observations reveal the pc-scale structure of 3C 216 that can be well described by compact jet models (Paragi et al. 2000). Using the measured brightness temperature Paragi et al. (2000) estimated a lower-limit of the Doppler boosting factor of 3 for the core-jet with the viewing angle less than 19 ^ , and thus they concluded that the observed small projected size of 3C 216 is probably caused by both interaction and projection effects.

sources.summary: The paper says 5BZUJ 0909+4253 is strongly detected in Pass 8 data without strong flaring, and its point-like gamma-ray signature is confirmed by a TS map.

quotes.quote: 5BZUJ 0909+4253. This blazar is strongly detected when integrating over 7.5 yrs with Pass 8 data (TS ^ Pass 8 _ 7.5 yrs = 74.4), but shows no strong flaring activity as can be seen in Fig. bzu0909 . A likelihood analysis with the 3FGL setup gives TS ^ Pass 7 _ 4.0 yrs 14.2, with power-law parameters N _0 = 2.4 0.8 10 ^ 13 ph/cm ^2 /s/MeV, = 3.04 0.38, using E _0 = 1000 MeV as pivot energy, which agrees with values reported in Table detectiontab . This is another example for which it would be beneficial to have preliminary information about faint signatures with TS in between 10 to 25, which were already available with 4 yr Pass 7 data. The TS map (Fig. bzu0909 ) confirms that the observed \ signature emerges as a point-like source from a low-TS background.

sources.summary: The paper treats source 148 as an X-ray detected AGN, reporting possible factor-of-two variability over about five years and noting it is the most radio-loud Z03 AGN.

quotes.quote: Two have \ detections (one in the PSPC and the other in both the PSPC and HRI); these \ counterparts lie at 0 1 (source 148) and 8 2 (source 204) from the optical position; given their relatively large fluxes (see Table 1), we do not expect either of these sources to be spurious.

For the \ detected source 148, a variability of a factor of 2 over time-scales of 5 yr seems to be present. This is not surprising since \ variability over time-scales of several years are often observed in AGN (Almaini et al. 2000; Markowitz, Edelson Vaughan 2003); furthermore, this source is also the most radio-loud AGN in Z03 sample (see Table 1), so enhanced variability, perhaps related to a jet component, might be present.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C 216 as a convincing high jet-power quasar, with Q_Edd above 3.3 verified by independent black-hole-mass estimators.

quotes.quote: Firstly, the detailed investigation of 3C 216 in illustrates the existence of Q _ Edd >1 QSOs very convincingly. The Q _ Edd >3.3 condition in 3C 216 is verified by three independent estimators of M_ bh , F(H ), F(Mg II), and the host bulge luminosity.

However, the example of 3C 216 demonstrates that Q _ Edd >1 sources exist, indicating that the more reasonable explanation of figure 1 is that the errors in the estimates are randomly distributed and they are not skewed preferentially to produce low M_ bh .

gmw_00004720.name: 4C 15.05
gmw_00004720.overview: The active galactic nucleus 4C 15.05 is a steep-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:1502.07176] with a compact triple morphology consisting of a central core and double lobes [arXiv:1605.08488, arXiv:2005.11535]. It exhibits variability in both radio and gamma-ray bands [arXiv:1502.07176] and displays an extremely high intrinsic rotation measure [arXiv:2102.04520].

sources.summary: The paper highlights J0204.8+1514 as a gamma-associated steep-spectrum radio quasar and possible compact steep-spectrum source, with kpc-scale size, faint extended emission, variability, and a VLBA milliarcsecond core-jet.

quotes.quote: It is worth noting that the source J0204.8+1514 is the only SSRQ amongst those associated to a ray object. This source is also classified as a possible CSS in Table tab:css-gps . The angular size reported by Cooper07 for J0204.8+1514 observed with the VLA A-array at 1.4 GHz after beam deconvolution is 1.58 arcsec as major axis, 1.42 arcsec as minor axis, at a position angle of 40.5 deg. This corresponds to a linear size LS =4.7 kpc, which classifies it as CSS. However, Cooper07 also find a faint extended emission above the 3 noise level of their image.

Mantovani11 and Nolan12 found the source variable in the radio- and ray bands, respectively, which is rather peculiar in a CSS. J0204.8+1514 was included in the MOJAVE programme selected because it was classified flat spectrum source, which does not seem to be the case. VLBA observations at 15 GHz show a mas core-jet structure for J0204.8+1514.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0202 + 149 as an unusual low-power, blazar-like AGN/QSO with CSS-like radio structure, compact triple morphology, perpendicular parsec-scale jet, and recently restarted nuclear activity.

quotes.quote: PKS 0202 + 149 is a low-power radio source with blazar-like ray AGN characteristics. We investigate its properties and classification in relation to its ray characteristics. This source shows a hint of low frequency turnover at about 200 MHz. Radio imaging data of 0202 + 149 at different frequencies show differing morphologies on both kiloparsec (kpc) and parsec (pc) scales. The overall source shows a triple structure of a core and double lobes with a total projected size of 1.3 kpc.

The source PKS 0202 + 149 4C + 15.05, NRAO 91 - Romney84 , is an unusual radio source PS65,Gow67 that is identified as a QSO Stickel94 . A redshift of z = 0.833 based upon the [ O 3 ] 3727 and [ Ne I ] 3833 lines was estimated by Stickel96 , but a more recent and smaller redshift of z = 0.405 Perlman98,Hea08 was reported; the latter will be used in this paper. At intermediate resolution, PKS 0202 + 149 has a compact triple morphology extending across 200 mas (corresponding to a projected size of 1 kpc) along a northeast-southwest direction with two relatively weaker lobe components straddling a central bright core Wang03 .

sources.summary: The paper describes 4C 15.05 as a gamma-ray AGN/CSS candidate with disputed redshift, a core plus double lobes, perpendicular pc-scale jet structure, and superluminal motion.

quotes.quote: 4C 15.05 also known as NRAO 91 and PKS 0202+149. On the basis of [ O  III ] 3727 and [ Ne  I ] 3833 lines, it was estimated to be located at z=0.833 (Stickel et al. 1996), but a smaller redshift of z=0.405 was reported by Perlman et al. (1998). Recently, Jones et al. (2018) suggested that the neutral hydrogen absorption feature of this source agrees very well with the value of z=0.833 . Its mean spectral index between 400 MHz to 8 GHz is =0.33 (Herbig Readhead 1992), which is slightly steeper than that of blazars (Fan et al. 2010; Pei et al. 2019). This source displays the structure of a core and double lobes with the total projected size of 1.3 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PCCS1 G147.84-44.04/4C 15.05 as the sample's extreme rotation-measure outlier, with two high-RM epochs implying an intrinsic rest-frame RM near 2 x 10^5 rad m^-2.

quotes.quote: PCCS1 G147.84-44.04 or 4C 15.05 is an extreme outlier in RM, having RM 56000 and 64000 rad m ^ 2 in the two observation epochs; the polarization fits for the first epoch are shown in Fig. Fi:sample_pol_fits . At the redshift z=0.833 of the source our observed frequency _ obs = 34 GHz corresponds to an emitted frequency _ em = (1+z) _ obs = 62 GHz and the observed RMs correspond to an intrinsic RM of (1+z)^2 RM _ obs 2 10^5 rad m ^ 2 in the source rest-frame. To our knowledge this source has not been identified as having a particularly high RM in any other work. 2004ApJ...612..749Z and 2012AJ....144..105H both fail to measure RMs for this source which may be a consequence of the rapid depolarization down to their lower frequencies (see Fig. Fi:sample_pol_fits ). The intrinsic RM is larger than the intrinsic RM measured by 2019A&A...623A.111H for 3C 273 of 3.5 10^4 rad m ^ 2 at 62 GHz (extrapolating the measurement of 6.7 10^5 rad m ^ 2 at observed wavelength 234 GHz using RM _ int _ em ^2 ) and 6 10^4 rad m ^ 2 found for 3C 84 (extrapolating the measurement of 8.7 10^5 rad m ^ 2 at observed wavelength 230 GHz) from 2014ApJ...797...66P .

sources.summary: The MOJAVE analysis describes 0202+149 as a core-dominated, moderately high-redshift quasar with several compact jet components and assigns its core to the easternmost feature.

quotes.quote: 0202+149: This source contains several jet components within 0.5 mas of the bright eastern feature. High resolution 7 mm VLBA images by indicate a strong component 0.5 mas from the core, which corresponds to the brightest feature in our 2 cm images. Since there is little evidence of a counterjet in VLBA images published at other wavelengths, and the source is a core-dominated, moderately high-redshift quasar, we assigned the core position to the easternmost feature in the jet.

gmw_00004721.name: NGC 4441
gmw_00004721.overview: NGC 4441 is an early-type galaxy likely formed from the merger of a spiral and an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:2009.07856]. It features a centrally crossing dust lane [arXiv:1008.2095] and exhibits a low star formation rate, possibly due to its diffuse molecular gas [arXiv:1008.2095].

sources.summary: The paper revisits NGC 4441 distance estimates, describing it as a likely spiral-elliptical merger and favoring a kinematic distance consistent with Cosmicflows modeling.

quotes.quote: Verifying the Distance to NGC 4441 adopted a distance of 42.5 2.1  Mpc to NGC 4441 derived from the 2M++ peculiar velocity model carrick2015 which is inconsistent with the surface brightness fluctuation (SBF) distance of 19  Mpc from tonry2001 . NGC 4441 is likely the merger of a spiral and an elliptical galaxy manthey2008 but SBF distance measurements are best for single-age stellar populations and can be skewed by spatial variations caused by dust or recent star-formation bothun1998 which may explain the difference between the kinematic and SBF distances. The SBF distance also implies a high peculiar velocity of 1 300  for NGC 4441 so we attempt to verify the validity of the kinematic distance by comparing the 2M++ results to the peculiar velocity field derived by .

For NGC 4441, the model provides an expected distance of D= 43.5  Mpc, consistent within 2 of the 2M++ result of . In addition to constraints from peculiar velocity reconstructions, we also check if NGC 4441 belongs to a nearby galaxy group. Based on the original SBF distance from , label NGC 4441 as a field galaxy with no other group members.

sources.summary: NGC 4441 is treated as an evolved Medusa-like minor merger, with similar morphology, a crossing dust lane, lower star formation, and possibly diffuse molecular gas.

quotes.quote: Interestingly NGC 4441 appears to be an evolved version of the Medusa with similar morphology and luminosity and high resolution 1--0 observations of NGC 4441 show that it traces a centrally crossing dust lane. The star formation rate of NGC 4441 is much lower than that of the Medusa - likely due to the diffuse state of its molecular medium. In this paper we suggest that a fraction of the Medusa molecular ISM may be the diffuse - it is possible that the entire molecular gas content of NGC 4441 is in this state.
Perhaps it will be left with a remnant, feeble dust lane, a small reservoir of diffuse gas and only low level star formation, like NGC 4441.

gmw_00004722.name: NGC 4216
gmw_00004722.overview: NGC 4216 is an edge-on [arXiv:1302.6611, arXiv:1304.1279] spiral galaxy in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:1302.6611, arXiv:astro-ph/0508629] characterized by low star formation activity [arXiv:1304.1279] and extraplanar X-ray emission [arXiv:2312.04050]. It is surrounded by multiple stellar streams, loops, and plumes from ongoing dwarf satellite destruction [arXiv:1302.6611].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4216 as a barred Virgo spiral undergoing unusually heavy satellite bombardment, with multiple streams, plumes, loops, companions, and tidal features.

quotes.quote: We present an investigation into the origins of a series of interlaced narrow filamentary stellar structures, loops and plumes in the vicinity of the Virgo Cluster, edge-on spiral galaxy, NGC 4216 that were previously identified by the Blackbird Telescope. Using the deeper, higher-resolution and precisely calibrated optical CFHT/MegaCam images obtained as part of the Next Generation Virgo Cluster Survey (NGVS), we confirm the previously identified features and identify a few additional structures. The NGVS data allowed us to make a physical study of these low-surface brightness features and investigate their origin. The likely progenitors of the structures were identified as either already catalogued VCC dwarfs or newly discovered satellites caught in the act of being destroyed.

The observed complex structures, including in particular a stream apparently emanating from a satellite of a satellite, point to a high rate of ongoing dwarf destruction/accretion in the region of the Virgo Cluster where NGC 4216 is located. We discuss the age of the interactions and whether they occurred in a group that is just falling into the cluster and shows signs of so-called "pre-processing" before it gets affected by the cluster environment, or in a group which already ventured towards the central regions of Virgo Cluster. In any case, compared to the other spiral galaxies in the Virgo Cluster, but also to those located in lower density environments, NGC 4216 seems to suffer an unusually high heavy bombardment. Further studies will be needed to determine whether, given the surface brightness limit of our survey, about 29 mag arcsec ^ 2 , the number of observed streams around that galaxy is as predicted by cosmological simulations or conversely, whether the possible lack of similar structures in other galaxies poses a challenge to the merger-based model of galaxy mass assembly.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4216 as the nearby Virgo spiral hosting a likely dust-obscured radio supernova and uses optical monitoring to constrain the event.

quotes.quote: VLA121550.2+130654 ( 4 in Table 1) is a non-nuclear steep-spectrum source in the nearby galaxy NGC 4216. This source continued to brighten, then peaked during our observations, and Figure 1 shows the light curve compiled from all available data. Our modelling (e.g., Soderberg, Nakar, Kulkarni 2005a) shows that the location and light curve of this radio transient are consistent with it being a sub-relativistic Type II supernova (SN II) in NGC 4216, but they are also consistent with the relativistic ejecta resulting from an off-axis GRB.

NGC 4216 is a member of the Virgo cluster of galaxies. Assuming a distance of 15.9 Mpc to the Virgo cluster (Graham et al. 1999), the peak flux of this event was 2.7 10^ 27 erg s ^ 1 Hz ^ 1 , also typical of known radio SNe (Weiler et al. 2002). At the bottom of Figure 1 we mark the periods of time in which a bright optical SN in NGC 4216 would have been visible in the archival images we have collected ( 2.2.2 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4216 as an anemic edge-on galaxy with low H I and star formation, extremely weak thin radio emission, steep spectral index, and no detected polarization.

quotes.quote: NGC 4216 This anemic edge-on galaxies has a very low H i column density and star formation activity (H ). It shows the lowest radio contiuum surface brightness ( 100 m Jy/beam at 6 cm). The disk emission is barely detected at 20 cm and exceptionally thin at 6 cm. The spectral index is steep ( 0.9 ) everywhere in the disk. Polarized emission at 20 or 6 cm is not detected in this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4216 as one of three Virgo edge-on late-type galaxies with significant extraplanar X-ray emission and among the highest-mass galaxies in the sample.

quotes.quote: Somewhat surprisingly, only three galaxies (NGC 4192, NGC 4216 and NGC 4388) are found to have S/N > 3 , indicating significant extraplanar X-ray emission. To our knowledge, this is the first report of significant extended X-ray emission from NGC 4192 and NGC 4216, although the S/N is only moderate ( 4 ).

Unfortunately, the limited S/N of these three galaxies does not permit a meaningful X-ray spectral analysis for the extraplanar hot gas, for which we also adopt the above photon flux-to-luminosity conversion. In the case of NGC 4192, whose corona region slightly exceeds the ACIS FoV, we have assumed a spatially uniform corona surface brightness to correct for the derived X-ray hot gas corona luminosity. The optical image, the X-ray flux map, and the X-ray intensity profile along the minor axis of these three galaxies are shown in Figure fig:image .
They are in fact the three galaxies with the highest SFR, and two of them (NGC 4192 and NGC 4216) have the highest stellar mass among the Virgo sample.

gmw_00004723.name: VCC 165
gmw_00004723.overview: VCC 165 is a dwarf lenticular galaxy located at a projected distance of 19.2 kpc north of NGC 4216 [arXiv:1302.6611]. It features nearby filamentary structures, including a 6-kpc long filament that wraps around the dwarf companion [arXiv:1302.6611].

sources.summary: VCC 165 is described as a close S0 companion of NGC 4216 with nearby filamentary structures, including a 6-kpc feature wrapping around the dwarf.

quotes.quote: In the close vicinity of NGC 4216, towards the North lies VCC 165, at a projected distance of 19.2 kpc. VCC 165 is classified by as an S0 _1 galaxy.

Other filamentary structures can be seen (best on Figure dwarf ) around VCC 165, the closest companion of NGC 4216. On the displayed g-band image, a model of VCC 165 obtained with the IRAF task bmodel has been subtracted to further enhance the filaments. The most prominent one, the 6-kpc long structure labeled F4, wraps around the dwarf companion on one side, and points towards the galaxy center on the other. There is a further hint that the galaxy extends further towards the East in the direction of the spiral.

gmw_00004724.name: VCC 200
gmw_00004724.overview: VCC 200 is a nucleated early-type dwarf galaxy located 58.0 kpc from the center of NGC 4216 [arXiv:1302.6611]. It is a luminous dwarf satellite of NGC 4216, and although a broad filament points toward VCC 200, a physical connection between the two galaxies is not firmly established [arXiv:1302.6611].

sources.summary: VCC 200 is identified as a nucleated early-type dwarf companion of NGC 4216; a broad filament points toward it, but no bridge is confirmed.

quotes.quote: South-East of NGC 4216, at 58.0 kpc distance from its centre, at a similar radial velocity, lies a nucleated early type dwarf galaxy VCC 200 Cote06 .
The very broad filament F7 points in the direction of the dwarf companion VCC 200, but a physical connection between the two galaxies cannot be firmly established.

NGC 4216 has at least one relatively massive companion, the edge-on spiral NGC 4222, and two luminous dwarf satellites, VCC 165 and VCC 200 (see Table stb ). The latter two galaxies are likely not massive enough to tidally disrupt their host (though the filament F7 pointing towards VCC 200 is intriguing).

gmw_00004725.name: NGC 3799
gmw_00004725.overview: NGC 3799 is a face-on barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:2401.05490, arXiv:2307.04297] classified as a LINER [arXiv:2401.11773, arXiv:2307.04297] with weak active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:2401.05490, arXiv:2307.04297]. The galaxy is currently undergoing star formation [arXiv:2401.05490, arXiv:2307.04297] and is interacting with its neighbor, NGC 3800 [arXiv:2401.05490].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3799 as a peculiar interacting barred spiral LINER host, still forming stars, with weak AGN activity and no prior archival X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 3799 is a well-observed galaxy morphologically classified as a SB(s)b:pec devacouleurs91 galaxy. It is designated as a peculiar galaxy because it is interacting with NGC 3800. There is evidence that galaxies that have undergone recent mergers may be more likely to host a TDE prieto16, hammerstein21 . NGC 3799 is a Low Ionization Nuclear Emission Line Region (LINER) AGN Toba14 . The archival SDSS spectrum york00 shows H , Mg b 5175, \ D 5890, 5896, and \ KHG 3934, 3968, 4308, \ 8542, 8662, and \ 8807 absorption and [ ] 4959,5007, [ ] 6584, [ ] 6717, 6731, and moderate H emission.

Finally, we note that NGC 3799 has a W1-W2 color of 0.03 mag, which indicates no strong AGN activity Stern12 . In summary, Figure fig:galaxy shows NGC 3799 is still undergoing star formation and hosts weak AGN activity. We use the photometry in Table tab:host to estimate several host-galaxy parameters using the Fitting and Assessment of Synthetic Templates package ( |FAST|; kriek09 ).

sources.summary: The paper studies AT 2023clx in NGC 3799, identifying the interacting LINER host, its redshift and distance, nuclear transient location, BPT LINER classification, and host extinction.

quotes.quote: We present an extensive analysis of the optical and ultraviolet (UV) properties of AT 2023clx, the closest tidal disruption event (TDE) to date, that occurred in the nucleus of the interacting LINER galaxy, NGC 3799 ( z=0.01107 ). From several standard methods, we estimate the mass of the central supermassive black hole (SMBH) to be M _ BH 10^ 6.0 .

On 23-02-2023 the ASAS-SN collaboration reported on the Transient Name Server (TNS The TNS is the official IAU mechanism for reporting new astronomical transients https://wis-tns.weizmann.ac.il/ ) the discovery of a transient (ASASSN-23bd, IAU name: AT 2023clx) in the nucleus of the nearby galaxy NGC 3799 and noted in the remarks that it could potentially be a TDE Stanek2023 . In 26-02-2023 the transient was classified as a TDE Taguchi2023 based on a SEIMEI telescope spectrum. It is important to note that the Sherlock package https://github.com/thespacedoctor/sherlock Young2023,Smith2020 (that predicts the classification of a reported new transient by cross-matching data against a library of historical and on-going astronomical survey data e.g. nearby galaxies, known CVs, AGNs, etc) used by the Lasair https://lasair-ztf.lsst.ac.uk/ broker Smith2019 identified this transient as being within 0.5 arcseconds of the core of NGC 3799. At this separation, Sherlock would classify the source either as an NT (nuclear transient) or AGN depending on what information exists in galaxy catalogues. NGC 3799 is labelled as a potential AGN in the Milliquas catalogue Flesch2023 and hence an AGN label was applied both in the Lasair stream for the ZTF detection and in the ATLAS database.

In the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED https://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ ) and in SIMBAD ( SIMBAD https://simbad.cds.unistra.fr/ ), the host is classified as a LINER and has a spectroscopic redshift of z = 0.01107 0.00001 , reported in SDSS based on the archival host galaxy spectrum. This corresponds to a distance of 47.8 Mpc assuming a CDM cosmology with H _ 0 = 67.4 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , _ m = 0.315 and _ = 0.685 Aghanim2020 , that we will use throughout this work. We note that distances to NGC 3799 that include corrections for Virgo and Great Attractor infall as well as the Shapley supercluster, are all consistent within the errors with the above value.

sources.summary: NGC 3799 is presented as the nearby face-on spiral host of AT 2023clx, with LINER-like nuclear lines, star-forming main-sequence properties, and small AGN contribution.

quotes.quote: In this letter, we present the discovery of the faintest and closest optical TDE so far in the nearby galaxy NGC 3799 at a redshift of 0.01107, which corresponds to a distance of 47.8 Mpc.

Host-galaxy Properties NGC 3799, the host of AT 2023clx, is a well-resolved face-on spiral galaxy (see top panels of Figure LCs ). The pre-outburst SDSS spectra centered on the galaxy center have placed it in the locus of low-ionization nuclear emission-line region region (LINER) in the Baldwin-Phillips-Terlevich (BPT) diagram ( Baldwin1981,Veilleux1987 ) based on its narrow-line ratios, indicating weak active galactic nuclear (AGN) activity albeit without broad emission lines. Ramos2020 performed a spectral energy distribution (SED) analysis of a sample of 189 nearby galaxies, including NGC 3799. Their fitting considered the AGN emission and obtained a stellar mass of log (M_*/M_ ) = 9.87 0.02 and a star formation rate (SFR) of logSFR = -0.09 0.02 for NGC 3799.

Additionally, the fractional AGN contribution of NGC 3799 is indeed small ( f_ AGN 0.2 ) according to their fitting. We emphasize that the overall star formation activity does not conflict with its LINER classification in the BPT diagram since the central regions of the SF galaxies are commonly found to be quenched ( Tacchella2015,Ellison2018 ).

gmw_00004726.name: Case I
gmw_00004726.overview: Case I is an interacting galaxy at a redshift of z=3.34 that exhibits a disturbed stellar structure with apparent tails [arXiv:1206.6930]. The galaxy features asymmetric, extended Lyman-alpha emission, which is likely caused by gas falling onto the system [arXiv:1206.6930].

sources.summary: Case I is an interacting z=3.34 galaxy with disturbed stellar tails, asymmetric extended Ly emission, infalling gas, and possible ionizing-radiation escape through halo openings.

quotes.quote: Case I: radiation escaping from an interacting galaxy at z=3.34 The first case shows a "fan" of low surface brightness emission extending over about 35 kpc (proper; fig. sys1 ). The underlying galaxy is a disturbed V=27 object with apparent stellar tails sticking out on two sides. The relatively complex spectral and spatial shape of the Ly emission can be interpreted as being caused by gas falling in from the back onto the galaxy (fig. sys1_scheme ). The in-falling nature of the gas can be ascertained from the fact that the blue-shifted emission is sharply cut out on one side by the foreground damped Ly absorption from the galaxy.

The simple double humped structure of the emission line may arise when ionizing radiation directly impacts a filament of HI gas, "reflecting" part of the energy back in the form of Ly , as opposed to the more highly modified line profiles expected for Ly emerging through multiple scattering from the depth of an intact halo. The ionized photons produced by the stars in that galaxy can account for the entire observed flux in Ly if we assume that about 50 of the photons escape the halo to hit the external HI. In the absence of other possible sources of ionization this may be an actual observation of a massive escape of stellar ionizing radiation from an interacting galaxy.

gmw_00004727.name: Case II
gmw_00004727.overview: Case II is an interacting galaxy system at a redshift of 2.63 [arXiv:1206.6930]. The system features a filamentary Lyman-alpha emission structure [arXiv:1206.6930], diffuse tidal features [arXiv:1206.6930], and a young, metal-poor starburst [arXiv:1206.2680, arXiv:1206.6930].

sources.summary: The paper identifies object A as the brightest galaxy in a z=2.63 Ly-emitting halo, with blue colors, a young starburst, an older stellar population, and possible but unsupported AGN activity.

quotes.quote: The position of the Ly emitter along the slit coincides in projection with what appears to be a small group of galaxies within a few arc seconds of each other (fig. prettypics , middle and right panels). The position of a continuum trace (hard to see with the color stretch applied here, and indicated in fig. specraw by a horizontal arrow) appears offset to the south from the peak of the extended emission. It is caused mainly by an object at 03:32:38.888 -27:44:29.07 we denote as "A" in fig. prettypics , with a fainter nearby object here called "C". The Ly emission appears to stretch North (and redward) in a "finger" of emission reaching somewhat beyond the declination of object "B", suggesting that B may possibly belong to the same halo as A, and so may C.

We have fitted the GOODS-CFDS-MUSIC broad band magnitudes of object A in the ESO WFI U38 (hereafter "U"), HST ACS WFC F435W ("B"), F606W ("V"), F775W ("I"), and F850LP ("z") bands with spectral synthesis models of instantaneous starbursts and continuous star-formation included in the Starburst99 program (Leitherer et al 1999). Good fits at the observed Ly redshift were obtained for a burst with the default input parameters, plus a total stellar mass of 6.7 10^7 M_ , a metallicity of 0.020 solar, and an age of 4 10^6 yr (fig. starburst ). Continuous star formation with a star formation rate of 14 M yr ^ 1 observed at a very similar age gave a similarly good representation of the bluer band colors.

sources.summary: Case II is a z=2.63 interacting system with filamentary Ly emission, diffuse tidal features, young in situ star formation, and conditions favoring ionizing escape.

quotes.quote: Case II: filamentary structure and young, in situ star formation in a Milky-Way sized halo of interacting galaxies at z=2.63 The second object is at z=2.63, and the spectrum again shows a complex and extended, partly filamentary emission line ( ; left-most panel in fig. 2ndsys ). A second filament ("T2" central panel; mostly outside of the spectrograph slit) appears in the B-band image but not in the V-band, suggesting that its flux may also be dominated by Ly line radiation, however, with a very large equivalent width. The underlying continuum sources here again exhibit several diffuse emission features that may be of tidal origin.

In the current case we cannot discern direct evidence for escaping ionizing radiation, but the filamentary features suggest that the galaxies have interacted in ways that produced an extended stellar distribution, with possible tidal tails or stripped wakes exhibiting signs of recent star formation. The large Ly equivalent widths inferred and the fact that the stars appear to be forming in situ in a metal-poor gaseous halo suggests that these may be hot stars with enhanced yields of ionizing photons (e.g., , ). The location of the stars outside of the optically thickest regions of the individual galaxies would also favor the escape of ionizing radiation. Thus, this object meets several of the hypothetical criteria outlined above for the galactic sources of ionizing photons.

gmw_00004728.name: J1307
gmw_00004728.overview: J1307 is classified as an extreme broad-line quasar [arXiv:2010.12589]. It is surrounded by a nebula with a weak filamentary structure and a circularly symmetric component [arXiv:2010.12589].

sources.summary: The paper highlights J1307 as an extreme broad-line QSO whose nebula has weak filamentary structure, a compatible bright-QSO surface-brightness profile, and a bright Ly line.

quotes.quote: Two objects show evidence of filamentary structures ( J0854 \ and J1307 ) ( 4 and 6) , although much less extended than the spectacular examples of Borisova . J0854 \ and J1307 \ are however quite different from each other. J1307 \ is dominated by a circularly symmetric component with the filament just barely above the detection threshold.
QSO J1307 \ is perhaps the most extreme example. The lines do appear to have broad \ wings however, and the QSOs have other broad lines. Hence, these are still classified as broad-line QSOs.

Some objects (Nebulae J0859 \ and J1307 6 and 9 ) are perfectly compatible with the mean profile of the brighter QSO nebulae. QSO J0859 \ has the brightest absolute magnitude in the faint sample, see Table tab:sample . On the other hand, QSO J1307 \ has a fainter UV continuum (M _i (z=2) 26), but has one of the brightest QSO \ lines.

gmw_00004729.name: J1000+0223
gmw_00004729.overview: J1000+0223 is a faint quasar originally identified in the zCOSMOS deep survey [arXiv:2010.12589]. Deep radio observations show that the source is radio quiet, and its nebular Lyman-alpha profile matches the narrow component of the quasar profile [arXiv:2010.12589].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1000+0223 as the faint zCOSMOS quasar J1000, finds it radio quiet, and shows its nebular Ly profile matches the QSO narrow component.

quotes.quote: A single target (J1000+0223, J1000 ) is drawn from the zCOSMOS deep survey zCOSMOSa, zCOSMOSb in an attempt to probe quasars even fainter than the SDSS selection.

One source, J1000 , is covered by deeper VLA data in the COSMOS field VLA_COSMOS and is not detected. Although this quasar is the faintest within our sample, the deep data show that it is radio quiet ( R<6.7 ).

One very clear example is J1000 12 , where the QSO \ line has a narrow peak on top of a much broader component. In this case, the \ line of the nebula very clearly matches the narrow component of the QSO profile. In this extreme example the peak flux of the spectrum of the nebula is even comparable to that of the QSO.

gmw_00004730.name: J0859
gmw_00004730.overview: The quasar J0859 is surrounded by a circumgalactic nebula that exhibits a one-sided brightness distribution in its inner regions [arXiv:2010.12589]. This nebula has a surface-brightness profile compatible with brighter quasar nebulae, though it lacks a blue-shifted absorption feature seen in the central quasar itself [arXiv:2010.12589].

sources.summary: The paper notes J0859 has one-sided inner nebular brightness, an absorption mismatch between QSO and nebula, and a surface-brightness profile like brighter QSO nebulae.

quotes.quote: In the inner regions a few nebulae are brighter on one side of the QSO than the other (e.g. J0859 \ and J0148 ) 3 and 9) , this is also similar to some cases in the Borisova sample.
Nebula J0859 9 seems to show the opposite behaviour, where the QSO has an absorption feature just bluewards of the \ peak and yet the nebula shows no coincident absorption.

Some objects (Nebulae J0859 \ and J1307 6 and 9 ) are perfectly compatible with the mean profile of the brighter QSO nebulae. QSO J0859 \ has the brightest absolute magnitude in the faint sample, see Table tab:sample .

gmw_00004731.name: J0759
gmw_00004731.overview: J0759 is a quasar associated with a faint-surface-brightness nebula [arXiv:2010.12589]. This nebula is detected on only one side of the quasar and exhibits a typical line morphology [arXiv:2010.12589].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0759 as a faint-surface-brightness QSO nebula detected on one side, with typical QSO line morphology and a normal mean-profile shape.

quotes.quote: For some of the faintest surface brightness nebulae, the emission is only detected on one side of the QSO ( J0759 \ and J1000 ) ( 10 and 12) , but we cannot exclude the possibility that these are analogous to the previous case of asymmetry but rescaled to lower surface brightness and truncated by the SNR limit.
Our sample also includes QSOs with \ line morphologies more typical of bright QSOs, such as QSOs J0002 \ and J0759 2 and 10 .

Even for the much fainter nebulae (e.g J0759 \ and J1000 10 and 12 ), there is still no evidence of a departure from the shape of the mean profile of Borisova , even with QSOs which are almost 5 magnitudes fainter in UV continuum.

gmw_00004732.name: J0854
gmw_00004732.overview: The high-redshift quasar J0854 exhibits a filamentary structure [arXiv:2010.12589]. This object appears to have an intrinsically asymmetric nebular morphology [arXiv:2010.12589].

sources.summary: The paper reports that J0854 shows filamentary structure, differs from J1307, and appears to have an intrinsically asymmetric nebular morphology.

quotes.quote: Two objects show evidence of filamentary structures ( J0854 \ and J1307 ) ( 4 and 6) , although much less extended than the spectacular examples of Borisova . J0854 \ and J1307 \ are however quite different from each other. J1307 \ is dominated by a circularly symmetric component with the filament just barely above the detection threshold. This type of asymmetry could be common but it might simply fall below the detection limit in fainter nebulae. J0854 \ seems to have an intrinsically asymmetric morphology.

gmw_00004733.name: J0002
gmw_00004733.overview: The quasar J0002 exhibits a bright-quasar Lyman-alpha line morphology [arXiv:2010.12589]. Its associated nebula is redshifted by 1360 km/s relative to the quasar's Lyman-alpha peak, which is affected by multiple absorption lines [arXiv:2010.12589].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0002 as having typical bright-QSO line morphology, while its nebula is redshifted from the QSO Ly peak and its QSO line shows absorption.

quotes.quote: Our sample also includes QSOs with \ line morphologies more typical of bright QSOs, such as QSOs J0002 \ and J0759 2 and 10 .

One notable exception in our sample is Nebula J0002 which is redshifted with respect to the peak of the QSO's \ line by 1360 km/s. Note that the \ line of QSO J0002 \ is clearly affected by multiple absorption lines, which may be somehow related to this offset.

gmw_00004734.name: NGC 4485/4490
gmw_00004734.overview: NGC 4485/4490 is an interacting, low-mass binary galaxy pair consisting of the warped spiral galaxy NGC 4490 and the irregular companion NGC 4485 [arXiv:0804.1411, arXiv:1412.4796]. The system is embedded in a massive neutral hydrogen envelope [arXiv:1412.4796, arXiv:2304.13964] and exhibits enhanced star formation triggered by tidal interaction [arXiv:0804.1411, arXiv:1412.4796].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4485/4490 as a closely interacting spiral/irregular pair with tidal disruption, enhanced star formation, measured NGC 4490 mass and star-formation rate, and ROSAT source detections.

quotes.quote: NGC 4485/4490 NGC 4485 and NGC 4490 are a closely interacting pair of galaxies, variously classified as spiral or irregular systems (see Fig. fig:dss ). In NGC 4490 is classified as type SB(s)d, and the smaller companion NGC 4485 as type IB(s)m. Both galaxies show signs of tidal disruption. tully1988 gives separate distances of 7.8 Mpc (NGC 4490) and 9.3 Mpc (NGC 4485) to the two galaxies, assuming a Hubble constant value of 75 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 .

NGC 4485/90 has been studied at radio, infrared, and optical wavelengths and shows evidence for enhanced star formation viallefond1980,klein1983,thronson1989 . estimate a star formation rate in NGC 4490 of 4.7 M_ yr ^ 1 and a dynamical mass of 1.6 10^ 10 M_ within 8 kpc (the companion NGC 4485 being less massive by about a factor of 8). The system was first observed in X-rays with the ROSAT PSPC in 1991. Analyzing those data, read1997 detected four point-like sources, three in NGC 4490 and one in NGC 4485. The galaxy pair was subsequently observed three times with the ROSAT HRI in 1995 and 1996. Analyzing two of those observations, roberts2000 only found one additional point source, an apparently transient source coincident with NGC 4490. liu2005 analyzed all three ROSAT observations as part of their study of ULXs using the ROSAT HRI archive.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4490/4485 as a nearby interacting galaxy pair containing multiple ULXs, with NGC 4490 a star-forming spiral and NGC 4485 an irregular companion.

quotes.quote: Another well-known interacting galaxy is NGC4490/NGC4485 at a distance of 7.8 Mpc (Tully 1988). NGC 4490 is a late-type spiral galaxy and NGC 4485 is an irregular galaxy. Their linear sizes are 15 kpc for NGC 4490 and 5.6 kpc for NGC 4485. Radio observations show that the star formation in NGC 4490 has been ongoing with constant rate of 4.7 M_ yr ^ 1 (Clemens et al. 1999). Also NGC 4490 has a giant HI envelope that probably originated from the star formation (Clemens Alexander 2002).

(2008) searched long term variability of 38 X-ray sources in this galaxy pair using three Chandra observations. Eight of these sources were classified as ULXs in the luminosity range (0.6-3) 10^ 39 erg s ^ 1 . One of them is a transient ULX detected in a single observation (ID 4726).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4490/85 as an interacting pair with coherent dwarf satellites, a shared distance, intermediate luminosity, and a massive HI-shell aligned with group kinematics.

quotes.quote: The interacting binary system NGC 4490/85 = Arp 269 is intermediate in mass between the Milky Way/Large Magellanic Cloud and the Large/Small Magellanic Cloud binary systems. It is a system of 14 known galaxies. We estimate the total Newtonian gravitating mass of the NGC 4490/85 group to be M_T = (1.37 0.43) 10^ 12 M_ using radial velocities and projected separations of its 13 candidate members. The system of dwarf satellites in the group demonstrates signs of coherent rotation in the same direction as that of the extended HI-shell surrounding the central interacting galaxy pair.

The spiral galaxy NGC 4490 together with its neighbour, NGC 4485, form the known pair Holm 414 = Arp 269 = VV 30 = KPG 341. The galaxies NGC 4490 and NGC 4485 have, respectively, radial velocities of 623 km s ^ 1 and 517 km s ^ 1 in the Local Group rest frame. According to the Extragalactic Distance Database ( = EDD, ana2021 ), the pair is located at a distance of 8.91 Mpc, which we use in our calculations. Thus 1'=2.59 kpc at that distance.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC4485/4490 as a nearby interacting dwarf-pair benchmark with an H I bridge/envelope, disturbed morphologies, star formation in NGC4485, and a warped NGC4490.

quotes.quote: The Magellanic pair NGC4485/4490, a well-studied pair of interacting dwarfs, is connected by an HI, star forming bridge smith4490 , analogous to the HI in the Magellanic Bridge kerr, putmanstream . Due to the relative isolation of the pair from more massive galaxies karacat , the disturbed optical and \ morphologies are likely a result of the interaction between the pair. The interaction appears to have triggered a starburst (EQW(H + [ NII ] ) = 76 ; SFR = 0.14 M _ yr ^ 1 ; ) in the smaller galaxy NGC4485. Knots of star formation are observed on the side nearest the more massive dwarf NGC4490, which is in turn substantially warped. The pair is embedded in one of the largest \ envelopes known clemens98 with a total \ mass of M _ HI = 1.8 10 ^ 10 M _ hucht4490 .

Using the absolute B band magnitudes from (M _B =- 16.97 for NGC4485 and M _B =- 19.35 for NGC4490), a distance of 7.8 Mpc tully88 , and assuming a mass-to-light ratio of (M/L) _B = 1.03 bell03 , the resulting stellar masses (9.8 10 ^8 M _ and 8.6 10 ^9 M _ for NGC4485 and NGC4490 respectively) place NGC4490 slightly above the cut off for the TNT sample selection. The resulting gas fraction of f _ gas = 1.4M _ HI /(1.4M _ HI + M _* ) = 0.72 for the pair is unusually high for galaxies in this stellar mass range huang12 .

sources.summary: FAST observations show NGC 4490/85 has longer H I tidal tails, an extended gas envelope, complex interaction history, and connections to nearby dwarf companions.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a 100 kpc HI tail in the merging galaxy pair NGC 4490/85 detected by the Five-Hundred-meter Aperture Spherical radio Telescope (FAST). The tidal tails extended in both the south and north directions, and they are much longer than that reported previously based on the VLA interferometric maps. The NGC 4490/85 is surrounded by a large gas envelope, and a starburst low metallicity dwarf galaxy MAPS 1231+42 is found to be connected with the gas envelope, indicating that galaxy interaction trigged the intense star formation in it.

NGC 4485/90, also named Arp 269 ( arp1966atlas ), is a relatively isolated low-mass galaxy pair which consist of a smaller irregular galaxy NGC 4485 and a larger barred spiral galaxy NGC 4490. One remarkable feature of this system is that it is embedded in a very extended, low-density envelope of neutral hydrogen (HI). Such a feature was first noted by and . made a detailed HI study of this pair with the VLA, and found that the HI envelope is elongated and extended for a total length of about 90 kpc, and it is approximately perpendicular to the NGC 4490 disk.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC4490-4485 as a low-mass minor galaxy-binary benchmark, noting it passes the isolation criteria and that isolated analogs become roughly three times more common at z>3.

quotes.quote: For example, isolated mass analogs of the following pairs are included in each specified sample: the LMC--SMC and NGC4490-4485 NGC4490-4485 is a low mass ( M_* = 7.2 and 0.82 10^9 , respectively) galaxy binary that is 7 away from the MW Theureau2007,Pearson2018 . (low mass minor pair), the MW--LMC and M31--M33 (high mass minor pair), and the MW--M31 (high mass major pair). Note that both the NGC4490-4485 and MW--M31 pair pass all of our pair selection criteria, while the LMC--SMC, MW--LMC, and M31--M33 are non-isolated pairs within a more massive group environment. Mass analogs of the MW--M31 pair are most common today (pink solid line in the left panel of Fig. fig:pairfrac ). Given their large separation of 760 kpc, they would only be included in the <2.5 panel of Figure fig:vircut . We find that isolated mass analogs of the LMC--SMC and NGC4490-4485 are roughly three times as common at z>3 than at z=0 (green dotted line in the right panel of Fig. fig:pairfrac ).

These high z pairs may enter a more massive group halo to become satellites of a larger galaxy like the LMC--SMC, Besla2007,Patel2017a-Orbits , or if they remain isolated, may continue to merge in a manner similar to NGC4490-4485 Pearson2018 .
Isolated analogs of the LMC--SMC and NGC4490-4485 are 3 times more common for z>3 .

sources.summary: The paper studies Arp 269 as a nearby dwarfish interacting galaxy pair with young radio electrons, a radio continuum extension, an intergalactic bridge, and strong magnetic fields.

quotes.quote: We present a multiwavelength radio study of the nearby galaxy pair Arp 269 (NGC 4490 85). High sensitivity to extended structures gained by using the merged interferometric and single dish maps allowed us to reveal a previously undiscovered extension of the radio continuum emission. Its direction is significantly different from that of the neutral gas tail, suggesting that different physical processes might be involved in their creation. The population of radio-emitting electrons is generally young, signifying an ongoing, vigorous star formation -- this claim is supported by strong magnetic fields (over 20 G), similar to the ones found in much larger spiral galaxies.

Arp 269 is also interesting as it provides an unique possibility to study the issue of gas magnetisation in the eve of the interaction process. And as this galaxy pair is a dwarfish system, the merging process might be similar to that which gave rise to the current large galaxies -- agglutination of the smaller objects.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4490/85 as a closely interacting radio-bright pair whose companion-driven gas compression likely fuels intense star formation and whose continuum spectrum has been studied to 25 GHz.

quotes.quote: In the present sample, NGC 4490/85, NGC 5194/95 (M 51), and NGC 4631 (with nearby dwarf elliptical NGC 4627 and the more massive disk galaxy NGC 4652) have nearby and obviously interacting companion galaxies. These interactions are the likely cause of the intense ongoing star formation in these galaxies, with the close companions stirring up the gas and giving rise to gas compression and subsequent star formation out of the molecular gas. Radio continuum studies of NGC 4490/85, a closely interacting pair of galaxies, were reported by 1.4 GHz viallefond80 , 1.49, 4.86, 8.44, 15.2 GHz 1999MNRAS.307..481C , and by 24.5 GHz klein83a . nikiel16 published a multi-frequency radio continuum study of this system using archival VLA data and new GMRT observations at 610 MHz. The highest frequency at which NGC 4490/85 has been studied was 25 GHz klein83b .

gmw_00004735.name: GMP1990
gmw_00004735.overview: GMP1990, also known as IC 843, is a highly flattened, massive early-type galaxy [arXiv:1612.00364, arXiv:0811.2130] viewed at a nearly edge-on inclination [arXiv:0811.2130, arXiv:0710.2190]. It features a central dust lane [arXiv:0710.2190], mild diskiness in its isophotes [arXiv:0710.2190], and a mass structure that suggests little to no dark matter within three effective radii [arXiv:0709.0691, arXiv:0809.3906].

sources.summary: The paper studies IC 843 with SWIFT spectroscopy, measuring radial IMF-sensitive absorption features, strong NaI gradients, flat FeH profiles, a Salpeter-like global IMF, and less-consistent FeH-only IMF estimates.

quotes.quote: We make radial measurements of stellar initial mass function (IMF) sensitive absorption features in the two massive early-type galaxies NGC 1277 and IC 843. Using the Oxford Short Wavelength Integral Field SpecTrogaph (SWIFT), we obtain resolved measurements of the NaI0.82 and FeH0.99 indices, among others, finding both galaxies show strong gradients in NaI absorption combined with flat FeH profiles at 0.4 . We find these measurements may be explained by radial gradients in the IMF, appropriate abundance gradients in [ Na/Fe ] and [ Fe/H ] , or a combination of the two, and our data is unable to break this degeneracy.

We also use full spectral fitting to infer global properties from an integrated spectrum of each object, deriving a unimodal IMF slope consistent with Salpeter in IC 843 ( x=2.27 0.17 ) but steeper than Salpeter in NGC 1277 ( x=2.69 0.11 ), despite their similar FeH equivalent widths. Independently, we fit the strength of the FeH feature and compare to the E-MILES and CvD12 stellar population libraries, finding agreement between the models. The IMF values derived in this way are in close agreement with those from spectral fitting in NGC 1277 ( x_ CvD =2.59^ +0.25 _ 0.48 , x_ E-MILES =2.77 0.31 ), but are less consistent in IC 843, with the IMF derived from FeH alone leading to steeper slopes than when fitting the full spectrum ( x_ CvD =2.57^ +0.30 _ 0.41 , x_ E-MILES =2.72 0.25 ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies GMP1990 as one of two highly flattened Coma early-type galaxies with outstanding anisotropy and argues its near-edge-on inclination and extensive kinematic data make its model well constrained.

quotes.quote: The latter is most clearly seen in versus : two highly flattened galaxies (GMP1990 and GMP2440, 0.6 ) have 0 . One of these galaxies is likely close to edge-on, because of its high observed ellipticity ( max _ obs 0.625 , cf. the radial profile in Tho07 ) and its significant isophotal shape distortions. We expect the model of GMP1990 to be well constrained, because of the near edge-on inclination (minimal uncertainties in the deprojection) and its far-extending multi-slit kinematic data.

For three of the galaxies (GMP0756, GMP1176 and GMP1990) large ellipticities together with significantly discy/boxy isophotes indeed indicate close to edge-on inclinations (for example, GMP1176 exhibits a_4 > 10 ; Cor08 ).

sources.summary: The paper finds GMP1990 has little significant dark matter in the adopted decomposition, though its high mass-to-light ratio may mean dark matter follows the stellar light.

quotes.quote: In this context, GMP1990 is not surrounded by significant amounts of dark matter. However, before finally concluding upon dark matter in our galaxies a detailed comparison to independent estimates of the stellar mass is required (Thomas et al. 2007a, in preparation). For example, GMP1990 has a large mass-to-light ratio = 10.0 ( R_C band). If the actual stellar mass can only account for a fraction of it, then our result does not argue against dark matter in this galaxy, but merely implies that dark matter follows closely the light of the system.

sources.summary: The paper presents HST/WFPC2 photometry for GMP 1990 and describes a central dust lane, high ellipticity, and mild diskiness in its isophotes.

quotes.quote: As part of the HST Proposal 10884 (P.I. G. Wegner), the galaxies GMP 0756, GMP 1176, GMP 1990, and GMP2440 were observed with Wide Field Planetary Camera 2 (WFPC2) on board the HST on 18--24 April 2007.

A dust lane is also visible near the center of GMP 1990, causing the center coordinates to drift along the minor axis away from the dust lane and deviations from axisymmetric isophotes at the level of 2 .

GMP 0756 and GMP 1990 have also high ellipticity ( 0.65 ) but only mild ( 2 ) diskiness. GMP 2440 is slightly disky. Overall, the high ellipticities and the morphology of the dust distribution indicate that all the four objects are very nearly edge-on.

sources.summary: The paper excludes GMP1990 from its scaling analysis because its constant mass-to-light ratio or stellar-tracing dark matter makes its mass structure distinct from the sample.

quotes.quote: Secondly, we omit the E/S0 galaxy GMP1990, whose mass-to-light ratio is constant out to 3 . The galaxy either has no dark matter within this radius, or its dark matter density follows closer the stellar light profile than in any other Coma galaxy. In either case, the mass structure of this object is distinct from the rest of the sample galaxies.

gmw_00004736.name: GMP2440
gmw_00004736.overview: GMP2440 is a nearly edge-on galaxy [arXiv:0710.2190] that exhibits a slightly disky structure [arXiv:0710.2190] and a central dust lane [arXiv:0710.2190]. The galaxy has a high observed rotation rate [arXiv:0811.2130] and an orbital structure that deviates significantly from maximum-entropy models [arXiv:0811.2130].

sources.summary: The paper reports a central dust lane in GMP 2440, calibrates its R-band surface photometry, and describes the galaxy as slightly disky and nearly edge-on.

quotes.quote: A prominent dust lane present at the center of GMP 2440 was masked before performing the isophote analysis. Nevertheless deviations from axisymmetric isophotes are present at the level of 2 .
The surface photometry was calibrated in the R band by matching the profiles given by jorgensen1995 for GMP 2440 and by Paper I for the other three objects.
GMP 2440 is slightly disky. Overall, the high ellipticities and the morphology of the dust distribution indicate that all the four objects are very nearly edge-on.

sources.summary: The paper says GMP2440s intrinsic flattening mainly comes from a low model inclination and that its high rotation rate rules out maximum-entropy-like orbital models, implying unusually deviant anisotropy.

quotes.quote: For the other galaxy (GMP2440) Meh00 quote only a modest observed ellipticity _ obs = 0.33 at and the intrinsic flattening comes mostly from the low inclination of the model. Note that this galaxy is far above the isotropic rotator line in the right panel of Fig. eps_vsig (GMP2440 is the only non edge-on galaxy above the isotropic rotator line). A maximum-entropy like DF is ruled out for this galaxy, because even the maximally rotating version of the model would not allow for the high observed rotation rate. Thus, even if we would have underestimated the inclination of this system, Fig. eps_vsig shows that its orbital structure must be significantly deviant from maximum-entropy models.

gmw_00004737.name: SPT0538-50
gmw_00004737.overview: SPT0538-50 is a gravitationally lensed dusty star-forming galaxy at a redshift of z=2.783 [arXiv:1206.4550, arXiv:2403.04850]. Source-plane reconstructions indicate that the system consists of a merging galaxy pair with an extended distribution of molecular gas and star formation [arXiv:1602.00652].

sources.summary: The paper treats SPT 0538 50 as a galaxy-scale lens whose ALMA data prefer PL+MP angular structure, show light-mass disagreement, and may indicate a group-central lens environment.

quotes.quote: ALMA observations We obtained data from the ALMA archive for three gravitationally lensed dusty star-forming galaxies: SPT 0532 50 (SPT S J053250 5047.1), SPT 0538 50 (SPT S J053816 5030.8), and SDP 81 (H-ATLAS J090311.6 + 003906). These are galaxy-scale lenses with known source and lens redshifts ( Negrello:2010,Spilker:2016 ; Table table:obs ). Details of the lens systems and ALMA observations are given in Table table:obs . The observations of SPT 0532 50 and SPT 0538 50 consisted of multiple epochs in compact and extended antenna configurations: we considered only the long-baseline configurations for this experiment to maintain the higher angular resolution.

We find that gradient regularisation gives the strongest evidence for SDP 81 and SPT 0538 50, while the area-weighted gradient gives stronger evidence for SPT 0532 50. 1.5 Evidence for complex angular structure Table table:nested shows the marginalised posterior of the lens parameters and the relative evidence for the three datasets and the two different lens models tested here. The maximum a posteriori PL+MP lens model is shown in Fig. fig:lens_models for each system. The differences in the convergence between the lens models are shown in Fig. fig:kappa_diff and have convergence differences up to 5 to 10 percent at the Einstein radius. The sources are not visually different between the PL and PL+MP models, as can be seen in Fig. fig:sources . Nevertheless, the PL+MP is preferred over a PL for all three lenses, with a relative Bayes factor (difference in E ) of 28, 75, and 120 for SDP 81, SPT 0532 50 and SPT 0538 50, respectively (i.e. strong to decisive evidence in favour of more complex angular structure; Kass:1995 ).

For SPT 0532 50, the isophote ellipticity position angle is offset by about 40 deg relative to the isodensity profile at the Einstein radius, and for SPT 0538 50 this offset is around 25 deg. In all cases, the ellipticities ( 1-q ) are in good agreement at the Einstein radius. We also see that the isophotes for all three lens galaxies have some change in ellipticity and ellipticity position angle with radius, which is not accounted for in the lens model. We tested whether the position angles of the isophotes could be used to describe the mass distribution for SPT 0532 50 and SPT 0538 50, given the multipole flexibility.

sources.summary: The paper gives SPT 0538 50 a spectroscopic redshift of z=2.783, identifies multiple lensed submillimeter images around an elliptical foreground galaxy, and notes lensing-like submillimeter structure.

quotes.quote: SPT 0538 50 has a redshift of z=2.783 , derived from ionized silicon (Si iv 1400 ) emission in an optical spectrum with VLT/X-SHOOTER (284.A-5029; PI:Chapman) and carbon monoxide CO(7--6) and CO(8--7) with APEX/Z-Spec (086.A 0793 and 087.A 0815; PI: De Breuck, and 086.F 9318 and 087.F 9320; PI: Greve). Multiple source images surrounding a low-redshift elliptical galaxy are resolved in high-resolution 890 m imaging with the Submillimeter Array (SMA) (2009A-S076, PI: Marrone). The foreground lens has a measured spectroscopic redshift of z=0.443 .
Finally, in at least one other case (SPT 0538 50), we see structure in the submm emission consistent with gravitational lensing (Bothwell et al. , in prep.).

sources.summary: The paper says SPT0538-50 has lens-model magnification consistent with earlier work and cites source-plane reconstruction indicating a z=2.8 merging pair with extended molecular gas and star formation.

quotes.quote: Note that in the other two sources with lens models in Hezaveh et al. covered here (SPT0346-52 and SPT0538-50) the derived magnifications are fully consistent.

They perform a source-plane reconstruction of the distribution of molecular gas and star formation in two SPT DSFGs, SPT0538-50 and SPT0346-52 at z=2.8 and z=5.7 , respectively, both included in the present sample. In the first case, their results suggest a pair of merging galaxies with extended distribution of molecular gas, while in the second case, it shows disturbed dynamics.

gmw_00004738.name: SPT0346-52
gmw_00004738.overview: SPT0346-52 is a strongly lensed, gas-rich starburst galaxy located at a redshift of z=5.656 [arXiv:2009.00674]. It features an extremely compact star-forming region [arXiv:1911.09967] and exhibits disturbed dynamics that may indicate either a compact major merger [arXiv:1811.07800, arXiv:1905.12738] or a rotating disk [arXiv:2009.00674].

sources.summary: The paper reconstructs SPT0346-52 as a z=5.7 lensed dusty star-forming galaxy undergoing a compact major merger with two unstable components and a gas bridge.

quotes.quote: One of the most extreme DSFGs discovered by SPT, with the highest L_ FIR and _ SFR in the SPT sample, is SPT-S J034640-5204.9 (hereafter SPT0346-52). SPT0346-52 has been studied at radio, infrared, optical, and X-ray wavelengths vieira2013, weiss2013, hezaveh2013, gullberg2015,ma2015, spilker2015,ma2016, strandet2016,aravena2016,spilker2016 . SPT0346-52 is a gravitationally lensed galaxy at z=5.6559 weiss2013 , with lensing magnification = 5.6 0.1 spilker2016 .

The lensing reconstruction of SPT0346-52 reveals two separated components (see Figure fig:pv ). The centers of these components are separated by 1 \ kpc and 500 \ km s^ 1 . There is a significant decrease in emission at velocities between the center velocities of the two components, as shown in Figure fig:srcspec .
These components are in the process of merging. This merger is likely driving the intense star formation observed in SPT0346-52.

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA resolved imaging of SPT0346-52, finding compact high-J gas, velocity asymmetry consistent with merger or turbulent rotation, and a dense warm ISM.

quotes.quote: We present and study spatially resolved imaging obtained with the Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA) of multiple ^ 12 CO( J= 6 5, 8 7 and 9 8) and two H _2 O(2 _ 02 1 _ 11 and 2 _ 11 2 _ 02 ) emission lines and cold dust continuum toward the gravitationally lensed dusty star forming galaxy SPT0346-52 at z= 5.656 . Using a visibility-domain source-plane reconstruction we probe the structure and dynamics of the different components of the interstellar medium (ISM) in this galaxy down to scales of 1 kpc in the source plane.

The source shows significant velocity structure, and clear asymmetry where an elongated structure is observed in the source plane with significant variations in their reconstructed sizes. This could be attributed to a compact merger or turbulent disk rotation. The differences in velocity structure through the different line tracers, however, hint at the former scenario in agreement with previous [ CII ] line imaging results. Measurements of the CO line ratios and magnifications yield significant variations as a function of velocity, suggesting that modeling of the ISM using integrated values could be misinterpreted.
