Modeling of the ISM in SPT0346-52 based on delensed fluxes indicate a highly dense and warm medium, qualitatively similar to that observed in high redshift quasar hosts.

sources.summary: The paper observes SPT0346-52 with ALMA, constrains its gas and CO conversion through HD non-detection, and describes it as a strongly lensed, gas-rich z=5.656 starburst likely powered by star formation.

quotes.quote: In this work, we extend an additional molecular gas tracer to high-redshift studies by observing hydrogen deuteride (HD) emission in the strongly lensed z=5.656 galaxy SPT0346-52 with ALMA. While no HD(1-0) emission is detected, we are able to place an upper limit on the gas mass of M_ H_2 <6.4 10^ 11 M_ . This is used to find a limit on the L'_ CO conversion factor of _ CO <5.8 M _ (K km s ^ 1 pc ^2 ) ^ 1 .

SPT-S J034640-5204.9 (hereafter ) is a strongly lensed dusty starforming galaxy (DSFG) at z=5.656 , first studied in the ALMA survey of weis13 and viei13 . Detailed gravitational lens modeling shows that the galaxy is magnified by a factor =5.6 0.1 spil16 , while a source-plane reconstruction strongly resembles either an ongoing major merger or rotating disk ( spil15,litk19,dong19 ). The detection of a central outflow coincident with an extreme starburst rejects the merger scenario, adding additional credence to the rotator scenario jone19 .
These properties confirm that SPT0346-52 is a heavily starbursting, gas rich galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper reports a massive molecular outflow in high-redshift starburst SPT0346-52, arguing that its saturated outflow cannot regulate its compact runaway star formation.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a high-redshift, massive molecular outflow in the starburst galaxy SPT0346-52 ( z=5.656 ) via the detected absorption of high-excitation water transitions (H _2 O 4_ 2,3 4_ 1,4 and H _2 O 3_ 3,0 3_ 2,1 ) with the Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA). The host galaxy is one of the most powerful starburst galaxies at high redshift (star formation rate; SFR 3,600 M _ year ^ 1 ), with an extremely compact ( 320 pc) star formation region and a star formation rate surface density ( _ SFR 5,500 M _ year ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 ) five times higher than `maximum' (i.e. Eddington-limited) starbursts, implying a highly transient phase. The estimated outflow rate is 500 M _ year ^ 1 , which is much lower than the SFR, implying that in this extreme starburst the outflow capabilities saturate and the outflow is no longer capable of regulating star formation, resulting in a runaway process in which star formation will use up all available gas in less than 30 Myr. Finally, while previous kinematic investigations of this source revealed possible evidence for an ongoing major merger, the coincidence of the hyper-compact starburst and high-excitation water absorption indicates that this is a single starburst galaxy surrounded by a disc.

sources.summary: The paper says SPT0346-52 has lens-model magnification consistent with earlier work and cites source-plane reconstruction at z=5.7 indicating disturbed dynamics in this lensed dusty star-forming galaxy.

quotes.quote: Note that in the other two sources with lens models in Hezaveh et al. covered here (SPT0346-52 and SPT0538-50) the derived magnifications are fully consistent.

They perform a source-plane reconstruction of the distribution of molecular gas and star formation in two SPT DSFGs, SPT0538-50 and SPT0346-52 at z=2.8 and z=5.7 , respectively, both included in the present sample. In the first case, their results suggest a pair of merging galaxies with extended distribution of molecular gas, while in the second case, it shows disturbed dynamics.

gmw_00004739.name: SPT2134-50
gmw_00004739.overview: SPT2134-50 is a galaxy with a confirmed CO(1-0) emission line detected at a five-sigma significance level [arXiv:1602.00652]. The line width and strength of this CO(1-0) transition are consistent with its ALMA CO(3-2) line profile [arXiv:1602.00652].

sources.summary: The paper presents SPT2134-50s CO spectrum using broader channel averaging, reports a five-sigma detection, and confirms the CO(1-0) line width and strength agree with ALMA CO(3-2).

quotes.quote: The spectrum of SPT2134-50 is shown averaged over broader channels in Fig.1. In this case, the significance of the detection is 5 . The CO(1--0) line profile was checked against that measured in the CO(3--2) line from the ALMA spectroscopy weiss13 ; we confirm that the line width and strength of the lines are consistent.

gmw_00004740.name: PKS0034-01
gmw_00004740.overview: PKS0034-01 is a Fanaroff-Riley class I, low-excitation radio galaxy [arXiv:1903.10768]. It features a dust lane [arXiv:2211.13031], a star-forming disk [arXiv:2211.13031], and compact central emission lines with velocity dispersion enhanced along its radio axis [arXiv:1903.10768].

sources.summary: MUSE finds only slightly extended central emission lines in 3C 015, preventing a resolved gas velocity field, with velocity dispersion enhanced along the radio axis.

quotes.quote: 3C 015: FR I/LEG, 1 = 1.40 kpc. The emission lines are only slightly extended and confined within the central 3 \ ( 4 kpc). The compactness of this source prevents us from producing a well resolved gas velocity field. The velocity dispersion is larger along the radio axis, reaching 800 .

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS0034-01 as a lower-redshift source with uncertain long-wavelength lobe and core contamination, possible thermal dust emission, a dust lane, and a star-forming disk.

quotes.quote: For the remaining five objects with uncertain extended jet/lobe/hotspot contribution (PKS0034-01, PKS0039-44, PKS0409-75, PKS1938-15, PKS2250-41), the uncertainty is exclusively for the longer wavelengths > 200 . Of these, all but PKS0034-01 are at the higher redshift end of the sample ( 0.34 < z < 0.7 ).
Using this criterion, six objects show potential for non-thermal core contamination at far-IR wavelengths: PKS0034-01, PKS0038+09, PKS0442-28, PKS0625-53, PKS1602+01 and PKS1932-46.

The only exception is PKS0034-01, which is similar to the case of PKS0625-53 discussed above, with some uncertainty on whether or not it shows thermal dust emission, because of the relatively large error on its 160 flux We note that PKS0034-01 is likely to have thermal dust emission at some level -- even if not clear from its far-IR SED -- given the detection of a dust lane in HST optical images martel99 and a star-forming disk in HST UV images baldi08 . .

gmw_00004741.name: PKS0625-53
gmw_00004741.overview: PKS0625-53 is a radio galaxy located at the center of the cluster A 3391 [arXiv:astro-ph/0011455]. It is associated with the eastern component of a dumbbell galaxy pair [arXiv:astro-ph/0011455], and its radio-to-infrared spectral energy distribution is dominated by non-thermal core emission [arXiv:2211.13031].

sources.summary: The paper describes MRC 0625-536 as the bright cluster-center member of A 3391, a wide-angle-tail radio source whose X-ray emission is mostly extended thermal plasma with possible nuclear contribution.

quotes.quote: MRC 0625-536 This radio source is associated with the eastern component of a pair of galaxies (also classified as a dumbbell galaxy) and is the brightest member at the center of the cluster A 3391. The radio morphology shows a wide-angle tail structure (Morganti et al. 1999, and references therein), with a compact core of flux S _ , 4.8 GHz = 42 mJy, which contributes only 2 to the total emission. X-ray studies are presented by Otani et al. (1998) and by Gliozzi et al. (1999). The ASCA and ROSAT/PSPC data are consistent with a thermal spectrum with T 6 - 6.5 keV and 0.35 . The PSPC observations also show that the flux from the innermost 2^ is consistent with a power law spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper says PKS0625-53 has possible non-thermal core contamination and ambiguous thermal emission, with a radio-to-infrared SED otherwise dominated by core emission and a sharp mid-infrared cutoff.

quotes.quote: Using this criterion, six objects show potential for non-thermal core contamination at far-IR wavelengths: PKS0034-01, PKS0038+09, PKS0442-28, PKS0625-53, PKS1602+01 and PKS1932-46.

The other case -- PKS0625-53 -- is more ambiguous, since although its 160 flux falls below its 100 flux (possible evidence for a thermal bump), its 160 flux has relatively large uncertainties. Otherwise, the shape of the radio-to-IR SED of this object is consistent with it being dominated by non-thermal emission from the core, but with a fairly sharp cut-off at mid-IR wavelengths.

The only exception is PKS0034-01, which is similar to the case of PKS0625-53 discussed above, with some uncertainty on whether or not it shows thermal dust emission, because of the relatively large error on its 160 flux We note that PKS0034-01 is likely to have thermal dust emission at some level -- even if not clear from its far-IR SED -- given the detection of a dust lane in HST optical images martel99 and a star-forming disk in HST UV images baldi08 . .

gmw_00004742.name: PKS1934-63
gmw_00004742.overview: PKS1934-63 is a compact double radio source [arXiv:astro-ph/0301522] associated with a Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:2004.00454] in a highly disturbed double system [arXiv:1008.2683]. The host galaxy features two bright nuclei, tidal tails [arXiv:1008.2683], and a nearby companion galaxy [arXiv:2211.13031].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1934-63 as a powerful compact double with no detected radio nucleus; free-free absorption is suggested, and a molecular torus is constrained to within 4 pc.

quotes.quote: PKS 1934-63 is a powerful compact double with a separation of 158 pc. VLBI observation failed to locate a nucleus between these components even at 8 GHz (Tzioumis et al. 1999). It has been suggested that very strong free-free absorption might be responsible (Woltjer 2000). HI measurements (V eron-Cetty et al. 2000) also failed to find HI absorption in front of the radio source, except for a narrow weak feature, presumably due to an isolated cloud.

Since no nucleus has been observed at 8 GHz we infer _ ff >3 at that frequency, corresponding to _ ff >98 at 1.4 GHz. However since the dynamic range in the 8 GHz observations is not very high this condition may be a bit too strict. From Eq. (7) we then obtain 0.1 n_6+2 r_ pc < 0.66+1.1 L_ 44 The permitted parameter space for a molecular torus in PKS 1934-63 is therefore the shaded region at the bottom-right of Fig. 3b. The distance of the absorber must be lower than 4 pc.

sources.summary: The paper revisits 1934 63 with Chandra, finds only an upper limit on its iron-line equivalent width, and does not confirm the previous Compton-thick interpretation.

quotes.quote: 1934 63 and 1946 + 708 risaliti2003 reported that 1934 63 and 1946 + 708 showed Beppo-SAX spectra typical of the Compton-thick sources with N_H> 2.5 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 , strong Fe K line, and the reflection hump. They also considered Compton-thin absorbers as a secondary explanation of the data. We have revisited both sources with Chandra . We could only obtain a 3 upper limit on the line equivalent width of <0.96 keV in 1934 63 and of <7.2 keV in 1946 + 708, assuming that the lines are unresolved ( = 10 eV) and located at the rest energies, 6.3 keV and 6.9 keV, respectively (as in risaliti2003 ). Future more sensitive X-ray observations are needed to constrain the parameters of these lines.

We measure a relatively low intrinsic absorption column density in both sources ( N_H < 2 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 ) and do not confirm their Compton-thick nature (see Table tab:chandra-fit ). We conclude that either the BeppoSAX spectra have been contaminated by other X-ray sources in the field, given the 2 arcmin source extraction regions in the BeppoSAX observations, or the Compton-thin model is more appropriate explanation of the data, or that these sources belong to the AGN class that switches between the Compton thick and thin states.

sources.summary: The paper presents 1934--638 as a GPS source in a two-galaxy system, with two extended UV components and significant polarized UV emission likely including scattered AGN light.

quotes.quote: 1934--638 : A long known GPS Bolton63 . VLBI maps e.g., Tzioumis98 show two components separated by 40 mas 0.12 kpc at z = 0.183, Tadhunter93 . R band observations Jauncey86 show a system of two galaxies, separated by 3 , consistent with our observations. The GALFIT model yields two extended components with magnitudes 21.08 and 21.75 and a fainter point source with magnitude 23.81. 1934--638 shows significant polarization in the UV 3.5 , e.g., Tadhunter94,Morganti97 , with the position angle of the electric vector perpendicular to the radio axis.

Scattered AGN light probably makes a significant contribution to the UV excess in this source Tadhunter02 . Optical polarization observations also suggest an anomalous environment Tadhunter94, Morgatnti97 .

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS B1934-638 as a compact steep-spectrum source associated with a Seyfert 2 galaxy and a stable southern radio calibrator whose smooth spectrum is modulated by other field sources in calibration models.

quotes.quote: The radio source PKS B1934-638 (J2000 19h39m25.02671s -63d42m45.6255s) is a compact steep spectrum source see e.g. odea98 associated with Seyfert 2 type galaxy at a redshift of z  = 0.183 holt08 . It is heavily relied upon as a primary calibrator source for radio interferometers in the southern hemisphere as it is extremely stable ojha04 , exhibits no structure on scales larger than 40 milli-arcseconds tzioumis10 , and is effectively unpolarized (Hugo, priv. comm.), with limits: Q^ 2 + U^ 2 I^ 2 32   dB and Q^ 2 + U^ 2 + V^ 2 I^ 2 30  dB .

The underlying response to PKS B1934-638 is best seen on the right-hand panel. As expected for a point source at the phase centre, the primary calibrator source is stable in time with a spectrum that is curved in frequency, with minimum and maximum amplitudes of 13.49 and 14.49 Jy respectively. The smooth and stable response to PKS B1934-638 is modulated by the presence of the additional sources in the model.

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS B 1934-638 as a quasar/GPS source with double-lobed VLBI structure, used as PKS 1934-638 and found undetected in polarization.

quotes.quote: PKS B 1934-638 is a quasar and GPS source at redshift 0.18 while PKS B 0407-658 is a quasar and possible compact steep-spectrum (CSS) source ODea1991,Labiano2006 at a redshift of 0.96. VLBI imaging of PKS B 1934-638 shows that it has a double lobed structure Tzioumis1989 . Henceforth, we use the name "PKS 1934-638 " for PKS B 1934-638 in this work as this name is most commonly used for the source.

PKS 1934-638 : This source is undetected in polarization. The peak emission in the Faraday spectrum is near = 50.0 rad m ^ 2 with a peak |F| 0.061 ( 0.064 when the residuals are added in), which is set as the instrumental polarization level.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1934-63 as a powerful radio source whose GMOS-S morphology shows disturbed structure, companions, shells, fans, dust, tails, bridges, or multiple nuclei indicating interaction or merger activity.

quotes.quote: This compact NLRG/GPS source is hosted in a double and highly disturbed system. Our optical image (Figure pks1934_online ) shows two bright nuclei separated by 9 kpc, embedded in a common and irregular envelope. Two tidal tails extending towards the SW from the radio galaxy and to the North from its companion are detected with surface brightnesses of 23.1 and 23.4 mag arcsec ^ 2 , respectively. All the previous features were also detected by Heckman86 with the same _V values.

sources.summary: The paper says PKS1934-63 has a nearby companion requiring PACS deblending, reports GetSources extraction of its PACS fluxes, and finds its PACS-band non-thermal contamination likely negligible.

quotes.quote: One object, PKS1934-63, has a nearby companion galaxy that requires us to deblend the fluxes at PACS wavelengths (see below).
Therefore, we were able to use GetSources to extract the fluxes for PKS1934-63 from the PACS images, and the fluxes for PKS0915-11 and PKS2356-61 from the SPIRE images.

For two of the remaining three CSS/GPS (PKS0252-71 and PKS1934-63), we find that the non-thermal contamination is likely to be negligible in the PACS bands Note that, although the non-thermal contamination is likely to be negligible in PACSs band for these objects, it could be significant at longer far-IR wavelengths, but we lack the SPIRE data to test this. .

gmw_00004743.name: PKS1306-09
gmw_00004743.overview: PKS1306-09 is a radio galaxy [arXiv:2009.11175, arXiv:1008.2683] that exhibits a double nucleus and a shell structure, indicating ongoing interactions within its host galaxy group [arXiv:1008.2683]. The galaxy features jet-aligned off-nuclear line emission [arXiv:2009.11175], and its infrared spectral energy distribution is dominated by non-thermal emission [arXiv:2211.13031].

sources.summary: The paper presents new HST imaging of PKS 1306--09, interprets its off-nuclear line emission as jet-aligned outflow material, and discusses a possible asymmetric radio-source geometry.

quotes.quote: HST observations In order to determine the extents of their warm outflows, three of the sources in our sample --- PKS 0023--26, PKS 1306--09, and PKS 1549--79 --- were observed using the High Resolution Camera (HRC) or Wide Field Camera (WFC) of the Advanced Camera for Surveys (ACS) mounted on the HST. The reduction and analysis of the ACS/HRC \ imaging observations for PKS 1549--79 is presented in Batcheldor2007 , and further analysis and discussion on the relationship between the \ and radio structures in this source is presented in Oosterloo2019 . Here we present new ACS/WFC observations for PKS 0023--26 and PKS 1306--09, which were taken under HST programme GO12579 (PI J. Holt). The new HST observations are detailed in Table hst .

However, it is perhaps surprising that the brightest off nuclear emission-line region in PKS 1306--09 is apparently situated well beyond the radio source, despite its close alignment with the radio source axis suggesting a jet-cloud interaction. One possible explanation for this is that, rather than the two bright radio components detected in the VLBI observations of PKS 1306--09 being symmetrically placed on either side of the nucleus, as we have assumed, one such ``lobe'' is centred on the nucleus (i.e. the source is highly asymmetric). In that case, the radio source extent (2.2 kpc) would be similar to the \ extent (1.9 kpc). We note that find that only 30 of the total radio emission at 2.29 GHz in PKS 1306--09 is recovered in their VLBI observations.

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS1306-09 as a possible starburst candidate with marginal optical polarization, possible non-thermal MFIR contamination, and relatively cool 70/24 micron colors.

quotes.quote: PKS1306-09 . This object was discussed in tadhunter02 , and shows marginal polarization at optical wavelengths, which could be due to scattered light or a non-thermal optical continuum component. In addition, the extrapolation of the radio component in the SED (see D08) is consistent with non-thermal contamination of the thermal MFIR continuum. So far, the optical spectra are inconclusive regarding the presence of a young stellar component in this object, but it is difficult to entirely rule out the presence of such a component.
Moreover, one of the non-starburst objects whose starburst status is uncertain -- PKS1306-09 -- also has relatively cool colours (70 /24 \ = 4.7).
Incidentally, this includes PKS0347+05 and PKS1306-09 , which we discussed in sec:looking as possible starburst candidates.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1306-09 as a powerful radio source whose GMOS-S morphology shows disturbed structure, companions, shells, fans, dust, tails, bridges, or multiple nuclei indicating interaction or merger activity.

quotes.quote: Our GMOS-S image (Figure pks1306_online ) reveals a secondary nucleus for this NLRG/CSS source, which is also detected in the residuals of the model-subtracted K-band image analyzed by Inskip10 . The galaxy lies in a group of galaxies. From both the optical and NIR data, the radio galaxy is very likely undergoing interactions with other objects in the group. In addition to the double nucleus, we detect a sharply-defined shell 20 kpc to SE of the radio galaxy, of surface brightness _V =24.5 mag arcsec ^ 2 .

sources.summary: The paper says PKS1306-09s infrared SED is fully non-thermal, obscuring any thermal dust component, while Herschel PACS follows the radio-to-submillimeter power-law rather than Spitzer.

quotes.quote: However, in the case of the third object (PKS1306-09) non-thermal emission completely dominates the infrared SED, so it is not possible to clearly identify a thermal dust component. We note that the presence of a dominant non-thermal component in this source is consistent with its optical polarization properties .

PKS1306-09 - The Spitzer MIPS 70 \ flux first reported in dicken08 now appears to be high compared with the new Herschel photometry results. The Herschel PACS measurements fall on a straightforward power-law extrapolation of the radio to sub-mm SED, but the Spitzer MIPS 70 \ point falls significantly above this line.

gmw_00004744.name: ESO272 G025
gmw_00004744.overview: ESO272 G025 is a dwarf irregular galaxy with a negative tidal index, indicating that it is not experiencing any strong present-day tidal interactions [arXiv:astro-ph/0701315]. It exhibits an unusually low HI surface mass density and a low gas fraction, which suggests a past interaction may have reduced its gas content and induced its current star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0701315].

sources.summary: The paper derives a direct oxygen abundance for ESO272 G025 and notes low gas fraction, negative tidal index, unusually low HI surface density, and possible past interaction.

quotes.quote: For ESO272 G025 and ESO324 G024, direct oxygen abundances of 12 + log(O/H) = 7.76 0.09 and 7.94 0.11 are derived, respectively.

We noted above that ESO 272 G025 and ESO 383 G087 appeared to have low gas fractions for their oxygen abundances, compared to other dwarfs. Both have negative tidal indices, which indicate only that there is no strong present-day tidal interactions.

We note that ESO 272 G025 (Cen A dI) has an unusually low \ surface mass density for its tidal index ( = -1.5 , log _ HI 6). This suggests that this dwarf may have encountered a strong interaction, which has reduced its \ content and has induced present-day star formation (i.e., presence of \ regions).

gmw_00004745.name: ESO324 G024
gmw_00004745.overview: ESO324 G024 is a dwarf irregular galaxy with a direct oxygen abundance of 12 + log(O/H) = 7.94 [arXiv:astro-ph/0701315]. It is a companion galaxy to Centaurus A and is considered a likely member of the Centaurus A satellite plane [arXiv:2301.07113].

sources.summary: The paper uses ESO324-024 as a likely CASP member example: many transverse velocities keep it near the plane with aligned orbital poles, though distance uncertainties make its orbit volatile.

quotes.quote: In its base map, ESO324-024 demonstrates a relatively large number of TVs which result in orbits remaining within d_ max < 1.5 of the CASP. This is further accentuated in the least-constrained TV map -- over 50 per cent of TVs resulting in realistic orbital distances have the potential to generate 1.5 orbits.

While this is in part due to the satellite's initial proximity to Centaurus A, a notable fraction of 1.5 TVs also demonstrate orbital pole alignments of 30^ or better, fulfilling our requirements to be considered a CASP member. However, also note that distance uncertainties heavily contribute to ESO324-024's orbital volatility -- in the most-constrained map, all TVs resulting in realistic orbital distances that also ensure a 1.5 orbit additionally risk tidal disruption from a close encounter with Centaurus A.

sources.summary: ESO324 G024 receives a direct oxygen abundance in the paper, with discussion of earlier line detections, an absent abundance, and the authors higher-quality follow-up spectrum.

quotes.quote: For ESO272 G025 and ESO324 G024, direct oxygen abundances of 12 + log(O/H) = 7.76 0.09 and 7.94 0.11 are derived, respectively.
Only \ and \ were detected in ESO 324 G024, and an oxygen abundance was not derived.
For ESO 324 G024, we obtained a higher-quality spectrum compared to the observation described in , but we did not detect .

gmw_00004746.name: ESO 383 G087
gmw_00004746.overview: ESO 383 G087 is a dwarf irregular galaxy located in the Centaurus A group [arXiv:astro-ph/0701315]. It exhibits a low gas fraction relative to its oxygen abundance and has a negative tidal index, indicating that it is not undergoing any strong present-day tidal interactions [arXiv:astro-ph/0701315].

sources.summary: ESO 383 G087 is discussed as a Cen A dwarf with a detected line, derived abundances, low gas fraction for its oxygen abundance, and negative tidal index.

quotes.quote: \ was detected in ESO 383 G087, and bright-line abundances were derived for the remaining four galaxies.
The two Cen A dI's with direct abundances and low gas fractions are ESO 272 G025 and ESO 383 G087.

We noted above that ESO 272 G025 and ESO 383 G087 appeared to have low gas fractions for their oxygen abundances, compared to other dwarfs. Both have negative tidal indices, which indicate only that there is no strong present-day tidal interactions.

gmw_00004747.name: NGC 5264
gmw_00004747.overview: NGC 5264 is a dwarf galaxy that may be a dwarf spiral [arXiv:astro-ph/0301492, arXiv:astro-ph/0701315]. It exhibits an unusually high oxygen abundance for its luminosity [arXiv:astro-ph/0301492, arXiv:astro-ph/0701315] and has a low H I gas content that may result from interactions with M 83 [arXiv:astro-ph/0301492].

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 5264 has unexpectedly high oxygen and nitrogen abundances, may be a dwarf spiral, and may have low H I from interaction with M 83.

quotes.quote: NGC 5264 has a higher oxygen abundance and a nitrogen-to-oxygen ratio than expected for its galaxy luminosity. This galaxy may be an example of a dwarf spiral (e.g., schombert95 ), whose luminosity is similar to the SMC, but whose oxygen abundance is a factor of four larger than the SMC. A systematic spectroscopic survey of H II regions in NGC 5264 should show whether or not the galaxy has a radial gradient in oxygen abundance.

Compared to typical dIs, the M_ HI /L_B value for NGC 5264 is somewhat low compared to other late-type galaxies of similar luminosity. NGC 5264 is located one degree to the east of the luminous spiral NGC 5236 (M 83), where an interaction with the latter could remove H I gas from NGC 5264. Interestingly, there is in the vicinity another Cen A group member dI, UGCA 365 (ESO444 G078), which appears to be even closer in projection to M 83 (see Fig. 1 in kara02b ). There may be an interaction between M 83, UGCA 365, and NGC 5264, which could explain the comparatively lower H I content in the latter galaxy.

sources.summary: NGC 5264 is cited as having unusually high bright-line oxygen abundance for its luminosity, suggesting a more spiral-like nature that warrants better spectra.

quotes.quote: In fact, reported an unusually high (bright-line) abundance for NGC 5264 in the Cen A group. Additional high signal-to-noise spectra would be valuable in confirming this result.

Although the spectra for NGC 5264 are of lower signal-to-noise, this galaxy exhibits an unusually high oxygen abundance for its luminosity, which suggests a more spiral-like nature to the galaxy and warrants future measurements.

gmw_00004748.name: J0900
gmw_00004748.overview: J0900 is a high-redshift, gravitationally lensed galaxy [arXiv:1309.3281]. It is characterized as a ultraviolet-bright object with a low metallicity [arXiv:1309.3281].

sources.summary: J0900 was one of three new PdBI CO targets, selected for low metallicity among Herschel-detected sources; CO(3-2) was not detected, giving a 0.44 Jy line-flux upper limit.

quotes.quote: In July-October 2011 we obtained CO(3-2) maps (rest frequency of 345.998 GHz) for three additional UV-bright lensed objects with the IRAM PdBI guilloteau92 . The three targets, J0900, J1137 and J1226, were chosen to extend the parameter space of objects with molecular gas measurements. Specifically, J0900 has the lowest metallicity of the sources with reliable Herschel flux measurements, and J1137 and J1226 are at the very low and very high ends of the redshift range of the sample, respectively.
The CO(3-2) line is clearly detected in J1137, but not in J0900 and J1226. Assuming 200 \ line widths, the 3 upper limit on the line flux is 0.16 Jy \ for J1226 and 0.44 Jy \ for J0900.

gmw_00004749.name: J0901
gmw_00004749.overview: J0901 is a strongly lensed, star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 2.26 [arXiv:1009.0773, arXiv:2004.02891]. It exhibits ultraluminous infrared luminosity [arXiv:1009.0773], hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1309.3281, arXiv:2004.02891], and features a compact nuclear outflow [arXiv:2004.02891].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J090122.37+181432.3 as a lensed z=2.26 star-forming galaxy with strong PAH features, little infrared AGN contribution, and ULIRG luminosity.

quotes.quote: We present low-resolution, rest-frame 5-12 m Spitzer /IRS spectra of two lensed z 2 UV-bright star-forming galaxies, SDSS J120602.09+514229.5 and SDSS J090122.37+181432.3. Using the magnification boost from lensing, we are able to study the physical properties of these objects in greater detail than is possible for unlensed systems. In both targets, we detect strong PAH emission at 6.2, 7.7, and 11.3 m, indicating the presence of vigorous star formation. For J1206, we find a steeply rising continuum and significant [ S 4 ] emission, suggesting that a moderately hard radiation field is powering continuum emission from small dust grains. The strength of the [ S 4 ] emission also implies a sub-solar metallicity of 0.5 Z_ , confirming published rest-frame optical measurements. In J0901, the PAH lines have large rest-frame equivalent widths ( >1 m) and the continuum rises slowly with wavelength, suggesting that any AGN contribution to L_ IR is insignificant, in contrast to the implications of optical emission-line diagnostics. Using [ O 3 ] line flux as a proxy for AGN strength, we estimate that the AGN in J0901 provides only a small fraction of its mid-infrared continuum flux.
The second, SDSS J090122.37+181432.3 (hereafter J0901) is a z = 2.26 arc discovered by 2009ApJ...707..686D ; preliminary lens modelling implies a magnification 8 (A. West et al. 2010 in preparation).

Again (as in Section 3.1) exploiting the similarity to the spectrum of M82, and adopting a lens magnification of M 8 (A. West et al. 2010, in preparation), we estimate its intrinsic far-IR luminosity to be 3.8 10^ 12 L_ , placing it in the ULIRG regime.

sources.summary: The paper treats J0901+1814 as a strongly lensed z=2.259 AGN galaxy; source-plane spectra reveal a compact, marginally resolved nuclear outflow that likely cannot escape its halo.

quotes.quote: J0901 SDSS J090122.37+181432.3 (abbreviated to J0901) is a strongly lensed, triply imaged galaxy at z = 2.259, first reported by Diehl09 .

Outflow Velocity The nuclear spectrum of J0901 was extracted from the source plane datacube (which was created by applying the source plane reconstruction to each spectral channel individually), and is shown in Figure fig:j0901_nuc_fit . The \ line is significantly broader than the \ line, and our two component fitting reveals that this is because the \ emission peak is dominated by the galaxy component whereas the \ emission peak is dominated by the broader outflow component. From the FWZP of the + \ complex, we measure an outflow velocity of 650 46 . This is a factor of 2 smaller than the outflow velocities measured for K20-ID5 and COS4-11337 .

Kinematic modelling indicates that J0901 has an inclination-corrected circular velocity of 260 \ Rhoads14,Sharon19 , corresponding to an escape velocity of 780 . The outflow velocity is approximately 85 of the escape velocity, and therefore the majority of the outflowing material detected in our data is unlikely to be able to escape from the galaxy halo.

sources.summary: J0901 has a clear MAMBO detection requiring aperture scaling, an adopted primary-beam-corrected CO(3-2) flux, and a metallicity inferred from the mass-metallicity relation because of AGN contamination.

quotes.quote: For J0901, we obtained a clear MAMBO detection at the target position centered on the southern bright lensed component but both the PACS maps and IRAM-PdB CO maps clearly indicate that the 11 \ MAMBO beam is missing flux. Based on the CO and PACS 100 m maps, the ratio between the total flux of J0901 and the flux in the southern component is independently measured to be 2.4 and 2.5, respectively. We hence scale the MAMBO flux from the southern component by a factor of 2.4 to infer the total flux before further use.

J0901 sharon13 reports on both EVLA CO(1-0) and PdBI CO(3-2) observations. For consistency with the rest of the dataset, we adopt the CO(3-2) flux of 19.8 2.0 Jy , which is obtained after primary beam correction given the large angular size of the source.

For the special case of J0901, we adopt the metallicity value derived from the MZ relation even in the presence of a measured [ NII ] /H ratio as this value is likely affected by the presence of an AGN (details in J0901agn ).

gmw_00004750.name: J1137
gmw_00004750.overview: J1137 is a galaxy-scale gravitational lens system featuring a background source galaxy at a redshift of z = 1.41 [arXiv:1309.3281]. This background source, which forms a bright blue arc, has a clear CO(3-2) molecular gas detection [arXiv:1309.3281].

sources.summary: J1137 is a low-redshift-end lensed source with a clear CO(3-2) detection, measured line flux and FWHM, and a galaxy-scale lens geometry involving two source redshifts.

quotes.quote: The three targets, J0900, J1137 and J1226, were chosen to extend the parameter space of objects with molecular gas measurements. Specifically, J0900 has the lowest metallicity of the sources with reliable Herschel flux measurements, and J1137 and J1226 are at the very low and very high ends of the redshift range of the sample, respectively.

The CO(3-2) line is clearly detected in J1137, but not in J0900 and J1226. Assuming 200 \ line widths, the 3 upper limit on the line flux is 0.16 Jy \ for J1226 and 0.44 Jy \ for J0900. The measured CO(3-2) integrated line flux of J1137 is 1.16 0.12 Jy , and the line FWHM is 137 \ as determined by a gaussian fit. The integrated CO(3-2) line map and spectrum of J1137 are shown in Figure J1137spectrum .

J1137 is a galaxy-scale gravitational lens system, where a luminous red galaxy (i.e. the lens galaxy) at z = 0.45 is forming a bright blue arc of the background source (i.e. the source galaxy). Follow-up spectroscopy of the bright blue arc by kubo09 confirms a primary source galaxy redshift of z = 1.41 and shows evidence of a secondary source galaxy at z = 1.38 .

gmw_00004751.name: S4 1030+61
gmw_00004751.overview: S4 1030+61 is a variable flat-spectrum radio quasar that exhibits a stable, straight jet [arXiv:1607.05852]. This jet displays an apparent superluminal velocity and is accompanied by radio and gamma-ray flaring activity [arXiv:1607.05852].

sources.summary: The paper studies S4 1030+61 as a variable flat-spectrum radio quasar, measuring superluminal jet motion, radio/ray flare separation, polarization changes, and a stable conical jet.

quotes.quote: We present a study of the parsec-scale multi-frequency properties of the quasar S4 1030+61 during a prolonged radio and ray activity. Observations were performed within Fermi ray telescope, OVRO 40-m telescope and MOJAVE VLBA monitoring programs, covering five years from 2009. The data are supplemented by four-epoch VLBA observations at 5, 8, 15, 24, and 43 GHz, which were triggered by the bright ray flare, registered in the quasar in 2010. The S4 1030+61 jet exhibits an apparent superluminal velocity of (6.4 0.4) and does not show ejections of new components in the observed period, while decomposition of the radio light curve reveals nine prominent flares.

Combined analysis of radio and ray emission implies a spatial separation between emitting regions at these bands of about 12 pc and locates the ray emission within a parsec from the central engine. We detected changes in the value and direction of the linear polarization and the Faraday rotation measure. The value of the intrinsic brightness temperature of the core is above the equipartition state, while its value as a function of distance from the core is well approximated by the power-law. Altogether these results show that the radio flaring activity of the quasar is accompanied by injection of relativistic particles and energy losses at the jet base, while S4 1030+61 has a stable, straight jet well described by standard conical jet theories.

gmw_00004752.name: IC3443
gmw_00004752.overview: IC3443 is a dwarf elliptical galaxy classified as type dE(N) [arXiv:1612.09578]. It exhibits a diffuse appearance due to its shallow profile slope [arXiv:1612.09578].

sources.summary: The paper uses IC3443 as a dE(N) comparison galaxy, emphasizing its similar luminosity and ellipticity to VCC1627 but more diffuse appearance from a shallower profile slope.

quotes.quote: Figure compare displays a low contrast greyscale of a visually classified dwarf elliptical (IC3443, type dE(N) from Lisker 2008) side-by-side with a faint normal elliptical (VCC1627, whose profile is in Figure special ). Each galaxy was selected to be similar in ellipticity and luminosity. The diffuse appearance to IC3443 is the quality that morphologists use to definite the dE class and is obvious in the greyscale image (see also, Figure 3 from Wirth Gallagher 1984). This distinction is important, as both galaxies have similar stellar masses and the difference relates to individual galaxy's profile slope, where the more shallow profile produces a more diffuse appearance.

gmw_00004753.name: NGC 3838
gmw_00004753.overview: NGC 3838 features a faint neutral hydrogen ring that is poorly resolved along its minor axis [arXiv:1509.05236]. Data-cube modeling of this ring yields a best-fitting inclination of 66 degrees and a circular velocity of 159 kilometers per second [arXiv:1509.05236].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3838 to illustrate data-cube modeling for a faint, poorly resolved H I ring, deriving inclination, circular velocity, and uncertainty from fitted model cubes.

quotes.quote: We illustrate this method showing its application to NGC 3838. The \ in NGC 3838 is faint and not well resolved by the WSRT beam, causing a large uncertainty in the intensity-weighted velocity. Fig. 3838_nhi shows the column density map.

The ring as reported by serra2012 is not resolved along the minor axis and therefore a basic rotcur tilted-ring fit and a harmonic decomposition of the velocity field will not yield any reliable results. Using fits to the data cube, we find a best fitting inclination and circular velocity of 66 deg and 159 km s ^ 1 , respectively. We derive the uncertainty on the latter value by building a number of \ velocity fields for a range of inclination values around the best-fitting one (changing inclination in steps of 1 deg).

gmw_00004754.name: 3C 320
gmw_00004754.overview: The classical FR II radio galaxy 3C 320 is a brightest cluster galaxy located at the center of a rich, X-ray-emitting galaxy cluster [arXiv:1902.04778, arXiv:2302.01357]. Chandra observations of the system reveal a pair of X-ray cavities and weak shocks surrounding its radio bubbles [arXiv:1902.04778].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Chandra observations of the FR II radio galaxy 3C 320, detecting X-ray cavities, weak shocks, and enough cavity power to offset cooling in its cluster environment.

quotes.quote: We present results obtained from the analysis of a total of 110 ks Chandra observations of 3C 320 FR II radio galaxy, located at the centre of a cluster of galaxies at a redshift z=0.342 . A pair of X-ray cavities have been detected at an average distance of 38 kpc along the East and West directions with the cavity energy, age and total power equal to 7.7 10 ^ 59 erg, 7 10 ^7 yr and 3.5 10 ^ 44 erg s ^ 1 , respectively. The cooling luminosity within the cooling radius of 100 kpc was found to be L_ cool 8.5 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 . Comparison of these two estimates implies that the cavity power is sufficiently high to balance the radiative loss. A pair of weak shocks have also been evidenced at distances of 47 kpc and 76 kpc surrounding the radio bubbles.

3C 320 is a classical FR II radio galaxy situated at the centre of a cluster 1985PASP...97..932S at a redshift of z=0.342 , positioned at RA=15h 31m 25.38s; DEC=+35d 33m 40.46s 1996AJ....111.1945D . 3C 320 is a powerful radio galaxy and has been studied in several of the radio bands 2002AJ....124.1239H,1991ApJ...371..478M,1986A&AS...65..485R,2001MNRAS.321...37S .

sources.summary: The paper images 3C 320 as a BCG FR-II radio galaxy in an X-ray cluster, finding low-frequency scythes, filaments, complex hotspots, and jet behavior best matched by a splatter-spot model.

quotes.quote: 3C320 is a classical FR-II radio galaxy with a radio luminosity of L_ 178 =3.3 10^ 27 W Hz^ 1 , at a redshift of z=0.342 . It is identified as a brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) in the centre of a rich X-ray-emitting cluster massaro13 , where a large-scale shock surrounding the lobes was interpreted from the X-ray emission. This shock was analysed with deep 0.5-5 keV Chandra observations maha20 , while studied the large X-ray cavities associated with the radio lobes.

3C320 is known to have a complex hotspot behaviour in the eastern lobe when viewed at sub-arcsecond resolution maha20 , while the western lobe shows only one compact flat-spectrum region, consistent with the findings of that more complex hotspot structures are favoured on the counter-jet side It should be noted that a jet is also detected on the western side, albeit much fainter than the eastern side, and hence the jets are orientated close to the plane of the sky, as per the case of 3C34. . In Figure fig:3c320_spix we display the four-point spectral index map of 3C 320 between 144 MHz and 6000 MHz.

The dynamic hotspot structure in the lobes reveal that the jet termination point is fragmented into multiple closely situated regions of very compact regions of similar spectral indices, but also diffuse regions of flat spectral index in 3C 320. We argue that the dentist-drill or precessing models and the splatter-spot model best describe the jet behaviours in 3C 34 and 3C 320, respectively.

gmw_00004755.name: NGC 3108
gmw_00004755.overview: NGC 3108 is a dust-lane early-type galaxy containing a large amount of neutral hydrogen gas distributed in a large-scale disk [arXiv:0711.3232, arXiv:2305.01075]. This gas is associated with a forming stellar disk, which is composed of a nuclear bar and an extended disk component [arXiv:0711.3232].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3108 as a dust-lane early-type galaxy with an HI-linked forming stellar disk, but argues its current star formation cannot make it spiral-like.

quotes.quote: A good candidate for such a ``hermaphrodite'' galaxy is NGC 3108: a dust-lane early-type galaxy which has a large amount of HI gas distributed in a large scale disk. We present narrow band H and R-band imaging, and compare the results with the HI distribution from the literature. The emission is in two components: a nuclear bar and an extended disk component which coincides with the HI distribution. This suggests that a stellar disk is currently being formed out of the HI gas.

It is likely that the star formation rate (SFR) in NGC 3108 was higher in the past. With the current SFR, the disk in NGC 3108 will grow to be 6.2 10^9 \ in stellar mass within the next 5.5 Gyr. While this is substantial, the disk will be insignificant compared with the large bulge mass: the final stellar mass disk-to-bulge ratio will be 0.02 . NGC 3108 will fail to transform into anything resembling a spiral without a boost in the SFR and additional supply of gas.

sources.summary: The paper detects H I associated with NGC 3108, compares it with previous observations, and explains that ATCA recovered only part of its reported H I mass because of primary-beam sensitivity.

quotes.quote: In particular, we detect \ gas associated to the disk of NGC 3095, NGC 3108, ESO 435-28 , EQ J100-3124 , and - for the very first time - in two dwarf galaxies ( DwN , DwW ), and in a cloud located in the sky area between \ and its companion, NGC 3095 ( Cl ).

ATCA observations a factor 1.4 less sensitive than the one presented in this paper, at comparable resolutions, previously observed the \ in NGC 3108 Oosterloo:2002,hau:2008,serra:2008 . The same observations also discovered \ in EQ J100-3124 , referring to this source as `anonymous galaxy' A1000--31 . Our results confirm the \ mass and systemic velocity of the latter, while we recover only partially the \ mass of NGC 3108 reported by Oosterloo:2002 ( 4.6 10^9 , at the distance of 53 Mpc). This is likely due to the fact that this galaxy is 30 \ away from , at the edge of the field of view of our ATCA observations, where the sensitivity of the primary beam drops below 10 .

gmw_00004756.name: Q1623-BX502
gmw_00004756.overview: The high-redshift galaxy Q1623-BX502 is characterized by a low stellar mass [arXiv:astro-ph/0604041]. It is a young system with a relatively high E(B-V) color excess [arXiv:astro-ph/0604041].

sources.summary: The paper uses Q1623-BX502 as one of two Mdyn/Mstar galaxies with low stellar mass, young age, high E(B-V), and confidence intervals favoring young stellar populations.

quotes.quote: The first two objects, Q1623-BX502 and Q2343-BX493, have low stellar masses, young ages and relatively high values of E(B-V) .

Confidence intervals for the age-- E(B-V) fits for two of the M_ dyn M_ galaxies (Q1623-BX502 and Q2343-BX493) are shown in Figure fig:confint . These are representative of this set of objects. Young ages (and correspondingly high values of E(B-V) ) are clearly strongly favored, but there is a tail of acceptable solutions extending to higher ages.

gmw_00004757.name: Q2343-BX493
gmw_00004757.overview: Q2343-BX493 is a high-redshift galaxy characterized by a low stellar mass [arXiv:astro-ph/0604041]. It features a young stellar population and a relatively high E(B-V) color excess [arXiv:astro-ph/0604041].

sources.summary: The paper uses Q2343-BX493 as one of two Mdyn/Mstar galaxies with low stellar mass, young age, high E(B-V), and confidence intervals favoring young stellar populations.

quotes.quote: The first two objects, Q1623-BX502 and Q2343-BX493, have low stellar masses, young ages and relatively high values of E(B-V) .

Confidence intervals for the age-- E(B-V) fits for two of the M_ dyn M_ galaxies (Q1623-BX502 and Q2343-BX493) are shown in Figure fig:confint . These are representative of this set of objects. Young ages (and correspondingly high values of E(B-V) ) are clearly strongly favored, but there is a tail of acceptable solutions extending to higher ages.

gmw_00004758.name: Q1700-MD94
gmw_00004758.overview: Q1700-MD94 is a massive, active star-forming galaxy at redshift z = 2.333 [arXiv:1507.05652, arXiv:2109.01684]. It is classified as an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1507.05652, arXiv:astro-ph/0604041] and features a resolved molecular disk containing warm, dense molecular gas [arXiv:1507.05652, arXiv:2109.01684].

sources.summary: The paper presents CO observations of MD94, describing it as a massive z 2.3 main-sequence star-forming galaxy, an AGN, with a resolved molecular disk and large molecular gas mass.

quotes.quote: Employing the parametrization of the main sequence of star-forming galaxies derived for the AEGIS and COSMOS fields by WHITAKER2012 , both MD94 and BX610 are very representative of the massive end of the main sequence at their z 2.3 redshift. Their specific star formation rates are sSFR 1.8 10^ 9 and 2.1 10^ 9 yr ^ 1 respectively, which at their inferred stellar masses (M _* 1.5 10^ 11 and 1.0 10^ 11 \ respectively) places them well within the main sequence 0.3 dex scatter. Both are very active star-forming galaxies, with respective SFR 271 95 and 212 74 \ TACCONI2013 . MD94, the most massive of the pair, displays a broad H line and is classified as an AGN ERB2006 .

MD94 is clearly resolved in the observations (.f., Table tab:photometry ). The simple Gaussian fit (CASA imfit ) yields an elongated source in the N-S direction, with deconvolved FWHM diameter (after averaging the C-only and C+D robust results) of 1 3 1 0 at high significance ( 5 ).
Under these assumptions, the molecular masses inferred from CO \ for MD94 and BX610 are M_ mol 1.7 10^ 11 and 1.1 10^ 11 M _ respectively.

sources.summary: The paper studies Q1700-MD94/MD94, a massive z=2.333 star-forming main-sequence galaxy, using NOEMA molecular-line and dust observations to infer warm, dense molecular gas across its disk.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: MD94 is located at a declination of +64d 11m 24.2s

quotes.quote: The subject of study is Q1700-MD94 (hereafter MD94), a massive ( M_ =1.5 10^ 11 M_ ), star-forming ( SFR =271 M_ yr ^ 1 ) system at z=2.333 tacconi2013 located at the massive end of the main-sequence of galaxies at z 2 e.g., whitaker12 . MD94 is located at a declination of +64d 11m 24.2s, and has been previously observed in CO(1-0) bolatto2015 and CO(3-2) line emission as part of the PHIBSS survey tacconi2013 . We observed MD94 with NOEMA located in the French Alps.

Our large velocity gradient (LVG) modeling using RADEX of the CO and \ spectral line energy distributions (SLEDs) suggests the presence of relatively warm ( T_ kin =41 K), dense ( n_ H_2 =8 10^ 3 cm ^ 3 ) molecular gas, comparable to the high-excitation molecular gas component observed in main-sequence, star-forming galaxies at z 1 . The galaxy size in the CO(1-0) and CO(7-6) line emission are comparable, which suggests that the highly-excited molecular gas is distributed throughout the disk powered by intense star formation activity. To confirm this scenario will require spatially resolved observations of the CO and \ lines which can now be obtained with NOEMA upgraded capabilities.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Q1700-MD94 as an AGN with the sample's largest dynamical mass estimate, caused by a very broad emission line and excluded from later mass-ratio analysis.

quotes.quote: The two largest dynamical masses in the sample are M_ dyn =5.7 10^ 11 \ (Q1700-MD174) and M_ dyn =3.8 10^ 12 \ (Q1700-MD94); both of these objects are AGN, and their broad \ lines are therefore not a reflection of the gravitational potential.

It is potentially more interesting to consider the small but significant fraction of the objects for which M_ dyn M_ ; we focus on the 15 of the sample (11/68) for which M_ dyn /M_ >10 (one of these is the AGN Q1700-MD94, which has a dynamical mass of 3.8 10^ 12 \ due to its very broad \ line; this is clearly a problematic estimate of dynamical mass, and we exclude this object in the following discussion).

There are only two objects for which M_ dyn /M_ bar >10 ; these are the AGN Q1700-MD94 (upper right), and the galaxy Q1623-BX376, which has an anomalously large velocity dispersion that may be influenced by its complicated spatial structure ess+03 .

gmw_00004759.name: PMN J1603 4904
gmw_00004759.overview: PMN J1603 4904 is a bright, variable gamma-ray source historically classified as a low-synchrotron-peaked BL Lac object [arXiv:1510.06763, arXiv:1801.01702]. However, its symmetric, double-sided jet structure and non-blazar spectral properties suggest it is instead a young compact symmetric object [arXiv:1607.06669, arXiv:2310.10206].

sources.summary: The paper describes PMN 1603-4904 as a bright Fermi source whose symmetric, stable VLBI morphology challenges a BL Lac interpretation and favors a young radio galaxy.

quotes.quote: The radio source has been known as a calibrator source (PKS 1600 48) for Southern Hemisphere radio observations, but it attracted attention due to its association with one of the brightest, flat-spectrum ray sources detected during the first few months of Fermi /LAT monitoring 0fgl,Kovalev2009_LBAS . Since then, it is is a bright, significantly detected ray source 3FGL 1603.9--4903; 1fgl,2fgl,3fgl,1fhl,2fhl . Based on sparse optical and broadband data, the source was classified as a low-synchrotron peaked BL Lac object without known redshift Shaw2013a . Due to the high-confidence association of the known radio source with the ray object 1FGL J1603.8 4903 1fgl , it was included in the TANAMI sample.

Our VLBI data show a symmetric brightness distribution on mas-scales, which is constant over 4 years of monitoring. The brightest component in the center has the flattest spectrum and highest brightness temperature values. We find no significant proper motion.

Since PMN 1603-4904 has a hard ray spectrum, it is a likely candidate source for TeV instruments like H.E.S.S. or in future CTA, and therefore it could play an important role in investigating the high-energy properties in misaligned sources.

sources.summary: The paper says PMN J1603 4904 is cataloged as a BL Lac but has symmetric VLBI structure, unusual SED features, and X-ray evidence challenging that classification.

quotes.quote: PMN J1603 4904 has been classified as a low-synchrotron-peaked BL Lac object in the Second Fermi LAT Source Catalog (Nolan et al. 2012). The source is bright and variable in rays, in agreement with this classification. However, TANAMI VLBI observations showed a resolved and symmetric brightness distribution on milliarcsecond scales (M u ller et al. 2014). The brightest component in the center shows a flat spectrum and the highest brightness temperature.

This result is in contrast to the typical BL Lac structure, where the optically thick core component is expected to be at the bright end of a one-sided jet structure. Moreover, the SED shows a high Compton dominance and a peculiar IR excess with T = 1600 K not expected in BL Lac objects. The detection of a redshifted Iron line in the X-ray spectra collected by XMM-Newton and Suzaku suggests that PMN J1603 4904 is observed at a larger viewing angle than that expected for blazars, challenging its classification as a BL Lac object (M u ller et al. 2015).

sources.summary: The paper describes PMN J1603 4904 as a young compact symmetric AGN, detected by Fermi/LAT, classified as an LSP BL Lac object, and listed in high-energy catalogs.

quotes.quote: PMN J1603 4904 is a radio source PMN . It was recently confirmed to be a compact symmetric object from MHz data, which makes it a young or frustated AGN PMN2016r . It was detected by /LAT and classified as a low synchrotron peaked (LSP) BL Lac object 2FGL J1603.8 4904, 3FGL J1603.9 4903; Nolan2012,FGL3,LAC3 .

It is also listed in the LAT catalogs of sources detected above 10 GeV and 50 GeV 1FHL J1603.7 4903,2FHL J1603.9-4903, 3FHL J1603.8 4903 Ackermann2013,fhl2,fhl3 . It is reported as a variable source in the 3FGL catalog.

sources.summary: The paper describes 1600 489 as a TANAMI-imaged compact symmetric object with double-sided structure, gamma-ray brightness, and new S-band images showing a bright core and double-sided jet.

quotes.quote: 1600 489: First 8.4 GHz TANAMI observations of the object were published in müller14 . The source shows a double-sided structure and due to its non-blazar like spectral properties it is classified as a compact symmetric object (CSO). The source is bright in the rays abdo09 . Our new S-band images showing a double-sided jet structure and bright central core are also reminiscent of the CSO class.

gmw_00004760.name: J0146 1133
gmw_00004760.overview: J0146 1133 is a system featuring two optically detected lensed images of approximately equal brightness [arXiv:2403.19357]. These optical images are accompanied by a faint, nearby lensing galaxy and have a separation of 1.69 arcseconds [arXiv:2403.19357].

sources.summary: The paper finds optical lensed images and a nearby faint lensing galaxy for J0146 1133, but the radio separation and orientation differ from the optical system.

quotes.quote: J0146 1133 detected two lensed images in the optical, approximately equal in brightness, together with a faint lensing galaxy very close to the northern component. These lensed images have a separation of 1 69 2018MNRAS.479.5060L , with an error probably of order 0 01. Our fitting gives a separation of 2 01 0 09, a significant difference from the optical separation, but the two radio components also appear oriented at a different angle to the line joining the optical components. There is no obvious explanation for this difference, other than one of the radio components being spurious (which is possible due to its relative faintness).

gmw_00004761.name: J0203+1612
gmw_00004761.overview: J0203+1612 is classified as an unconfirmed probable gravitational lens system [arXiv:2403.19357]. Although radio emission is not detected from the potentially lensed components, a slightly extended radio source is detected at the position of the proposed lensing galaxy [arXiv:2403.19357].

sources.summary: The paper treats J0203+1612 as an unconfirmed probable lens with no radio emission from candidate lensed components but a slightly extended radio source at the proposed lensing galaxy.

quotes.quote: J0203+1612 This system is listed as a ``probable lens'' by as it does not have final spectroscopic confirmation. We are also unable to confirm it as we do not detect radio emission from either of the potentially lensed components. Instead, we detect a 46 4 Jy source, which appears slightly extended, at the position of the proposed lensing galaxy.

gmw_00004762.name: HE 0230 2130
gmw_00004762.overview: HE 0230 2130 is a quadruply lensed quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0510465] that is gravitationally lensed by two nearby galaxies [arXiv:2308.05181, arXiv:astro-ph/0408338]. This complex lensing configuration is expected to produce five quasar images, but the fifth image remains unobserved [arXiv:2308.05181, arXiv:2403.19357].

sources.summary: The paper reports new structural measurements and lens models for HE 0230-2130, noting a two-isothermal model, an unobserved fifth image, and problematic second-galaxy dispersion.

quotes.quote: For HE 0230-2130 we present newly determined effective radii (Schechter and Levinson, in preparation) and ellipticities and position angles measured with GALFIT (Peng et al. 2002) from images obtained at the Magellan Clay telescope.

The model for HE 0230-2130 as two isothermals differs from that of Pooley et al. (2007) who modeled the density of the second galaxy as a power law, = _0(/r_0)^ ' with an exponent ' = 1.65 . The latter gives magnifications that are larger than the former, by factors of 1.25 for images a, b, and and 1.7 for image d . In 7.4 we show that use of the Pooley et al. (2007) model produces negligible change in our principal result. But our double isothermal model produces an unobserved fifth image, a saddle point (Shin and Evans 2008), which is found where one would expect an image if the two lensing galaxies were to merge, adjusting the quadrupole to keep a, b and at approximately the same positions. The image should be as bright as image and is clearly not present.

Both models attribute a much smaller velocity dispersion to the second galaxy than would be warranted by its absolute magnitude. A more satisfactory model awaits measurement of mid-IR or forbidden line flux ratios, as discussed in 7.8.

sources.summary: The paper models HE0230 2130 as a rare double-galaxy lensed quasar whose expected fifth image is missing, using that anomaly to constrain the lens mass distribution.

quotes.quote: The quasar HE0230 2130 is lensed by two galaxies at similar redshifts ( z 0.003 ) into four observed images. Using modeled quasar positions from fitting the brightness of the quasar images in ground-based imaging data from the Magellan telescope, we find that lens mass models where both galaxies are each parametrized with a singular power-law (PL) profile predict five quasar images. One of the predicted images is unobserved even though it is distinctively offset from the lensing galaxies and is bright enough to be observable. This missing image gives rise to new opportunities to study the galaxies' mass distribution.

The lensed quasar PS J0630 1201 Ostrovski2018,Lemon2018 has a very similar configuration compared to HE0230 2130, but a fifth image is observed. Double galaxy lenses like HE0230 2130 and PS J0630 1201 are rare and can cause exotic lens configurations with complex critical and caustic curves Orban2008 . Understanding these systems can open up new doors to better probe the underlying lens mass distribution, or in the case of high image magnification to resolve even more distant objects than before.

sources.summary: The paper treats HE0230 2130 as a quadruply lensed quasar whose lens spectrum fits mixed S0/Sa templates at z=0.522 and is probably an early-type galaxy.

quotes.quote: HE0230 2130 : Discovered by Wisotzki et al. (1999), this is a quadruply lensed quasar with redshift z=2.162 and a maximum image separation of 2 1 . The lensing galaxy spectrum, shown in Fig. HE0230 , is well matched by an equal mix of a S0 and Sa templates, with a redshift of z=0.522 .

Following the same test as in the case of HE0047 1756, above, we find that the non-parallactic slit angle could lead to a change in slope of 30 , and as before, this would likely be in the sense of making the object redder. Between the S0 and Sa templates, the slope in the observed frame 6000-8500 range changes by 70 . Thus, observational effects could possibly play a partial role in the blue color of the lens galaxy. In terms of galaxy mass, the derived SIS velocity dispersion in this case is _ SIS 240 km s ^ 1 , which would be highly unusual for a spiral galaxy. We conclude that the lens galaxy is of early type with a blue color probably due to its being observed at a lookback time of 5 Gyr.

sources.summary: HE 0230 is one of two quasars with anomalously decreasing half-light radius versus wavelength; the paper favors unusual microlensing caustics over physical temperature-profile explanations.

quotes.quote: Quasars with half-light radii which decrease with increasing wavelength Two quasars in B11 (HE 0230 and WFI J2033) appear to have half-light radii that decrease with increasing wavelength, albeit with large uncertainties. A temperature profile which increases with radius and then abruptly decreases can in principle reproduce the sign of this trend. (Conceptually, such a temperature profile might arise if the accretion luminosity is dominated by a shock at some radius R_b , with cooling gas undergoing radial infall within R_b .) The two quasars above have maximum r_ 1/2 = -0.61 0.54 and r_ 1/2 = -0.45 0.54 , respectively, from _ min to _ max .

B11 note that the anomalous wavelength dependence of these two objects' size estimates might also be produced by unusual microlensing caustic patterns such as those found at the center of an astroid caustic. (The magnification increases away from the center of an astroid caustic, leading to larger flux anomalies for larger sources.) That explanation for these objects' half-light radii seems more likely after consideration of the difficulty in reproducing the results with actual disc temperature profiles. We predict that further observations of these two objects will not show the anomalous size estimates, as long as the time baseline between old and new observations is sufficient for the unusual microlensing caustic pattern to have moved out of our line of sight to the continuum source.

sources.summary: The paper presents HE0230-2130 as a multiple-lens system with two nearby lensing galaxies whose dark-matter distribution complicates time-delay modeling.

quotes.quote: Multiple lenses Another problem associated with modeling lensing galaxies is illustrated by the system HE0230-2130. There is not one lensing galaxy, but two, as seen in figure fig:he0230 . The second, fainter galaxy provides the quadrupole moment that makes this a 4-image system and not a 2-image system. When we see two galaxies separated by only a few kpc, we must wonder how the non-baryonic matter is distributed. Are the halos for the two galaxies distinct? Or have they merged?

sources.summary: The paper describes HE 0230 2130 as a complex non-double lens with two lensing galaxies, an expected missing fifth image, and three detected radio images.

quotes.quote: HE 0230 2130 discovered this lens system, which is one of the few objects in the sample that is not a double-image lens system. It has two lensing galaxies, and the resulting complex Fermat surface would be expected to result in five images; one is not observed, possibly due to a dark-matter subhalo 2023arXiv230805181E . We detect emission from three images, including the two bright merging images A and B. These are fitted separately, together with a third component to represent image C.

sources.summary: The paper uses VIMOS integral-field spectroscopy to study HE 0230-2130 as a lensed quasar and finds that image D is likely affected by microlensing magnification.

quotes.quote: We present VIMOS integral field observations of 27 27 \ fields of view around four lensed quasars: HE 0230-2130, RX J0911.4+0551, H 1413+117 and B 1359+154. Using the low, medium and high resolution modes, we study the quasar images and the quasar environments.

In HE 0230-2130, image D is likely to be affected by microlensing magnification; in RX J0911.4+0551, images A1 and A3 are likely to be modified by microlensing de-magnification and in 1413 at least image D is affected by microlensing.

gmw_00004763.name: SDSS J0256+0153
gmw_00004763.overview: SDSS J0256+0153 is a strong gravitational lensing system containing two lensed components and a diffuse lensing galaxy [arXiv:2403.19357]. A single radio component is detected near the brighter lensed component, which is likely emission originating from the lensing galaxy itself [arXiv:2403.19357].

sources.summary: The paper argues the lone radio component in SDSS J0256+0153 is probably from the diffuse lensing galaxy rather than the lensed quasar images.

quotes.quote: SDSS J0256+0153 Optical imaging 2016MNRAS.456.1595M shows two lensed components together with a more diffuse lensing galaxy. We clearly detect one radio component at 114 5 Jy, close to the brighter A component. However, astrometry conducted by places the lensing galaxy only 0 5 from A, with the B component being only about 0.3-0.4 magnitudes fainter than A. Since we detect no other radio component at the ratio of at least 5:1, and given the astrometric errors, it is likely that the radio detection is in fact emission from the lensing galaxy.

gmw_00004764.name: ULAS J0743+2457
gmw_00004764.overview: ULAS J0743+2457 is a gravitationally lensed system containing a quasar at a redshift of z = 2.167 [arXiv:1109.4325]. The system is resolved into two optical components [arXiv:1109.4325, arXiv:2403.19357], with a marginal radio detection coincident with the brighter component [arXiv:2403.19357].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ULAS 074352.6+245743 as a likely lensed quasar: UKIDSS shows wavelength-dependent component separation, spectra detect extended line emission, and a faint red lens galaxy appears beyond 550 nm.

quotes.quote: ULAS 074352.6+245743 contains a z =2.167 quasar, and the UKIDSS images reveal a small separation (about 1 0). The apparent separation between the fitted components is noticeably smaller in the infrared than in the optical, although some of the SDSS images are difficult to fit due to the object being barely resolved. The spectral fitting procedure has been applied to , because \ is of lower signal-to-noise and further into the blue where the seeing is worse, and \ is badly contaminated by skylines. Fitting gives a 10 detection of an extension to the line (Fig. 2) at a ratio of 6.4:1 between primary and secondary. The galaxy is detected at low signal-to-noise, and only at wavelengths of > 550 nm.

sources.summary: The paper reports only a marginal radio detection for ULAS J0743+2457, coincident with the brighter optical component in a resolved double-lens system.

quotes.quote: ULAS J0743+2457 This object is detected only marginally above the 20- Jy beam ^ 1 threshold, in an image with slightly raised noise levels due to residuals associated with nearby sources. Its position is coincident with the brighter component of an optical double lens system. Adaptive optics imaging 2016MNRAS.458....2R shows the two separate resolved components, with the much weaker one very close to the quasar.

gmw_00004765.name: SDSS J0806+2006
gmw_00004765.overview: SDSS J0806+2006 is a two-image gravitationally lensed quasar at a redshift of 1.540 [arXiv:astro-ph/0612419, arXiv:2403.19357]. The system is lensed by an elliptical galaxy at a redshift of 0.573 [arXiv:astro-ph/0612419], which exhibits chromatic flux ratio variations likely caused by quasar variability or microlensing [arXiv:1101.1917, arXiv:1206.0731].

sources.summary: The paper highlights SDSS 0806+2006 as the strongest chromatic case, with image B undetected in L despite shorter-wavelength detections and likely variability or microlensing.

quotes.quote: SDSS 0806+2006. It is striking that we do not see image B in the L' band observations ( F_B/F_A<0.164 at 3 ), especially considering that F_B/F_A>0.4 at shorter wavelengths. Changes in the flux ratio with both wavelength and time (see Table tab:existing ) suggest that quasar variability and/or microlensing are at work in this lens (but not dust extinction; 2008A&A...492L..39S ). It is not clear, though, whether either effect could explain the non-detection in L' . For z_s=1.54 the L' emission corresponds to rest-frame 1.5 m, so it presumably contains some contribution from the dusty torus, and thus ought to be less susceptible to intrinsic variations or microlensing than the shorter-wavelength bands. In any case, the wavelength dependence of the flux ratios is very interesting and should be investigated further with both modeling and follow-up observations.

sources.summary: SDSS J0806-2006 has good but lower-S/N deconvolution; its continuum ratio is consistent with being flat while microlensing affects only continuum emission.

quotes.quote: (e) SDSS J0806-2006: Deconvolution: The deconvolution is good but the spectra have a significantly lower signal to noise than for the other systems. The residual flux under the point-like images reaches up to 0.2 of the flux of the QSO image. The lensing galaxy is well deblended from the QSO images. Chromaticity: The estimate of A at wavelengths shorter than   is uncertain due to the proximity of the noisy edge of the spectrum. Despite of this, the measurements are consistent with a flat ratio B/A in the continuum from 4000 to 8000  and ML affecting only the continuum emission.

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J0806 + 2006 as a two-image lensed quasar and measures the lens redshift at 0.573 from spectra consistent with an elliptical galaxy.

quotes.quote: SDSS J0806 + 2006 : This two-image gravitationally lensed quasar (z =1.540 ) was recently discovered by Inada et al. ( ). The two quasar images are separated by 1.40 . Several absorption lines are found in the quasar spectra, such as the ( Mg II , Ca II H K and Fe II ) lines, at a redshift of =0.573 . The redshift we obtain from our spectrum is =0.573 0.001 . The spectrum (see Fig. SDSS0806_lens ) matches very well the template spectrum of an elliptical galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J0806+2006 as a two-image lensed quasar whose radio detection is likely emission from the lensing galaxy near the fainter image.

quotes.quote: SDSS J0806+2006 VLT and Keck adaptive optics imaging in the near-infrared 2008A&A...492L..39S,2021MNRAS.503.1557S shows two images of the lensed quasar, with the brighter one to the northeast, approximately 0.8 magnitudes brighter at 1.6 m than the fainter one. The lensing galaxy lies close to the fainter image. Since our radio detection is also very close to the fainter infrared image, it is likely to be a detection of radio emission from the lensing galaxy.

gmw_00004766.name: ULAS J0820+0812
gmw_00004766.overview: ULAS J0820+0812 is a strong gravitationally lensed system containing a quasar with a 2.3 arcsecond separation between its lensed images [arXiv:2403.19357]. The system exhibits a high 6:1 optical flux ratio, and its radio emission originates from the brighter lensed component [arXiv:2403.19357].

sources.summary: The paper finds ULAS J0820+0812 has a high optical flux ratio, lensing galaxy near the faint component, and radio emission from the brighter lensed image only.

quotes.quote: ULAS J0820+0812 This lens system has a 2 3 separation between the lensed images 2008MNRAS.387..741J and a high (6:1) optical flux ratio, with the fainter object just visible to the northwest in Fig. images . Higher-resolution imaging by shows the lensing galaxy closer to the faint component. The coincidence of the radio detection with the brighter component on the Pan-STARRS image strongly implies that the radio emission comes from the lensed object, with the other lensed image not detected (its expected flux density would be about 15 Jy). Fitting an extended Gaussian to the radio detection, instead of a point source model, gives an upper limit of 0 3 (2.5 kpc) on the size of the source.

gmw_00004767.name: J0941+0518
gmw_00004767.overview: J0941+0518 is a wide-separation gravitational lens system featuring a bright lensing galaxy at a redshift of 0.343 [arXiv:1706.01506, arXiv:2403.19357]. This galaxy, which is embedded within a galaxy group [arXiv:1803.07601], lenses a background quasar at a redshift of 1.54 into two distinct images [arXiv:1706.01506].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0941+0518 as a lens with a CFHT Einstein ring, discrepant velocity dispersions, group environment, and an estimated 270-day time delay.

quotes.quote: J0941+0518 This system and J1640+1045 were concurrently discovered by williams2017 . Deep CFHT data reveals an Einstein ring of the quasar host galaxy in the u , g and bands, as shown in Figure fig:J0941_cfht . We calculate the velocity dispersion of our SIE model to be 365 km s ^ 1 and compare this to the measured velocity dispersion of the galaxy from an SDSS spectrum of 313 18 km s ^ 1 . This discrepancy can be accounted for by a shallower than isothermal density profile of 1.8 see Figure 4 of auger2010 .

However this lens is embedded within a galaxy group and so close companions would suggest a steeper profile dobke2007, auger2008 . Since a quasar emission line from the closer, fainter quasar image is present in the galaxy spectrum, the SDSS velocity dispersion might not be trustworthy. A deeper spectrum and deeper optical imaging of the Einstein ring will help constrain the mass model. We calculate the time delay for this system as B lagging A by 270 days.

sources.summary: The paper confirms SDSS J0941+0518 as a quasar lens with two images around a lens galaxy, source and lens redshifts, and an SIE model tied to the lens light.

quotes.quote: Images show two point sources separated by 5.46 ^ on either side of the lens galaxy. The spectra (Figure fig:spectra , top panel) are consistent with coming from the same object at redshift z_s = 1.54 , as identified by the C III ] and Mg II emission lines. The extracted galaxy spectrum with the Ca H and K, G-band, Mg b, and Na D lines give z_l = 0.343 . These values agree with the SDSS fiber spectra which give z_s = 1.55 and z_l = 0.343 .

The SIE model fit infers an elliptical lensing object with ellipticity e = 0.37 0.01 and position angle _e = -28.2 0.9 degrees, measured East of North. The inferred mass centroid from the model agrees with the light centroid from the images. The resulting critical lines and caustics are plotted on the images in Figure fig:images along with the inferred source position.

sources.summary: The paper describes J0941+0518 as a wide-separation lens with a bright lensing galaxy, two VLA-detected lensed images, and a relatively flat in-band radio spectral index.

quotes.quote: J0941+0518 Optical imaging reveals a wide-separation (5 4) lens system with a relatively bright lensing galaxy. In the VLA image, we clearly detect both lensed images and do not detect the lensing galaxy. The in-band spectral index, derived from splitting the band in two, is relatively flat (Section subsection:spectral ).

gmw_00004768.name: SDSS J0946+1835
gmw_00004768.overview: SDSS J0946+1835 is a strongly lensed quasar system containing two optical and near-infrared condensations [arXiv:2403.19357]. The northern condensation contains image A of the lensed quasar, while the southern condensation contains image B and the lens galaxy [arXiv:2403.19357].

sources.summary: The paper detects only image A of SDSS J0946+1835 in the radio, implying a larger radio than near-infrared flux ratio and possible microlensing or variability.

quotes.quote: SDSS J0946+1835 Optical/NIR imaging of this system 2016MNRAS.458....2R shows two condensations, the northern one containing image A of the lensed quasar, and the southern one containing image B and the lens galaxy, with image B being 0.47 magnitudes fainter in K^ (2.1 m). We detect image A at 33 4 Jy, but even a detection limit of 20 Jy in the VLA images implies a larger A/B flux ratio in the radio than the near-infrared. Possible explanations include microlensing at the shorter wavelengths, or variability coupled with time delay effects.

gmw_00004769.name: SDSS J1254+1857
gmw_00004769.overview: SDSS J1254+1857 is a strong gravitational lensing system containing a quasar [arXiv:2403.19357]. Mapping this object is difficult due to a nearby bright source, and its single detected radio component may be the lensing galaxy [arXiv:2403.19357].

sources.summary: The paper says SDSS J1254+1857 is hard to map because of a nearby bright source, and its detected radio component may be the lensing galaxy.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1254+1857 Mapping this object is relatively difficult because of the presence of a brighter, 75-mJy source about 2 arcmin away. Although at least one radio component is detected, the noise is high and it is difficult to evaluate whether a second is present. The detected component may be the lensing galaxy since the quasar images differ by only 0.24 magnitude in flux 2016MNRAS.456.1595M .

gmw_00004770.name: SDSS J1304+2001
gmw_00004770.overview: SDSS J1304+2001 is a strong gravitational lens system featuring a lensed quasar with a source redshift of 2.175 [arXiv:0912.1462, arXiv:2403.19357]. The system includes two associated, red galaxies, G1 and G2, both of which have been detected as radio sources [arXiv:0912.1462, arXiv:2403.19357].

sources.summary: The paper separates SDSS J1304+2001 into two condensations, detecting radio emission from nearby galaxy G2 and likely from lensing galaxy G1 between the quasar images.
