quotes.quote: SDSS J1304+2001 There are two optical condensations detected in optical imaging associated with the discovery paper for this lens system 2010AJ....139.1614K , conducted with the University of Hawaii 2.2-m and Subaru telescopes. The southern condensation consists of a nearby galaxy G2, which is detected as a radio source here. The northern condensation consists of two quasar images and a galaxy G1.

G1 lies between the lensed images and is about 3 5 from G2. We detect the presence of a faint radio component between the two quasar images, which is likely to be the galaxy G1. There is a further possible radio component to the north of this, and slightly to the west of the fainter quasar image, whose presence we are unable to explain via lensing .

sources.summary: The paper models SDSS J1304+2001 with two PSFs and two Sersic galaxies, finding similarly red G1 and G2, a measured separation and redshift, and statistical-sample exclusion.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1304+2001. We fit the images with two PSFs (A and B) and two S ersic profiles (G1 and G2), and the best-fit S ersic indices are 2.9 and 6.0, respectively. Galaxy G1 lies between the two quasars and galaxy G2 is located about 3 5 to the south of G1. G1 and G2 have similar, red colors ( R-I=0.7 1.0 and V-R=0.8 0.9 ), suggesting that they are probably associated with each other. The image separation is =1 865 0 004 and the source redshift is z_s=2.175 0.002 .

gmw_00004771.name: SDSS J1320+1644
gmw_00004771.overview: SDSS J1320+1644 is a large-separation double-image gravitationally lensed quasar candidate [arXiv:1505.00502, arXiv:2403.19357]. The system features two primary lensing galaxies [arXiv:2403.19357] and exhibits a severe discrepancy between its optical and radio flux ratios [arXiv:2403.19357].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J1320+1644 as a probable large-separation lensed quasar with lensing galaxies embedded in a dark halo, then compares reproduced glafic-based lens models.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1320+1644 SDSSJ1320+1644 was initially described by and , and is a large separation lensed quasar candidate identified in the SDSS, with a separation of 8 .585 0 .002 at z_ source =1.487 Rusu2013 . Both an elliptical and disk-like galaxy were identified almost symmetrically between the quasars at redshift z_ lens =0.899. A detailed lens model analysis of this system was conducted by , using glafic software. Based on their analysis, they conclude that SDSSJ1320+1644 is a probable gravitationally lensed quasar, and if it is, this would be the largest separation two-imaged lensed quasar known. They show that the gravitational lens hypothesis implies that the galaxies are not isolated, but are embedded in a dark matter halo, using an NFW model and an SIS model.

We use the 'SIS free' model as the basis of the comparison study, as defined by , which models the three galaxies (referred to as G1, G2 and G4) as SIS potentials and leaves the position of the dark matter halo (also modeled as a SIS) as a free parameter. The model used by Rusu includes priors for the velocity dispersion of the dark matter halo ( = [ 400,800 ] ). The same priors were used in the models of SDSS J1320+1644 in this study.

sources.summary: The paper highlights SDSS J1320+1644 as a large-separation double lens with undetected primary lensing galaxies and a severe optical-radio flux-ratio discrepancy likely involving microlensing or extinction.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1320+1644 This object is a large-separation (8 6) double-image lens system 2013ApJ...765..139R . There are two primary lensing galaxies, situated either side of the line between the lensed components; neither are detected in the radio. The noteworthy feature of this system is the extreme discrepancy in the flux ratio between the two lensed images; the western image is denoted as A by as it is generally brighter in the optical, typically by 0.3--0.4 magnitudes.

However, the eastern image is a factor of 5.6 times brighter in the radio, with the western image barely detectable. Such a discrepancy requires either extreme variability or, more likely, a significant change in the optical fluxes by the presence of microlensing or extinction. This is discussed further in Section section:discussion .

gmw_00004772.name: ULAS J1405+0959
gmw_00004772.overview: ULAS J1405+0959 is a background quasar system that exhibits two lensed radio components [arXiv:2403.19357]. An additional radio component is detected to the east, coinciding with a very red nearby galaxy candidate [arXiv:2403.19357].

sources.summary: The paper detects two lensed radio components in ULAS J1405+0959 plus an extra radio component coincident with a very red nearby galaxy candidate.

quotes.quote: ULAS J1405+0959 We detect two radio components in this system, which are almost certainly lensed images of the background quasar. We also detect an extra radio component, to the east of the main north-south axis of the lensed images. This coincides 2012MNRAS.419.2014J with a very red object seen in the data from the UKIRT Infrared Deep Sky Survey (UKIDSS, 2007MNRAS.379.1599L ), which is probably a nearby galaxy, the lensing galaxy being close to one of the lensed images 2014AJ....147..153I .

gmw_00004773.name: SDSS J1458 0202
gmw_00004773.overview: SDSS J1458 0202 is a doubly imaged lensed quasar system with an image separation of 2.1 arcseconds along a northeast-southwest axis [arXiv:2403.19357]. The diffuse lensing galaxy is positioned between the lensed images, and the system has no detected radio flux [arXiv:2403.19357].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J1458 0202 as a doubly imaged quasar with a diffuse lensing galaxy dominating the Pan-STARRS map and no detected radio flux.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1458 0202 This lens system was identified by as a doubly-imaged quasar with separation 2 1 along a NE-SW axis. The lensing galaxy is in between the lensed images, and is diffuse and dominates the Pan-STARRS map. We do not detect any radio flux from this object.

gmw_00004774.name: SDSS J1620+1203
gmw_00004774.overview: SDSS J1620+1203 is a strong gravitational lens system containing a lensing galaxy and lensed quasar images [arXiv:2403.19357]. A single detected radio component is located close to the fainter optical image and likely originates from the lensing galaxy itself [arXiv:2403.19357].

sources.summary: The paper argues the single radio component in SDSS J1620+1203 likely comes from the lensing galaxy near the fainter optical image, not the quasar images.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1620+1203 discovered this lens system and detected the lensing galaxy close to the fainter (southeastern) optical image. The single radio component detected in these observations is also very close to the fainter optical image and is, therefore, likely to originate in the lensing galaxy, leaving the lensed quasar images undetected. The alternative explanation, that there is an extreme difference in flux ratio between the optical and radio, appears less likely.

gmw_00004775.name: PS J2305+3714
gmw_00004775.overview: PS J2305+3714 is a strong gravitationally lensed system [arXiv:2403.19357]. It features a radio condensation coincident with its brighter optical lensed image, as well as a marginal second radio component that may be associated with the lensing galaxy [arXiv:2403.19357].

sources.summary: The paper detects a radio condensation coincident with the brighter lensed image in PS J2305+3714 and a marginal second component that may be the lensing galaxy.

quotes.quote: PS J2305+3714 We detect two radio condensations in this system, one of which is coincident with the brighter optical lensed image. Given the 1 mag difference in the optical fluxes of the images 2018MNRAS.479.5060L , we would not expect to detect the fainter optical image. We also marginally detect a second radio component, which may be associated with the lensing galaxy given the coincidence with the position from , or may be spurious since it is very close to our detection threshold of 20 Jy beam ^ 1 .

gmw_00004776.name: ULAS J2343 0050
gmw_00004776.overview: ULAS J2343 0050 is a double-image strong gravitational lens system with an image separation of 1.4 arcseconds [arXiv:2403.19357]. The lens galaxy is located closer to the brighter western optical and near-infrared image, while the single radio detection corresponds to the eastern component [arXiv:2403.19357].

sources.summary: The paper reports ULAS J2343 0050 as a double-image lens with the lens galaxy near the brighter western image and a radio flux ratio unlike the optical ratio.

quotes.quote: ULAS J2343 0050 This double-image lens system 2008MNRAS.387..741J has an image separation of 1 4, with the brighter optical/NIR image (by 0.2 magnitudes in R and 0.6 in g ) at the western end. The lens galaxy is closer to the brighter, western image. Here we have a single radio detection, which is likely to correspond to the eastern component, with possibly a small hint of some radio flux further west. The radio flux ratio, with the detected radio component a factor of 2 above the detection threshold, is therefore significantly different from the optical flux ratio.

gmw_00004777.name: VII Zw 466
gmw_00004777.overview: VII Zw 466 is a collisional ring galaxy [arXiv:1611.08968] that features an outer ring of H II regions and is interacting with a likely spiral intruder to its south [arXiv:astro-ph/9912210]. The galaxy exhibits a high star formation rate despite a molecular gas deficiency [arXiv:1611.08968], with its infrared emission loosely following the distribution of optical emission-line sites [arXiv:astro-ph/9912210].

sources.summary: The paper reports CO observations of VII Zw466, finding a collisional ring galaxy whose gas ratios suggest either molecular-gas deficiency or substantial CO-dark gas.

quotes.quote: We report an early science discovery of the \ emission line in the collisional ring galaxy, VII Zw466, using the Redshift Search Receiver instrument on the Large Millimeter Telescope Alfonso Serrano. The apparent molecular-to-atomic gas ratio either places the ISM of VII Zw466 in the dominated regime or implies a large quantity of CO-dark molecular gas, given its high star formation rate. The molecular gas densities and star formation rate densities of VII Zw466 are consistent with the standard Kennicutt-Schmidt star formation law even though we find this galaxy to be deficient.

Hence, we find that the collisional ring galaxy, VII Zw466, is either largely deficient in both \ and \ or contains a large mass of CO-dark gas. A low molecular gas fraction could be due to the enhancement of feedback processes from previous episodes of star formation as a result of the star-forming ISM being confined to the ring. We conclude that collisional ring galaxy formation is an extreme form of galaxy interaction that triggers a strong galactic-wide burst of star formation that may provide immediate negative feedback towards subsequent episodes of star formation---resulting in a short-lived star formation history or, at least, the appearance of a molecular gas deficit.

sources.summary: The paper presents ISO detections of VII Zw 466, describing its powerful HII-region ring, likely spiral intruder, and mismatched infrared, optical-line, and radio emission patterns.

quotes.quote: Figure 1 shows an example of one classical ``smoke ring'' called VII Zw 466 VII Zw 466 observed with ISOCAM and detected in three filters (from Appleton et al. 1999). This galaxy has been studied in considerable detail from the ground and comprises of an outer ring of powerful HII regions and fainter redder light which fills the interior of the ring (see Bransford et al. 1999a for further references). The likely intruder (the edge-on spiral to the south of the ring, G1-see Appleton Charmandaris Struck (1996) for details) is also detected.

Figure 2 displays how the IR emission follows loosely the distribution of optical emission-line sites, but that it is not a one to one correlation. Similarly, VLA radio observations, which trace thermal and non-thermal plasma compressed in the expanding ring, do not follow exactly the distribution of either the warm dust or the optical nebulae. Rather the radio emission tends to be concentrated on the inside-edge of the ring.

gmw_00004778.name: Arp 118
gmw_00004778.overview: Arp 118 is a Seyfert ring galaxy system [arXiv:astro-ph/9912210]. Its mid-infrared emission is composed of a featureless continuum source associated with the nucleus and bright extra-nuclear star-forming regions [arXiv:astro-ph/9912210].

sources.summary: The paper describes Arp 118 as a Seyfert ring system whose MIR emission combines a featureless nuclear continuum with bright extra-nuclear star-forming regions dominated by UIB features.

quotes.quote: In the case of Arp 118 Arp 118 (Charmandaris et al., in preparation), the MIR emission originates from a bright featureless continuum source associated with the nucleus, and a very extended but bright concentrations of emission associated with a powerful extra-nuclear HII regions. This latter emission is dominated by UIB spectral features (probably Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarborns), and emphasizes the composite nature of the MIR spectra of some Seyfert galaxies.

In Arp 118, the Seyfert nucleus is devoid of UIB features, but very powerful extra- nuclear starformation sites are dominated by UIB features. Such a galaxy, if viewed from a distance, would exhibit a composite MIR spectrum, complicating the clear separation of AGN and starburst on the basis of MIR spectral features alone.

gmw_00004779.name: JADES-GS+53.15613-27.77584
gmw_00004779.overview: JADES-GS+53.15613-27.77584 is a high-redshift galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0607039, arXiv:2302.04298] that consists of a two-component mixture [arXiv:astro-ph/0607039]. It contains neutral gas [arXiv:2302.04298] and exhibits emission line ratios that suggest the presence of shock-heated gas in its interstellar medium [arXiv:2302.04298].

sources.summary: The paper obtains excellent SED fits for object 7ab, but notes it is a two-component mixture with poorly constrained stellar mass and model-dependent age and star-formation estimates.

quotes.quote: By contrast, we obtain excellent fits for object 7ab. This is somewhat surprising given that 7ab is clearly a mixture of two components (Figure 1 in yan05 ), and probably owes largely to the fact that it does not show anomalous single-band fluxes in the way that HCM 6A, SBM03 3, and GLARE 3001 do. The photometric redshift is constrained to 6.2 0.2 , 1.5 higher than the photometric redshift determined by yan05 (although they do not quote an uncertainty). Stellar mass is relatively poorly constrained owing to the relatively low signal-to-noise in the observed 3.6 m band.

As Figure fig:degAll shows, when we apply with our simulated models, the poorly-constrained stellar mass leads directly to a poorly-constrained SFR. Comparing with the one-parameter models, we find that these span an even larger (overlapping) space. This object illustrates the importance of high-quality near-infrared data in order to understand the physical properties of high-redshift galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper reports a tentative O I detection for JADES-GS+53.15613-27.77584, interpreting its O1 ratio as evidence for neutral gas and possible shock-heated ISM, with unusually low O32 for the sample.

quotes.quote: We find one tentative detection in our sample (JADES-GS+53.15613-27.77584), indicating the presence of neutral gas in this galaxy. The measured O1 ratio for this galaxy places it above the theoretical maximum starburst excitation limit from Kewley2001 , which could suggest the presence of shock-heated gas in the ISM Sutherland2017 . Interestingly, this galaxy is not one of the three galaxies with a detection from Figure fig:n2_bpt . However, it does have the second lowest O32 ratio of galaxies in this sample (Table tab:line_ratios ).

gmw_00004780.name: NGC 1056
gmw_00004780.overview: NGC 1056 is an isolated spiral galaxy [arXiv:1605.03991] classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:0704.2839]. It features complex stellar kinematics influenced by dust [arXiv:astro-ph/0603161], a possible inner disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0603161], and Wolf-Rayet emission despite low disk star formation activity [arXiv:1605.03991].

sources.summary: The SAURON notes describe NGC 1056 as a faint Sy 2 Markarian galaxy whose low [OIII]/H values confirm young central ages, with published central line strengths and surface photometry.

quotes.quote: NGC 1056 is a rather faint galaxy, classified in NED as Sy 2. It is part of the list of Markarian et al. (1989). Central line strength measurements are also available from Prugniel et al. (2001), who have similar conclusions from Mg _2 and Fe . The young central ages are confirmed by low [ OIII ] / \ values. Surface photometry in V and I is given by Heraudeau et al. (1996).

sources.summary: NGC 1056 has dust-affected complex stellar kinematics, a possible inner disk, patchy velocity dispersion, and smooth ionised-gas distribution with low line-ratio values across its main disc.

quotes.quote: NGC 1056 displays a complex stellar velocity field, due to the presence of significant amounts of dust. The inner parts, however, might reveal the presence of an inner disk. Unlike other cases in the \ survey, this feature is not accompanied by an anti-correlation with the h_3 parameter. The velocity dispersion is also patchy, although a central dip can be recognised.

sources.summary: The paper finds WR emission in isolated Sa galaxy NGC 1056 and highlights R1 continuum fitting, where the bump disappears in the observed integrated spectrum.

quotes.quote: It is surprising to find WR emission in an isolated Sa galaxy (NGC 1056), with low disc star formation activity than later type galaxies Giuricin94,Plauchu-Frayn12 , and in a dwarf elliptical (IC 0225; also detected in B08a, WR346).
This fact is very notorious around 4500 , where the fitted continuum (solid orange line) follows well the observed continuum (solid green line) in the case of R1 in NGC 1056 .
In NGC 1056 , the bump actually disappears in the observed spectrum ( _ orig = 2.6 ).

gmw_00004781.name: NGC 2844
gmw_00004781.overview: NGC 2844 is the brightest member of a small group of galaxies [arXiv:astro-ph/0603161]. The galaxy features a highly inclined, ring-like structure of dust and younger stars where ongoing star formation is taking place [arXiv:astro-ph/0603161, arXiv:0704.2839].

sources.summary: NGC 2844 is presented as the brightest member of a small group, with regular stellar kinematics, ring-like emission matching a dust ring, and ongoing star formation along the ring.

quotes.quote: NGC 2844 is the brightest member of a group of galaxies including also NGC 2852 and NGC 2853 gmcp00 . The stellar kinematics display regular velocity and velocity dispersion fields. The \ and \ flux maps reveal a ring-like structure, consistent with a dust ring seen in the unsharp-masking. The / \ ratio and the gas velocity dispersion are low along the ring, which it is indicative of ongoing star formation.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 2844 has a young stellar ring whose line-strength-map appearance is affected by inclination, with ongoing star formation and dust extinction in a highly inclined ring.

quotes.quote: Starting with the second category: we find rings of younger stars in NGC 2844, 4220, 4245, 4274, 4314, 5953 and 7742. The appearance of the line strength maps in e.g. NGC 2844 and 4220 is different from those in e.g. NGC 4314, because of the effects of inclination.
NGC 2844 is an \ galaxy (NED) showing ongoing star formation, accompanied by dust extinction, in a rather highly inclined ring.

gmw_00004782.name: NGC 4274
gmw_00004782.overview: NGC 4274 is a double-barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:2303.00763] containing both a classical bulge and a dusty, star-forming pseudo-bulge [arXiv:0709.4331]. Its inner regions feature a fast-rotating stellar ring [arXiv:0603161] where young stars form from dynamically cold gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0511607].

sources.summary: NGC 4274 is discussed as a double-barred spiral in a complex group, with distance uncertainties and satellite candidates likely affected by high velocity separations and contamination.

quotes.quote: NGC 4274 and 4310 (NGC 4274 Group) NGC 4274 and 4310 is a pair with a complex local environment. They are often classified as members of the NGC 4274 (sub)group of 17 members fouque_groups_1992,garcia_general_1993 that belong to the Coma I Group, which is part of a larger infalling group to Virgo 1977ApJ...213..345G,2011MNRAS.412.2498M,kourkchi_galaxy_2017 . The two galaxies are placed at nearly the same 3D distance in the ALFALFA catalog, and their derived three-dimensional separation is only 154 kpc. NGC 4274 is a double-barred spiral where the outer arms form a ring structure 1995MNRAS.274..369S,erwin_double-barred_2004,2012ApJ...754...67F . Significant non-convergence in literature distances challenges its mass estimation.

The satellite sample of NGC 4274 has large host-satellite velocity separations (all exceeding V_ esc , some approaching the 300 km s^ 1 upper limit), moderate to small spatial (projected and line-of-sight) separations, and large distance errors. We recognize that for all five 2R_ 200 candidates, the large velocity differences lead to a very small likelihood of host-satellite gravitational association. The candidates are possibly foreground (AGC 724774 and UGC 7438) and background (UGC 7300, AGC 747848, 220408) contaminations.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4274 as a spiral with both pseudo-bulge and classical-bulge components, a strong sigma-drop, fast-rotating low-dispersion stars, dust, spiral structure, and younger central populations.

quotes.quote: We illustrate our picture using 2 galaxies (Fig. 3): The first one, NGC 4274, for which the surface brightness profile is fitted best by a S ersic profile with n =1.3, has a strong sigma-drop. In the region where it dominates the stars rotate fast and have a low velocity dispersion (SAURON). Such an inner disc would be called a pseudo-bulge by Kormendy Kennicutt (2004). It is dusty, has spiral structure (HST image) and shows younger stellar populations (SAURON).
This region corresponds to an elliptical-like bulge, to which Kormendy Kennicutt (2004) would refer as a classical bulge. NGC 4274 therefore would contain a pseudo-bulge AND a classical bulge.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4274 as a star-forming ring case with dust-correlated star formation, low ionized-gas dispersion, dynamically cold gas, and a sigma-drop at young stars.

quotes.quote: In Figure 3, we present the cases of NGC 4245, NGC 4274, and NGC 4314 to illustrate the most common properties of the stars and gas in these SF regions. There is a remarkable correlation between the dust and the regions where SF is very intense (i.e., large \ flux). At the same time, the / \ ratio is very low at the same locations, confirming the presence of star formation. The velocity dispersion of the ionised gas is also low ( 50 ) along the star-forming rings. We are thus seeing stars being formed from dynamically cold gas.
In the examples shown in Figure 3, only NGC 4274 displays a sigma-drop at the location of the young stars.

sources.summary: NGC 4274 is described as a dusty double-barred galaxy with regular main-disc rotation, an inner fast-rotating ring, and an ionised-gas signature of a star-forming ring.

quotes.quote: NGC 4274 is a double barred galaxy sapg95,erwin04 with significant amounts of dust in the inner parts. Despite the dust, the stellar kinematics maps show regular rotation on the main disc component of the galaxy, and also the presence of an inner fast rotating ring (detected in the V and maps). As in NGC 4245, the signatures of the star-forming ring are easily identifiable in the ionised-gas maps. An \ image is presented in hameed05 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4274 as a double-barred dusty galaxy whose kinematics are dominated by an inner disc, with young stars mainly forming in a ring around it.

quotes.quote: NGC 4274 is a double barred galaxy (Erwin 2004) with significant amounts of dust in the inner parts. The kinematics here is dominated by an inner disc, which is also prominent in molecular CO (Koda et al. 2005). Young stars are formed mainly in a ring around this disc.

sources.summary: The paper leaves NGC 4274 out of the EPG analysis because disc masking removes nearly all usable anomalous emission in its low-resolution datacube.

quotes.quote: In NGC 4274 and NGC 4448, instead, the masking of the \ disc leaves virtually no EPG emission to work with. These are two of the systems with the fewest resolution elements; their datacubes show very asymmetric \ profiles which - according to our mask - are largely caused by beam-smearing rather than by the presence of kinematically anomalous gas. Our method relies on the modelling of a kinematically distinct component and can not be used here, but we can not exclude that some EPG may actually be present in these systems.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4274 as a companion-rich field whose nearby galaxies align with its disk rotation; it also reports cloud candidates and a possible dark-galaxy companion.

quotes.quote: NGC 4274 is another rich field with at least three companion galaxies, NGC 4286, NGC 4283 and NGC 4278. All are about 90 kpc to the south of the target and much closer to each other than to NGC 4274. Their systemic velocities and locations are aligned with the rotation in the disk of NGC 4274.

gmw_00004783.name: NGC 4369
gmw_00004783.overview: NGC 4369 is a low-inclination spiral galaxy [arXiv:1108.3662, arXiv:0704.2839] with a strong, short central bar [arXiv:1108.3662] and a dust-affected central region [arXiv:0704.2839, arXiv:astro-ph/0603161]. Its nucleus is classified as an HII region [arXiv:0704.2839], showing star formation activity [arXiv:0704.2839] and abundant central molecular gas [arXiv:0704.2839].

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 4369's outer profile as II.o + III and describes bar evidence, spiral structure, rings shaping its measured surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 4369 (II.o + III; (R)SA(rs)a) : Although classified as unbarred, near-IR images show a very strong, short bar knapen00 . In our image we detect a ring between 36 and 57 . The coincidence of this ring with the Type II break might suggest an OLR break; however, the break is at 5 times the bar radius, which is well outside the usual range seen in epb08 Paper I. Consequently, we leave the inner classification at II.o. Since the galaxy is essentially face-on, with no visible evidence for spirals beyond the break at 112 (9 kpc), we are unable to sub-classify the outer part of the profile.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4369 as a faint low-inclination galaxy with dusty central regions, an HII nuclear spectrum indicating circumnuclear star formation, and abundant central molecular gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 4369 is a faint low-inclination galaxy. The central regions are strongly affected by dust (Falc on-Barroso et al. 2006). Ho et al. (1997) classify the nuclear spectrum as an HII nucleus, indicating a circumnuclear region with star formation activity but without AGN. Koda et al. (2005) show that a significant amount of molecular gas (about 10 ^8 M _ ) is found in the central regions of this galaxy.

sources.summary: NGC 4369 is described as a low-inclination galaxy with small stellar rotation amplitude, dust-affected kinematics, two ionised-gas star-forming regions, and overall low line-ratio values.

quotes.quote: NGC 4369 is a low-inclined galaxy that shows a small rotational velocity amplitude in our stellar velocity field. The stellar kinematics is significantly affected by dust, as seen from the patchiness of the velocity dispersion map. The ionised-gas flux maps show evidence for two star-forming regions at both sides of the galactic centre ust98 . The / \ map displays overall very low values overall.

gmw_00004784.name: NGC 4383
gmw_00004784.overview: NGC 4383 is a gas-rich, amorphous starburst galaxy located in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:2404.12616, arXiv:astro-ph/0406243]. It hosts a massive, starburst-driven ionized gas outflow [arXiv:2404.12616] that forms a biconical filamentary structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0406243, arXiv:astro-ph/0603161], alongside ongoing nuclear star formation [arXiv:1303.4584].

sources.summary: NGC 4383 is described as a gas-rich peculiar galaxy hosting a starburst-driven ionised outflow, with MUSE constraining its opening angle, velocity, ionisation structure, metallicity enrichment, and mass-loading factor.

quotes.quote: We have not used the ALMA data from the VERTICO survey brown21 in this work, instead, we demonstrated the excellent MUSE data quality and focused our analysis on NGC 4383, a gas-rich peculiar galaxy that hosts a massive outflow. Our main results are as follows: NGC 4383 hosts a starburst-driven outflow. The ionised gas outflow has an opening half-angle of =25-35^ and extends at least to the spatial limit of our data, i.e. at least 5.9 kpc above the disc. The outflow emission has an average velocity of v_ out 210 , with a maximum outflow velocity of v_ max 300 , which agrees with expectations from empirical relations based on the galaxy's integrated stellar mass and SFR (see below).

The outflow shows an interesting ionisation structure, where the base and core of the cone are clearly consistent with photoionisation, but the line ratios gradually get closer to a shock-ionised origin towards the edge of the cone. Using strong line metallicity calibrations, we find that the outflow is metal-enriched compared to the disc but has lower metallicity than the central starburst region. The mass outflow rate of the ionised gas integrated over the outflow-dominated regions is estimated to be 1.9 , corresponding to a mass-loading factor of _ M 1.7-2.3 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4383 as a Virgo starburst with shallow nuclear velocity rise, ionized-gas filaments and possible outflow, strong [OIII], and young inner stellar populations.

quotes.quote: NGC 4383 is another starburst galaxy in the Virgo cluster (Rubin et al. 1999). Rubin et al. state that this galaxy has an unusually shallow nuclear velocity rise, confirmed in Paper VII. The H + [ N II ] image of NGC 4383 shows filaments of ionised gas, suggesting significant noncircular gas motions due to the starburst (Koopmann et al. 2004). The SAURON ionised gas velocity field is also possibly suggestive of a starburst outflow. The [ OIII ] emission in this galaxy is so large that we have some difficulty removing all of it, resulting in very low Fe 5015 absorption line indices.
Despite of this the light in the inner regions is dominated by young stellar populations.

sources.summary: The paper places NGC 4383 in MIR class-3, citing its PAH ratio, unusually strong MIR continuum, starburst diagnostic location, and evidence for ongoing nuclear star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 4383 (S0:) This galaxy was included in MIR class-3 because of its PAH inter-band ratio (PAH _ 7.7 m / PAH _ 11.3 m = 4.6 ). However, it presents the steepest and strongest MIR continuum of any ETG in class-3. The analysis of the spectrum shows that the slope of the MIR continuum, calculated as F _ (30 m)/ F _ (15 m)= 6.7 , is consistent with the values found in starburst galaxies see e.g. Brandl06 . Furthermore, the [ NeIII ] 15.5 m / [ NeII ] 12.8 m vs. [ SIII ] 33.5 m/ [ SiII ] 34.8 m diagnostic diagram see Dale06 locates the galaxy in the starburst region. All of these indicate that the class-3 classification points to the presence of on-going star formation in the nucleus.

sources.summary: NGC 4383 is presented as an amorphous, HI-rich Virgo galaxy with a large bulge-to-disk ratio, central and outer HII structures, probable starburst outflow, and enhanced star formation likely from tides or gas accretion.

quotes.quote: NGC 4383 is an amorphous (BST) galaxy with a large bulge-to-disk ratio ( C30 =0.63). Most of the isolated galaxies of similar concentration are S0 galaxies without significant HII regions. The H image shows a biconical filamentary structure strongly suggestive of a starburst outflow, numerous HII regions in the central 2 kpc, and an irregular string of bright HII regions extending into the outer galaxy. The dS0 galaxy UGC 7504 (VCC 794), 3.3 magnitudes fainter than NGC 4383, is very nearby in projection (2.5 \ = 12 kpc), with a line-of-sight velocity difference of 800 km s ^ 1 , and no H emission. NGC 4383 is HI rich (HI def = -0.53) and has no sharp outer boundary in H , suggesting that a process other than ICM pressure, probably a tidal interaction and/or gas accretion, is responsible for its enhanced star formation.

sources.summary: NGC 4383 is described as a Virgo starburst with dust-complicated stellar kinematics, ionised-gas maps consistent with a bi-conical filamentary outflow, and enhanced star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 4383 is a starburst galaxy in the Virgo cluster rwk99 . The stellar kinematics show a complex picture with no clear sense of rotation in the inner parts, possibly due to the presence of significant amounts of dust. The \ ionised-gas maps are also difficult to interpret, although they are consistent with \ observations by kk04 , which reveal a bi-conical filamentary structure suggestive of a starburst outflow. The galaxy also displays enhanced star formation kk04 .

gmw_00004785.name: NGC 4405
gmw_00004785.overview: NGC 4405 is an Sa spiral galaxy with central dust lanes and a regular stellar velocity map [arXiv:astro-ph/0603161]. It features a severely truncated star-forming disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0406243], an H II nucleus [arXiv:0704.2839], and clumpy central gas emission indicating starburst-like star formation [arXiv:0704.2839, arXiv:astro-ph/0603161].

sources.summary: NGC 4405 is described as a peculiar, severely truncated Virgo galaxy whose star formation is enhanced near the truncation radius and whose asymmetric HII regions and regular gas kinematics suggest ICM stripping.

quotes.quote: Three galaxies, NGC 4580, NGC 4405, and IC 3392, have severely truncated H disks with enhanced star formation at the H truncation radius, but appear fairly normal inside the truncation radius. These galaxies were assigned `mixed' Hubble-type classifications of Sc/Sa and Sc/S0 by Binggeli, Sandage, Tammann (1985; hereafter BST) because of their peculiar morphology. The R images (Figure stmix ) show that the stellar disks beyond the edge of the star-forming disk are mostly regular and featureless, except for NGC 4580, which has spiral arms. NGC 4580 and IC 3392 have symmetric rings of star formation near the truncation radius, containing a large fraction of the H emission. NGC 4405 has a knottier and asymmetric HII region distribution, with an arc or partial ring to the southwest.

H major axis spectra of all three galaxies show regular, rising rotation curves for the ionized gas (Rubin, Waterman, Kenney 1999). The truncated H distribution, combined with the regular stellar isophotes and regular gas kinematics, strongly suggest that these galaxies are victims of ICM-ISM stripping.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4405 as a faint galaxy with an HII nucleus, central extinction, clumpy gas indicating starburst-like star formation, and generally young central populations.

quotes.quote: NGC 4405 is another faint galaxy with an HII nucleus. This galaxy also has a considerable amount of extinction in the central regions, although less than NGC 4369 or NGC 4383. The \ gas map displays several clumps around the centre indicating the starbursting nature of the star formation in this galaxy. The line indices show generally young stellar populations in the central regions.

sources.summary: NGC 4405 is presented as an Sa galaxy with central dust lanes, regular stellar velocity, no inner stellar component, and clumpy central gas emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 4405 is a Sa galaxy with no prominent spiral structure, but with evidence of central dust lanes. The stellar velocity map is regular. Although there is a drop in velocity dispersion at the centre of the galaxy, no evidence for an inner component is found in either the stellar velocity or h_3 maps. The \ gas map displays several clumps around the centre, as opposed to the \ map where the distribution is smoother.

gmw_00004786.name: NGC 4596
gmw_00004786.overview: NGC 4596 is a non-interacting, strongly barred S0/a galaxy [arXiv:0704.2839, arXiv:1610.02330] featuring an inner stellar disc [arXiv:0704.2839, arXiv:astro-ph/0603161] and a featureless barlens [arXiv:1610.02330]. It contains predominantly old stellar populations [arXiv:0704.2839], little dust [arXiv:1610.02330], and an asymmetric, outflow-like nuclear H-alpha morphology [arXiv:2201.05080].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4596 as a strongly barred non-interacting galaxy with bar-misaligned regular rotation, an inner disc, little ionized gas, and predominantly old stellar populations.

quotes.quote: NGC 4596 is a non-interacting, strongly barred, galaxy (Gerssen et al. 1999). The SAURON stellar velocity field displays regular rotation along an axis misaligned with respect to the photometric major axis, due to the presence of a strong bar. The galaxy has an inner disc which dominates the inner 5 \ (Paper VII). The galaxy has little ionised gas, and its stellar populations from the SAURON absorption lines are predominantly old. Gonz alez-Delgado et al. (2004), from central STIS absorption line spectra, classify this galaxy as a galaxy with strong [ OI ] lines and old stellar populations.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4596 as an S0/a barlens galaxy with a red central peak, featureless barlens colors, little dust or intermediate-age star formation, and possible small classical bulge.

quotes.quote: NGC 4596 (RP): is an S0/a galaxy with inclination of i = 35.5 ^ . This galaxy has a red central peak at 5 , which has colors of (g-) = 0.91 and (i-z) = 0.39. At (g-) this is a typical color of an elliptical galaxy (compared with 0.83 0.14 in shimasaku2001 ), and at (i-z) slightly redder than the mean value (compared with 0.27 0.06 in shimasaku2001 ). The barlens is featureless even at (g-) color showing very little intermediate aged star formation or features of dust. This is an example of galaxies in which small classical bulges might be present in the central regions.

sources.summary: The atlas identifies NGC 4596 as a strong barred galaxy whose H-alpha image reveals outflow-like asymmetric nuclear morphology, while SAURON data show aligned stellar and gas rotation.

quotes.quote: NGC 4596 (see Fig. Figure_AB7 ): It is a strong barred galaxy Kent1990, Gerssen1999,Laurikainen2017 with a faint dust spiral and a compact source in the nucleus GD2008 . Our image of the H emission reveals an outflow-like morphology, with an asymmetric profile with respect to the nucleus. studied this galaxy within the SAURON sample Bacon2001 , mentioning the misalignment in the kinematic and photometric axes of this galaxy, due to a bar. The stellar and gaseous component are aligned, both with a regular rotation pattern. In the SAURON map, the H gas is centrally concentrated FalconBarroso2006 , similar to what we see for H in our image.

sources.summary: NGC 4596 is described as a non-interacting strongly barred galaxy with regular but misaligned stellar rotation, an inner disc-like component, and aligned gas and stellar kinematics.

quotes.quote: NGC 4596 is a non-interacting strongly barred galaxy gkm99 . The \ stellar velocity field displays regular rotation along an axis misaligned with respect to the photometric major axis. There is evidence for a disc-like component in the inner 5 \ region, that it is also apparent in the velocity dispersion and h_3 maps. The distribution and kinematics of both \ and \ are aligned with the stellar kinematic major axis.

gmw_00004787.name: NGC 5475
gmw_00004787.overview: NGC 5475 is an isolated, nearly edge-on galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0603161, arXiv:0704.2839] that features a fast-rotating stellar disc [arXiv:astro-ph/0603161]. Its ionized gas emission extends along the minor axis [arXiv:astro-ph/0603161, arXiv:0704.2839], with disturbed kinematics that suggest polar rotation or a potential polar ring [arXiv:astro-ph/0603161, arXiv:0704.2839].

sources.summary: NGC 5475 is described as an isolated nearly edge-on galaxy with a fast rotating stellar disc, extended minor-axis emission, and disturbed gas kinematics suggesting polar rotation.

quotes.quote: NGC 5475 is an isolated, nearly edge-on galaxy bp94 . The \ stellar kinematics exhibits a fast rotating stellar disc aligned with the photometric major axis of the galaxy (see also fbpv03 ). The \ emission, however, extends further out and along the galaxy minor axis. Although disturbed, the ionised-gas kinematics further suggests evidence for polar rotation along the major axis of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5475 as an isolated nearly edge-on galaxy with published optical/near-infrared color profiles and H emission extending mainly along the minor axis, perhaps in a polar ring.

quotes.quote: NGC 5475 is an isolated, nearly edge-on galaxy (Balcells Peletier 1994). Colour profiles in optical and near-infrared have been presented in Peletier Balcells (1996, 1997). The \ emission extends out mainly along the galaxy minor axis, possibly in a polar ring.

gmw_00004788.name: NGC 5636
gmw_00004788.overview: NGC 5636 is a strongly barred lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1101.0903, arXiv:astro-ph/0603161] that forms a pair with the elliptical galaxy NGC 5638 [arXiv:1101.0903, arXiv:astro-ph/0603161]. It features a well-defined ultraviolet ring [arXiv:1101.0903] and exhibits ongoing star formation near its center [arXiv:0704.2839].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5636 as a barred S0 interacting with NGC 5638, with a well-defined UV ring and green-valley, star-forming ring structure.

quotes.quote: The sample includes five S0 galaxies: NGC 1533, NGC 2962, NGC 2974, NGC 4245 and NGC 5636 (interacting with NGC 5638, an elliptical galaxy). Notice that NGC 2974 is erroneously classified as elliptical both in RC3 and RSA, although an exponential disk is evident from the optical and UV luminosity profiles see also Jeong07, Jeong09, Marino10a . In addition, in NGC 2974 Krajnovic05 found, with SAURON observations deZeeuw02 , the existence of non-axisymmetric perturbations consistent with the presence of inner bars.

NGC 1533 UV image shows an incomplete, clumpy ring-like structure, while NGC 2962, NGC 4245 and NGC 5636 which have well defined and complete rings, although not uniform, in the FUV and NUV bands. Spiral arm-like structures are visible in both NGC 2962 and in NGC 2974. Both the ring and the arm-like structure appear bluer than the nucleus and the galaxy body.

S0s with rings like NGC 4245 and NGC 5636 are located in the so called green valley, where evolving objects are found, while the ring structures are located in the blue sequence. The blue, star forming rings in NGC 4245, NGC 5636 and NGC 1533 are located at the end of the bar structure. The optical and UV images, shown in Figure  fig1 , suggests that NGC 2962 has two rings, one at the edge of the bar, the other is an outer ring. Only the outer ring in NGC 2962 is visible in UV GALEX images.

sources.summary: NGC 5636 is presented as a strongly barred non-interacting pair member, the smallest and faintest sample galaxy, with misaligned stellar rotation and low stellar velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: NGC 5636 is a strongly barred galaxy in a non-interacting pair with NGC 5638 at 2 . This galaxy is the smallest and has the lowest apparent magnitude of our sample (m _B = 13.7, from RC3). The direction of the maximum stellar rotation is misaligned with respect to the bar major axis. The stellar velocity dispersion is one of the lowest in the sample.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5636 as a faint strongly barred member of a non-interacting pair, with noisy maps showing old populations plus younger central stars and gas consistent with ongoing star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 5636 is a faint, strongly barred galaxy in a non-interacting pair with NGC 5638 at 2 , with a stellar velocity dispersion that is one of the lowest in the sample. Our line strength maps are rather noisy, indicating overall old stellar populations with younger stars near the centre. This agrees with the ionised gas maps, which show very low / \ values, suggesting ongoing star formation there.

gmw_00004789.name: NGC 5689
gmw_00004789.overview: NGC 5689 is an almost edge-on barred galaxy featuring a box-shaped bulge [arXiv:0704.2839, arXiv:astro-ph/0603161]. It is the brightest non-interacting member of its galaxy group and hosts a central dust lane that obscures its nucleus [arXiv:0704.2839, arXiv:astro-ph/0603161].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5689 as an almost edge-on barred galaxy with a box-shaped bulge, a central dust lane, dust-obscured center, and dust-lane star formation lowering inferred ages.

quotes.quote: NGC 5689 is an almost edge-on barred galaxy with a box-shaped bulge (L utticke et al. 2000). A strong dust lane is found at 5 \ South of the nucleus. Colour maps of Peletier Balcells (1997) show that this dust lane is also obscuring the centre of the galaxy. Carollo et al. (1997) present an optical HST-image of this galaxy, although no bulge-disc decomposition is done because of the large amount of dust obscuration. Star formation associated to this central dust lane could be responsible for the lower ages we see from the absorption line maps.

sources.summary: NGC 5689 is described as an almost edge-on barred galaxy with a box-shaped bulge, group membership, a southern dust lane, and prominent inner fast-rotating disc signatures.

quotes.quote: NGC 5689 is an almost edge-on barred galaxy with a box-shaped bulge ldp00 . It is the brightest non-interacting member of a group formed by NGC 5682, NGC 5683, and NGC 5693 gmcp00 . The unsharp-masked image displays a dust lane 5 \ south of the nucleus. The stellar kinematics shows prominent signatures of an inner, fast-rotating disc component (i.e. central increase in rotational velocity, velocity dispersion drop, and h_3-V anti-correlation).

gmw_00004790.name: NGC 6501
gmw_00004790.overview: NGC 6501 is a massive group galaxy that belongs to a group containing NGC 6467, NGC 6495, PGC 61102, and NGC 6500 [arXiv:astro-ph/0603161, arXiv:0704.2839]. It exhibits regular stellar rotation along its major axis, hosts old stellar populations, and has no detected ionized gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0603161, arXiv:0704.2839].

sources.summary: NGC 6501 is described as a group galaxy with regular major-axis stellar rotation, the highest sample velocity-dispersion peak, a steep outward gradient, and no detected ionised gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 6501 is galaxy that is part of a group formed by NGC 6467, NGC 6495, PGC 61102 and NGC 6500 gmcp00 . The \ stellar velocity field displays regular rotation along the major axis of the galaxy. The stellar velocity dispersion map shows the highest value in our sample ( 270 ) and a steep gradient decreasing towards the outer parts. No ionised-gas was detected in this galaxy, in agreement with earlier work cid04 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6501 as a massive group galaxy, the most distant in the sample, with regular rotation, no detected ionized gas, and old stellar populations.

quotes.quote: NGC 6501 is part of a group together with NGC 6467, NGC 6495, PGC 61102 and NGC 6500 (Giuricin et al. 2000). It is a massive galaxy, the most distant of our sample, showing regular rotation and no detection of ionised gas (Paper VII). The absorption line indices are consistent with old stellar populations, confirming the results of Cid-Fernandes et al. (2004, 2005), which are based on an absorption line study using a larger wavelength range.

gmw_00004791.name: Segue 1
gmw_00004791.overview: Segue 1 is an ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:1110.4358, arXiv:1507.03589] located approximately 23 kiloparsecs from the Sun [arXiv:1110.4358, arXiv:1010.4706]. It is a dark-matter-dominated satellite of the Milky Way [arXiv:1110.4358] whose tidal radius remains well beyond its stellar distribution [arXiv:1804.10230].

sources.summary: The paper presents Segue 1 as an ultra-faint, dark-matter-dominated dwarf spheroidal observed by VERITAS, with no gamma-ray excess and derived constraints on dark matter annihilation models.

quotes.quote: Segue 1 is an ultra faint dSph discovered in 2006 as an overdensity of resolved stars in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey . It is located at a distance of 23 2 kpc from the Sun at (Ra,Dec) = (10 ^ h 07 ^ m 03.2 ^ s ,16d04'25''), well above the Galactic plane. Because of its proximity to the Sagittarius stream, the nature of Segue 1 overdensity has recently been disputed, some people arguing that it was a tidally disrupting star cluster originally associated with the Sagittarius dSph . However, a recent kinematic study of a larger member star sample (66 stars compared to the previous 24 stars sample) has firmly confirmed that Segue 1 is an ultra faint MW satellite galaxy . According to the study of its star kinematics, Segue 1 is the most DM-dominated dSph and is often highlighted as the best dSph target for indirect DM searches .

Observations of the Segue 1 dSph were performed between January 2010 and May 2011, at a mean zenith angle of 20^ . Only data taken under good weather conditions and with no major hardware problems were selected. The total exposure, after dead time correction, is nearly 48 hours.

sources.summary: The paper compares Segue 1 with prior proper-motion work, rules out strong stellar stripping, and compiles extensive spectroscopy into nine bright members.

quotes.quote: Tucana III & 3^ +1 _ 1 & 49^ +6 _ 5 & 0.90^ +0.01 _ 0.01 & 0.7 In addition to Boo I (Section  sec:boo1test ), the other ultra-faint dwarf with a literature proper motion measurement is Segue 1. determined a proper motion of = -0.37 0.57   , = -3.39 0.58   \ using ground-based data over a time baseline of 10 years. Using the mass determined from the internal kinematics of its stars, simon11 showed that the tidal radius of Segue 1 remains well beyond its stellar distribution unless the galaxy has an orbital pericenter of 4  kpc or less. My results here show that Segue 1 cannot have experienced significant stellar stripping (also see fritz17 for independent confirmation).

Segue 1 was spectroscopically confirmed by geha09 , who identified 24 member stars with Keck/DEIMOS. simon11 subsumed the geha09 data set into a much larger DEIMOS spectroscopic survey, finding a total of 71 Segue 1 members. In the interim, norris10 also obtained AAT spectroscopy of bright stars over a wider field, including one additional Segue 1 member star outside the simon11 coverage area and another member candidate at a radius of 24 . The entire sample of confirmed Segue 1 red giants has been followed up at high-resolution by norris10c and fsk14 . In total, Segue 1 contains 9 member stars brighter than g=20 (7 RGB stars and 2 HB stars) plus the norris10 candidate located at a radius of 5.5 r_ 1/2 .
Segue 1 and Boo I have orbital eccentricities of 0.5 , and the remainder of the sample has eccentricities ranging from 0.67 to 0.96.

sources.summary: The paper treats Segue 1 as an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy and dark-matter target, using Swift XRT data to set sterile-neutrino limits and reporting no emission-line evidence.

quotes.quote: We use a short ( 5 ks) archival X-ray observation of Segue 1 obtained with the X-Ray Telescope (XRT) on board the Swift satellite to exclude the presence of sterile neutrinos in the 1.6--14 keV mass range down to a flux limit of 6 10^ 12 ergs cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 within 67 pc of its center. With an estimated mass-to-light ratio of 3400 M _ /L _ , Segue 1 is the darkest ultra-faint dwarf galaxy currently measured.

We obtained the spectrum using a 10 circle radius centered on the position of Segue 1 (J2000.0) RA. 10^ h 07^ m 03. ^ s 2 , Dec. +16^ 04 25 determined by martin . The radius corresponds to a physical size of 67 pc at the distance of 23 kpc, equivalent to 2.3 half-light radii simon . The resulting spectrum contains 650 net counts in the 0.2--7 keV band. Since the extraction region nearly covers the totality of the Swift XRT field, we obviate any background subtraction. We reason that for the desired upper bound on the parameter space of sterile neutrinos of Segue 1, it is better to provide a conservative estimate that includes the background contribution rather than to gamble with an inadequate instrumental background subtraction see a similar argument by riemer . It is important to note that an extrapolation of the counts in the area outside the extraction region indicates that the purported Segue 1 emission has signal-to-noise ratio smaller than 3. The extracted spectrum was grouped with grppha to ensure at least 20 counts per bin. Subsequently, an ancillary response function (ARF) was generated through xrtmkarf using the response matrix file (RMF) from the latest distribution in Swift Calibration Database http://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/docs/heasarc/caldb/swift/ . We then fitted the spectrum with a simple absorbed power-law model implemented in XSPEC arnaud . Between 0.4 and 5 keV, the spectrum is reasonably fit (reduced ^ 2 = 1.04 ) with a power-law index = 1.4 0.3 with N _ H fixed at the Galactic value. Fig. figure2 shows the compounded (source + instrumental background) spectrum of Segue 1 with a power law. As shown in this Fig. figure2 , we find no evidence for the presence of emission lines in the compounded spectrum of Segue 1.

sources.summary: The paper targets Segue I as an ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy for radio constraints on dark matter annihilation, emphasizing its high mass-to-light ratio and Milky Way halo environment.

quotes.quote: Green Bank Telescope Constraints on Dark Matter Annihilation in Segue I Aravind Natarajan anat01@me.com Department of Physics, Engineering Physics, and Astronomy, Queen's University, Kingston, Ontario K7L 3N6, Canada Department of Physics and Astronomy, University of Pennsylvania, 209 South 33rd Street, Philadelphia, PA 19104, USA James E. Aguirre Department of Physics and Astronomy, University of Pennsylvania, 209 South 33rd Street, Philadelphia, PA 19104, USA Kristine Spekkens Department of Physics, Royal Military College of Canada, PO Box 17000, Station Forces, Kingston, Ontario, Canada K7K 7B4 Brian S. Mason National Radio Astronomy Observatory, 520 Edgemont Road, Charlottesville, VA 22903-2475, USA We use a non-detection in = 1.4 GHz Green Bank Telescope observations of the ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy , which could be immersed in a non-negligible halo magnetic field of the Milky Way, to place bounds on particle dark matter properties. We model the galaxy using an Einasto dark matter profile, and compute the expected synchrotron flux from dark matter annihilation as a function of the magnetic field strength B , diffusion coefficient D_0 , and particle mass m_ for different annihilation channels.

\ (RA = 10:07:03.2 1.7 ^s , DEC = +16:04:25 15'') is an ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy at a distance 23 kpc from the sun, with a mass-to-light ratio M/L 3400 \ , making it one of the darkest galaxies known so far. The large mass-to-light ratio combined with the close proximity of \ make it an excellent target for indirect dark matter searches. More importantly, \ is among the closest dwarf spheroidal galaxies to the sun, which means that it could be immersed in the outer halo magnetic field of the Milky Way itself . We therefore expect a non-negligible ambient magnetic field at the location of , which makes it a unique target for synchrotron radiation searches for annihilating dark matter.

gmw_00004792.name: NGC1427
gmw_00004792.overview: NGC 1427 is a low-luminosity elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1404.3644, arXiv:2201.06704] in the Fornax cluster [arXiv:2203.15822] that has been classified as a cD galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9705214]. It is a slow rotator [arXiv:1906.08187] containing a kinematically decoupled core [arXiv:0710.5529] and a hot inner stellar halo indicating a massive merger that ended approximately 8 Gyr ago [arXiv:2203.15822].

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 1427 as a cD galaxy and finds only a bulge in most decompositions; same-band fits agree, while a possible R-band disk remains a small, uncertain perturbation.

quotes.quote: NGC 1427 This galaxy has been classified as a cD galaxy with total magnitude in the B system m_ B = 11.85 (RC3). In this decomposition we only found a bulge. Since we had two images in the Gunn v and g filters and once in the filter, we performed the decomposition separately on each image, to check the self-consistency of the algorithm. We find that it agrees well with itself where we have two images in the same wavelength, suggesting that the differences in scale length between different filters may have physical significance.

The difference between scale lengths with and without masking of the central pixels is remarkable in the R band image. The intensities compensate for the difference, however so that the final _ ^ 2 are almost equal. In the R band, the disk is not wholly negligible unlike the other two bands where the D/B ratio is extremely small. This is, however, a small perturbation on the bulge, as the bulge intensity is greater than that of the disk out to a large radius (about 40 pixels), and it is doubtful, but possible, that there is physical significance to this disk in the R band.

sources.summary: FCC 276 is described as a bright eastern elliptical and ancient infaller, one of two slow rotators, with a central distinct component and enhanced line strengths.

quotes.quote: FCC 276 (NGC 1427) -- Fig. fig:FCC276map FCC 276 is the brightest elliptical galaxy on the eastern side of the cluster at R_ proj =0.8^ from FCC 213 (NGC 1399). It is the only ancient infaller on this side of the transition region from high to low density. This is one of the two slow rotators of the F3D sample. The stellar kinematic maps clearly show the presence of a distinct component in the centre ( R 10 arcsec), which has v_ max 50 km s ^ 1 and 180 km s ^ 1 . In the region of the distinct component, the Mg b and Fe5015 line-strength indices are larger ( 5 ) than outside ( 3 ) while H is nearly constant at all radii ( 2 ).
Differently from the other distinct components detected in the galaxies close to the cluster core, this feature has bluer colours Carollo1997, Iodice2018 .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1427 as a Fornax early-type galaxy with asymmetric blue outskirts and a hot inner stellar halo indicating a massive merger ending about 8 Gyr ago.

quotes.quote: NGC 1380 (FCC 167) and NGC 1427 (FCC276) are two galaxies in the Fornax cluster observed as part of the FDS Iodice2019a and Fornax3D survey Sarzi2018 . NGC 1427 is the brightest elliptical galaxy located on the eastern side of the cluster at 50 arcmin from the BCG, and at a distance of 19.6 Mpc from us Blakeslee2009 . NGC 1427 has m_r =10.15 mag and R_e =44.7 =4.2 kpc.

This galaxy becomes bluer with increasing radius and in the outskirts, and its outer and fainter isophotes appear asymmetric at R 3 arcmin Iodice2019a . NGC 1427 was observed with two MUSE pointings out to a radius of 100 along the major axis and up to 40 along the minor axis (Figure fig:bestfit_1427 ). The merger in NGC 1427 ended at t 8 Gyr ago based on the stellar populations in its hot inner stellar halo, in a way comparable to how the merger time in M31 was estimated DSouza2018b .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1427 as an E4 Fornax galaxy with anomalously bright K-band surface-brightness fluctuations, a kinematically decoupled core, and old metal-rich central indices.

quotes.quote: NGC 1427 - this E4 galaxy also has anomalously bright K-band SBF (see NGC 1419 SBF references above). It also contains a KDC don95 .

However, two galaxies in common with our sample (NGC 1419 and 1427) were found to have brighter (larger) K-band SBF relative to the mean relationship (see also Mei et al. 2001). A larger K is thought theoretically to arise from the presence of an extended giant branch, i.e. more cool, bright, M giants. In the spectroscopic data discussed here, there are no indications of such extended giant branches in these galaxies -- their central line indices are consistent with an integrated stellar populations dominated by an old, metal-rich population.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC1427 as a low-luminosity Fornax elliptical with bimodal globular clusters, two ULXs, and one optically variable X-ray-source counterpart.

quotes.quote: NGC1427 is a low luminosity elliptical galaxy in Fornax cluster and its globular cluster association has been studied by and . The photometry studies reveal a bimodal cluster population in this galaxy and suggest that the formation mechanism of globular clusters in low luminosity galaxies shows similarities with giant galaxies. The Chandra ACIS Survey of X-ray point sources Liu11 identified two ULXs in this galaxy with luminosity 2 10^ 39 ergs s^ 1 . Among them, one source is inside the D_ 25 region of the galaxy, and the other is outside the D_ 25 region.

After taking into account of systematic errors, estimated using the field optical sources as reference, we find that four of the X-ray sources (three in NGC1399 and one in NGC1427) have variable optical counterparts at a high significance level.

sources.summary: The paper characterizes NGC1427 as a low-luminosity elliptical whose GC map is dominated by the extended, complex E4 structure plus several additional blue-GC structures.

quotes.quote: The study of the GC system of NGC1427 shown that this low-luminosity elliptical presents a strongly centrally concentrated red sub-population and a blue component with a much shallower radial profile. The spatial distribution of ACSFCS GCs in NGC1427 (Figure fig:ngc1427 ) is dominated by the very large and statistically significant structure E4 that extends radially and displays a complex morphology with at least two main overdensity regions: the first one, roughly circular, is located W of the center of the galaxy, while the second, much larger region lies across the D _ 25 isophote in the NW quadrant of the galaxy. E4 morphological complexity and extension suggest that it might be the result of the superposition of two distinct overdensities. E4 is visible in both the red and blue residual maps, although in the blue GCs the two density peaks are clearly separated by a large gap unlike in the red residuals they are connected. Four additional large spatial structures are observed.

sources.summary: NGC 1427 shows a wiggly velocity-dispersion profile, anti-correlated rotation-curve and dispersion wiggles, and possible central-disk evidence from an inner inflexion.

quotes.quote: NGC 1427 This galaxy has a wiggly VDP which peaks around 180 km s ^ 1 and falls to 140 km s ^ 1 at the data boundary. The CVD is 13 km s ^ 1 below the peak value which occurs 4 W from the center. The RC, after reaching a first maximum of 25 km s ^ 1 at 5 , shows a series of secondary maxima on both sides, that are anti-correlated to the wiggles in the VDP. There is also marginal evidence for an inflexion in the center which, if real, and if related to the dip in the VDP could constitute kinematical evidence for the central faint disk detected by Carollo et al. (1997, see also Forbes et al. 1995).

gmw_00004793.name: SDSS J1539+3954
gmw_00004793.overview: SDSS J1539+3954 is a luminous, radio-quiet weak-line quasar located at a redshift of z = 1.935 [arXiv:2001.08216]. It exhibits extreme X-ray variability, including a factor-of-20 flux rise followed by a factor-of-9 drop back to an X-ray weak state within three rest-frame months [arXiv:2001.08216, arXiv:2202.05279].

sources.summary: The paper reports SDSS J1539+3954 as a luminous weak-line quasar with a factor-20 X-ray rise, unchanged UV spectrum, and possible thick-disk or reflection explanations.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of an extreme X-ray flux rise (by a factor of 20) of the weak-line quasar SDSS J153913.47+395423.4 (hereafter SDSS J1539+3954) at z = 1.935. SDSS J1539+3954 is the most-luminous object among radio-quiet type 1 AGNs where such dramatic X-ray variability has been observed. Before the X-ray flux rise, SDSS J1539+3954 appeared X-ray weak compared with the expectation from its UV flux; after the rise, the ratio of its X-ray flux and UV flux is consistent with the majority of the AGN population. We also present a contemporaneous HET spectrum of SDSS J1539+3954, which demonstrates that its UV continuum level remains generally unchanged despite the dramatic increase in the X-ray flux, and its \ emission line remains weak.

We have reported a dramatic increase in the \ flux of a luminous WLQ, SDSS J1539+3954, by a factor of 20 . We obtained a contemporaneous HET spectrum of SDSS J1539+3954 after observing this X-ray flux rise with . We found that its overall UV continuum and emission-line properties do not show significant changes compared with previous SDSS and BOSS spectra. We propose that the extreme X-ray variability event can be explained by a thick inner accretion-disk model with a slight change in the thickness of the disk, or a reflection model where strong gravitational light bending occurs.

sources.summary: SDSS J1539+3954 is an extreme-variable WLQ that returned from X-ray normal to weak within three rest-frame months after a factor-9 flux drop.

quotes.quote: One of the WLQs in the sample that was known to have extreme X-ray variability, SDSS J1539+3954, exhibited dramatic X-ray variability again: it changed from an X-ray normal state to an X-ray weak state within 3 months in the rest frame.

As can be seen in Table stackxraytable , most of the WLQs do not show significant variations in count rate between the available \ epochs except for the object SDSS J153913.47+395423.4 (hereafter SDSS J1539+3954).

For one of the WLQs in our sample, SDSS J1539+3954, we found that after an observed \ flux rise by a factor of 20 reported in Ni2020 , it changed from an X-ray normal state back to an X-ray weak state within 3 months in the rest frame, with a downward variation in the \ flux by a factor of 9.

gmw_00004794.name: RXJ- z9500
gmw_00004794.overview: RXJ- z9500 is a highly magnified galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of z=9.51 [arXiv:2210.15699, arXiv:2212.02890]. It is characterized by a low stellar mass [arXiv:2210.15699], a star-formation rate of 0.26 solar masses per year [arXiv:2210.15699], low dust extinction [arXiv:2210.15699], and low gas-phase metallicity [arXiv:2212.02890].

sources.summary: The paper reports ID 11027 as a highly magnified z=9.51 galaxy with JWST spectroscopy, strong oxygen and hydrogen lines, low stellar mass, star formation, and low metallicity.

quotes.quote: Here we report the discovery, in James Webb Space Telescope ( JWST ) imaging, of a highly magnified, low-mass ( (M_*/ M _ )=7.70^ +0.11 _ 0.09 ) galaxy visible when the Universe was only 510 Myr old, and follow-up prism spectroscopy of the galaxy extending from Lyman to [ O III ] 5007 in its rest frame. Our JWST spectrum provides [ O III ] 5007 and H detections with a respective signal-to-noise ratio (S/N) of 33 and 7, as well as four additional lines with S/N > 2 . These emission lines yield a redshift of z=9.51 and star-formation rate of 0.26^ +0.07 _ 0.05 M _ yr ^ 1 . The galaxy's large inferred value of [ O III ] / [ O II ] =16 6 suggests that this galaxy has an escape fraction of ionizing radiation larger than 10 , indicating that a population of similar objects could contribute substantially to the reionization budget.

We obtained a photometric redshift of z=9.38^ +0.29 _ 0.15 for the galaxy, and our spectroscopic redshift of z=9.51 0.01 confirms the accuracy of the imaging-only photometric redshift. We use the BAGPIPES (Bayesian Analysis of Galaxies for Physical Inference and Parameter EStimation) page to infer physical properties of the galaxy from NIRCam photometry and non-detections from archival HST imaging, using a simple exponentially declining star-formation history. We correct our constraints on the galaxy properties for the effect of magnification due to lensing ( =19.2 3.6 ; see Supplementary Material) and infer a very low stellar mass (M_*/ M _ )=7.70^ +0.11 _ 0.09 and an age of 110^ +20 _ 10 Myr. We find low extinction due to dust, with A_V=0.03^ +0.04 _ 0.02 mag.

sources.summary: The paper singles out RXJ- z9500 as a highly magnified high-redshift galaxy whose marginal auroral-line detection supports direct-method metallicity constraints consistent with the R3 calibration.

quotes.quote: The magnification factors are summarized in Table tab:props , typically of the order = 1-2 except for the galaxy RXJ- z9500 which has = 19.2 3.6 .

For the one galaxy RXJ- z9500 , first presented by Williams et al. , we constrain the metallicity based on the marginal detection of the auroral [ ] 4363 line at f_ OIII 4363 5.0 10^ 19 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 , see Fig. efig:auroralmet . We follow the iterations outlined by Izotov et al. and solve for the oxygen abundance as a function of electron temperature and density. We derive electron temperatures T_e 2.8 10^ 4 K and gas-phase metallicities 12+ (O/H) 7.2 for all n_e < 10^ 4 cm ^ 3 . This is consistent with the inferred estimate from the R3 calibration of 12+ (O/H) = 7.56^ +0.16 _ 0.17 and with previous results from Williams et al. .

gmw_00004795.name: J1503+3644
gmw_00004795.overview: J1503+3644 is a galaxy at a redshift of 0.36 [arXiv:2008.06059] that is a known Lyman continuum emitter [arXiv:1802.06378, arXiv:2008.06059]. Spatially-resolved spectroscopy reveals strong magnesium II emission [arXiv:2008.06059], with most of the galaxy being optically thin to ionizing photons [arXiv:2008.06059].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1503+3644 as a known LyC emitter and shows that refitting it with an SMC attenuation law gives a consistent H i covering fraction.

quotes.quote: The other nine galaxies are known LyC emitters J1503+3644, J0925+1409, J1152+3400, J1333+6246, J1442-0209, J0921+4509, Tol1247-232, Tol0440-381, and Mrk 54 from izotov2016a, izotov2016b, borthakur2014, leitherer2016 .

We refit J1503+3644 and GP0911+1831, which are two galaxies with high and low C_f , using a Small Magellanic Cloud (SMC) attenuation law Values have been taken from the IDL routine from J. Xavier Prochaska: https://github.com/profxj/xidl/tree/master/Dust still assuming a uniform dust foreground. The SMC law is significantly steeper than the reddy2016dustlaw law. With the SMC dust law, we measured C_f ( H i ) of 0.653 0.109 and 0.744 0.156, respectively. These are consistent, within 1 , with the C_f ( H i ) estimated using the reddy2016dustlaw attenuation law (0.634 0.109 and 0.731 0.150).

sources.summary: The paper studies J1503+3644 as a z 0.36 LyC emitter selected for strong Mg II emission; KCWI spectroscopy indicates most spaxels are optically thin to ionizing photons.

quotes.quote: Here, we present new Keck Cosmic Web Imager spatially-resolved spectroscopy of the resonant 2800 \ doublet from a redshift 0.36 galaxy, J1503+3644 , with a previously observed escape fraction of 6 .

Observations and Data Reduction We selected SDSS J150342.83+364450.75 hereafter J1503; izotov16b because the galaxy has the largest 2800 \ emission flux from public Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) observations sdss in the izotov16b LyC emitting sample.
The \ inferred from R in most spaxels (98.4 ) of J1503+3644 is optically thin to ionizing photons, with a median value of = 4 1 10^ 16 cm ^ 2 ( fig:doub_hist ).

gmw_00004796.name: J1152+3400
gmw_00004796.overview: J1152+3400 is a known Lyman continuum emitting galaxy [arXiv:1802.06378]. Modeling with a clumpy dust geometry indicates that the galaxy has a low neutral hydrogen covering fraction and a consistent Lyman continuum escape fraction [arXiv:1802.06378].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1152+3400 as a known LyC emitter and refits it with a clumpy dust geometry, deriving low H i covering fraction and a consistent LyC escape fraction.

quotes.quote: The other nine galaxies are known LyC emitters J1503+3644, J0925+1409, J1152+3400, J1333+6246, J1442-0209, J0921+4509, Tol1247-232, Tol0440-381, and Mrk 54 from izotov2016a, izotov2016b, borthakur2014, leitherer2016 .

We refit two galaxies: J0921+4509 and J1152+3400, which have an average C_f ( H i ) and low C_f ( H i ), respectively. Adopting a clumpy geometry (b) , we derive =0.236 and 0.239 (versus \ = 0.224 and 0.134 in model (a) ) and C_f ( H i ) =0.976 and 0.912 (versus C_f = 0.754 and 0.506 in model (a) ), respectively. These values and Eq. eq:f_holes predict a LyC escape fraction of =0.024 and 0.088 at = 912 , which is consistent, within 1 , with the values derived using the uniform dust screen model ( =0.017 0.007 and 0.092 0.027 ).

gmw_00004797.name: Mrk54
gmw_00004797.overview: Mrk 54 is a local star-forming galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0106306, arXiv:2303.11036] that has been studied as a Lyman continuum emitter [arXiv:1802.06378, arXiv:2303.11036]. Far-infrared spectroscopy reveals strong carbon emission [arXiv:2303.11036], while its neutral hydrogen absorption lines indicate that interstellar material limits the escape of ionizing photons [arXiv:astro-ph/0106306].

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk54 as a known LyC emitter whose low redshift complicates Lyman-series fitting, requiring nonparametric H i treatment and O i 1302 measurements.

quotes.quote: We note that Tol0440-381 and Mrk54 have much lower redshifts than the rest of the sample. Consequently there are more Milky Way absorption lines near the Lyman series. This high density of Milky Way Lyman series absorption lines means that we cannot accurately fit the Lyman series with Voigt profiles. Rather, we used nonparametric fits to describe the H i properties (see Sect. method:depth ). The H i column densities are affected by large uncertainties because the low S/N and insufficient resolution of the spectra do not allow a reliable estimate of \ given the saturation of the H i absorption lines (see Sect. simcold ).

Except for Tol0440-381 and Mrk54, where we did not fit the absorption lines with the method in Sect. fitting , we computed the final C_f ( H i ) as the weighted mean of the C_f depth and the fitted C_f values (column 8 in Table table:hiresult ).
However, O i is undetected for J0925+1409 (see Fig. fig:J0925 ), and we used O i 1302 \ for Tol0440-381 and Mrk54.

sources.summary: FUSE observations of Mrk 54 found no Lyman-continuum detection, set f_esc < 0.062, and linked the low escape to neutral hydrogen absorption in the star-forming galaxy.

quotes.quote: We present Far Ultraviolet Spectroscopic Explorer observations of the star-forming galaxy Mrk 54 at z = 0.0448. The Lyman continuum radiation is not detected above the H i absorption edge in our Galaxy. An upper limit is evaluated by comparison with the background measured in regions of the detector adjacent to the observed spectrum.

An upper limit of 6.15 10^ 16 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 A ^ 1 is obtained for the flux at 900 in the rest frame of Mrk 54. By comparison with the number of ionizing photons derived from the H flux, this limit translates into an upper limit of f_ esc < 0.062 for the fraction of Lyman continuum photons that escape the galaxy without being absorbed by interstellar material.

In Mrk 54 the neutral hydrogen is directly seen along the line of sight as Lyman series absorption lines (down to Ly7) at the redshift of the parent galaxy. These features are accompanied by a number of metal absorption-lines with different possible origins (interstellar gas, stellar photospheres and stellar winds) as currently found in starburst galaxies (e.g. Heckman et al. and references therein).

sources.summary: The paper places Mrk 54 in the control sample, saying its LyC flux is consistent with zero and its outflow properties match the control galaxies.

quotes.quote: First, one galaxy from the Leitherer16 sample, Mrk 54, has a consistent with zero (see the Appendix). We include this galaxy in the control sample, and it has outflow properties that are consistent with the control sample ( equivalent width of 1.3 \ and of 655 ; see below).
Note that MRK 54 is not considered a LyC leaker, and it is included in the control sample.
The shadow flux of Mrk54 is consistent with zero, and we do not consider these observations as a LyC detection.

sources.summary: The paper presents SOFIA FIR spectroscopy of Mrk 54, finding strong emission, moderate emission in another line, and a low line-luminosity ratio.

quotes.quote: We present integral field, far-infrared (FIR) spectroscopy of Mrk 54, a local Lyman Continuum Emitter (LCE), obtained with FIFI-LS on the Stratospheric Observatory for Infrared Astronomy. This is only the second time, after Haro 11, that 158 \ and 88 \ spectroscopy of the known LCEs have been obtained. We find that Mrk 54 has a strong \ emission that accounts for 1 of the total FIR luminosity, whereas it has only moderate \ emission, resulting in the low / \ luminosity ratio of 0.22 0.06 . In order to investigate whether / \ is a useful tracer of f_ esc (LyC escape fraction), we examine the correlations of / \ and (i) the optical line ratio of O_ 32 5007 / 3727 , (ii) specific star formation rate, (iii) 88 / 63 \ ratio, (iv) gas phase metallicity, and (v) dust temperature based on a combined sample of Mrk 54 and the literature data from the Herschel Dwarf Galaxy Survey and the LITTLE THINGS Survey.

gmw_00004798.name: UGC 5272b
gmw_00004798.overview: UGC 5272b is an interacting galaxy located approximately 2 arcminutes south of its companion, UGC 5272 [arXiv:2310.02595]. The two galaxies are connected by a neutral hydrogen bridge and contain several identified far-ultraviolet subclumps [arXiv:2310.02595].

sources.summary: The paper presents UGC 5272b as the southern member of an interacting pair with UGC 5272, linked by an H I bridge and containing identified FUV subclumps.

quotes.quote: The bottom-left panel displays an interacting system, UGC 5272 and UGC 5272b, which has a HI bridge connecting the two galaxies 2002A&A...390..829S . The UGC 5272 is in the centre and UGC 5272b is 2 arcmin towards the south. The bottom-right panel shows the subclumps (in dark blue) identified inside the UGC 5272--UGC 5272b system.

gmw_00004799.name: JADES-GS-z7-01-QU
gmw_00004799.overview: JADES-GS-z7-01-QU is a quiescent dwarf galaxy at redshift z = 7.3 [arXiv:2310.03065, arXiv:2404.07163] with a stellar mass of approximately 5 * 10^8 solar masses [arXiv:2305.07066, arXiv:2302.14155]. It experienced a short, intense starburst followed by rapid quenching [arXiv:2404.07163, arXiv:2302.14155], leaving it with a complete absence of nebular emission lines [arXiv:2404.07163, arXiv:2302.14155].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes JADES-GS-z7-01-QU as a z=7.3 post-starburst quenched galaxy whose burst and halo properties make supernova feedback insufficient for quenching.

quotes.quote: Interpreting JADES-GS-z7-01-QU JADES-GS-z7-01-QU Looser23 has a redshift of z=7.3 , and a stellar mass M_ = 10^ 8.6 M_ . Based on these measurements, using the stellar-to-halo mass relation by , we find M_h = 10^ 11 M_ .
