This implies a virial temperature at that redshift of T_ vir = 10^ 5.9 K (see Table tab:gal_properties for a summary of the galaxy's properties).

Let us now directly compare the model with the SFH of JADES-GS-z7-01-QU. The post-starburst galaxy has experienced a short and intense burst of star formation followed by an abrupt SF drop. Its SFH, as inferred by using bagpipes carnall_bagpipes , can be approximated as a top-hat with SFR 15 and t_ b 30 Myr . The measured stellar metallicity is Z_ 10^ 2 . Therefore, to guide the eye, the curve to compare the data with is the corresponding green one.

sources.summary: The paper presents JADES-GS-z7-01-QU as a z=7.29 recently quenched dwarf galaxy with weak lines, a minor Balmer break, rapid recent quenching, and photometry challenging simulations.

quotes.quote: This new observational era begins with the serendipitous spectroscopic discovery of a recently-quenched galaxy at z = 7.29 JADES-GS-z7-01-QU hereafter, Looser2023 which was selected for deep (28 h) NIRSpec PRISM follow-up by the JADES GTO team based solely off its HST + NIRCam photometry indicating that it was a robust high-redshift Lyman-break candidate. The subsequent spectroscopy revealed that it was much more than that, actually being the first observational example of a quiescent dwarf galaxy ( M_* 10^ 8.7 M _ ) in the EoR with no emission lines (the otherwise typically strong \ + \ being non-detected) and a minor Balmer break ( 0.3 mag). As pointed out by Looser2023 , this young quiescent galaxy (which they refer to as ``(mini-)quenched'', depending on if it is a temporarily/permanently quenched galaxy) would not be identified as passive using the traditional rest-frame UVJ colour diagram Williams2009 due to its weak Balmer break.

Furthermore the deep NIRSpec continuum spectroscopy of JADES-GS-z7-01-QU also places valuable constraints on its star-formation history (SFH). This can yield further insights on star formation and feedback processes in these first dwarf galaxies, as theoretical models are required to reproduce the inferred histories within a physically-motivated framework that describes the gas flows in these systems. Utilising the SED-fitting code Bagpipes Carnall2018 , Looser2023 infer JADES-GS-z7-01-QU to have undergone a short 30 Myr burst of star formation, followed by abrupt quenching 10 Myr prior to the epoch of observation. Using the SERRA cosmological zoom-in simulations and an analytical model, respectively, Gelli2023 and Gelli2023b find that supernova feedback operates too slowly, releasing insufficient energy to reproduce the observed star formation history in this 10^ 8.7 M _ galaxy. They argue that the observed rapid quenching is therefore due to either (dusty) radiation-driven winds powered by young massive stars in a high specific star formation rate (sSFR) system or active galactic nucleus (AGN) feedback. Likewise, Dome2024 find that high-redshift galaxies in the VELA and IllustrisTNG simulations are not bursty enough on small time-scales ( 40 Myr) to reproduce the photometry (and thus the SFH) of JADES-GS-z7-01-QU, perhaps requiring further calibration of sub-grid models.

sources.summary: The paper compares simulations to JADES-GS-z7-01-QU, finding its photometry requires artificially bursty recent star formation and noting possible dust obscuration or an obscured AGN.

quotes.quote: To bridge the gap with observations, we search for the \ mini-quenching event that is most similar to JADES-GS-z7-01-QU ( z=7.3 and M_ = 5 10^8 \ M_ ). \ galaxies at z 7 span the mass range M_ 5.3 10^6 -7.4 10^8 \ M_ , yet the few galaxies with M_ 10^8 \ M_ are far from undergoing mini-quenching. We thus focus on one galaxy (labeled V5 and highlighted in Fig. f_dms ) and its mini-quenching event at z=6.7 and M_ = 2.1 10^7 \ M_ as the reference galaxy to compare to JADES-GS-z7-01-QU. The account for differences in redshift and stellar mass between V5 and JADES-GS-z7-01-QU, we apply flux correction factors d_L(7.3)^2/d_L(6.7)^2 ( d_L(z) being the luminosity distance at redshift z ) and M_ , V5 /M_ , GS-z7-01-QU assuming mass-to-light ratio scaling, see Schombert_2019 . However, incorporating these correction factors we find that the flux density of JADES-GS-z7-01-QU is about a factor of 5 stronger than V5 (see Fig. f_seds , right panel).

Only when allowing a maximum burst of star formation (top-hat SFH), matching t_ quench = 15 Myr with the observed one, can the normalization of SEDs be reconciled. While the exact value observed in the red filter F444W is hard to reproduce in mocks, the overall SED shape inferred for a top-hat SFH is in good agreement with JADES-GS-z7-01-QU, including Balmer absorption lines, lack of emission lines, and UV continuum. There is a possibility that JADES-GS-z7-01-QU is in fact an obscured AGN, which would not only provide a mechanism for abrupt quenching but possibly explain the flux discrepancy in the F444W filter.

sources.summary: The paper examines JADES-GS-z7-01-QU as a z=7.3 seemingly quiescent galaxy, arguing bursty star formation and patchy dust can explain its blue continuum and missing emission lines.

quotes.quote: However, the exact spectral energy distribution (SED) of JADES-GS-z7-01-QU could not be reproduced so far see, e.g., gelli23 . In this study, we aim at explaining the observation of a galaxy such as by two (not mutually exclusive) ideas: (i) the stochastic variations in the SFH of high- z galaxies (Section sec:stoch ), and (ii) spatial variations in the dust attenuation (Section sec:dust ).

The seemingly quiescent galaxy at z=7.3 is a curious case. The combination of lacking emission lines and blue UV to optical ratio is difficult to reconcile in simulations and theoretical models. Specifically, a galaxy like would not be classified as ``quiescent'' on common color diagrams (Figure fig:uvj_model ), but rather as a galaxy with a short-term deficit of star formation. We have explored the observational properties of galaxies such as in relation to (i) a stochastic varying (bursty) star formation, and (ii) a spatially varying dust attenuation causing a blue and red composite spectrum. The observed properties of can be achieved shortly after a starburst (Figure fig:uvj_model ).

sources.summary: The paper reports a z=7.3 quiescent galaxy with absent nebular emission, detected Balmer features, rapid quenching after an intense burst, and a stellar mass of 4-6 10^8 M_.

quotes.quote: Here we report the discovery of a quiescent galaxy at z = 7.3, when the Universe was only 700 Myr old - about 5 of its current age. The /NIRSpec spectrum of this galaxy from our JADES programme exhibits a complete absence of nebular emission lines, while the Balmer break and drop are unambiguously detected. We infer that this galaxy experienced a short and intense burst of star formation followed by rapid quenching, about 10--20 Myr before the epoch of observation. Particularly interesting is that the mass of this quiescent galaxy is only 4-6 10^8 M_ . This mass range is sensitive to various feedback mechanisms that can result in temporary or permanent quiescence.

gmw_00004800.name: S427
gmw_00004800.overview: S427 is an infrared-faint radio source that harbors an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0809.4922, arXiv:1011.2391]. It exhibits a point-like morphology with almost no extended structure [arXiv:1506.02883], and its properties are consistent with a high-redshift compact steep-spectrum source [arXiv:0809.4922, arXiv:1011.2391].

sources.summary: The paper reports VLBI detection of the IFRS S427, finding compact non-thermal AGN emission, significant extension, steep spectrum, and properties consistent with a high-redshift compact steep-spectrum source.

quotes.quote: The image of S427 in Fig. fig:S427 displays a slightly, though significantly extended point source, with the highest sidelobes at a level of 0.3 mJy. Even though the extension is weak, it is unlikely to be an imaging artefact because a) the image quality could be improved considerably by including the extension in the model, and b) because the flux density measured on the shortest baseline was about a factor of two higher than that measured on the longest baseline. The peak flux density in the image is 8.2 mJy and the integrated flux density is 12.5 mJy.

Second, the extension visible in Fig. fig:S427 allows one to make statements about the source geometry and to compare it to observations at lower resolution. S427 has an arcsec-scale flux density of (21.4 1.07) mJy ( Middelberg2008a ), 58 of which we recovered on baselines longer than 500 k , or on scales smaller than 410 mas, and 33 of which are detected on baselines longer than 6.5 M , or on scales smaller than 32 mas.

The main result of our observations is that S427 harbours an AGN, and that it is not simply a radio lobe of an unidentified radio galaxy. The size, spectrum, and radio and IR luminosity of the detected IFRS S427 are consistent with those of a high-redshift Compact Steep-Spectrum source, and are inconsistent with a standard L _* galaxy at z < 1, or a FR I/II galaxy at any redshift.

sources.summary: The paper describes ES427 as a strong, compact IFRS whose high radio-to-infrared ratio and VLBI-imaged CSS-like properties lead the authors to classify it as an AGN.

quotes.quote: ES427 is similar to ES509 in that it is a strong, compact radio source at 1.4 GHz with a spectral index of 1.08 , and has a S20/S3.6 ratio of 9070. However, there are two significant differences to ES509: ES427 is unpolarised in all our radio images ( < 1 ), but was detected and imaged by using VLBI, who suggested that its properties are consistent with it being a CSS. We therefore conclude that this is an AGN.

sources.summary: The paper treats S427 as an IFRS with a reported 3.6 micron SERVS counterpart, point-like morphology with little extended structure, and otherwise radio-only detections in the Herschel-observed IFRS set.

quotes.quote: We note that Maini2013submitted find a 3 counterpart at 3.6 m based in the Spitzer Extragalactic Representative Volume Survey (SERVS; Mauduit2012 ) for IFRS S427 after the observations with Herschel were carried out.
IFRS S427, S509, and S798 are point-like sources and show almost no extended structure.

This allowed the templates to be scaled to match the mean radio flux density of the IFRS observed with Herschel ( S_ 1.4 GHz 15 mJy), since these sources are solely detected in the radio regime apart from the weak 3.6 m counterpart of S427.

gmw_00004801.name: S703
gmw_00004801.overview: S703 is an infrared-faint radio source located in the Chandra Deep Field South [arXiv:1506.02883]. It is classified as an active galactic nucleus and is a compact, strong radio source with a steep spectrum and polarization at all measured frequencies [arXiv:1011.2391].

sources.summary: The paper locates S703 in the CDFS, notes it lacks Chandra coverage, rejects a double-lobe interpretation because the other radio component has a 3.6 micron counterpart, and keeps S703 as a proper IFRS.

quotes.quote: IFRS S703, located in the CDFS field, has not been covered by the CDFS Chandra 4 Ms survey Xue2011 .
Infrared-faint radio source S703 is located in CDFS while the other five sources are located in the ELAIS-S1 field.

For S703, in contrast, it might be suggested because of the additional radio component at around RA 03h35m33.5s Dec 27^ 26'40" and the bridge to the IFRS itself that the IFRS is part of a double lobe radio source with the IFRS and the additional component as radio lobes and the host galaxy in between those two components. However, the additional radio component shows a weak 3.6 m counterpart, excluding this component as a radio lobe. Therefore, S703 can be considered as a proper IFRS.

sources.summary: The paper classifies CS703 as an AGN, describing it as a very compact, strong, steep-spectrum IFRS with a high radio-to-infrared ratio and polarization at all measured frequencies.

quotes.quote: CS703 is a very compact, strong radio source with a well-defined, rather steep spectrum with index 0.99 and a high S20/S3.6 ratio of 8980. The non-detection at 74 MHz can probably be attributed to synchrotron self-absorption. It is polarised at all frequencies (7 at 1.4 GHz), and since we do not see any evidence of it being resolved, we classify this source as an AGN.

gmw_00004802.name: S749
gmw_00004802.overview: S749 is an infrared-faint radio source [arXiv:1506.02883] classified as an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1011.2391]. It exhibits a somewhat extended radio structure at 1.4 GHz [arXiv:1506.02883, arXiv:1011.2391], a steep radio spectrum, and unpolarized emission [arXiv:1011.2391].

sources.summary: The paper describes S749 as an extended radio IFRS near the noisy edge of the field; a wrong Herschel position required custom flux uncertainty estimation and exclusion from stacking.

quotes.quote: Infrared-faint radio source S749 shows an extended structure in the radio map, too. However, this source is located close to the edge of the ATLAS ELAIS-S1 field and, therefore, the noise is higher than for the other IFRS discussed in this work. In the scheduling of the Herschel observations, a slightly wrong position of IFRS S749 had been used. Thus, this source is not located in the centre of the Herschel maps but closer to the edge, resulting in a higher noise compared to the other sources, particularly at shorter wavelengths.

For S749, where the IFRS was not in the centre of the Herschel maps as discussed in Sect. observation , we placed 100 similar apertures in a field with a maximum distance to the source of five times the full width at half maximum (FWHM) of a point source to estimate the flux uncertainty.
However, we excluded IFRS S749 from the stacking analysis because of the higher noise in the FIR maps (see discussion in Sect. observation ).

sources.summary: The paper classifies ES749 as an AGN, citing its somewhat extended 1.4 GHz radio morphology, high radio-to-infrared ratio, steep spectrum, weak high-frequency flux, and lack of polarization.

quotes.quote: ES749 is a somewhat extended radio source at 1.4 GHz with a high S20/S3.6 of 4170. Its extended morphology suggests that it is AGN-driven, and this is confirmed by its rather steep spectrum with index 1.08 . Its 8.6 GHz flux density is lower than expected from lower-frequency extrapolation, and it is unpolarised ( < 7 ). We classify this source as an AGN.

gmw_00004803.name: S973
gmw_00004803.overview: S973 is an infrared-faint radio source that exhibits an extended structure consisting of two radio components [arXiv:1506.02883]. The radio-brighter component is considered the center of the host galaxy, while the weaker component is likely a jet [arXiv:1506.02883].

sources.summary: The paper describes S973 as an extended two-component IFRS, adopts the radio-brighter component as the host center with the weaker component likely a jet, and notes tentative SPIRE cleaned-map detections.

quotes.quote: S973 consists of two radio components. Here, we used the position of the strongest component as the position of the source, in contrast to Middelberg2008ELAIS-S1 who used the centre between both components.

Infrared-faint radio source S973 shows an extended structure and has been listed as two components (RA 00h38m44.723m Dec 43^ 19'14.58'' and RA 00h38m43.489s Dec 43^ 19'26.94'' ) by Middelberg2008ELAIS-S1 , with the source position in between the two components. Here, however, we considered the position of the second, radio-brighter component to be the centre of the host with the weaker component more likely to be a jet. However, the choice of host position does not affect our overall conclusion.

For S973, we found 3 detections in the cleaned maps at 350 m and 500 m. The positions of these two FIR counterparts were in agreement with each other and were 12 distant from the IFRS radio position, which is only slightly below our distance criterion.

gmw_00004804.name: 2MASX J04035024-0239275
gmw_00004804.overview: 2MASX J04035024-0239275 is a bright red elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1803.09277]. It acts as a strong gravitational lens for a background galaxy, with an Einstein radius consistent with an isothermal density profile [arXiv:1803.09277].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASX J04035024-0239275 as a nearby red elliptical strong lens, models it with an isothermal profile, and finds about 18% dark matter within the Einstein radius.

quotes.quote: 2MASX J04035024-0239275 is a bright red elliptical galaxy at R.A.=04 ^ h 03 ^ m 50 26, Dec= 02 ^ 39 27 64 with a radial velocity cz 19,952 km s ^ 1 2009MNRAS.399..683J , corresponding to an angular scale of 1.256 kpc arcsec ^ 1 .

We reported the discovery of a strong lensed galaxy at z =0.1915 by the lens 2MASX J04035024-0239275 at z =0.0661 in IFS observations from MUSE at the 8.2m VLT of Cerro Paranal. Two images from the source are identified and, after a careful analysis, confirmed as lensed images coming from the same source. We modeled the lens with a SIE mass profile and a S ersic background source, finding an Einstein radius of 1.45 0.04 (1.9 kpc), consistent with an isothermal density profile. Independent of our discovery, a recent archival search of public MUSE data by collier2018 also identified this galaxy as a strong gravitational lens.

While they predict the dark matter fraction from cosmological hydrodynamical simulations, we derive our stellar mass estimate from the SSP synthesis with STARLIGHT to the MUSE data integrated within the Einstein radius. In both cases, we find a consistent fraction of dark matter within the Einstein radius ( 18 ).

gmw_00004805.name: 3C 119
gmw_00004805.overview: Associated with a possible quasar at a redshift of 1.023 [arXiv:1005.2950, arXiv:1806.11125], 3C 119 is a compact steep-spectrum radio source [arXiv:1806.11125]. It features a complex, spiral-like jet structure [arXiv:1005.2950, arXiv:1806.11125] that exhibits wiggles and interacts with dense environmental clouds [arXiv:1005.2950, arXiv:1806.11125].

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 119 as a possible quasar with a strong core-jet structure, a variable likely core, jet wiggles, polarised blobs, and high rotation measure from jet-cloud interaction.

quotes.quote: 3C 119, which is associated with a possible quasar, has a complex shape when observed at low resolution, but has strong core-jet structure on resolutions of a few mas. We suggest that Component A, which shows a convex spectrum, flux density variability, and either no, or at best marginal, polarisation above the detection limits, is the core. The jet exhibits ``wiggles''. From published images at sub-mas resolution, 3C 119 appears to maintain its collimation while presenting an overall spiral-like shape.

Two bright, polarised blobs are seen along the jet. The brighter of these has a high rotation measure and strong depolarisation between 8.4 and 5 GHz, which we interpret as an indication of strong interaction between the jet and a cloud in the ISM, possibly a dense narrow-line region (NLR) cloud. Although this interaction does not disrupt the jet, the jet does seem to change direction.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3CR 119 as a compact steep-spectrum radio source with complex jet morphology likely shaped by dense environmental clouds and point-like X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: 3CR 119 is a CSS radio source odea98 and its updated redshift is z =1.023 eracleous94 . The source, which in our VLA 8.4 GHz map is a compact 0 .5 structure ( 5 kpc at the source redshift), shows a very complex radio morphology on the 1-kpc scale observed with the VLBI at 5 GHz: the nucleus was identified with a weak compact ( < 0 .003) component with flat spectrum, while the extended structure has a spiral-like form with two knots along the jet fanti86 . This distorted morphology was interpreted as the result of the source interaction with dense clouds in its environment and this idea was supported by the presence of a large gradient of the rotation measure (i.e., 2300 rad/m ^2 /mas in the 8.4-GHz band) and strong depolarisation between 8.4 and 5 GHz nan99,mantovani10 . The X-ray snapshot observation revealed point-like emission on a scale of 2 , corresponding to 15 kpc at the source redshift, shown in Figure fig:3CR119 .

gmw_00004806.name: 3C 343
gmw_00004806.overview: The compact steep-spectrum source 3C 343 is a quasar that contains two bright polarized emission peaks [arXiv:1005.2950]. These peaks are embedded in a diffuse region of weak emission, which includes a curved radio jet [arXiv:1005.2950].

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 343 as a quasar with two polarised peaks, diffuse curved jet emission, complex field directions, high RM, and an uncertain core candidate east of component A.

quotes.quote: The CSS source 3C 343 contains two peaks of emission and a curved jet embedded in more diffuse emission. It exhibits complex field directions near the emission peaks, which indicate rest-frame RM values in excess of 6000 rad m ^ 2 . The locations of the cores in 3C 318 and 3C 343 are not clear.

Among the three sources observed, 3C 343, which is associated with a quasar, presents the most unusual structure. The two brightest polarised components are embedded in a diffuse region of weak emission. It is unlikely that either is the core of the source. We suggest the component clearly visible to the east of component A in the X-band image, instead, as the possible core candidate.

gmw_00004807.name: UDG 32
gmw_00004807.overview: UDG 32 is an ultra-diffuse galaxy located in the Hydra I cluster [arXiv:2107.04486]. It is spatially coincident with the stellar filaments of NGC 3314A, suggesting it may have formed from filament material via a tidal origin [arXiv:2107.04486].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UDG 32 as an exceptionally faint, diffuse Hydra I UDG embedded in NGC 3314A filaments, possibly formed from filament material as a detached bound system with properties favoring tidal formation.

quotes.quote: Within these filaments, at a projected distance of 40 kpc from the galaxy, we discover an ultra-diffuse galaxy (UDG) with a central surface brightness of _ 0,g 26 mag arcsec ^ 2 and effective radius R_e 3.8 kpc. This UDG, named UDG 32, is one of the faintest and most diffuse low-surface brightness galaxies in the Hydra I cluster. Based on the available data, we cannot exclude that this object is just seen in projection on top of the stellar filaments, thus being instead a foreground or background UDG in the cluster. However, the clear spatial coincidence of UDG 32 with the stellar filaments of NGC 3314A suggests that it might have formed from the material in the filaments, becoming a detached, gravitationally bound system.

In this scenario, the origin of UDG 32 depends on the nature of the stellar filaments in NGC 3314A, which is still unknown. They could result from ram-pressure stripping or have a tidal origin. In this letter, we focus on the comparison of the observed properties of the stellar filaments and UDG 32, and speculate about their possible origin. The relatively red colour ( g-=0.54 0.14 mag) of the UDG, similar to that of the disk in NGC 3314A, combined with an age older than 1 Gyr, and the possible presence of a few compact stellar systems, all point towards a tidal formation scenario inferred for the UDG 32.

gmw_00004808.name: ULAS 140515.4+095931
gmw_00004808.overview: ULAS 140515.4+095931 is a strong gravitational lens system containing a quasar at a redshift of 1.810 [arXiv:1109.4325]. The system appears as a double source in optical wavebands, with the fainter component likely associated with a lensing galaxy at a probable redshift of 0.66 [arXiv:1109.4325].

sources.summary: The paper presents ULAS 140515.4+095931 as a likely quasar lens: optical components, infrared emission, distinct colors, and a probable lens-galaxy redshift support the interpretation.

quotes.quote: This system, containing a quasar of redshift 1.810, was selected for observation because of a noticeable apparent decrease in separation with wavelength of its two components. It appears as a double system in most optical wavebands, but has an additional component to the east in the infrared. During the observations, the spectrograph slit was positioned N-S along the two brightest components. These are separated by 1 95 (Fig. 3) with emission lines clearly present in both components. Spectra reveal an approximately 2:1 ratio in flux between the z=1.81 quasar components, with very prominent associated absorption in both \ and Ly .

The continuum colours of the two condensations are different, and consistent with the fainter quasar component being associated with a lensing galaxy. The lensing galaxy itself displays a likely 4000 \ break, and possible emission lines at 655 and 660 nm; although these are of low signal-to-noise and require confirmation, this would imply an identification of CaH,K and a redshift of 0.66 for the lensing galaxy. Like ULAS 074352.6+245743, this object has independently been identified as a likely lens by SQLS (unpublished; Oguri, private communication).

gmw_00004809.name: ULAS 152720.1+014139
gmw_00004809.overview: ULAS 152720.1+014139 is a strong gravitational lens system consisting of two lensed images of a quasar at a redshift of 1.439 [arXiv:1109.4325]. The lensing object is likely an early-type galaxy with an estimated redshift of approximately 0.3 [arXiv:1109.4325].

sources.summary: The paper calls ULAS 152720.1+014139 a clear new lens system, with two quasar images, color differences excluding a binary quasar, and evidence for an early-type lens galaxy.

quotes.quote: This is a clear example of a new lens system. It consists of two images of a z =1.439 quasar, separated by 2 4. The spectrum around all of the major emission lines shows evidence of emission lines being clearly present in the secondary. The object moved along the slit between individual exposures in this object, but in all the exposures the two objects are seen with the same separation. The spectrum (Fig. 4) shows the , \ and \ lines in both objects, with a flux ratio of 7.0 0.5:1.

The two condensations in the optical and infrared images have very different colours, due to the weakness of the secondary lensed quasar image and consequent dominance of the lens galaxy over it at all but the bluest wavelengths. The different colours also immediately rule out the hypothesis that we are dealing with a binary quasar system as opposed to a gravitational lens. No absorption lines are present which might suggest the redshift of the galaxy, but the pronounced upturn in the spectrum between 500 and 600 nm is likely to be the 400 nm break feature of an early-type galaxy, leading to a probable redshift of about 0.3 for the lens galaxy.

gmw_00004810.name: ULAS 152938.9+103803
gmw_00004810.overview: ULAS 152938.9+103803 is a quasar at a redshift of z=1.97 [arXiv:1109.4325]. It exhibits a nearby secondary image and asymmetric spectral profiles, which provide evidence that the system is a gravitationally lensed quasar [arXiv:1109.4325].

sources.summary: The paper finds ULAS 152938.9+103803 has a nearby secondary image, extended line emission, and repeated asymmetric spectral profiles that support a lensed-quasar interpretation.

quotes.quote: This z=1.97 quasar clearly has a secondary close to it. Fitting around the \ line, as previously described, yields a secondary in the emission line with a flux ratio of 4.9 0.5 between the primary and secondary (Fig. 5) and a notional significance of 10 . Fitting to the \ line, after subtraction, also shows an extended line, although here the continuum is more difficult to subtract.

Both the absorption line and emission line spatial profiles show asymmetry in the same direction, in all three frames. The fact that all three frames behave similarly suggests that telescope guiding errors are not the cause of the asymmetry in the spatial profiles. The fact that the asymmetry appears in the same direction in emission and absorption suggests that mis-subtraction of the continuum is not responsible, as an under-subtraction in the emission part of the line would correspond to an over-subtraction in the absorption part. This result should be robust for any type of mis-subtraction, including no subtraction of the continuum at all. We therefore conclude that the spectra provide evidence for an asymmetric profile of line emission, and hence the presence of a secondary image of the quasar.

gmw_00004811.name: UGC 5189A
gmw_00004811.overview: UGC 5189A is a low-metallicity galaxy [arXiv:1507.06059] that features a warped disk [arXiv:1706.00881] and has hosted the luminous supernova SN 2010jl [arXiv:1507.06059, arXiv:1012.3461]. The galaxy contains a bright, metal-poor star-forming region known as SB 80 [arXiv:1909.10574, arXiv:1706.00881] which is associated with a detected X-ray source [arXiv:1909.10574].

sources.summary: UGC 5189A is described as the low-metallicity host galaxy of SN 2010jl and a weak X-ray emitter whose modeled flux and absorption do not significantly contaminate the supernova.

quotes.quote: stoll11 found that the host galaxy for is of low metallicity, supporting the emerging trend that luminous supernovae (SNe) occur in low metallicity environments. They determined the metallicity Z of the SN region to be 0.3  Z_ . We take this upper limit as the metallicity of the gas in the galaxy.

The flux in Box C matches the total flux from Boxes A and B within the error bars. In addition, the UGC 5189A flux is 40 times weaker than the SN flux. Thus the contamination to the SN flux due to UGC 5189A is insignificant in the data. Now, we carry out a joint fit to the spectra from UGC 5189A (Box B) at all five epochs of observation with an absorbed power law model.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 5189, host of SN 2010jl, as metal-poor, with low gas-phase oxygen abundance supported by SDSS-based strong-line and direct-method estimates near the supernova site.

quotes.quote: Its host galaxy, UGC 5189, has a low gas-phase oxygen abundance ( 12 + log(O/H) = 8.2 0.1 ), which reinforces the emerging trend that over-luminous core-collapse supernovae are found in the low-metallicity tail of the galaxy distribution, similar to the known trend for the hosts of long GRBs. We compile oxygen abundances from the literature and from our own observations of UGC 5189, and we present an unpublished spectrum of the luminous type Ic SN 2010gx that we use to estimate its host metallicity.

prieto10 point out that the supernova's host galaxy, UGC 5189, has been shown to be metal-poor based on a spectrum obtained by the SDSS survey a few arcseconds away from the site of the supernova. It is included in the tremonti04 DR4 catalog of galaxy metallicities, which estimates 12 + log(O/H) = 8.15 0.1  dex based on strong recombination and forbidden emission lines in the spectrum. pilyugin07 estimate 12 + log(O/H) = 8.3  dex from the same spectrum based on the ``direct'' photoionization-based abundance method using an estimate of the electron temperature ( T_e ) from the [ O 3 ] 4363 auroral line.

sources.summary: The paper treats SB 80 as a star-forming region in UGC 5189 with nebular emission, repeated Chandra coverage, and a detected coincident X-ray source.

quotes.quote: SB 80 is a bright star-forming region embedded in UGC 5189 at 46 Mpc distance, and hosts moderately metal-poor gas at 12+ O/H =8.24 0.06 senchynaUltravioletSpectraExtreme2017 . Our deep Keck/ESI spectrum reveals both a broad stellar and a clearly-differentiated nebular component to , with the narrow component measured at /H =0.0031 0.0003 . This system was observed in seven different pointings targeting the luminous supernova 2010jl positioned 1.7' away from the star-forming region under study; stollSN2010jlUGC2011 from 2010--2018.

The longest and most recent of these observations (ObsID: 19164; 46.8 ks, taken 2018-01-03) reveals a source at 0.2'' measured separation from the optical target, with =4.9 2.2 38 erg/s ( 2.0_ 0.8 ^ +0.9 10^ 15 erg/s/cm^2 ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies SB 80 as a bright region in UGC 5189's warped disk and adopts a redshift-based distance consistent with prior work, setting the COS aperture scale.

quotes.quote: SB 80: (SDSS J094256.74+092816.2, Plate-MJD-Fiber 1305-52757-269) A bright region embedded in the warped disk of UGC 5189. We find a distance of 46.5 Mpc from the infall-corrected redshift, in good agreement with the similarly-derived result of 46.4 Mpc Baillard2011 . The aperture radius subtends 280 pc at this distance.

gmw_00004812.name: 1ES 2322-409
gmw_00004812.overview: 1ES 2322-409 is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1810.04641] and standard high-frequency peaked BL Lac source [arXiv:2311.12695] located at a redshift of z = 0.1738 [arXiv:2012.05176]. It is a very-high-energy gamma-ray emitter [arXiv:1810.04641, arXiv:2012.05176] that hosts a radio jet extending toward the east [arXiv:2310.10206].

sources.summary: The paper reports H.E.S.S. discovery of VHE emission from the BL Lac object 1ES 2322-409, measuring a weak Crab-level spectrum and emphasizing its uncertain redshift.

quotes.quote: A hotspot at a position compatible with the BL Lac object was serendipitously detected with H.E.S.S. during observations performed in 2004 and 2006 on the blazar PKS 2316 423. Additional data on were taken in 2011 and 2012, leading to a total live-time of 22.3h. Point-like very-high-energy (VHE; E> 100 ) ray emission is detected from a source centred on the position, with an excess of 116.7 events at a significance of 6.0 . The average VHE ray spectrum is well described with a power law with a photon index =3.40 0.66_ stat 0.20_ sys and an integral flux (E> 200 GeV ) = (3.11 0.71_ stat 0.62_ sys ) 10^ 12 ^ 2 ^ 1 , which corresponds to 1.1 of the Crab nebula flux above 200 GeV .

The redshift of is unknown. The value z 0.174 reported by Jones2009 should be considered with caution, as it is based on a low signal-to-noise-ratio spectrum which shows weak evidence for absorption lines corresponding to this redshift, namely a single line at 6900 . Beyond the fact that this is not enough to indicate unambiguously the redshift of the source, it possibly corresponds to residual telluric absorption. This paper presents the discovery of VHE ray emission from with the H.E.S.S. telescopes (Section  sec:H.E.S.S. ).

sources.summary: The paper confirms 1ES 2322 409 as a z=0.1738 TeV emitter from weak absorption features in a high-S/N spectrum.

quotes.quote: 1ES 2322 409 is a bright TeV emitter, serendipitously discovered by H.E.S.S. see HESS19 . The redshift of this source is uncertain, a value z = 0.174 has been suggested on the basis of a low S/N 6dF spectrum Jon04, Jon09 . We note that the redshift measurement was mostly based on a weak feature at 6900 , in a region where there is a strong telluric absorption. A higher signal to noise FORS/VLT spectrum was featureless Lan13 .

In order to clarify this issue we took a high signal-to-noise EFOSC2 spectrum (Fig. fig_spec3 , second row). The source was at magnitude R _ =16.1 and the spectrum is dominated by a non-thermal continuum. In the continuum we were able to detect several weak features: CaHK, CaIG, Mgb and NaID at a common redshift z = 0.1738. We consider this a firm redshift.

sources.summary: The paper describes 1ES 2322 409 as an H.E.S.S. hard-ray excess with a soft VHE spectrum, published VLBI jet, and an eastward TANAMI S-band morphology.

quotes.quote: 2322 409: While observing PKS 2316 423, abdalla19 detected an excess in hard rays in the position of 1ES 2322 409 with H.E.S.S. The VHE spectrum can be described with a power-law with the soft photon index of =3.40 0.86 . The only published VLBI image of the source is presented by schinzel17 with the VLBA at 7.62 GHz. The source has a jet extending 4 mas towards the northeast. Our S band image shows a similar morphology, but the jet points towards east.

sources.summary: The paper classifies 1ES 2322-409 as a standard HBL rather than an eHBL, with a relatively soft spectral index and synchrotron peak below 0.3 keV.

quotes.quote: This paper reports on recent observations carried out using \ and \ of five sources: 1ES 0120+340 (redshift z=0.272 ), RGB J0710+591 ( z=0.125 ), 1ES 1101-232 ( z=0.186 ), 1ES 1741+196 ( z=0.084 ), and 1ES 2322-409 ( z=0.1736 ), each displaying a unique range of spectral characteristics. Among these, RGB J0710+591 and 1ES 1101-232 are well-known for being extreme TeV sources with hard intrinsic TeV spectra. Although TeV data is unavailable for 1ES 0120+340, it presents itself as a potential \ candidate with hard X-ray and GeV spectra. Additionally, 1ES 1741+196 demonstrates hints of extreme Syn nature in its XRT spectrum, while 1ES 2322-409 appears as a standard HBL.

We selected five HBL sources, 1ES 0120+340, RGB J0710+591, 1ES 1101-232, 1ES 1741+196 and 1ES 2322-409 for this work based on the available AstroSat data from our proposed observations. Four of them (all except 1ES 2322-409) are known to exhibit the nature of eHBLs.
However, 1ES 2322-409 is an exception, satisfying the criteria of a regular HBL with a relatively soft spectral index and synchrotron peak located below 0.3 keV.

gmw_00004813.name: Y3
gmw_00004813.overview: Y3 is a galaxy identified as a plausible high-redshift candidate [arXiv:1104.2520]. It qualifies as a high-redshift candidate under a luminosity prior, and its non-detection at 24 microns helps rule out certain low-redshift templates [arXiv:1104.2520].

sources.summary: Y3 remains a plausible high-redshift candidate: its 24 non-detection rules out one low-redshift template, low-redshift fits mismatch optical/J bands, and it survives a luminosity prior.

quotes.quote: Y3: Although the 24 \ non-detection of this object helps to exclude at least one of the GRASIL templates at low- z , it is still consistent with other templates at low and high- z . Probably the main failure of the low- z templates is again the mismatch in J and in the optical bands.
As seen in this figure, four candidates still exhibit a dominant high- z solution, namely z1, Y3, Y4, and J1, whereas one candidate is degenerate between low and high- z (Y5).
On the other hand, only Y3 qualifies as a high- z candidate when using a luminosity prior (see Sect. photoz_prior ).

gmw_00004814.name: Y4
gmw_00004814.overview: Y4 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy [arXiv:1104.2520]. It has weak constraints at 24 microns and is formally detected in the z_1 band, though it remains undetected in the 5.8, 8, and z_2 bands [arXiv:1104.2520].

sources.summary: Y4 has weak 24 constraints; nondetections at 5.8 and 8 are notable because both redshift solutions predict marginal detections, while its z_1 detection is not repeated.

quotes.quote: Y4: As for Y3, the upper limit detection at 24 \ provides weak additional constraints on the redshift. The non-detection at 5.8 and 8 \ are interesting because both solutions at low and high- z seem to predict a detection at 2-3 level. The object is formally detected at the 2 level in the z_1 band, but it is not detected in the z_2 image.

gmw_00004815.name: Y7
gmw_00004815.overview: The galaxy Y7 is a candidate high-redshift source [arXiv:1104.2520]. It exhibits an observed 24-micron flux that is unusually high for a relatively isolated object, regardless of its template redshift [arXiv:1104.2520].

sources.summary: Y7's 24 flux is similar for high- and low-redshift fits, so MIPS photometry alone cannot reject a high-redshift solution despite an unusually high observed flux.

quotes.quote: Y7: For this object, the expected 24 \ fluxes for the best-fit at high and low redshifts are very similar. In particular, the flux of the z 9 fit is even quite similar to the brightest flux predicted from dusty galaxy templates at z 1.6 . Therefore, this object cannot be excluded as a genuine high- z based on its MIPS photometry without introducing a luminosity prior. Besides, the observed 24 \ flux seems too high irrespective of the template redshift for a relatively isolated source (see Fig. trombino ).

gmw_00004816.name: Y8
gmw_00004816.overview: The object Y8 exhibits stellar-like colors and a high stellarity index [arXiv:1104.2520]. However, its apparent detection at 24 microns rules out a Galactic late-type star, suggesting it is instead a dusty, low-redshift galaxy [arXiv:1104.2520].

sources.summary: Y8 has stellar-like colors and high stellarity, but an apparent blended 24 detection rules out a Galactic late-type star; the paper favors a dusty low-redshift galaxy.

quotes.quote: Object Y8, which also shows a high stellarity index, has colors indistinguishable within the errors from late-type stars (cf. \ also Fig. \ CC_ZJK ). However, although its flux could not be properly extracted due to blending, it seems to be detected at 24 , which excludes a Galactic late-type star. Most likely, it is therefore a dusty, low redshift galaxy, cf. \ Fig. \ fig_mips .

gmw_00004817.name: J1
gmw_00004817.overview: J1 is a lensed galaxy originally identified as a high-redshift J-band dropout candidate [arXiv:1104.2520, arXiv:1205.6815]. However, spectroscopic observations place the galaxy at a lower redshift of z=2.082, identifying it as either a heavily reddened starburst or an old stellar system with a minor secondary burst of star formation [arXiv:1205.6815].

sources.summary: J1 is the lone J-drop and faintest candidate; its best-fit SED remains high-z, but z_850/HST detection and weak 24 constraints make a high-z solution unlikely.

quotes.quote: Only one candidate, J1, fulfills the selection criteria (b) based on JHKs , and is also consistent with the z 9 J- drop selection criteria by Bouwens et al. \ (2010) (see Fig. CC_JHK ). This object was formally detected in the I band at 2 level (double-image mode), as indicated in Table tab_catalog2 , although no convincing counterpart is seen in this image (see also Fig. trombino ). This source has a counterpart detected on the HST F850LP/ACS image, as discussed in Sect. observed below.

J1: This is the faintest candidate in our sample, and the only one which is located on the central area covered by the HST. The best-fit solution is found at high- z even when including the z_ 850 band flux and a luminosity prior, as seen in Table tab_prop . However, the detection on the z_ 850 band makes the high- z solution unlikely. The upper limit detection at 24 \ provides weak additional constraints on the redshift. The detection on the HST image is fully consistent with the large break identified on ground-based images.

sources.summary: The paper shows A2667-J1 is not a z~10 dropout but a z=2.082 emission-line interloper, with either a dusty starburst or old stellar population plus secondary burst.

quotes.quote: Laporte2011 reported a very high redshift galaxy candidate: a lensed J- band dropout (A2667-J1). J1 has a photometric redshift of z=9.6-12 , the probability density function for which permits no low or intermediate z solution. We here report new spectroscopic observations of this galaxy with VLT/XShooter, which show clear 5007 , , , and \ emission and place the galaxy firmly at z=2.082 . The oxygen lines contribute only 25 to the H- band flux, and do not significantly affect the dropout selection of J1.

After correcting the broadband fluxes for line emission, we identify two roughly equally plausible natures for A2667-J1: either it is young heavily reddened starburst, or a maximally old system with a very pronounced 4000 \ break, upon which a minor secondary burst of star formation is superimposed. Fits show that to make a 3 detection of this object in the B- band ( V- band), imaging of depth AB=30.2 (29.5) would be required -- despite the relatively bright NIR magnitude, we would need optical data of equivalent depth to the Hubble Ultra Deep Field to rule out the mid- z solution on purely photometric grounds.

gmw_00004818.name: IRAS 23436+5257
gmw_00004818.overview: IRAS 23436+5257 is a bright luminous infrared galaxy that consists of two nuclei [arXiv:2006.01518]. Long-term pre-outburst mid-infrared variability hints at the presence of an obscured active galactic nucleus, likely located in the northern nucleus [arXiv:2006.01518].

sources.summary: The paper models IRAS 23436+5257 as a bright two-nucleus LIRG whose pre-outburst mid-IR variability hints at an obscured AGN in the northern nucleus.

quotes.quote: Host galaxy SED fitting IRAS 23436+5257 is a bright LIRG with an IR luminosity of L_ IR 4 10^ 11 L _ adjusted to H _0 = 70 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 sanders2003 . This LIRG was included in the SUNBIRD sample because the expected core-collapse SN (CCSN) rate inferred from its L_ IR mattila2001 is as high as 1 yr ^ 1 . This empirical relation, however, assumes a negligible contribution to the IR luminosity of the galaxy from a potential AGN.

There has not been any evidence in the literature of the presence of an AGN in IRAS 23436+5257, based on hard X-ray (14-195 keV) observations koss2013 , equivalent width of the PAH feature yamada2013 , or mid-IR W1 W2 colours from WISE assef2018a . However, the long-term pre-outburst variability in the mid-IR (see Fig. fig:iras23436_wise_spitzer ) hints at the presence of an obscured AGN in the host galaxy of AT 2017gbl, as AGN are known to show such low-amplitude smooth variability in the mid-IR kozlowski2016 .

IRAS 23436+5257 consists of two nuclei, and the model fit is based on flux densities for the whole galaxy in which the two nuclei are not resolved. In order to determine the host nucleus of the possible AGN, we compare contours from a 24 \ MIPS image with a near-IR K_s band NOT image in Fig. fig:iras23436_mips . As shown, most of the 24 \ emission from IRAS 23436+5257 originates in the Northern nucleus.

gmw_00004819.name: J1548 3216
gmw_00004819.overview: J1548 3216 is a giant double-double radio galaxy [arXiv:0912.1484, arXiv:1702.01939]. Its active galactic nucleus features newly restarted jets that propagate through a bright cocoon remnant from a previous active phase [arXiv:0912.1484].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes J1548 3216 as a double-double radio galaxy where restarted jets propagate through an old cocoon, deriving spectral and dynamical ages for its lobes.

quotes.quote: The radio galaxy J1548 3216 (PKS B1545 321) is a remarkable example of DDRG in which the newly restarted jets propagate through the remnant cocoon of a previous active phase (Subrahmanyan, Saripalli Hunstead 1996; Saripalli, Subrahmanyan Shankar 2003, hereafter referred to as SSS2003). This galaxy has recently been extensively studied by Safouris et al. (2008, hereafter referred to as S2008), especially under the aspect of an observational constraint for the 2D and 3D numerical simulations of the restarted jet provided by Clarke Burns (1991) and Clarke (1997), respectively. In S2008, the authors suggest that observational data are consistent with a picture that the restarted jets generate narrow-bow shocks, and the inner lobes in this galaxy are a mixture of cocoon plasma re-accelerated at the bow shock and new jet material re-accelerated at the termination shock. They propose that the evolution of the restarted jets and the inner lobes is strongly influenced by an entrainment of the external IGM into the pre-existing cocoon. In this paper the spectral-ageing and dynamical analyses of J1548 3216 are performed with the aim of (i) determining the synchrotron age distribution in the outer lobes of this galaxy (in the old cocoon), (ii) estimating of this age in the inner lobes, (iii) comparing these ages with the dynamical ages estimated with the DYNAGE algorithm of Machalski et al. (2007), (iv) determining the jet powers during the first and the second phase of activity, as well as other physical parameters characterizing the lobes and their environments, such as the particle density, energy density, internal pressure, magnetic field strength and its density, propagation speeds of the lobes along the jets' axis, etc.

The new low-frequency and high-frequency radio continuum maps of the double-double radio galaxy J1548 3216 (PKS 1545 321) are used to complement its already published maps at 22 and 13 cm (Saripalli, Subrahmanyan Shankar 2003; Safouris et al. 2008) and to perform both the spectral-ageing and the dynamical analysis of this remarkable giant DDRG, in which the newly restarted jets propagate through the bright cocoon formed by a previous active phase of its AGN. The current activity is indicated by the radio core. The maps of the outer and the inner radio lobes at five observing frequencies from 334 to 4860 MHz allow determination of the spectral index distribution along the outer lobes, as well as extension of the spectral index frequency range in the inner lobes.

sources.summary: The paper describes J1548 3216 as a giant DDRG and compares KDA, KDA EXT, and MJK2010 spectra, finding KDA EXT reproduces the observed spectrum better.

quotes.quote: J1548 3216 J1548 3216 (PKS B1545 321) is another example of a giant DDRG. This low-redshift radio galaxy was discovered by Saripalli et al. (2003). Its dynamical analysis was published by Safouris et al. (2008) concluding that the interruption of the jet activity was brief, no more than a small percent of the actual age of the whole source. Nevertheless, based on new low-frequency observations with the GMRT array, Machalski et al. (2010) has repeated the dynamical age analysis but applied to the opposite lobes in both the outer and the inner pairs.

The spectra resulting from the predicted flux densities S_ MOD (KDA) and S_ MOD (KDA EXT) and the goodness of the fit for each model are given in Table 7 and are shown in Figure 18. Also for this source, the KDA EXT model reproduces the observed spectrum better then the continuous injection models.

gmw_00004820.name: ESO 207-19
gmw_00004820.overview: ESO 207-19 is the brightest cluster galaxy of the galaxy cluster A3407 [arXiv:1605.02656]. It is offset from the cluster's X-ray peak by 30 kiloparsecs, but its location cannot be rejected as being on the dynamical center of the velocity field [arXiv:1605.02656].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO 207-19 as A3407's brightest cluster galaxy, offset from the X-ray peak but without significant peculiar motion relative to the velocity field.

quotes.quote: The brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) of A3407, ESO 207-19, an object of absolute magnitude M_R=-22.64 , is 51^ ( 30 kpc) away from this peak, and 7^ ( 240 kpc) away from the luminosity-weighted centroid of the distribution (see Figure fig4 ).

Also in this figure we depict the position of the BCG galaxy , V_ BCG =12307 24 km s^ 1 . Following TCG we test if this galaxy can be central in the A3407 + A3408 velocity field using S_V = |C_ BI V_ BCG | [ v_ cl ^2 + v_ BCG ^2 ] ^ 1/2 , where v_ cl and v_ BCG are the errors in the biweight and the BCG velocities. Values of S_V 2 mean the BCG peculiar motion is probably significant. For our sample, S_V 1.29 , indicating that we can not reject the BCG location as being on the dynamical center of the field (see Figure fig6 ). Indeed, the BCG is offset relative to the X-ray peak by only 30 kpc, and offset scales up to 50 kpc can be explained by small amplitude oscillations of the central galaxy around the bottom of the cluster potential well see LC .

gmw_00004821.name: J1404
gmw_00004821.overview: The far-infrared-bright galaxy J1404 exhibits double-peaked emission in its line profile [arXiv:2003.00525]. Although it has no detected companion galaxies, this double-peaked feature may signal the late evolutionary stage of a merger event [arXiv:2003.00525].

sources.summary: The paper reports J1404 has double-peaked line emission, strong rotational signatures, no detected companions, and a profile that may indicate a late-stage merger.

quotes.quote: The majority of our objects have a single peak line profile except for J1404 which exhibits double peak emission in the \ line, and the SMG companion to J1447. The double feature seen in J1404 has two peaks separated 350 \ from each other, while the SMG of J1447 shows two components to the \ line separated by 600 . The velocity map of J1404 in Figure fig:vel shows a single source with strong rotational signatures and a large total velocity amplitude of 400 , roughly the same separation we see in the spectrum. This FIR-bright source does not show the presence of companions, but the double peak could signal the late evolutionary stage of a merger event.

gmw_00004822.name: J2057
gmw_00004822.overview: J2057 is a multi-component merger system at redshift 4.68 that spans at least 50 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2404.11658]. The system consists of a central quasar and a dusty star-forming companion galaxy [arXiv:2404.11658], which are surrounded by several smaller clumps and a probable tidal tail [arXiv:2404.11658].

sources.summary: J2057-0030 is described as a z=4.68 multi-component merger spanning at least 50 kpc, with a QSO, DSFG companion, clumps, and a probable tidal tail.

quotes.quote: We present ALMA band-7 observations of J2057-0030, a multi-component merger system at z 4.68 spanning at least 50 kpc in size, using the [ C ii ] 157.74 m line and underlying far-infrared (FIR) continuum. We find two main components, the quasar (QSO) and a dusty star-forming galaxy (DSFG), both detected in [ C ii ] and continuum emission as well as multiple neighboring clumps detected only in [ C ii ] . Three of these clumps form a (tidal) tail that extends from the QSO in a straight direction to the west, covering a projected distance of 10 kpc. This perturbed morphology, added to a spatial distance of 20 kpc and a velocity offset of v = 68 km s ^ 1 between the QSO and the DSFG, strongly supports a merging scenario.

Based on ALMA Cycle 6 observations, the J2057-0030 system can be described as composed of a QSO and a DSFG, surrounded by several smaller structures, which are undetected in continuum emission. These clumps support the idea that we are witnessing a strongly interacting, where the sources have been perturbed by gravitational forces. This is supported by our measurements, with the QSO and the DSFG having a projected spatial separation of 20 kpc with a small velocity line of sight offset of v = 68 km s ^ 1 . This result is in agreement with merger systems observed at high- z e.g., Banados_2018, Ginolfi_2020, Romano_2021 .

sources.summary: The QSO component is detected in [C ii], shows a broad asymmetric line, is interacting with the DSFG, and has a modeled star formation rate of 402 solar masses per year.

quotes.quote: For the QSO, the [ C ii ] line covers a wide range of frequencies with a FWHM 345 km s ^ 1 and a peak of 11 mJy, in good agreement with N20. The spectrum is not completely symmetric showing slightly more flux at higher velocities ( 200 km s ^ 1 ). However, we notice that a single Gaussian fit still works better than fitting two components. The DSFG spectra indicates a velocity shift with respect to the QSO's systemic redshift of about v = 68 km s ^ 1 ( z 0.0015). This small velocity offset reinforces our idea of an interaction between the QSO and the DSFG as their velocity difference is comparable to (and in some cases smaller than) other shifts seen in ongoing mergers at high- z e.g., Jones_2019, Ginolfi_2020, Romano_2021 .

By fitting a spectral energy distribution model to the continuum data, we estimate star formation rates of 402 M_ yr ^ 1 for the QSO host and 244 M_ yr ^ 1 for the DSFG, which locate them on or close to the main sequence of star-forming galaxies. The J2057-0030 QSO was selected for being one of the brightest unobscured quasars at its redshift while presenting a rather modest star formation rate.

sources.summary: The paper describes J2057 as dynamically complex: its companion SMG has two dust peaks, and the quasar shows disrupted emission with disk, debris, and tadpole-like structure.

quotes.quote: Finally, J2057 also presents some interesting dynamical features. Besides the presence of two dust continuum peaks and a complex velocity map shown by the companion SMG, the quasar itself shows strong evidence for dynamical disruption: its \ emission appears as consistent with a 100 rotating disk plus debris material and a 20 kpc-long collimated ‘tadpole-like' structure orientated roughly in the E-W direction, which is constrained to a very narrow velocity range. This structure is not apparent in Figure fig:vel because of the velocity binning. J2057 and its SMG companion will be the subject of a future paper.

gmw_00004823.name: VDES J0330--4025
gmw_00004823.overview: VDES J0330--4025 is a high-redshift quasar hosting a supermassive black hole with a mass of approximately 4.96 billion solar masses [arXiv:2002.01811]. It exhibits a small proximity zone and may reside in a cosmic overdense region shared with the nearby quasar VDES J0323--4701 [arXiv:2002.01811].

sources.summary: VDES J0330--4025 has a small uncontaminated proximity zone, the largest black hole mass in the sample, and may share an overdense region with VDES J0323--4701.

quotes.quote: VDES J0330--4025 This object exhibits a small proximity zone of R_p=1.68^ +0.62 _ 0.38 pMpc ( R_ p, corr =2.11^ +0.78 _ 0.48 pMpc) with no signs of close absorption systems (see Fig. fig:abs_J0330 ). It also has the largest black hole mass within our sample, i.e. M_ BH =(4.96 0.51) 10^9 M_ .

VDES J0323--4701 It has been speculated that this quasar as well as VDES J0330--4025 might lie in an overdense region of the universe, since they are located within 10 degrees on the sky Reed2017 . The new, very similar redshift estimates for both objects based on their \ emission lines of z_ Mg II 6.241 with a very small velocity difference of v 50 km s^ 1 supports this hypothesis.

gmw_00004824.name: CFHQS J2100--1715
gmw_00004824.overview: CFHQS J2100--1715 is a high-redshift quasar that exhibits a very red spectral slope [arXiv:2002.01811]. The quasar has a companion galaxy located at a projected separation of 60 kpc, which suggests the two systems may be in an early stage of interaction [arXiv:2002.01811].

sources.summary: CFHQS J2100--1715 has the smallest uncontaminated proximity zone detected, a companion galaxy 60 kpc away, and a very red spectral slope.

quotes.quote: CFHQS J2100--1715 This quasar exhibits the smallest proximity zone R_p=0.37 0.14 pMpc ( R_ p, corr =0.66 0.25 pMpc) detected to date with no signs of contamination from associated absorption systems (see Fig. fig:absorption2 ). Decarli2017 reported the detection of a companion galaxy at a projected separation of 60 kpc, suggesting that the two objects might be at an early stage of interaction. Its spectrum shows a very red spectral slope Willott2009 .

gmw_00004825.name: CFHQS J2229+1457
gmw_00004825.overview: CFHQS J2229+1457 is a faint, high-redshift quasar [arXiv:1703.02539] with a precise host-galaxy redshift of z=6.1517 [arXiv:1703.02539] and no broad absorption line features [arXiv:1703.02539]. The quasar is not multiply imaged [arXiv:2304.09256], though its background emission is likely affected by weak lensing from a nearby foreground galaxy [arXiv:2304.09256].

sources.summary: The paper’s ALMA observations detect J2229+1457 in CII, find only marginal continuum and spatial extension, and estimate its intrinsic emission size and inclination for host-galaxy dynamics.

quotes.quote: CFHQS J005502+014618 (hereafter J0055+0146) and CFHQS J222901+145709 (hereafter J2229+1457) were observed with ALMA on the 28, 29 and 30 November 2013 for Cycle 1 project 2012.1.00676.S. Between 22 and 26 12 m diameter antennae were used. The typical long baselines were 400 m providing similar spatial resolution to our Cycle 0 observations. Observations of the science targets were interleaved with nearby phase calibrators, J0108+0135 and J2232+1143. The amplitude calibrator was Neptune and the bandpass calibrators J2258-2758 and J2148+0657. Total on-source integration times were 4610 s for J0055+0146 and 5490 s for J2229+1457.

The spectrum of J2229+1457 is plotted in Figure fig:linespecj2229 . For this quasar the \ line is centred in the band with no significant offset from the \ redshift. The line is consistent with a single Gaussian with a best fit FWHM = 351 39 km s ^ 1 , similar to the value for J0055+0146. The continuum level of the fit is again consistent with the independent continuum level determined from the three line-free basebands. Measurements from the spectra are given in Table tab:data . Figure fig:contlinemaps shows maps of the 1.2 mm continuum (white contours) plus the \ line (color scale) for the quasars. For both quasars there is no significant offset between any of the continuum or line centroids or the optical quasar position. The < 1 mm-optical offset is within the relative uncertainty of the optical astrometry. The more accurate \ positions for the two quasars are 00:55:02.92 +01.46.17.80 and 22:29:01.66 +14.57.08.30. For J2229+1457 there is only a marginal 2 detection of the continuum located coincident with the peak of the line emission.
The \ emission of J2229+1457 is only marginally spatially resolved ( 0 85 versus beam size of 0 76 ) and we estimate an intrinsic FWHM of 0 4 (2.4 kpc).

sources.summary: The paper identifies CFHQS J2229+1457 as a faint high-redshift quasar with an exceptionally small proximity zone, precise host-galaxy redshift, and no BAL features.

quotes.quote: The proximity zones of two objects, i.e. CFHQS J2229+1457 and SDSS J1335+3533 , are R_ p < 1 pMpc and R_ p, corr 1 pMpc.

CFHQS J2229+1457 This relatively faint quasar, M_ 1450 =-24.78 Banados2016 , was discovered in the Canada-France High-z Quasar Survey (CFHQS) and published by Willott2009 . The measurements of the proximity zone are R_p=0.45 0.14 pMpc and, when normalized to a magnitude of M_ 1450 =-27 , R_ p, corr = 1.07 0.33 pMpc. The quasar has a precise redshift measurement of z=6.1517 from emission from the host galaxy Willott2015 and hence a very small redshift uncertainty v=100 km/s resulting in an uncertainty of the proximity measurement of R_p 0.14 pMpc. The measurements of the proximity zone are independent of the choice of PCS used to model the quasar continuum, i.e. continuum uncertainties do not influence our measurements.
In the upper panel of Fig. fig:spectra_small we show the whole spectrum, revealing no BAL features.

sources.summary: The paper notes a foreground object 1.03 arcsec from CFHQS J2229+1457, but finds the quasar is not multiply imaged and treats any impact as weak lensing.

quotes.quote: Additional Notes on CFHQS J2229+1457 Figure fig:imaging shows that there is a foreground object in the NE direction that is 1 03 away from the quasar J2229+1457. In the HST image, this object can be well described by a S ersic profile with a half-light radius of R_e=0 4 , a S ersic index of n=3.45 , and an axis ratio of q=0.57 . The magnitudes of this object is m_ F555W =25.2 and m_ F850LP =23.8 .

Given its detection in the F555W image, this object must be a foreground object, which could introduce a magnification to the background quasar. Without the spectra and the redshift of the foreground object, we are not able to accurately calculate its contribution to the total magnification of the background quasar. However, we notice that the quasar is not multiply imaged, i.e., the impact of the foreground galaxy can be described by weak lensing.

sources.summary: CFHQS J2229+1457 has a confirmed small proximity zone without associated absorbers, while its black hole mass estimate is uncertain and differs from prior work.

quotes.quote: CFHQS J2229+1457 This quasar's small proximity zone has been confirmed in this study, i.e. R_p=0.47 0.14 pMpc ( R_ p, corr =1.12 0.33 pMpc). Its X-Shooter spectrum does not reveal any associated absorption systems that could truncate or contaminate its proximity zone, although the SNR of the data redwards of the \ emission line is still very low (see Fig fig:abs_J2229 ). Note that the black hole mass measurement for this object should be taken with caution, because the \ emission line falls on top of a telluric feature. Our measurement differs from a previous measurement by Willott2010 who made use of a lower resolution spectrum ( R 520 ) observed with NIRI/Gemini by more than one order of magnitude.

gmw_00004826.name: PSO J056--16
gmw_00004826.overview: PSO J056--16 is a high-redshift quasar [arXiv:2002.01811]. The object has a high accretion rate, indicated by an estimated large Eddington ratio of 1.26 [arXiv:2002.01811].

sources.summary: PSO J056--16 has a pDLA-truncated proximity zone, is excluded from proximity-zone analysis, and has a large Eddington ratio indicating high accretion.

quotes.quote: PSO J056--16 As shown in Fig. fig:absorption this quasar's proximity zone has been prematurely truncated due to a pDLA along our line-of-sight. Thus, we exclude this object from any further analysis of its proximity zone. The estimated large Eddington ratio of _ Edd =1.26 0.08 indicates that this quasar has a high accretion rate.

gmw_00004827.name: VDES J0323--4701
gmw_00004827.overview: VDES J0323--4701 is a high-redshift quasar with a redshift estimate of 6.241 [arXiv:2002.01811]. It may reside in an overdense region of the universe shared with VDES J0330--4025, and it exhibits a very high estimated Eddington ratio of 1.76 [arXiv:2002.01811].

sources.summary: VDES J0323--4701 may share an overdense region with VDES J0330--4025, has a very high but uncertain Eddington ratio, and low-SNR Mg II data.

quotes.quote: VDES J0323--4701 It has been speculated that this quasar as well as VDES J0330--4025 might lie in an overdense region of the universe, since they are located within 10 degrees on the sky Reed2017 . The new, very similar redshift estimates for both objects based on their \ emission lines of z_ Mg II 6.241 with a very small velocity difference of v 50 km s^ 1 supports this hypothesis. The estimated Eddington ratio is very high, i.e. _ Edd =1.76 1.24 . However, the NIR spectrum of this object and in particular the \ emission line have a very low SNR and thus these estimates have large uncertainties and should be taken with caution.

gmw_00004828.name: SDSS J1143+3803
gmw_00004828.overview: SDSS J1143+3803 is a high-redshift quasar [arXiv:2002.01811]. Based on its CO(6-5) emission line, the galaxy has a redshift of approximately 5.8366 and hosts a large proximity zone [arXiv:2002.01811].

sources.summary: SDSS J1143+3803 has a higher CO-based redshift than the Ly estimate and the largest proximity zone, whose endpoint falls in a DEIMOS detector gap.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1143+3803 Based on the new redshift estimate from the CO(6--5) emission line ( z_ CO(6-5) 5.8366 ), which is significantly higher than the preliminary estimate from the \ emission line ( z_ Ly 5.805 ), this quasar has the largest proximity zone ( R_p=3.93 0.63 pMpc) in our sample. Unfortunately, the end of its zone falls right in between the two DEIMOS detectors, which are separated by a =11 wide gap. Thus, we adopted the middle of the detector gap as the best estimate of the proximity zone size and the width of the gap as the uncertainty on R_p .

gmw_00004829.name: PSO J239--07
gmw_00004829.overview: PSO J239--07 is a high-redshift quasar that exhibits a very small proximity zone [arXiv:2002.01811]. Its optical and near-infrared spectrum shows broad absorption line features, and two other dust continuum sources have been detected in its vicinity [arXiv:2002.01811].

sources.summary: PSO J239--07 has a very small proximity zone, but broad absorption line features may contaminate it, so the paper excludes it from further analysis.

quotes.quote: PSO J239--07 Although this quasar exhibits a very small proximity zone, i.e. R_p=1.29 0.14 pMpc, the broad absorption line features detected in its optical/NIR spectrum might have contaminated its zone. Thus, we exclude this object from any further analysis. The dust continuum map shown in Fig. fig:cont_map shows two other continuum sources in the vicinity of this quasar.

gmw_00004830.name: NGVSUDG-05
gmw_00004830.overview: NGVSUDG-05 is an ultra-diffuse dwarf galaxy located in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:2305.06369]. It exhibits a very high velocity dispersion and is characterized by a large dark matter fraction and an overly massive dark matter halo for its stellar mass [arXiv:2305.06369].

sources.summary: NGVSUDG-05 is among the Virgo UDGs with very high velocity dispersion, large dark matter content, and candidate overly massive halos for its stellar mass.

quotes.quote: These outliers include: NGVSUDG-05, NGVSUDG-11, NGVSUDG-19, NGVSUDG-20 and NGVSUDG-A04, with NGVSUDG-09 being borderline compatible.

Extreme UDGs in Virgo The most important of our results is that a large number of UDGs in our sample (NGVSUDG-05, NGVSUDG-11, NGVSUDG-19, NGVSUDG-20 and NGVSUDG-A04) show very high velocity dispersion, 30 80 km/s, several times what is expected for dwarf galaxies of similar stellar mass. This suggests that they inhabit overly massive dark matter halos, providing tentative support to the original ``failed MW" or ``failed galaxy" scenario for the highest M_ 1/2 UDGs.

We estimate the dark matter fraction within the radius that contains half of the dynamical tracers in each galaxy using the relation: f_ DM, 1/2 = M_ 1/2 M^*_ 1/2 M_ 1/2 Table table:spec shows the dark matter fraction for each of our UDGs, which ranges from consistent with 0 dark matter in NGVSUDG-10 to 100 for NGVSUDG-05, NGVSUDG-09, NGVSUDG-11, NGVSUDG-19, and NGVSUDG-20, with a median dark matter fraction for all the UDGs in our sample f_ DM,1/2 98 .

gmw_00004831.name: NGVSUDG-09
gmw_00004831.overview: NGVSUDG-09 is a large ultra-diffuse galaxy located in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:2305.06369]. It is a borderline high-dispersion outlier with a large dark matter fraction, situated in the cluster's high-velocity tail as an isolated object [arXiv:2305.06369].

sources.summary: NGVSUDG-09 is borderline in the high-dispersion outlier set, has a large dark matter fraction, and lies in Virgo's high-velocity tail as an isolated large object.

quotes.quote: These outliers include: NGVSUDG-05, NGVSUDG-11, NGVSUDG-19, NGVSUDG-20 and NGVSUDG-A04, with NGVSUDG-09 being borderline compatible.

However, the remaining six UDGs in our sample (NGVSUDG-A04, NGVSUDG-05, NGVSUDG-11, NGVSUDG-19, NGVSUDG-20, and NGVSUDG-09) are clearly off this relation and show a much larger dark matter fraction within R_ 1/2 than dEs and ETGs.
NGVSUDG-09, NGVSUDG-19, and NGVSUDG-A04 belong to the high-velocity tail in phase space, are also large objects, and are quite isolated without any nearby bright galaxy.

gmw_00004832.name: NGVSUDG-10
gmw_00004832.overview: NGVSUDG-10 is a dark-matter-poor ultra-diffuse galaxy with a low velocity dispersion and a luminosity similar to NGC1052-DF2 [arXiv:2305.06369]. It exhibits a peculiar morphology with possible spiral arms or tidal streams and active star formation, suggesting it may have been shaped by tidal interactions [arXiv:2305.06369].

sources.summary: NGVSUDG-10 is a dark-matter-poor UDG with low velocity dispersion, DF2-like luminosity and dispersion, and peculiar morphology suggesting tidal-interaction formation.

quotes.quote: To attest to this kinematical diversity in UDGs, it is worth noting that NGVSUDG-10 in our sample shows a dispersion and luminosity that are very similar to NGC1052-DF2, which has been claimed to be devoid of dark matter see vanDokkum2018a,vanDokkum2018b,Danieli2019,Emsellem2019 , showing the exact opposite behavior to the subsample highlighted above: a velocity dispersion too low for their stellar content. As discussed in Lim2020 , NGVSUDG-10 shows a peculiar morphology with possible spiral arms or tidal streams with some star formation, this suggests that tidal interactions with other galaxies may be a viable mechanism for the formation of UDGs with low velocity dispersion Sales2020, Doppel2021, Moreno2022 .

As expected, NGVSUDG-10 and NGC1052-DF2 lie on the one-to-one relation, followed closely by massive (squares) and dwarf (stars) early-type galaxies (ETGs and dEs, respectively), where most of the dynamical mass can be explained by the stars, at least within their R_e Cappellari2006,Cappellari2013,Toloba2014b .

gmw_00004833.name: NGVSUDG-19
gmw_00004833.overview: NGVSUDG-19 is a large ultra-diffuse galaxy located in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:2305.06369]. It resides in an isolated environment without any nearby bright galaxies and belongs to a high-velocity tail in phase space [arXiv:2305.06369].

sources.summary: NGVSUDG-19 is a high-dispersion UDG with large dark matter fraction, uncertain half-tracer radius, and an isolated high-velocity-tail location in Virgo.

quotes.quote: These outliers include: NGVSUDG-05, NGVSUDG-11, NGVSUDG-19, NGVSUDG-20 and NGVSUDG-A04, with NGVSUDG-09 being borderline compatible.
The R_ 1/2 parameter values for NGVSUDG-10 and NGVSUDG-19 are extremely uncertain; we estimate the R_ 1/2 value for these galaxies based on their half-light radius ( R_e ).
NGVSUDG-09, NGVSUDG-19, and NGVSUDG-A04 belong to the high-velocity tail in phase space, are also large objects, and are quite isolated without any nearby bright galaxy.

gmw_00004834.name: NGVSUDG-20
gmw_00004834.overview: NGVSUDG-20 is an ultra-diffuse dwarf galaxy located in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:2305.06369]. It exhibits an exceptionally high velocity dispersion and a large dark matter fraction, suggesting it resides within an overly massive dark matter halo [arXiv:2305.06369].

sources.summary: NGVSUDG-20 is one of the extreme Virgo UDGs with high velocity dispersion, large inferred dark matter fraction, and an overly massive halo candidate.

quotes.quote: These outliers include: NGVSUDG-05, NGVSUDG-11, NGVSUDG-19, NGVSUDG-20 and NGVSUDG-A04, with NGVSUDG-09 being borderline compatible.

Extreme UDGs in Virgo The most important of our results is that a large number of UDGs in our sample (NGVSUDG-05, NGVSUDG-11, NGVSUDG-19, NGVSUDG-20 and NGVSUDG-A04) show very high velocity dispersion, 30 80 km/s, several times what is expected for dwarf galaxies of similar stellar mass. This suggests that they inhabit overly massive dark matter halos, providing tentative support to the original ``failed MW" or ``failed galaxy" scenario for the highest M_ 1/2 UDGs.

However, the remaining six UDGs in our sample (NGVSUDG-A04, NGVSUDG-05, NGVSUDG-11, NGVSUDG-19, NGVSUDG-20, and NGVSUDG-09) are clearly off this relation and show a much larger dark matter fraction within R_ 1/2 than dEs and ETGs.

gmw_00004835.name: NGVSUDG-A04
gmw_00004835.overview: NGVSUDG-A04 is an extreme ultra-diffuse galaxy in the Virgo Cluster that exhibits a very high velocity dispersion [arXiv:2305.06369]. It is classified as a type 1 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2009.11498] and features an overly bright, offset nucleus-like object [arXiv:2305.06369].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 850 1 J as a bright core sub-mm galaxy whose photometric redshifts disagree; despite possible interloper status, it is retained as a potential cluster member.

quotes.quote: 0.85 Bright Cluster Core Sources In 2018Coogan no spectral redshifts were detected for sources 850 1 I and 850 1 J (A5 and A4 in 2018Coogan ).

When we look at the other source, 850 1 J we see that indeed EAZY placed it well outside of the cluster (z 4.3) whereas CIGALE placed it within the cluster (Figure fig:A4 ). Combined 850 1 I and 850 1 J contribute 70 of the 870 m flux for the cluster core, meaning that the chance of these galaxies being interlopers is extremely small.
For the rest of our analysis we include 850 1 J as a potential member based on its CIGALE redshift. When appropriate we also consider the situation that this galaxy is not in the cluster.

sources.summary: The paper identifies A04 as a previously classified type 1 AGN with a central stellar-dispersion drop, gas-star kinematic mismatch suggesting outflow, and high central gas dispersion.

quotes.quote: Six objects, namely, A01, A03, A04, A07, A08, A10 were previously classified as type 1 AGN by 2006A&A...455..773V . The classification is presumably based on the presence of a broad \ component.

A few targets (i.e., A04 and A09) show a drop with a lower stellar velocity dispersion at the very center, which has been reported in the literature for other galaxies e.g., Kent+90, Bender+94, Pinkney+03, Kang+13 .

For example, A02, A04, A05, A06, A07, and A08 show a radial velocity profile of gas, which is inconsistent with that of stars, suggesting that that ionized gas is under the effect of outflows. The radial profile of gas velocity dispersion also shows a different pattern compared to that of stars. Some of these targets (e.g., A04, A07, A09, and A10) show much higher velocity dispersion of \ than stars at the center, indicating the presence of non-gravitational effects. The different kinematics between gas and stars are expected from the \ emission line profile since all but the three targets (A02, A03, A05) show a broad wing component at the central region in Figure o3fit .

sources.summary: NGVSUDG-A04 may have an offset nucleus-like object and is an extreme, high-dispersion Virgo UDG located at the far end of the cluster.

quotes.quote: However, NGVSUDG-04 and NGVSUDG-A04 both have an overly bright GC-like object that is offset from the center of the stellar light distribution, this object could be considered a nucleus as previously mentioned in Toloba2018 and Mihos2022 .

Extreme UDGs in Virgo The most important of our results is that a large number of UDGs in our sample (NGVSUDG-05, NGVSUDG-11, NGVSUDG-19, NGVSUDG-20 and NGVSUDG-A04) show very high velocity dispersion, 30 80 km/s, several times what is expected for dwarf galaxies of similar stellar mass.
Our HST imaging for NGVSUDG-A04 and NGVSUDG-11 shows that these galaxies are at distances of 17.7^ +0.6 _ 0.4 Mpc and 12.7^ +1.3 _ 1.1 Mpc, respectively Zhang et al.

gmw_00004836.name: Ton 599
gmw_00004836.overview: Ton 599 is a bright flat-spectrum radio quasar and blazar located at a redshift of approximately 0.725 [arXiv:2403.00572]. First detected in the gamma-ray band by EGRET, the active galactic nucleus recently exhibited its highest-ever recorded gamma-ray flux in January 2023 [arXiv:2403.00572].

sources.summary: The paper studies Ton 599's January 2023 flare, identifying it as a bright FSRQ/blazar with record gamma-ray flux and multiwavelength variability.

quotes.quote: Ton 599, identified as a bright FSRQ, is situated at a redshift z 0.725 2010MNRAS.405.2302H . Positioned at equatorial coordinates R.A = 179.883 and Dec. = 29.2455 degrees, it was first detected by the Energetic Gamma Ray Experiment Telescope (EGRET) in ray energy 1995ApJS..101..259T . Following the launch of the Fermi LAT satellite, Ton 599 was detected within the first three months 2010Natur.463..919A .

In the daily binned ray light curve, the source has shown a peak flux of 3.63 10^ 6 ph cm^ 2 s^ 1 on MJD 59954.50. This is the highest-ever flux reported from Ton 599 till now. It is about 13 times larger than the base flux of the source.

gmw_00004837.name: radio galaxy in ClG J0242-2132
gmw_00004837.overview: The radio galaxy in ClG J0242-2132, also known as PKS 0240-217, is a low-redshift active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1702.04788] classified as a young gigahertz-peaked spectrum source [arXiv:2303.04821]. It is unresolved on kiloparsec scales but extends 200 parsecs on milliarcsecond scales [arXiv:2303.04821].

sources.summary: The paper targets this low-redshift radio source for associated 21-cm and OH absorption, reports the GMRT setup and imaging quality, and finds no absorption detection.
