quotes.quote: PKS 0240--217 : The \ band was observed for a total of 0.90 hours. Only one antenna (20) was non-functioning, leaving 406 baseline pairs. Some minimal RFI was removed from the first half of the observation and after self calibration of the phases a high quality image was produced, although there is still some narrow-band RFI spikes present in the extracted spectrum. Unfortunately, this is concentrated at 1082 MHz, close to the expected absorption frequency (Fig. spectra ). The source was unresolved by the 10.4 3.4 synthesised beam.

We have undertaken a survey for associated \ 21-cm and OH 18-cm absorption in six z 0.2-0.4 radio sources with the GMRT. Despite selecting targets which have ionising photon rates lower than the critical value, above which the neutral gas is believed to be completely ionised, and reaching the sensitivities of previous detections, we do not detect either transition. Given that the OH absorption strength is expected to be 10^ 4 times that of the \ 21-cm absorption cdbw07 , the non-detection of this radical is not surprising, especially in the knowledge that our targets are not as reddened as originally believed.

sources.summary: The paper treats the radio galaxy in ClG J0242-2132 as a young GPS source with 200 pc parsec-scale size; its cluster has symmetric cool ICM, extended H-alpha filaments, and chaotic warm-gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: Both radio galaxies in RX J1350+0940 and ClG J0242-2132 are unresolved on arcsecond (kiloparsec) scales ( 2020ApJS..247...53K and 2015MNRAS.453.1223H , respectively), and reveal small extension only on milliarcsecond (parsec) scales 2015MNRAS.453.1223H . The nondetection of extended emission at MHz frequencies implies -- assuming a typical magnetic field of a few G (e.g., 2004IJMPD..13.1549G ) -- a timescale of at least 10^ 8 yr since the last radio activity (e.g., 2017A&A...600A..65S ). Information on the parsec-scale properties of both sources are available from the VLBA Calibrator List Tool https://obs.vlba.nrao.edu/cst/ ; 2020A&A...644A.159C . : at 2.3 GHz, the radio galaxy in RX J1350+0940 has a largest linear size (LLS) of 90 pc, while that in ClG J0242-2132 has a LLS of 200 pc.

The cluster ClG J0242-2132 shows a rather symmetric morphology from the large-scale Chandra image and the RGB zoom-in. The 0.3 -- 1.2 keV ICM is evenly distributed around the central AGN, and the warm gas phase traced by the H contours (L _ H = 1.7 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 ) has a filamentary structure extending roughly equally in all directions. The optical data reveal several radial filaments across the whole azimuth, with LLS 40 kpc and a chaotic velocity field.

gmw_00004838.name: ATHDFS J223343.7-603651
gmw_00004838.overview: ATHDFS J223343.7-603651 is a faint, radio-loud galaxy with an ultra-steep radio spectrum [arXiv:0803.4394]. This classification is consistent with the source being a high-redshift radio galaxy [arXiv:0803.4394].

sources.summary: The paper classifies ATHDFS J223343.7-603651 as radio-loud, notes its faint optical detection and ultra-steep radio spectrum, and says that classification is consistent with a high-redshift radio galaxy.

quotes.quote: One interesting source classified as radio-loud is ATHDFS J223343.7-603651 which has a 1.4 GHz flux density of 0.070 mJy. This faint source is at the detection limit of the CTIO images, and we have no redshift information for it. In Paper III we quote a radio spectral index of _ 1.4 GHz ^ 2.5 GHz =-1.14 0.39 for this source, and identified it as an ultra-steep spectrum radio source. The radio-loud classification of this source is consistent with it being a high redshift radio galaxy.

gmw_00004839.overview: The galaxy 0059+581 is a bright and compact source that exhibits flux density variability on both short and long timescales [arXiv:2203.08590]. It has a flat spectrum and has shown a bright four-band emission flare [arXiv:2203.08590].

sources.summary: The paper describes 0059+581 as a compact fringe finder with multiwavelength variability, detects a bright four-band flare near MJD 59361, and finds a flat spectrum.

quotes.quote: 0059+581 has been used as a bright and compact fringe finder during VGOS commissioning work. It does however exhibit significant flux density variability on short and long timescales, and has been included in multiple monitoring campaigns at radio Lister_et_al_2018, Lister_et_al_2021 , gamma-ray Paliya_et_al_2021 and optical Blinov_et_al_2021 wavelengths. This source was the one most often included in our monitoring, already from the start and our FM-results are shown in Fig. fig:fm_0059 .

We detect at least one apparent flare of emission, in all four bands, with the peak around MJD 59361. We do not detect any significant short-term variability in our IDMV sessions, presented in Fig. fig:idvm_0059+581 . We find our data to be consistent with a flat spectrum, where the tentative excess emission at the lowest band 1 could be due to other nearby synchrotron sources in the field.

gmw_00004840.overview: The object 1617+229 is classified as a blazar [arXiv:2203.08590]. It represents a weak source that exhibits a relatively stable and flat spectrum of approximately 0.5 Jy [arXiv:2203.08590].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1617+229 as a blazar, the weakest sample source, with a stable flat spectrum near 0.5 Jy and internally consistent monitoring measurements.

quotes.quote: The blazar 1617+229 has also been included in monitoring campaigns Lister_et_al_2019,Richards_et_al_2014 . It is the weakest source in our sample, with a relatively stable and flat spectrum of about 0.5 Jy as can be seen in Fig. fig:fm_1617+229 . We note that after the maintenance work (Sect. sec:gcomo ) it appears that we obtain robust estimates of the flux density also in band 2. Although our measurements are internally consistent, and consistent with literature, the results - in particular for the lowest band 1 - could be improved with refined calibration procedures (e.g. combining multiple SPWs in fringe-fitting).

gmw_00004841.overview: 0552+398 is a high-redshift blazar and active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z = 2.363 [arXiv:1812.07350, arXiv:0909.5100]. It is a bright radio quasar that exhibits a flat spectrum [arXiv:1812.07350, arXiv:2203.08590], slow variability [arXiv:2203.08590], and a superluminal jet [arXiv:1812.07350].

sources.summary: The paper reports DA 193 as a z=2.363 high-redshift blazar newly detected by LAT, with soft average gamma emission, hard X-rays, a rare hard GeV flare, and radio-quasar jet properties.

quotes.quote: Here, we report, for the first time, the Large Area Telescope detection of the significant ray emission from the high-redshift blazar DA 193 ( z=2.363 ). Its time-averaged ray spectrum is soft ( ray photon index = 2.9 0.1 ) and together with a relatively flat hard X-ray spectrum (14 195 keV photon index = 1.5 0.4 ), DA 193 presents a case to study a typical high-redshift blazar with inverse Compton peak being located at MeV energies. An intense GeV flare was observed from this object in the first week of 2018 January, a phenomenon rarely observed from high-redshift sources. What makes this event a rare one is the observation of an extremely hard ray spectrum (photon index = 1.7 0.2 ), which is somewhat unexpected since high-redshift blazars typically exhibit a steep falling spectrum at GeV energies.

DA 193 lies close to the Galactic anti-center (Galactic longitude and latitude: 171 ^ .647 and 7 ^ .284, respectively) and is a bright radio quasar F_ 1.4 GHz = 1516 mJy; 1998AJ....115.1693C . It was the subject of various radio studies due to its peculiar Gigahertz peak spectrum e.g., 1981A&A....96..365S,1983ApJ...271...44S,1990A&AS...84..549O . It is one of the MOJAVE monitored blazars and the detection of superluminal motion has been reported at 15 GHz apparent jet velocity = 1.592 0.096 , 2016AJ....152...12L .

sources.summary: The paper discusses 0552+398/DA 193 as a high-redshift AGN often classified as GPS, but variable and polarized, with a two-component VLBA model showing slow separation growth.

quotes.quote: 0552+398 (DA 193): This AGN (z = 2.363) has been frequently classified as a gigahertz-peaked spectrum source in the literature. However, it displays considerable flux density variability above its turnover frequency TTT05 , and has persistent linear polarization on mas scales that is uncharacteristic of the GPS class. We were able to fit all of the 2 cm MOJAVE VLBA epochs between 1997 and 2007 with a two component Gaussian model, in which the separation is barely increasing at a rate of 4 1 ( 0.36 0.09 ).

sources.summary: The paper says 0552+398 has long been observed with VLBI, varies slowly, has a flat spectrum in bands 1-3, and matches past BVID monitoring.

quotes.quote: 0552+398 This source has also been the subject of VLBI observations for many years (e.g. Spangler_et_al_1983, Lister_et_al_2019 ). We find it to be slowly varying with a flat spectrum in bands 1, 2 and 3. The multi-frequency lightcurve given in Fig. fig:fm_0552+398 is consistent with the past monitoring data available via the BVID.

gmw_00004842.name: lensing galaxy G
gmw_00004842.overview: The lensing galaxy G is a main gravitational deflector located at a redshift of approximately 0.66 [arXiv:1905.08260, arXiv:astro-ph/0511026]. It is classified as an elliptical or S0 galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0511026] and is the seventh-brightest member of a large galaxy group [arXiv:1905.08800].

sources.summary: The paper studies a bright quadruply lensed quasar at the stated coordinates, measuring multi-year image time delays and assessing microlensing time-delay effects for cosmography.

quotes.quote: The present paper is part of a series focusing on the analysis of the quadruply lensed quasar . It presents new time delay measurements based on monitoring data from the COSMOGRAIL collaboration taken between 2004 and 2018. In parallel, Sluse et al. 2019 (submitted; hereafter H0LiCOW XI) presents measurements of the spectroscopic redshifts of galaxies in the environment of . Rusu et al. 2019, submitted (hereafter H0LiCOW XII) focuses on the modeling of , taking into account the environment from H0LiCOW XI and the time delays from the present work, to derive a value of the Hubble constant.

\ is a bright, quadruply lensed quasar in a fold configuration (2000): 20h33m42.08s; (2000): -47 ^ 23'43.0", Morgan2004 . The most recent spectroscopic measurements of the lens and source redshifts are z_d=0.6575 (H0LiCOW XI) and z_s=1.662 Sluse2012 , respectively. \ has been monitored since 2004 by the 1.2m Swiss Leonhard Euler telescope at the ESO La Silla Observatory in Chile, using the C2 instrument until October 2010 and the ECAM instrument since then, using the Rouge Gen eve filter, a modified version of the broad Bessel-R filter.

sources.summary: The paper treats the lensing galaxy G as the main deflector, measuring its velocity dispersion and identifying it as a seventh-brightest member of a z = 0.6588 group.

quotes.quote: We use the Gemini and ESO-Very Large telescopes to measure the spectroscopic redshifts of the brightest galaxies towards the lens, and use the ESO-MUSE integral field spectrograph to measure the velocity-dispersion of the lens ( _ los = 250^ +15 _ 21 ) and of several nearby galaxies.

In addition to the lensing galaxy G , the three galaxies with the largest flexion shift (i.e. G2 , G3 , G7 ; see Fig. fig:zoom and Tab. tab:flexion_galax ) are bright enough to enable a velocity-dispersion measurement with MUSE data.

We confirm earlier findings that the main lensing galaxy is part of a large group at z _ group = 0.6588 Morgan2004, Wilson2016 , for which we derive _ los = 500 80 . The number of spectroscopically confirmed members has increased by 30 owing to this work, and is now reaching 22 galaxies. The lensing galaxy is the seventh brightest galaxy of the group and is therefore suspected not to lie at the centre of its host halo.

sources.summary: The lensing galaxy in WFI J2033 4723 is fit by an elliptical or S0 template, with the paper measuring a redshift of 0.661.

quotes.quote: We confirm four previously measured redshifts: HE 0047 1756 ( =0.407 ), HE 0230 2130 ( =0.523 ), HE 0435 1223 ( =0.454 ) and WFI J2033 4723 ( =0.661 ).

WFI J2033 4723 : Morgan et al. ( ) discovered this quadruply lensed quasar with maximum image separation of 2.53 and redshift z=1.66 . Ofek et al. ( ) recently measured the lens redshift =0.658 . The lensing galaxy spectrum shown in Fig. WFI2033_lens is matched by an elliptical or S0 galaxy template with a redshift of =0.661 0.001 . Our measured redshift is compatible with but slighly higher than the one reported by Ofek et al. ( ).

gmw_00004843.name: G2
gmw_00004843.overview: G2 is a galaxy that acts as one of the largest flexion-shift perturbers in its lensing system [arXiv:1905.08800]. It is bright enough to enable a velocity-dispersion measurement using MUSE data, though modeling is required to remove contamination from nearby lensed quasar images [arXiv:1905.08800].

sources.summary: The paper identifies G2 as one of the largest flexion-shift perturbers, bright enough for a MUSE velocity-dispersion measurement after modeling contamination from the lensed quasar images.

quotes.quote: In addition to the lensing galaxy G , the three galaxies with the largest flexion shift (i.e. G2 , G3 , G7 ; see Fig. fig:zoom and Tab. tab:flexion_galax ) are bright enough to enable a velocity-dispersion measurement with MUSE data.

A brute force extraction of the spectra of G and G2 yields significant contamination of the galaxy spectra by the lensed quasar images, precluding a robust velocity-dispersion measurement. It is therefore necessary to remove the quasar flux and local sky residual prior to the velocity-dispersion measurement. For that purpose, we first model each slice of the data cube containing the lens system and G2 using a 2D model of the light distribution.
For G2, a third mask, containing only the known telluric absorption and the red edge of the spectrum ( > 9100 ) has also been used.

gmw_00004844.name: G8
gmw_00004844.overview: G8 is a galaxy that exhibits the spectroscopic characteristics of a spiral galaxy [arXiv:1905.08800]. It acts as a gravitational lensing perturber, though its stellar mass and resulting flexion shift may be overestimated due to unreliable multi-band photometry [arXiv:1905.08800].

sources.summary: The paper flags G8 as a high-flexion-shift perturber with unreliable photometry, an assumed stellar mass, spiral-like spectral characteristics, and likely overestimated impact on the lens model.

quotes.quote: Only four of them have a flexion shift _3 x > 10^ 4 arcsec, namely the galaxies labeled G2, G3, G7 and G8 on Fig. fig:zoom . Among those galaxies, G8 does not have reliable multi-band photometry, and therefore a stellar mass of (M / M_ ) = 10.17 has been assumed. This arbitrary choice may yield a substantial overestimate of the flexion shift. Indeed, this galaxy shows spectroscopic characteristics of a spiral galaxy, and is clearly fainter than G4 , another spiral located at about the same redshift than G8 , but with a photometric stellar mass of only (M / M_ ) [ 8.96, 9.48 ] . Assessing a stellar mass in that range for G8 yields flexion shifts _3 x [ 3.43 10^ 5 , 2.55 10^ 5 ] arcsec, well below the threshold above which that galaxy would have a substantial impact on the modelling.

gmw_00004845.name: IC 1199
gmw_00004845.overview: IC 1199 is a disk galaxy characterized by a structure consisting of a bulge and a broken-disk component [arXiv:1705.02120]. Its outer regions exhibit a high-quality spectrum containing a D4000 break, emission lines, and absorption features [arXiv:1705.02120].

sources.summary: IC 1199 is used as a CALIFA example with a bulge plus broken-disc decomposition, fitted colour profiles, and a high-quality outer spectrum showing continuum, emission, and absorption features.

quotes.quote: Figure II_jairo_plot shows the 2D decomposition into a bulge and a broken disc components for IC 1199 as a good example of the typical performance of GASP2D in our sample of galaxies see also 2017A&A...598A..32M . Top row displays three panels with the observed SDSS band image (left), the 2D model (middle), and the residuals (model - observed, right).

In Fig. II_colour_vs_ste_pop (top panels) we show the colour profiles and the results of the linear fits for IC 1199 as an example. The performed linear fits are error-weighted and take into account the observational errors of the radial points to derive the parameters of the fit, such as the gradient and its error.

In this particular case, we are showing a spectrum (solid black line) at an intermediate-to-large galactocentric distance from IC 1199 (24 arcsecs, 1.34 h _ in ). We must note the high quality of the spectrum (S/N = 41.3 per pixel in the continuum), highlighting specially some features such as the D4000 break, emission lines (H , , etc), or absorption features ( , MgI, etc).

gmw_00004846.name: HAE 3
gmw_00004846.overview: HAE 3 is a high-redshift protocluster galaxy detected in carbon monoxide, neutral carbon, and 1.1 mm continuum emissions [arXiv:2101.04691]. Its neutral carbon emission line is narrower than its carbon monoxide lines, suggesting these emissions have kinematically distinct origins [arXiv:2101.04691].

sources.summary: HAE 3 is one of the protocluster galaxies detected in [CI], CO, and 1.1 mm continuum. Its [CI] line is narrower than CO, suggesting kinematically distinct emission.

quotes.quote: We detect the line from five galaxies previously detected in CO (3--2) and CO (4--3) lines, i.e., HAE 3, 4, 8, 9 and 16. Three galaxies, HAE 3, 8, and 9, were detected in the 1.1 mm continuum data as well.

These two galaxies show signatures of mergers in their stellar and/or CO maps ( minju2017a,minju2019a ). For HAE 3, the line is narrower (FWHM = 172 106 km s ^ 1 ) than two CO lines (FWHM = 500 km s ^ 1 ) which may indicate kinematically different origins of the emission lines. If we integrate the [ C I ] cube of HAE 3 over the same velocity range defined by two CO lines, the 1D Gaussian fitted flux ( 0.15 0.08 ) is consistent within uncertainties.

gmw_00004847.name: HAE 4
gmw_00004847.overview: The high-redshift emission line galaxy HAE 4 is detected in both [CI] and CO lines [arXiv:2101.04691]. Its hydrogen, CO, and [CI] line peaks are spatially offset from each other by several kiloparsecs, which may indicate varying gas excitation conditions within the galaxy [arXiv:2101.04691].

sources.summary: HAE 4 is detected in [CI] and CO, but its H, CO, and line peaks are spatially offset by several kiloparsecs, suggesting varying gas excitation.

quotes.quote: We detect the line from five galaxies previously detected in CO (3--2) and CO (4--3) lines, i.e., HAE 3, 4, 8, 9 and 16.

HAE 4 shows a positional offset of 0.6'' between H and CO (3--2) peak positions ( minju2017a ) and extended H emission. The CO (4--3) peak is closer to the H peak in contrast to the offset from CO (3--2) peak position. The line peak is also offset from the other line peak positions by 0''.5-0''.6 ( 4-5 kpc). It may indicate different gas excitation conditions within the galaxy as discussed in minju2019a . For this reason, we take another flux measurement for HAE 4.

gmw_00004848.name: HAE 8
gmw_00004848.overview: HAE 8 is a high-redshift protocluster galaxy [arXiv:2101.04691]. It has been detected in the 1.1 mm continuum, as well as in carbon monoxide and neutral carbon emission lines [arXiv:2101.04691].

sources.summary: HAE 8 is a [CI], CO, and 1.1 mm continuum detected protocluster galaxy; the authors use its known narrow CO width and revise its CO(3-2) flux upward.

quotes.quote: We detect the line from five galaxies previously detected in CO (3--2) and CO (4--3) lines, i.e., HAE 3, 4, 8, 9 and 16. Three galaxies, HAE 3, 8, and 9, were detected in the 1.1 mm continuum data as well.
For HAE 8 and HAE 9, we use the 0''.5 tapered map and an aperture size of 1''.0 . For HAE 8, we use 50 km s ^ 1 considering the narrow width known from the previous CO line detections.

The CO (3--2) fluxes of HAE 8 and HAE 4 have increased roughly by a factor of two than the one in minju2017a , where we took a peak flux from the smoothed maps, instead of fitting Gaussian for all galaxies (see the Appendix for the related discussions on the flux measurements in minju2017a ). However, we note that the updated values are consistent with the values reported in Tadaki2019a within uncertainties.

gmw_00004849.name: HAE 9
gmw_00004849.overview: HAE 9 is a gas-rich merger galaxy [arXiv:2101.04691]. It has been detected in [CI] emission, CO lines, and the 1.1 mm continuum, though its red-shifted component is not detected in [CI] [arXiv:2101.04691].

sources.summary: HAE 9 is detected in [CI], CO, and continuum and is described as a gas-rich merger; its red-shifted component appears in CO(4-3) but not [CI].

quotes.quote: We detect the line from five galaxies previously detected in CO (3--2) and CO (4--3) lines, i.e., HAE 3, 4, 8, 9 and 16. Three galaxies, HAE 3, 8, and 9, were detected in the 1.1 mm continuum data as well.

For HAE 9, we discussed its kinematic properties in minju2019a that the galaxy is undergoing a gas-rich merger. The red-shifted component of HAE 9 is not detected in (or does not satisfy the detection criteria), which was detected in CO (4--3). Accordingly, in Table tab:lineflux , we list additional flux measurements for HAE 3 and HAE 9 (named HAE 3a and HAE 9a) to refer to the flux values for which integrating ranges are matched to those of the CO (3--2) lines.

gmw_00004850.name: HAE 10
gmw_00004850.overview: HAE 10 is a high-redshift galaxy located on the star-forming main sequence [arXiv:2101.04691]. The galaxy lacks a [CI] line detection, and its carbon monoxide gas excitation condition is close to a starburst-like phase [arXiv:2101.04691].

sources.summary: HAE 10 lacks a [CI] detection, but its dust gas mass matches the upper limit. The authors prefer a lower CO excitation factor while noting it remains main-sequence.

quotes.quote: In HAE 10 (see Appendix app:haes for CO and spectra), line is not detected and the dust-based gas mass is consistent with the based upper limit. To be consistent, _ CO R_ 13 should be lowered by a factor of 2.7, otherwise, both the X_ CI and dust temperature need to be recalibrated. The latter case is less likely, because X_ CI needs to be lowered by 0.35 dex, and the dust temperature should be lower than 20 K unless the difference is due to a higher gas-to-dust ratio.

Meanwhile, the abundance ratio which is lower by 0.35 dex than the assumed value ( (X_ CI ) = -4.8 ) is not a commonly observed value for high-redshift galaxies. The galaxy would be a unique galaxy on the main-sequence to match with CO-based measurements. Therefore, it would be more reasonable to conclude that the CO gas excitation condition of HAE 10 is close to, but not an extreme case of, starburst-like phase lowering the _ CO R_ 13 , while the galaxy is on the main-sequence by Speagle2014 ( ( MS) = 0.10 0.29 ; Figure fig:dev top right).

gmw_00004851.name: HAE 16
gmw_00004851.overview: HAE 16 is a high-redshift, main-sequence galaxy that has been detected in both [CI] and CO emission lines [arXiv:2101.04691]. Its [CI]-to-CO line luminosity ratio suggests the presence of dense gas with properties resembling those of dusty star-forming galaxies [arXiv:2101.04691].

sources.summary: HAE 16 is detected in [CI] and CO; its [CI]-to-CO ratio suggests dense gas and DSFG-like excitation while it remains on the main sequence.

quotes.quote: We detect the line from five galaxies previously detected in CO (3--2) and CO (4--3) lines, i.e., HAE 3, 4, 8, 9 and 16.
HAE 16 has a [ C I ] to-CO (4--3) line luminosity (in L_ ) ratio ( (L_ CI10 /L_ CO43 ) ) of 0.37 0.11 .

While these galaxies are not as luminous as high- z DSFGs, the CO(4--3)-to- [ CI ] line ratios of two galaxies are comparable to them. Using a PDR model ( Pound2008, Kaufman2006 ) http://dustem.astro.umd.edu/ , the line ratio suggests the existence of n_ H 10^ 4.5 cm ^ 3 gas both in HAE 16 and HAE 3a. If the ISM properties of HAE 3a and HAE 16 resembles those of DSFGs (those with lower _ CO and R_ 13 ), the _ CO R_ 13 value may be lower than what is assumed, which leads to more consistent values from different tracers. It would also be worth noting that both galaxies are on the main-sequence ( MS = -0.02 and -0.03 for HAE 3a and HAE 16, respectively; Figure fig:dev top right).

gmw_00004852.name: SDSS J1138
gmw_00004852.overview: SDSS J1138 is a quadruply imaged quasar located at a redshift of 2.438 [arXiv:1007.3142, arXiv:astro-ph/0511026]. The system is gravitationally lensed by a foreground galaxy at a redshift of 0.445 [arXiv:1007.3142, arXiv:astro-ph/0511026], which produces a faint Einstein ring [arXiv:1007.3142].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS J1138+0314 as a quadruply imaged quasar with measured source and lens redshifts, a faint Einstein ring, and simple lens models that fit well.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1138+0314 () This quadruply imaged object (RA = 11^ h 38^ m 03^ s .70 and DEC = +03^ 14'57 99 ) was discovered in Inada2008 in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) by Inada2008 The redshifts of the quasar and the lens were measured by Cosmograil3 and are respectively equal to z_ s =2.438 and z_ l =0.445 0.001 . No detailed modeling and no time delay have ever been published for this system.
SDSS J1138+0314 () A faint Einstein ring is revealed by the deconvolution process.
The quadruply imaged quasar SDSS J1138+0314 is well reproduced by simple lens models, which is not the case for the two other quads, RX J1131-1231 and WFI J2026-4536.

sources.summary: The paper measures SDSS1138+0314 line widths and UV continuum luminosity from VLT spectra, treating absorption and its narrow, peaky profile as lower-limit width constraints.

quotes.quote: SDSS1138+0314 --- To measure the width of \ and the UV continuum luminosity we use the FORS1 VLT spectra of images B and C obtained by eigenbrod06 . The \ line profile not only shows absorption in the blue side of the line, but is also particularly narrow and `peaky' with a broad base, similar to SBS0909+523. We were unable to reasonably approximate the profile shape with a sixth-order Gauss-Hermite polynomial. However, since the S/N of this spectrum was very high (see Table tab:uv_opt_lineboundaries ), we interpolated over the absorption with a 2nd order polynomial, measured the line width directly from the interpolated data, and used this interpolated spectrum and the error spectrum formed with equation ( eqn:widtherror ) to derive uncertainties in the \ width measurement. Because of the combined effects of absorption and the narrow line profile (i.e., where the absorption could be masking the true width), we treat our \ widths as lower limits.

sources.summary: SDSS J1138+0314 has good deconvolution with possible host-related residuals, weak chromatic change, and strong broad-line-region microlensing isolating narrow flux.

quotes.quote: (h) SDSS J1138+0314: Deconvolution: The deconvolution is good with some residual flux and background excess in the vicinity of image B. Its origin is possibly associated to the QSO host galaxy. Chromaticity: There is a small chromatic change of C/B from the blue to the red part of the spectrum which is hardly explained by contamination from the lensing galaxy. The amplitude of this effect is however very small and our measurement are compatible with no CML and no DE. Broad Lines: The ML of the BLR is large in this system, isolating the narrow component of the  flux in the non microlensed fraction of the spectrum.

sources.summary: SDSS J1138 is discussed as the sample object with the lowest black-hole mass, largest optical luminosity-to-mass ratio, large half-light radii, and poor model fits.

quotes.quote: The third case, SDSS J1138, has the lowest M_ BH and the largest ratio of L_ bol,opt to M_ BH in our sample. The SS73 and forming disc models can match the luminosity of SDSS J1138, but not its large half-light radii, even with =1. The magnetically restrained models with =1 can better match the shape of its half-light radius vs. wavelength curve, but still cannot match their large values, and is a worse fit to the observed luminosity. The large ^2_ min in this object could be reduced if future observations reveal that its M_ BH is larger than the value we adopt, which is quite plausible given the considerable uncertainties on M_ BH estimates see, e.g., the comprehensive review of 2013BASI...41...61S .

sources.summary: The lensing galaxy in SDSS J1138 + 0314 is visible in HST/NICMOS imaging and has its redshift newly measured at 0.445 from high signal-to-noise spectra.

quotes.quote: For the first time we measure the redshift of the lensing galaxy in three multiply-imaged quasars: SDSS J1138 + 0314 ( =0.445 ), SDSS J1226 0006 ( =0.517 ), SDSS J1335 + 0118 ( =0.440 ), and we give a tentative estimate of the redshift of the lensing galaxy in Q 1355 2257 ( =0.701 ).

SDSS J1138 + 0314 : This quadruply imaged quasar was discovered in the course of the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) by Burles et al. ( ). This lensed quasar has a maximum image separation of 1.46 . We obtain high signal-to-noise spectra of the quasar and determine its redshift z=2.438 . There is very little doubt that this system is lensed. The lensing galaxy is seen on archival HST/NICMOS images. We measure =0.445 0.001 (Fig. SDSS1138_lens ).

gmw_00004853.name: HZ9
gmw_00004853.overview: HZ9 is a high-redshift main-sequence galaxy located in the COSMOS field [arXiv:2005.07716]. It exhibits a velocity field that may indicate a nearly face-on, rapidly rotating disk [arXiv:1709.04954], and it shows tentative [N II] emission suggesting a low gas metallicity [arXiv:1812.00006].

sources.summary: The paper studies HZ9 as a z=5.541 main-sequence COSMOS galaxy observed in multiple ALMA programmes, including Band 8 data chosen to constrain its infrared SED.

quotes.quote: We focus on four main-sequence galaxies to which we refer to as HZ4 ( z=5.544 ), HZ6 ( z=5.293 ) This galaxy is also known as LBG-1 in RIECHERS14 . , HZ9 ( z=5.541 ), and HZ10 ( z=5.657 ) in the following. These galaxies have been previously discussed by RIECHERS14 and CAPAK15 and are initially spectroscopically selected via and UV absorption lines from a large spectroscopic campaign with Keck/DEIMOS HASINGER18 on the Cosmic Evolution Survey COSMOS, SCOVILLE07 field. All galaxies have been observed with different ALMA programmes covering their rest-frame wavelengths from 100 m to 200 m (see Table tab:observations ).

All four galaxies have been observed recently as part of the ALMA programme 2018.1.00348.S (PI: Faisst) at a frequency of 406.4 GHz (Band 8). This frequency was chosen to optimise the constraints on the infrared SED and to minimise the integration time to reach a S/N of 10 . At that frequency, the Band 8 atmospheric transmission is maximised and going to higher frequencies would increase the integration times significantly. On the other hand, Band 7 observes too low frequencies for robust constraints on the infrared SED together with the archival ALMA observations see Appendix in FAISST17b . In the rest-frame of HZ4 , HZ6 , HZ9 , and HZ10 , Band 8 corresponds to wavelengths of 112.8 m , 117.3 m , 112.9 m , and 111.0 m , respectively.

sources.summary: HZ9 is modeled as a z=5.5410 ALMA [C II] galaxy whose velocity field may indicate a nearly face-on, rapidly rotating disk despite low-resolution limitations.

quotes.quote: HZ9 ( z=5.5410 ) and HZ10 ( z=5.6566 ) were originally selected as LBGs in the COSMOS field leau07 , and were observed in [ C 2 ] and rest-frame-FIR emission in cycle 1 ALMA by capa15 . Both HZ9 and HZ10 were detected in rest-frame 150 m emission ( 0.52 0.04 mJy and 1.26 0.04 mJy) and [ C 2 ] ( 1.95 0.07 Jy km s ^ 1 and 4.5 0.3 Jy km s ^ 1 ). The star formation rate (SFR) of each source was estimated using SED fits to greybody models based on the 158 m continuum flux density and source intensities in COSMOS and Ultra-Vista images.

HZ9 While the velocity gradient observed in the data velocity field is replicated in the model, the two velocity fields show different East-West extents. In particular, while the other sources in our sample show elongation along the axis perpendicular to their isovelocity lines, HZ9 is nearly circular. This difference may imply that HZ9 is a nearly face-on disk that is rotating at a considerable rate, or that the distribution of emission is affected by our low resolution.

sources.summary: The paper tentatively detects [N II] in HZ9, interprets high line ratios as low metallicity, and discusses dust continuum and possible CO tests.

quotes.quote: We tentatively detect [ N ii ] 205 m emission toward LBG-1 (at 3.4 ), and HZ9 (at 3.1 ), we confidently detect it from HZ10 (at 6 ), and we provide a constraining upper limit for HZ4 (Figures fig:mom_cont fig:spectra ).

Therefore, in analogy to the case of local dwarf galaxies, we interpret our high line ratio measurements and limits for HZ9, LBG-1 and HZ4 as indicative of low gas (and stellar) metallicity, relative to z<5 galaxies of comparable masses ( 10^ 10 M_ ).

The intriguing finding of faint [ N ii ] emission together with relatively bright dust continuum in HZ9 therefore suggests the presence of additional variables controlling the relationship between the level of dust obscuration and the metallicity (or age of the most recent stellar population) which may be critical to diagnose the interplay between gas inflows, outflows and star formation. An important next step would require measuring the CO line luminosity from HZ9 as well as achieving a detection in LBG-1. In case of relatively bright CO emission (e.g., in relation to its FIR luminosity) from HZ9, the high [ C ii ] / [ N ii ] line ratio would not be explained by the analogy to local dwarf galaxies and would point to previously unexplored star formation conditions. However, faint CO line emission from HZ9 would either suggest variations in the dust SED shape or would intriguingly suggest the possibility of significant dust-obscured star formation even in more ``typical", lower metallicity, younger high redshift galaxies. The ratio of our continuum measurements tentatively suggests higher dust temperatures in HZ9 than in HZ10.

gmw_00004854.name: IGR J18027-1455
gmw_00004854.overview: IGR J18027-1455 is a newly discovered active galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.035 [arXiv:astro-ph/0606488]. It is classified as a type 1 Seyfert galaxy with a high-energy spectral cutoff that is difficult to constrain [arXiv:astro-ph/0606488].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IGR J18027-1455 as a newly discovered type 1 Seyfert active galaxy and fits its INTEGRAL spectrum, finding a photon index near 2 and only limited cutoff constraints.

quotes.quote: IGR J18027-1455 and IGR J21247+5058 IGR J18027-1455 and IGR J21247+5058 are totally new discoveries, and although they were soon recognised as AGN candidates, only recently they have been identified with active galaxies b21 . IGR J18027-1455 is a Seyfert of type 1 at a redshift z=0.035.

We have also searched for possible high energy cut-offs in the spectrum of both galaxies (again using the cutoffpl model) but again only lower limits have been found which are E _ cut > 40 keV and E _ cut > 30 keV for IGR J18027-1455 and IGR J21247+5058 respectively. We also tried to fix the photon index to 1.8 for both sources in order to better constrain the high energy cut-off, but while for IGR J18027-1455 we were able to find a range for E _ cut of 90-786 keV, for IGR J21247+5058 the value of E _ cut is still not constrainable.

gmw_00004855.name: IGR J21247+5058
gmw_00004855.overview: IGR J21247+5058 is a Fanaroff-Riley type II broad-line radio galaxy [arXiv:1008.0722] that is aligned with a foreground Galactic star [arXiv:astro-ph/0606488]. It is a weak radio-loud active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0809.0255] characterized by complex X-ray absorption [arXiv:0809.0255, arXiv:1008.0722] and a high-energy spectral cut-off [arXiv:0809.0255].

sources.summary: The paper treats IGR J21247+5058 as a weak FR II radio-loud AGN with complex absorption, a flat initial spectrum, weak iron line, reflection, and 80 keV cut-off.

quotes.quote: In particular it has been classified as an FR II galaxy by b18 with a L _ 5GHz 10 ^ 31 erg s ^ 1 Hz ^ 1 , an order of magnitude lower than the traditional limit, making this source one of the weakest FR II galaxies reported to date. It is also evident from table 3 and figure pl3 (right panel) that IGR J21247+5058, has a very flat power law slope and consequently shows a mismatch between X-ray and soft gamma-ray data. Hence, in the case of IGR J21247+5058, the simple intrinsic photoelectric absorption has been substituted by two partial covering components ( wa _g *pcfabs*pcfabs*(po+zga) in XSPEC , see results in table 3B); when this model is applied, the power law index becomes steeper and the X-ray/gamma-ray match is more acceptable (see figure 21247_3c390_bb left panel).

3C 111 and IGR J21247+5058; the cut-off is localised at around 100 keV for 3C 111 and 80 keV for IGR J21247+5058. The reflection component is also significantly required (at more than 99 confidence level) in 3C 111 and IGR J21247+5058, the two AGN which also have a high energy cut-off; since in these two objects the iron line is very weak or possibly even absent we expect to observe negligible or low reflection and we thus suspect that a better fit is achieved when both R and E _ cut are left free to vary (see next section). The intrinsic absorption measured in our sources is generally small or absent ( 2 10 ^ 21 atoms cm ^ 2 ), except for 3C 111 and IGR J21247+5058, and also similar to the values found for radio quiet type 1 AGN; in 3C 111 the extra absorption is probably due to intervening material between us and the source, while in the case of IGR J21247+5058 complex absorption is strongly required by the data.

sources.summary: The paper describes IGR J21247+5058 as a newly identified active galaxy, probably a background radio galaxy aligned with a Galactic star, and reports limited INTEGRAL cutoff constraints.

quotes.quote: IGR J18027-1455 and IGR J21247+5058 IGR J18027-1455 and IGR J21247+5058 are totally new discoveries, and although they were soon recognised as AGN candidates, only recently they have been identified with active galaxies b21 .

IGR J21247+5058, instead, is a radio source with a quite puzzling optical spectroscopic appearance showing a broad, redshifted H complex at z=0.020 superimposed onto a normal F/G-type Galactic star continuum. These features, together with the spatially coincident extended radio emission, suggest a chance alignment between a relatively nearby star and a background radio galaxy.

We have also searched for possible high energy cut-offs in the spectrum of both galaxies (again using the cutoffpl model) but again only lower limits have been found which are E _ cut > 40 keV and E _ cut > 30 keV for IGR J18027-1455 and IGR J21247+5058 respectively. We also tried to fix the photon index to 1.8 for both sources in order to better constrain the high energy cut-off, but while for IGR J18027-1455 we were able to find a range for E _ cut of 90-786 keV, for IGR J21247+5058 the value of E _ cut is still not constrainable.

sources.summary: The paper presents Suzaku X-ray spectra of 4C 50.55, a bright FR II broad-line radio galaxy, finding complex absorption, weak jet contribution, and high-Eddington accretion.

quotes.quote: 4C 50.55 (IGR J21247+5058) is a Fanaroff-Riley type II broad line radio galaxy (BLRG), discovered by INTEGRAL in its first survey catalogue Bir04 .

Though being the brightest BLRG in the hard X-ray sky above 10 keV, 4C 50.55, located at (l,b) = (93.32,0.3937) , had been unrecognized as a bright X-ray source until the INTEGRAL and Swift era, due to the obscuration by the Galactic plane.
With a typical bolometric correction factor ( L_ bol /L_ 2-10 keV = 30), we estimate the fraction of Eddington luminosity of 4C 50.55 to be L_ bol /L_ Edd 0.4 .

gmw_00004856.name: GRS 1734-292
gmw_00004856.overview: GRS 1734-292 is a luminous Seyfert 1 active galactic nucleus located near the Galactic plane at a redshift of z=0.0214 [arXiv:1612.05871, arXiv:astro-ph/0405259]. It exhibits a clear bipolar jet-like radio structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0406300] and has shown evidence of X-ray intensity variations [arXiv:astro-ph/0606488].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GRS 1734-292 as a Seyfert 1 active galactic nucleus and argues that INTEGRAL, radio, and X-ray evidence make it a likely counterpart to 3EG J 1736-2908.

quotes.quote: The possible identification by INTEGRAL of the EGRET source 3EG J 1736-2908 with the active galactic nucleus GRS 1734-292 is discussed. The latter was discovered in 1990 and later identified with a Seyfert 1 galaxy. At the time of the compilation of the 3rd EGRET Catalog, it was not considered as a possible counterpart of the source 3EG J 1736-2908 , which remained unidentified. A detailed multiwavelength study of the EGRET error circle is presented, by including archival radio, soft- and hard-X observations, suggesting that GRS 1734-292 could be a likely counterpart of 3EG J 1736-2908 , even though this poses very interesting questions about the production mechanisms of -rays with energies greater than 100 MeV.

GRS 1734-292 was optically classified by Mart et al. (1998) as a Seyfert 1, with m_ V =21.0 0.3 , on the basis of the strong and very broad emission of blended H and N [ II ] lines (the FWZI of the blended line is 2 10^ 4 km s ^ 1 ). The redshift was measured as z=0.0214 . In a VLA observation, GRS 1734-292 was resolved with a clear bipolar jet-like structure (Mart et al. 1998). Its radio spectrum could be well described by a power law S_ ^ with =0.75 0.03 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes GRS 1734-292 as a nearby Seyfert galaxy, measuring its X-ray continuum, low high-energy cutoff, warm absorber, iron line, reflection, and unusually cool corona.

quotes.quote: GRS1734-292 is a Seyfert 1 galaxy, located near the Galactic plane at z=0.0214 . The NuSTAR spectrum ( 3-80 keV) is dominated by a primary power-law continuum with =1.65 0.05 and a high-energy cutoff E_c=53^ +11 _ 8 keV, one of the lowest measured by NuSTAR in a Seyfert galaxy. Comptonization models show a temperature of the coronal plasma of kT_e=11.9^ +1.2 _ 0.9 keV and an optical depth, assuming a slab geometry, =2.98^ +0.16 _ 0.19 or a similar temperature and =6.7^ +0.3 _ 0.4 assuming a spherical geometry.

The nearby ( z=0.0214 , corresponding to a distance of 87 Mpc) Seyfert Galaxy GRS 1734-292 is a good candidate for such measurements. With an X-ray luminosity approaching 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 in the 0.5-4.5 keV energy band ( ), it is one of the most luminous AGNs within 100 Mpc ( ; ). GRS 1734-292 was originally discovered by the ART-P telescope aboard the GRANAT satellite ( ) and is located only 1.8^ from the Galactic Centre.

As obvious, given the low coronal temperature, GRS 1734-292 is located far away from the region of pair production in the _e plane, and is also located well below the e^--e^- coupling line (i.e. the line below which the electron-electron coupling time scale is shorter than the Compton cooling time scale).

sources.summary: The paper measures GRS 1734--292's broadband X-ray spectrum, describing it as a luminous obscured Seyfert 1 nucleus with a power-law AGN spectrum and redshift z=0.0214.

quotes.quote: Based on a deep survey of the Galactic Center region performed with the INTEGRAL observatory, we measured for the first time the hard X-ray (20--200 keV) spectrum of the Seyfert 1 galaxy \ located in the direction of the Galactic Center. We extended the spectrum to lower energies using archival GRANAT and ASCA observations. The broadband X-ray spectrum is similar to those of other nearby luminous AGNs, having a power law shape without cutoff up to at least 100 keV.

However, subsequent optical spectroscopic observations surprisingly revealed mmc+98 very strong, broad emission lines demonstrating that \ is the nucleus of a Seyfert 1 galaxy at a redshift z=0.0214 . The galaxy itself has so far escaped observation because of 6 magnitudes of visual absorption along the line of sight through the Galactic plane. With its X-ray luminosity approaching 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 (2--10 keV), \ is one of the 5 most luminous AGNs within 100 Mpc of us e.g. pmb+82,sr04 , which makes it a very interesting object for investigation.

sources.summary: The paper revisits GRS 1734-292 as a Seyfert galaxy, finding ASCA/INTEGRAL spectra require a cutoff below 100 keV, possible intensity variation, and evidence against a blazar interpretation.

quotes.quote: Spectral analysis has so far been reported for 5 Seyfert galaxies listed in this initial sample, 4 of type 2 (NGC 4945, Centaurus A, Circinus Galaxy and NGC 6300, b32 ) and one of type 1 (GRS 1734-292, b30 ) as well as for the Blazar PKS 1830-211 b11 .

GRS 1734-292 In this case ASCA/GIS and INTEGRAL/ISGRI data provide a broadband spectrum which is equally well fitted by an absorbed power law with no reflection (R=0) or with reflection R equal to 1 (see Table 3); in either case the primary power law component requires a cut-off at an energy below 100 keV (see figure grs_cont ). The fit with no reflection is slightly better ( ^ 2 =130.7 for 78 d.o.f.) and gives parameters ( =1.6, N _H =0.9 10 ^ 22 cm ^ 2 and E _ cut =44 keV) which, within uncertainties, are compatible with those reported in Table 3. In either case, the constant C introduced to account for instrument cross-calibration or variability is in the range 1.4-1.9, thus suggesting a change in the source intensity: a variation in flux of up to a factor of 2 is compatible with the X-ray time history of the source as reported in b30 . Our results are compatible with the best fit parameters obtained by these authors, except for the lower value of the cut-off energy which was poorly constrained to be above 100 keV in their analysis. If confirmed, the presence of a high energy cut-off at this value could put the basis for another argument against the hypothesis that GRS1734-292 is a Blazar type of AGNs (see discussion in b30 ), and thus question its association with the unidentified EGRET gamma-ray source 3EG J1736-2908 b13,b30 .

sources.summary: The paper models 3EG J1736-2908 as a Seyfert-associated gamma-ray source with variable EGRET emission, no HESS counterpart, and a broken-power-law spectrum.

quotes.quote: We conclude with a summary in Tab. tab:SRC of the source locations and integrated gamma-ray fluxes above 0.1, 1 and 5 GeV 3EG J1736-2908 Originally classified as unidentified , after INTEGRAL observations this EGRET source was identified with the X-ray source GRS1734-292 , associated with the active Galactic nucleus of a Seyfert 1 galaxy at a redshift of 0.0214 and 1.8 degrees from the Galactic center, having both radio jet and hard X-ray emissions . The analysis of ref. indicates that the EGRET source 3EG J1736-2908 exhibits significant time variability; Here, we consider the source median emission . 3EG J1736-2908 has no positional counterpart at TeV energies in the HESS survey of the inner Galaxy , which leads to an upper limit on the source above 100 GeV. Ref. showed that the best fit to 3EG J1736-2908 consists of a broken power law. Here we follow the analysis of ref. , and adopt for 3EG J1736-2908 the following spectrum: d N d E = 7.5 10^ 11 cm ^2 \ s \ MeV E 1 \ GeV ^ , =1.44 \ (E<1 \ GeV ), \ =5.7 \ (E>1 \ GeV ).

gmw_00004857.name: NGC 2998
gmw_00004857.overview: NGC 2998 is a massive spiral galaxy located approximately 60 to 67.4 Mpc away [arXiv:1501.06424, arXiv:2002.10076]. It features a nearly exponential disk with a minor bulge [arXiv:1501.06424] and resides in a dense group where it may interact gravitationally with NGC 3008 [arXiv:1603.02685, arXiv:2002.10076].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2998 as the most distant sample galaxy, adopts a Hubble-flow distance, uses K86 photometry, and notes that its symmetric gas field is sparsely sampled.

quotes.quote: NGC 2998 NGC 2998 is the most distant galaxy of the sample. The radial velocity corrected for Virgo-centric flow gives a Hubble distance of 67.4 Mpc. Considering the deviations from the Hubble flow, this distance is nearly as accurate as the Cepheid distances for the galaxies more close by. Photometry is presented in K86; the galaxy is close to exponential with only a minor bulge.

An accurate rotation curve has been determined by Broeils (1992a), which has unfortunately not been published in the refereed literature. The velocity field is very symmetric and regular and the gas extends far beyond the optical edge. Because of its large distance the structure is not as well resolved as that of the more nearby galaxies and consequently the rotation curve is more sparsely sampled. Moreover, the observed kinematics in the inner regions is affected by beam smearing and cannot be used.

sources.summary: The paper gives NGC 2998 coordinates, distance, and luminosity, then reports that an external field improves the rotation-curve fit and is plausibly sourced by NGC 3008.

quotes.quote: (10) NGC 2998 : This galaxy with right ascension and declination of ( 9^h48^m43.6^s, 44^d04^m53^s ) is located at a distance of about 61^ +8 _ 16 Mpc from us , and the luminosity of the galaxy is L_V=2.69 10^ 10 L_ n2998-dm, n3521-lu, n2998-md . A better RC fit with an external field of a_e=0.220^ +0.050 _ 0.065 a_0 is achieved. This external field can reduce ^2 from 2.7 to 1.2. NGC 3008 is probably a good candidate as a source of the external field for NGC 2998. NGC 3008 n3521-jj, n3008-jj with a luminosity of L_V=1.33 ( 0.27) 10^ 10 produces an external field of about a_e=1.06 10^ 3 a_0 (with the maximum and minimum values of a_e=8.2 10^ 2 a_0 and a_e=2.83 10^ 4 a_0 , respectively), which has a good overlap with theoretical value within the 95 confidence interval.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2998 as the massive spiral host/group galaxy near the dwarf pair, finding a dense environment and a regular, unperturbed H I disk.

quotes.quote: The most massive galaxy in the group, NGC 2998, is a MW mass spiral. NGC 2998 group is a relatively dense group which have at least 7 member galaxies of stellar mass larger than 10 ^ 8 M _ within the 300 kpc sky-projected radius and relative line-of-sight radial velocity 300 from the central galaxy, NGC 2998. is the nearest neighbour to the spiral galaxy NGC2998 at a sky-projected distance of 105 kpc We use NED quoted distance of 60 Mpc for the NGC 2998 group.

gmw_00004858.name: NGC 5585
gmw_00004858.overview: NGC 5585 is a small, low-surface-brightness spiral galaxy [arXiv:1509.02356, arXiv:2210.09383] with a Large-Magellanic-Cloud-like morphology [arXiv:1907.02934] and a slightly warped disk [arXiv:1501.06424, arXiv:2210.09383]. The galaxy exhibits pronounced asymmetry [arXiv:1509.02356], very little dust extinction [arXiv:0902.3195], and an essentially constant star formation rate [arXiv:1509.02356].

sources.summary: For NGC 5585, the paper adopts the Hubble distance, uses Cote et al. BVR photometry and rotation data, and notes an inner bumpy rotation feature matching a central photometric cusp.

quotes.quote: NGC 5585 In this case the Hubble distance of 6.2 Mpc is the most reliable value available. Photometry in the B,V, and R-bands has been obtained by C ot e et al. (1991) of which the R-band profile is used to calculate the rotation curve of the disc. This profile has been integrated to give the total R-band luminosity. An rotation curve is determined by C ot e et al. from a regular though slightly warped velocity field.

For radii within 120 \ the rotation of Blais-Ouellette et al. (1999) based on H Fabry-Perot observations is used. That inner rotation curve shows a specific bumpy feature which can be matched with the moderate central cusp in the photometry.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5585 as a very late low-surface-brightness galaxy with offset centers, the sample's bluest colors, nearly constant star formation, and young ring/bar populations.

quotes.quote: NGC 5585. This is a very late-type small galaxy with low surface brightness. It has a pronounced asymmetry: the centers of the galaxy, of the bar, and of the ring do not coincide bru10 . It is the bluest galaxy of our sample. The SFR in NGC 5585 is essentially constant: the positions of the components in the two-color diagrams correspond to _1 = 7-20 Gyr. The colors of the ring and outer parts of the bar suggest that the ages of their stellar populations do not exceed 100 Myr.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes 40 selected pairs in NGC 5585, describing its Large-Magellanic-Cloud-like morphology and an approximately constant steep propagation slope.

quotes.quote: The final sample includes 503 SC-H ii R pairs in NGC 628, 70 pairs in NGC 3184, 254 pairs in NGC 3726, 40 pairs in NGC 5585, and 577 pairs in NGC 6946.
NGC 5585 has rather the Large Magellanic Cloud type.

The dependence in the peculiar galaxy NGC 5585 shows the approximately constant slope 1.4-1.6 at all separation ranges. Unfortunately, the coefficient is determined with a very large error. SC-H ii R pairs in the asymmetric galaxy NGC 3726 show a rather chaotic distribution on the diagram t - S . This scatter can be caused by various parameters of the medium in the asymmetric disc of the galaxy. Nevertheless, the mean slope at a general range of S is equal to 1.22, that is close to the mean slope in NGC 5585.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5585 as a slightly warped spiral with an H I disk beyond the DSS optical detection and a barely resolved, possibly counter-rotating cloud candidate.

quotes.quote: This galaxy is a spiral galaxy with an \ disk that extends significantly beyond the optical detection in the DSS image and that is slightly warped. Besides the target, we detect a single cloud candidate at a projected distance of 58 kpc. The cloud candidate shows a hint of a velocity structure but as it is barely resolved this could be an artifact of the masking. If this cloud is part of the dynamical system describing NGC 5585 it would be counter rotating.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5585 as the bluest, smallest Sd galaxy in the sample, with little extinction and a V-H color map showing much less extinction than comparable nuclei.

quotes.quote: The colour maps show dust lanes in all galaxies except for NGC 5585, spiral arms with younger stars, inner spirals, nuclear clusters, etc.
The galaxy of morphological type 7 (Sd, NGC 5585) is by far the bluest, and the smallest and has little extinction.
The only galaxy in common with B oker et al. (2002) is NGC 5585, which is representative for the galaxies in this group, and clearly shows much less extinction in its V-H color map.

gmw_00004859.name: SDP.9
gmw_00004859.overview: SDP.9 is a gravitationally lensed, high-redshift starburst galaxy [arXiv:1701.05901] that features a complete Einstein ring in its near-infrared emission [arXiv:1311.5893]. Observations of its dense molecular gas tracers suggest that a large fraction of its molecular gas is highly dense [arXiv:1701.05901].

sources.summary: The paper studies SDP.9 as a lensed high-redshift starburst with detected dense-gas tracers, PDR-like HNC/HCN ratios, excited CO/HCN/HCO+ ladders, and a large dense molecular gas fraction.

quotes.quote: We present ALMA and VLA detections of the dense molecular gas tracers HCN, HCO ^+ and HNC in two lensed, high-redshift starbursts selected from the Herschel ATLAS survey: H ATLAS J090740.0 004200 (SDP.9, z 1.6 ) and H ATLAS J091043.1 000321 (SDP.11, z 1.8 ). ALMA observed the J = 3-2 transitions in both sources, while the VLA observed the J = 1-0 transitions in SDP.9. We have detected all observed HCN and HCO ^+ lines in SDP.9 and SDP.11, and also HNC(3--2) in SDP.9. The amplification factors for both galaxies have been determined from sub-arcsec resolution CO and dust emission observations carried out with NOEMA and the SMA. The HNC(1--0)/HCN(1--0) line ratio in SDP.9 suggests the presence of photon-dominated regions, as it happens to most local (U)LIRGs.

The CO, HCN and HCO ^+ SLEDs of SDP.9 are compatible to those found for many local, infrared (IR) bright galaxies, indicating that the molecular gas in local and high-redshift dusty starbursts can have similar excitation conditions.

Actually, the L^ ' _ HCN(1-0) /L^ ' _ CO(1-0) ratio (which is significantly higher than the average ratio found for (U)LIRGs in the local Universe) suggest that a large fraction of the molecular gas in SDP.9 is in the form of dense gas.

sources.summary: The paper describes J090740.0 004200 as a lens system dominated by a simple doubly imaged source whose near-infrared emission forms an Einstein ring.

quotes.quote: J090740.0 004200 (ID9): This lens system is dominated by a single doubly-imaged source with a simple morphology lying to the north of the lens galaxy centroid. The double imaging is consistent with the SMA data but the emission appears to originate from a different location in the source compared to what is observed in the near-IR HST data. The reconstructed source F110W-F160W colour map shows a reddening gradient which points from the near-IR source towards the SMA source centroid. Some of the fainter emission from the source in the near-IR crosses the caustic and gives rise to the observed complete Einstein ring.

gmw_00004860.name: SDP.11
gmw_00004860.overview: SDP.11 is a lensed, high-redshift starburst galaxy [arXiv:1701.05901] that exhibits a complete Einstein ring [arXiv:1311.5893]. This lensed emission contains structured small-scale knots [arXiv:1311.5893], while the galaxy's dense molecular gas ratios suggest its interstellar medium is dominated by photon-dominated regions [arXiv:1701.05901].

sources.summary: The paper finds J091043.1 000321 has a complete Einstein ring shaped by strong external shear, with structured lensed source emission and possible residual lens-galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: J091043.1 000321 (ID11): Like ID9, this system has a complete Einstein ring. The ring's significant ellipticity is the result of a relatively strong external shear field of strength =0.23 . The direction of this shear points almost exactly to the centre of a nearby edge-on spiral galaxy to the NW located at a redshift of z=0.39 0.09 (see N13). The implication is therefore that this spiral is almost entirely responsible for the shear perturbation. We attempted a model where external shear was replaced by a second singular isothermal lens to represent the spiral's total mass but found no significant improvement in the fit.

The reconstructed source exhibits clear small scale structure in both filters, required to fit the observed structure in the ring. The majority of the emission observed in the ring comes from a doubly imaged source lying just outside the caustic and the smaller-scale structure comes from smaller knots of emission in the source, some of which are quadruply imaged. As the residual plot in Figure recon indicates, the model image does not perfectly account for the observed features in the ring and this is also reflected in the fact that the model fit is only marginally acceptable.

sources.summary: The paper treats SDP.11 as a lensed high-redshift starburst, detecting HCN and HCO+ with ALMA, modeling lens amplification, and finding IR/dense-line and HCN/HNC ratios like star-forming galaxies.

quotes.quote: We present ALMA and VLA detections of the dense molecular gas tracers HCN, HCO ^+ and HNC in two lensed, high-redshift starbursts selected from the Herschel ATLAS survey: H ATLAS J090740.0 004200 (SDP.9, z 1.6 ) and H ATLAS J091043.1 000321 (SDP.11, z 1.8 ). ALMA observed the J = 3-2 transitions in both sources, while the VLA observed the J = 1-0 transitions in SDP.9. We have detected all observed HCN and HCO ^+ lines in SDP.9 and SDP.11, and also HNC(3--2) in SDP.9. The amplification factors for both galaxies have been determined from sub-arcsec resolution CO and dust emission observations carried out with NOEMA and the SMA.
We obtain that the correlation between total IR ( L_ IR ) and dense line ( L_ dense ) luminosity in SDP.9 and SDP.11 and local star-forming galaxies can be represented by a single relation.

The HCN/HNC ratios in SDP.9 and SDP.11 suggest that these galaxies are dominated by photon-dominated regions, similar to what it happens to most (U)LIRGs in the local Universe. Similarly, the HCN/HCO ^+ ratios in both sources are similar to those found for local (U)LIRGs, suggesting that the dense molecular ISM in local and some high-redshift starbursts might be very similar.

gmw_00004861.name: PKS 1740 517
gmw_00004861.overview: PKS 1740 517 is a young, gigahertz-peaked spectrum radio galaxy at redshift z = 0.44 [arXiv:1511.03067, arXiv:1810.08507] that is undergoing a tidal interaction with a lower-mass companion [arXiv:1810.08507]. The host galaxy contains a dense gaseous environment [arXiv:1511.03067] with a cold circumnuclear gas reservoir [arXiv:1810.08507] and a possible jet-driven neutral gas outflow [arXiv:1503.01265].

sources.summary: The paper reports intrinsic H I absorption in the host of PKS B1740 517, finding double-peaked oxygen lines, an obscured AGN, complex neutral gas, and possible jet-driven outflow.

quotes.quote: The absorption line is detected at z = 0.44 towards the GHz-peaked spectrum radio source PKS B1740 517 and demonstrates ASKAP's excellent capability for performing a future wide-field survey for H i absorption at these redshifts. Optical spectroscopy and imaging using the Gemini-South telescope indicates that the H i gas is intrinsic to the host galaxy of the radio source. The narrow [ O iii ] emission lines show clear double-peaked structure, indicating either large-scale outflow or rotation of the ionized gas. Archival data from the XMM-Newton satellite exhibit an absorbed X-ray spectrum that is consistent with a high column density obscuring medium around the active galactic nucleus. The H i absorption profile is complex, with four distinct components ranging in width from 5 to 300 km s ^ 1 and fractional depths from 0.2 to 20 per cent.

In addition to systemic H i gas, in a circumnuclear disc or ring structure aligned with the radio jet, we find evidence for a possible broad outflow of neutral gas moving at a radial velocity of v 300 km s ^ 1 . We infer that the expanding young radio source ( t_ age 2500 yr) is cocooned within a dense medium and may be driving circumnuclear neutral gas in an outflow of 1 M _ yr ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper reports ASKAP-BETA H i absorption toward PKS B1740 517, a young gigahertz-peaked radio source whose complex profile and X-ray absorption indicate dense gas around the host.

quotes.quote: At present our observations with the BETA telescope are ongoing, but we have recently achieved our first discovery of H i absorption with the ASKAP-BETA telescope ( Allison:2015 ). The background radio source, PKS B1740 517, is in the sample and has a gigahertz-peaked spectrum with a spectral age of approximately 2500 yrs. The absorption seen at z = 0.4413 is characteristically complex, with a deep narrow component that is almost unresolved at 5 km s ^ 1 and broader components with more typical widths (see Fig. figure:PKS1740-517_spectrum ). The complexity of this profile is indicative of gas associated with the host galaxy, where the different velocity components trace either the spatial structure of the radio source, or the gas kinematics caused by the AGN. Association with the host galaxy was confirmed by optical spectroscopy from follow up observations with the Gemini South Telescope, providing the first spectroscopic redshift for this source.

This young radio source is likely shrouded within a dense gaseous environment through which we are seeing absorption of the continuum, an interpretation that is supported by the strong absorption of soft X-ray emission seen in archival XMM-Newton data.

sources.summary: The paper detects CO and H I absorption in PKS B1740 517, inferring cold circumnuclear gas, tidal stripping, and delayed AGN triggering after interaction with a companion.

quotes.quote: We have used the Atacama Large Millimetre Array (ALMA) to detect a rare example of redshifted ^ 12 CO (2-1) absorption in PKS B1740 517, a young ( t 1.6 10^ 3 yr) and luminous ( L_ 5 GHz 6.6 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 ) radio galaxy at z = 0.44 that is undergoing a tidal interaction with at least one lower-mass companion. The coincident H i 21-cm and molecular absorption have very similar line profiles and reveal a reservoir of cold gas ( M_ gas 10^ 7 10^ 8 M_ ), likely distributed in a disc or ring within a few kiloparsecs of the nucleus. A separate H i component is kinematically distinct and has a very narrow line width ( v_ FWHM 5 km s ^ 1 ), consistent with a single diffuse cloud of cold ( T_ k 100 K) atomic gas. The ^ 12 CO (2-1) absorption is not associated with this component, which suggests that the cloud is either much smaller than 100 pc along our sight-line and/or located in low-metallicity gas that was possibly tidally stripped from the companion.

We argue that the gas reservoir in PKS B1740 517 may have accreted onto the host galaxy 50 Myr before the young radio AGN was triggered, but has only recently reached the nucleus. This is consistent with the paradigm that powerful luminous radio galaxies are triggered by minor mergers and interactions with low-mass satellites and represent a brief, possibly recurrent, active phase in the life cycle of massive early type galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper uses PKS 1740 517 as a prior BETA H I absorber at z=0.44, noting its diverse multi-wavelength properties and hard, soft-X-ray-absorbed spectrum.

quotes.quote: Despite its modest sensitivity, BETA has already highlighted the scope for exploration of H i in this unique parameter space with the discovery of associated H i absorption towards PKS 1740 517 at z = 0.44 2015MNRAS.453.1249A,Allison:2016ds . PKS 1740 517 presented a particularly interesting case study given its diverse multi-wavelength properties. It features a hard X-ray spectrum that is absorbed at the soft end ( < 2 keV), suggesting the presence of dense hydrogen along the line of sight to the source of X-ray emission.

sources.summary: PKS 1740 517 is discussed as an associated H I absorber in a young GPS radio AGN, supporting recently switched-on obscured-galaxy evolution models.

quotes.quote: PKS 1740 517 The H i absorption detected toward PKS 1740 517 was first reported and analysed by , and confirmed to be associated.
Like PKS 1829 718, PKS 1740 517 lacked spectroscopic redshift information prior to the detection of H i absorption.

As GPS radio sources are believed to be young or re-triggered radio jets, and PKS 1740 517 has an estimated radio age of 2,500 years Allison2015 , we find support for the galaxy evolution model in which postulates that such obscured galaxies represent a short transitionary stage in galaxy evolution during which the AGN has only recently switched on Glikman2012 .

gmw_00004862.name: PKS 1547 79
gmw_00004862.overview: PKS 1547 79 is a broad-line radio galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.483 that exhibits a double-lobed radio structure [arXiv:1707.01542]. It is an interacting system featuring a double nucleus, a 27 kpc tidal tail, and several diffuse companion galaxies [arXiv:1008.2683, arXiv:1707.01542].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1547-79 as a powerful radio source whose GMOS-S morphology shows disturbed structure, companions, shells, fans, dust, tails, bridges, or multiple nuclei indicating interaction or merger activity.

quotes.quote: We classify this BLRG/FRII as a double nucleus system, since the radio source has a fainter companion 9 kpc to the South of the nucleus, as revealed by our GMOS-S image (Figure pks1547_online ). In addition, our GMOS-S optical image reveals a tidal tail extending 27 kpc to the NE of the galaxy center of surface brightness _V =24.3 mag arcsec ^ 2 . In both the optical and the NIR, PKS 1547-79 appears as a clear interacting system, surrounded by several diffuse companions. Note that the companion galaxy 46 kpc to the West of the radio galaxy host has weak emission lines that suggest a similar redshift to PKS1547-79.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1547 79 as a linearly polarized, double-lobed broad-line radio galaxy at z=0.483, likely interacting with companions and showing high intrinsic X-ray absorption.

quotes.quote: PKS 1547 79 A source known to be linearly polarised at a level > 5 across the range from 10-30 cm wavelengths Gardner:1966wg , it was also included in the Kuehr:1981wo sample based on its 5 GHz flux density being > 1 Jy. It was imaged at 2 '' resolution by Duncan:1992ut revealing a double-lobed structure and central radio source. It is part of the 2 Jy sample of Tadhunter:1993kt who confirmed an optical redshift of z = 0.483, and has been identified as a broad line radio galaxy (BLRG) likely interacting with nearby companions. PKS 1547 79 was also part of an XMM-Newton study conducted by Mingo:2014bx , who found a high intrinsic N_ H from X-ray absorption of 9.9 10 ^ 23 cm ^ 2 potentially indicating a Compton-thick medium surrounding the AGN.

gmw_00004863.name: PKS 1657 298
gmw_00004863.overview: PKS 1657 298 is a little-studied radio galaxy identified as a compact double source [arXiv:1707.01542]. It exhibits a neutral hydrogen absorption line [arXiv:1707.01542] and has been classified as a potential type-1 quasar based on its X-ray luminosity [arXiv:2106.14551].

sources.summary: The paper reports a new H I absorption detection toward PKS 1657 298, a little-studied compact double radio galaxy, and links its high X-ray hardness, reddening, and z=0.42 absorption to dense gas.

quotes.quote: PKS 1657 298 This is a largely unknown and unstudied radio galaxy, identified as a compact double at VLBI-resolution. Due to its low Galactic latitude ( +7^ ), there has been no optical counterpart identified and no previously known redshift for the galaxy. During a study of ROSAT X-ray sources in globular clusters, 2001A&A...368..137V identified the X-ray counterpart for this galaxy within the field of view to a positional accuracy of 3 '' . Petrov:2012if provide the most accurate position for this source based on VLBI data at 22 GHz.

From our BETA pilot study, we discovered a new H i absorption line towards PKS 1657 298. Of the five sources observed, this radio source featured the largest X-ray hardness ratio of 424 (whereas the others were closer to 100), thus making this detection of H i absorption consistent with our findings in Section propcompare . As described in Section betaprop , there is very little known about this source in general, most probably due to its inconvenient location at a Galactic position of l = 353.7 ^ , b = 7.4 ^ , making it an unlikely target for optical and infrared studies.

sources.summary: The paper uses logistic regression to classify the z_abs=0.42 absorption toward PKS 1657--298 as associated; K-band redshift supports this, while X-ray luminosity suggests a type-1 quasar with uncertainty.

quotes.quote: We use the logistic regression model to classify the z_ abs =0.42 absorption towards PKS 1657--298 which, in the absence of an optical spectrum, is unknown mas+17 . Out of 1000 trials, the absorption is flagged as associated in 970 cases, which is consistent with mas+17 , who, from the K band magnitude--redshift correlation for galaxies, estimate a source redshift of z_ em z_ abs . However, this assumes the K band magnitude of a near-by source and the X-ray luminosity of L_ X 5 10^ 44  ergs s ^ 1 suggests that the continuum source is a type-1 object (quasar), for which the K band magnitude--redshift relation does not hold cm19 . Thus, although, we also find the absorption to be associated, there is some uncertainty in the mas+17 classification.

gmw_00004864.name: PKS 2008 068
gmw_00004864.overview: PKS 2008 068 is a radio galaxy located at a confirmed optical redshift of z = 0.547 [arXiv:1707.01542]. It is classified as a gigahertz-peaked spectrum source with a spectral peak at 1.6 GHz, exhibiting weak polarization and a compact structure on VLBI scales [arXiv:1707.01542].

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS 2008 068 as a relatively well-studied compact galaxy in the BETA sample, noting its GPS spectral peak, weak polarization, VLBI compactness, and optical redshift.

quotes.quote: PKS 2008 068 One of the more well-studied sources in our sample, this galaxy falls into the category of GPS sources with its peak at 1.6 GHz. Similar to our other sources, it is compact on VLBI-scales. It is known to be a weakly-polarised source Berge:1972hr , at a level of 1 at 10 GHz Inoue:1995us . It has a confirmed optical redshift z = 0.547 Snellen:2002hh .

gmw_00004865.name: PKS 2154 11
gmw_00004865.overview: PKS 2154 11 is a poorly known radio source with an extended structure at 20 GHz [arXiv:1707.01542]. Although its WISE colors place it in the QSO class, the host galaxy lacks a known optical counterpart and redshift [arXiv:1707.01542].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 2154 11 as a poorly known radio source whose host galaxy lacks an optical counterpart and redshift, while its WISE colours place it in the QSO class.

quotes.quote: PKS 2154 11 A mostly obscure radio source with little known about the properties of the host galaxy. It has been detected across the radio spectrum from 80 MHz to 20 GHz, and was reported as extended at 20 GHz Murphy:2010ej . Its WISE colours place it in the QSO class of Figure fig:wiseplot , but there is no known optical counterpart and no reported redshift.

gmw_00004866.name: HSC J084928+000949
gmw_00004866.overview: HSC J084928+000949 is an interacting galaxy pair that is classified as a highly probable strong gravitational lens system [arXiv:1604.08215]. The system has an estimated Einstein radius of 0.72 arcseconds, which encloses a predicted total mass of 10^10.59 solar masses [arXiv:1604.08215].

sources.summary: The paper identifies HSC J084928+000949 as an interacting galaxy-scale lens system with a valid mass model, estimating total, stellar, and dark matter mass within its 0.72 arcsec Einstein radius while noting follow-up is needed.

quotes.quote: There is one system with an interacting galaxy pair, HSC J084928+000949, that has a valid mass model. We predict the total mass enclosed by the Einstein radius of 0.72 ( 1.65 kpc) for this new expected lens system to be 10^ 10.59 M_ . Using the photometry in the grizy bands of the HSC survey and stellar population synthesis modeling with a Salpeter stellar initial mass function, we estimate the stellar mass within the Einstein radius to be 10^ 10.46 M_ . We thus find a dark matter mass fraction within the Einstein radius of 25 .

Even though we have a sensible lens mass model of J084928+000949, we classify this object as a highly probably lens system rather than a definite lens. Spectroscopically there are two distinct redshifts identified. However, since the GAMA spectroscopy does not have sufficient spatial resolution and the foreground lens galaxy also shows emission lines, it is not fully clear whether the background source has multiply lensed images. For the purpose of lens modeling, we have decided to use certain bluish features as lensed images of the background source, but high-resolution imaging or spatially resolved spectroscopy is needed to unambiguously confirm this potential lens system.
